gmw_00003933.overview: ESO 359-G002 is a faint low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9806331] classified as a dwarf elliptical [arXiv:1105.4809] or lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0607434]. It features an unresolved nucleus [arXiv:1105.4809], irregular central isophotes caused by dust [arXiv:1105.4809], and a slowly rising mean velocity curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0607434].

sources.summary: FCC 335 is the diffuse/dwarf elliptical in the sample, with an unresolved nucleus, irregular dusty central isophotes, and measured outer rotation and velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: FCC 335 It is the only diffuse/dwarf elliptical galaxy in the sample of spo2010 Its prominent nucleus is not resolved in our spectroscopic observations (we measure FWHM 0.5 arcsec). The central isophotes are irregular, which is caused by the presence of dust, clearly seen on the composite image The ACS Fornax Cluster Survey, https://www.cfa.harvard.edu/ ajordan/ACSFCS/Gallery.html . We could fit the spectra a little bit further than 1 , where the rotation velocity is V = 30 . The central velocity dispersion, = 25 , rises to 30 at 6 arcsec (on the FORS2 profile; the kinematics on the GMOS profile are less precise due to the lower resolution). Such drops are frequent in the dE galaxies; we note that it extends at a significantly larger scale than the nucleus.

sources.summary: FCC 335 is a faint low-surface-brightness galaxy with low dispersion, marginal outer dispersion increase, a rotation-curve decrease, and peak rotation near 0.5 effective radii.

quotes.quote: FCC 335 This is a faint galaxy, with a roughly flat VDP of 40 km s ^ 1 , close to the lower limit of our measurements. There is marginal evidence for an increase on the W side of the VDP of 10 km s ^ 1 around 13 . This is associated with a decrease of the RC by 15 km s ^ 1 over the same interval. The RC itself peaks at a maximum rotational velocity of 25 km s ^ 1 at 10 =0.5 R_e , where the W side \ then decreases its rotation and the E side \ remains flat to the boundary of our data.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 359-G002 as a fainter lenticular galaxy with a slowly rising velocity curve, generally compatible prior dispersion data, and a newly seen central dispersion peak.

quotes.quote: ESO 359-G002 As Figure fig:comp2 shows, this fainter lenticular galaxy has a slowly rising mean velocity curve, in good agreement with the previous data from Graham et al. (1998) and despite being below our estimated spectral resolution. The previous velocity dispersion measurements are in reasonable agreement with the new data, but seem to lie systematically lower. In particular, the sharp central peak in velocity dispersion, which is accompanies by a similar structure in h_4 (see Figure fig:G002dat ), was not seen in the earlier data. In this case both, the velocity curve and the dispersion profile should be viewed with some caution.

gmw_00003934.name: ESO 358-G006
gmw_00003934.overview: ESO 358-G006, also known as FCC 055, is a faint lenticular galaxy with a concentrated structure [arXiv:1105.4809]. It exhibits featureless rotational motion that rises to a plateau at large radii, alongside a velocity dispersion profile that has a slight central depression [arXiv:astro-ph/0607434].

sources.summary: FCC 055 is a faint but concentrated S0 with pointed inner isophotes, elliptical outskirts, measured rotation, and a small age-metallicity depression.

quotes.quote: FCC 055 It is a faint S0 with M _B 17.5 mag and _B 21.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 . However, it more concentrated (S e rsic n = 3) than a typical dwarf galaxy. In the central part the isophotes are pointed (as expected from a disc presence), while in the outer parts they are elliptical. The V_ max = 80 \ is reached at 19 arcsec. While the ages and metallicities are mostly constant along the radius, there is a small depression at 2-3 arcsec associated with a rise in the velocity dispersion.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first extended-kinematic study of ESO 358-G006, finding featureless rotation rising to a plateau and an unusual dispersion profile consistent with central-dispersion literature values.

quotes.quote: ESO 358-G006 There are no previous studies of the extended kinematics for this faint galaxy. Figure fig:G006dat reveals generic featureless rotational motions that rise to a plateau at large radii, and a rather strange velocity dispersion profile with a slight central depression and a hint of a rising profile at large radii. The existing values for the central velocity dispersion of 58 8 km s ^ 1 from Kuntschner (2000) and 46 3 km s ^ 1 (Bernadi et al. 2002) are in accord with the new dispersion measurements.

gmw_00003935.name: ESO 358-G059
gmw_00003935.overview: ESO 358-G059 is a faint elliptical or lenticular galaxy located within the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:1105.4809]. It features a distinct inner stellar disc and a slowly rotating outer envelope [arXiv:1906.08187], with a young, metal-rich nuclear star cluster shaped by in-situ star formation [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: FCC 301 is described as a faint elliptical recent infaller with distinct inner disc and outer envelope components that differ in kinematics, velocity dispersion, and line strengths.

quotes.quote: FCC 301 (ESO 358-G059) -- Fig. fig:FCC301map FCC 301 is one of the faintest elliptical galaxies inside the virial radius of the Fornax cluster. It is located at a distance of R_ proj =1.4^ from FCC 213 (NGC 1399) and is one of the recent infallers in the low-density region on the south-eastern side. As FCC 90 and FCC 147, which are the other two ETGs classified as recent infallers, also FCC 301 has different photometric and kinematic properties compared to the ETGs identified as ancient infallers. The central discy isophotes are consistent with the inner stellar disc detected in the maps of the velocity ( v_ max 60 km s ^ 1 ) and velocity dispersion ( 40 km s ^ 1 at R 10 arcsec).

At larger radii, the galaxy is characterised by a roundish envelope which is slowly rotating ( v_ max 30 km s ^ 1 ) and has a larger velocity dispersion ( 80 km s ^ 1 ) with respect to the inner disc. The inner disc and outer envelope also have different properties in the line-strength maps. The inner disc shows larger values of Mg b ( 3 ) and Fe5015 ( 5 ) compared to the envelope, whereas H remains almost constant ( 2 ) throughout the galaxy.

sources.summary: FCC 301 is classified as E-S0/S0, with slightly discy isophotes, changing ellipticity, solid-body central rotation, and an outward velocity-dispersion increase.

quotes.quote: FCC 301 The isophotes of FCC301 are slightly discy. We classify it as E-S0 and we assign it S0 group. Its ellipticity ( ) changes from 0.1 to 0.5 at 16 arcsec and then again descends to 0.14 at 150 arcsec. Out to 4 arcsec the velocity rises to 50 , as a solid body and decreases outwards to a plateau of 20 reached at a radius of 10 arcsec ( 1 ). The velocity dispersion is constant within the solid-body region ( = 40 ) and it increases by 10-20 per cent outwards.

sources.summary: The paper presents first extended kinematics for ESO 358-G059, finding a strange system with a rising then declining velocity profile and a dispersion profile that rises where rotation declines.

quotes.quote: ESO 358-G059 The extended kinematics of this faint galaxy have not previously been studied. Figure fig:G059dat shows the rather strange kinematics for this system. It has a relatively well-behaved mean velocity profile that rises to an amplitude of 50 km s ^ 1 at 5 arcsec , then declines slightly. The dispersion profile is remarkably flat in the region where the rotation increases, then starts to rise when the rotational velocities decline. The published values for the central velocity dispersion, 54 \ 9 km s ^ 1 (Kuntschner 2000) and 46 3 km s ^ 1 (Bernadi et al. 2002), are in reasonable agreement with the new data.

sources.summary: The paper says FCC 301 belongs to a set with similar galaxy and NSC properties, where young, metal-rich NSCs indicate dominant in-situ nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: FCC 301 and VCC 990 have similar galaxy ( M_ gal 10^ 9 M_ ) and NSC masses ( M_ NSC 7 10^6 M_ ), and their stellar population properties are also similar to that of FCC 255 which is slightly more massive. In these three galaxies, the NSCs are younger and more metal-rich than their hosts, their SFHs show a single peak at age 3 Gyr. The young ages and high metallicities indicate NSC formation dominated by in-situ star formation either in recently accreted young massive star clusters or directly in the NSC.

gmw_00003936.name: NGC 3412
gmw_00003936.overview: NGC 3412 is an early-type barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9905117]. Near-infrared surface photometry reveals that the galaxy hosts a triaxial bulge with isophotal twists and a very short bar [arXiv:astro-ph/9905117].

sources.summary: Near-infrared photometry shows NGC 3412 has a triaxial bulge, a very short bar, and stronger central concentration than NGC 3941, suggesting bar dissolution.

quotes.quote: We have conducted near-infrared ( J and H band) surface photometry for two early type barred galaxies, NGC 3412 and NGC 3941. The bulges of NGC 3412 and NGC 3941 show isophotal twists which indicate that they are triaxial. NGC 3412 has a very short bar and its bulge is more centrally concentrated than that of NGC 3941. The unusually short bar and the centrally concentrated triaxial bulge of NGC 3412 might be the result of bar dissolution.

gmw_00003937.name: 3C 303
gmw_00003937.overview: The broad-line radio galaxy 3C 303 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034, arXiv:1212.2957] situated in a sparsely populated intergalactic environment [arXiv:1106.1397]. It features a 50-kiloparsec-long one-sided jet with regular repeating knot structures and a large-scale axial electric current [arXiv:1106.1397, arXiv:1412.3835].

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C303 in radio polarization and Faraday rotation, deriving magnetoplasma parameters and a direct large-scale jet current in its one-sided radio/X-ray jet.

quotes.quote: We present radio emission, polarization, and Faraday rotation maps of the radio jet of the galaxy 3C303. From this data we derive the magnetoplasma and electrodynamic parameters of this 50 kpc long jet. For one component of this jet we obtain for the first time a direct determination of a galactic scale electric current ( 10^ 18 A) , and its direction positive away from the AGN. Our analysis strongly supports a model where the jet energy flow is mainly electromagnetic.

The 3C303 radio source at z=0.141 (Figures 1,2) has a one-sided prominent jet with regular repeating knot structures in both VLA radio (Kronberg, 1976, 1986; Lapenta Kronberg 2005 (LK05), and Chandra X-ray bands (Kataoka et al. 2003). It resides in a relatively sparsely populated intergalactic environment, and at a high galactic latitude [ ( l , b ) = +90 ^ .5, +57 ^ .5 ] . Being away from a galaxy cluster, it is well-suited to an analysis of Faraday rotation measures (RM) within the jet and lobes, since competing Faraday RM from an immediate cluster environment and the Galactic foreground are small. Given this situation, we applied improved, new determinations of the RM's of neighboring background radio sources (Kronberg Newton-McGee 2011). This allows the most accurate available estimate of the ``RM zero-level'', so that our observed RM gradients and RM=0 crossings are minimally affected by foreground plasma (Section 5) . The above combination of radio and X-ray images plus background source RM's permits the jet's physical parameters, including now its current, to be analysed in isolation.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C303 as the best observational case for an axial electric current in a radio-galaxy jet, with RM structure implying multi-kpc current flow and electromagnetic power transport.

quotes.quote: We describe the so far best observation-based estimate of the magnitude of the current flow along the axis of the jet extending from the nucleus of the active galaxy in 3C303. The current is measured to be I 10^ 18 Amp eres at 40 kpc away from the AGN. This indicates that organised current flow remains intact over multi-kpc distances.

In the observed image any Faraday rotation in the direction of 3C303 contains a foreground component from the Milky Way plus any intergalactic magneto-plasma around the 3C303 system, and between it and our Milky Way. Fortunately this correction is small at 3C303's location on the sky. After applying it, the RM variation across the jet crosses zero on the jet's axis, within the errors. The sign of ( RM) corresponds to a positive current directed, in this case, away from the galaxy nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra X-ray emission from 3C 303 hotspots and knots, models hotspot inverse-Compton emission, and finds a magnetic field far below equipartition.

quotes.quote: We report the detection at X-rays of the radio/optical hotspot and knots of 3C 303 from a short (15 ksec) Chandra exposure in 2001 March. The X-ray morphology is similar to that of the radio/optical emission with peaks in the X-ray emission found at 5.5 '' (knot B), 9 '' (knot C) and 17 '' (hotspot) from the core of 3C 303. Despite the limited signal-to-noise ratio of the short Chandra exposure, the X-ray photon spectrum was measured for the hotspot.

We construct the spectral energy distribution (SED) and find that the X-ray flux is well below the extrapolation of the radio-to-optical continuum, which we interpret as resulting from the production of X-rays via inverse Compton scattering of both synchrotron photons (SSC) and cosmic microwave background photons (EC/CMB). The magnetic field strength, region size, and the maximum energy of electrons are self-consistently determined for the hotspot to be B 4.3 G, R 6.5 10 ^ 21 cm, and _ max 1.4 10 ^7 . This implies a magnetic field strength a factor of 30 below the equipartition value; B_ eq 150 G.

sources.summary: The paper lists 3C303 as a tentative transverse Faraday-rotation-gradient source, citing a visible CCW gradient at knot E3 but insufficient uncertainty information.

quotes.quote: 3C303 .---Kronberg et al. (2011) report the presence of a transverse RM gradient at the location of knot E3, and use information about this gradient together with certain assumptions to estimate the associated current in the jet. The RM gradient is clearly visible in Fig. 3 of Kronberg et al. (2011), although it is quite narrow and is not well resolved. The direction of the gradient is CCW. Although this RM map has been published in the refereed literature, we include 3C303 in our list of tentative gradients because the original paper does not provide information about the uncertainties of the single-pixel RM values that can be used to estimate the significance of the RM gradient. We do not have access to these data, and so were not able to carry out our own quantitative analysis for this source.

sources.summary: The broad-line radio galaxy 3C 303 is a strong X-ray point source with a possible hotspot counterpart, though the authors favor a nearby quasar explanation.

quotes.quote: 3C 303 This broad-line radio galaxy is a strong point source in the X-ray. In the HRI image a faint point-like component, containing 25 6 counts, is approximately coincident (after aligning the radio core and X-ray centroid) with the western hotspot of 3C 303. We may therefore be seeing an X-ray counterpart to the radio and optical hotspots already known in this object (e.g. \ Meisenheimer, Yates R oser 1997). If so, this would be one of only a very few X-ray hotspots known.

However, the position of this X-ray component, given the low count rate, is at least equally consistent with that of a z=1.57 quasar 4 arcsec to the SW of the optical source identified with the hot spot (Kronberg 1976; Kronberg \ 1977), and it seems most likely that the X-ray emission comes from this quasar (see Fig. \ 303-overlay ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 303.0 as radio loud, notes it has one of the sample's highest radio-loudness values, and warns soft X-rays may include jet contamination.

quotes.quote: The two objects with the highest radio-loudness parameters are Mrk 463 and 3C 303.0. The object 3C 303.0 is expected to be radio loud based on its inclusion in the 3C catalog.

For 3C 303.0, we inspect the archival Chandra images and find that while the 2--10 keV emission has a negligible contribution from extended jet emission, the 0.5--2 keV emission may be contaminated by jet emission at a level of 17 of the nuclear emission. This is likely to influence our spectral fit for this object.

gmw_00003938.name: UGC 11707
gmw_00003938.overview: UGC 11707 is a small, low surface brightness spiral galaxy characterized by a faint central bulge and loosely bound, broken arms composed of individual stellar clusters [arXiv:1911.00369]. Classified as a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1405.4854], its gas velocity map displays a small pocket of high-velocity gas [arXiv:1405.4854].

sources.summary: The paper includes UGC 11707 in the dwarf-galaxy sample, discussing its pseudo-bulge-like central structure, shared HI map, and a high-velocity gas pocket in its velocity field.

quotes.quote: We present new constraints on the density profiles of dark matter (DM) halos in seven nearby dwarf galaxies from measurements of their integrated stellar light and gas kinematics. The gas kinematics of low mass galaxies frequently suggest that they contain constant density DM cores, while N-body simulations instead predict a cuspy profile. We present a data set of high resolution integral field spectroscopy on seven galaxies and measure the stellar and gas kinematics simultaneously.

UGC 11707 shows unrealistic behavior, but the problem is only present in the second smallest radial bin for both models. The gas velocity map of UGC 11707 shows a small pocket of high velocity gas at 12 \ off the major axis. The gas emission in this bin is quite patchy.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 11707 as a small spiral LSB target with poorly sampled rotation curves, uncertain model parameters, and a halo consistent with CDM models.

quotes.quote: UGC 11707 : UGC 11707 is a small spiral galaxy with loosely bound broken arms made of individual stellar clusters. It also has a very faint central bulge (Sd). Due to the lack of data, the H rotation curve of UGC 11707 is poorly sampled but the inner rise in the H rotation curve appears to have a steeper profile than the HI rotation curve. This galaxy appears to have a very large amount of freedom in its model parameters swaters1999 . This is partly due to the relatively large error bars for the inner data points, which is a reflection of the asymmetry between the receding and approaching rotation velocities for radii 7 kpc. The halo profile of UGC 11707 is consistent with CDM models bosch .

gmw_00003939.name: UGC 12632
gmw_00003939.overview: UGC 12632 is a weakly barred spiral, low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1911.00369]. Its neutral hydrogen gas is distributed relatively evenly over the disk [arXiv:1911.00369, arXiv:astro-ph/0203133], which exhibits differential rotation and a changing position angle between the inner and outer regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 12632 as a weakly barred spiral LSB galaxy with uniformly distributed HI and a rotation curve in excellent agreement with CDM halo models.

quotes.quote: UGC 12632 (DDO 217) : UGC 12632 is a weakly barred spiral galaxy (SABm). The observational data shows that the HI gas is uniformly distributed over its disk but has a very high velocity bump on the blue side of the H rotation curve. The position velocity map indicates a narrow rise of the rotational velocity near the centre and a gradual increase in the outer disk. So the observed rotation curve of UGC 12632 is in excellent agreement with CDM models of halos. Thus it shows that this galaxy is consistent with CDM models bosch .

sources.summary: DDO 217 is described with evenly distributed disk HI, differential rotation over most of the galaxy, changing position angle between inner and outer disk, and a rising rotation curve.

quotes.quote: DDO 217: The HI is distributed relatively evenly over the disk. The velocity field shows differential rotation over most of the galaxy. The difference in position angle between the inner region and the outer disk is clearly visible in the velocity field map. The XV map shows a rapid rise of the rotation velocity near the center and a gradual increase in the outer disk.

gmw_00003940.name: UGC 12732
gmw_00003940.overview: UGC 12732 is a nearby, low-surface-brightness Sm or SABm spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203, arXiv:1911.00369] that features a weak bar [arXiv:1911.00369] and a single long arm [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203, arXiv:1911.00369]. Its neutral hydrogen gas extends nearly four times beyond the optical diameter [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203], and its nearest neighbor is the Scd galaxy NGC 7741 [arXiv:astro-ph/0004203].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12732 as a nearby Sm, low-surface-brightness galaxy with a smooth inner region, a single YSG arm, and H I extending roughly four times beyond the optical galaxy.

quotes.quote: According to the data in the RC3, the galaxy UGC 12732, is classified as type Sm with an integrated B magnitude of 13.8 and optical diameter D _ 25 =3 . This galaxy is at 12.4 Mpc distance which corresponds to a scale of 17 pc/pixel in our images. Its nearest neighbor is the Scd galaxy NGC 7741 which is at a projected distance of 160 kpc and has a radial velocity that is within 6 km/s of UGC 12732's velocity. Our images show that the galaxy consists of a smooth low surface brightness inner region with a single long arm of YSGs starting from SW of the centre and circling around to the north side of the galaxy.

The HI properties of UGC 12732 were recently studied by Schulman et al. (1997) based upon data from Arecibo and the VLA. Their data show that the HI extends throughout the field of our images with a diameter of 11.6 at an HI level of 10 ^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 . This diameter is almost four times larger than the optical galaxy. There is a depression in the HI gas coinciding with the optical centre of the galaxy while the two arms visible in our optical data are detected in HI as well at a level of 5-6 10^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12732 as a weakly barred spiral LSB galaxy with a faint ring and arm, and rotation curves consistent with CDM models.

quotes.quote: UGC 12732 : UGC 12732 is a weakly barred spiral galaxy (SABm), similar to UGC 12632. Studies suggest that there is a ring of approximate size 0.5^ 0.5^ in UGC 12732 associated with one long thin arm of very low surface brightness. The H and HI rotation curves of UGC 12732 are in good agreement and it is also consistent with CDM models bosch,swaters2009 .

gmw_00003941.name: SDSS J130408.52+521846.4
gmw_00003941.overview: SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 is a faint host galaxy [arXiv:1711.00862, arXiv:2106.06690] with a low stellar mass of approximately 2.6 × 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:1711.00862, arXiv:2106.06690]. The galaxy exhibits active star formation [arXiv:1711.00862, arXiv:2106.06690] and has a low oxygen abundance upper limit [arXiv:1711.00862].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 as the faint host of ASASSN-14ms, deriving UV-optical SED properties, redshift, star formation, and a low metallicity limit.

quotes.quote: Host Galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 In order to constrain the properties of the host galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4, we performed a search of archival databases.We retrieved the archival ugriz model magnitudes from the Twelfth Data Release of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; Alam15 . The host was also detected in the NUV filter by the Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX; Martin05 . The host is not significantly spatially extended on the sky, so there is no worry that the cataloged magnitudes sample drastically different spatial scales.

We modeled the SDSS+GALEX host galaxy SED with the Fitting and Assessment of Synthetic Templates FAST; Kriek09 code. We adopted a Cardelli89 extinction law with R_V = 3.1 and a Galactic extinction of A_V = 0.031 mag Schlafly11 , an exponentially declining star-formation history, a Salpeter IMF, and the Bruzual03 stellar population models. We obtained a reasonable fit ( ^2_ = 0.42 ) which yielded estimates of the stellar mass and star formation rates of M_* = (2.6_ 1.5 ^ +0.8 ) 10^8 M_ and SFR 0.02 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The line ratio is constrained to be N II 6583 /H 0.075 , corresponding to an upper limit on the oxygen abundance of 12+ (O/H) 8.3 Pettini04 . This places SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 near the lower end of the 12+ (O/H) dex distribution for SNe Ibn host galaxies PastorelloVI,TaddiaMetallicity .

sources.summary: The paper obtains Keck and Subaru spectra of the faint host galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4, deriving its redshift, star-formation rate, stellar mass, and specific star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Host Galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 As redshift is not available for the galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 from the public catalogue, we collected the host-galaxy spectra from two telescopes. As the Keck spectrum was taken 50 days after maximum brightness when the SN light was comparable to that of the host galaxy, it was also used to extract the host-galaxy spectrum to determine the redshift. Another spectrum of the host galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 was obtained on 2016 June 21.4 (t 6 months after the peak) with the 8.2 m Subaru telescope to cross-check the result from the Keck telescope. Figure 2 shows the host-galaxy spectra of ASASSN-14ms from Keck (blue) and Subaru (red). One can see that the narrow emission line at 6915  , with a measured full width at half-maximum intensity (FWHM) of 300 km s ^ 1 , is clearly present in both spectra.

From the H _ luminosity of flux-calibrated Subaru spectrum, we estimated the star formation rate using the conversion relation from Kennicutt (1998), which is half of the value derived by 2018MNRAS.475.2344V , i.e., 0.005 M _ yr ^ 1 . This difference is likely due to that the Subaru spectrum has relatively low signal-to-noise ratio. Modeling of the photometric spectral energy distribution for galaxy SDSS J130408.52+521846.4 reveals that this galaxy has a stellar mass M_ * =2.6 ^ +0.8 _ 1.5 10 ^8 M _ 2018MNRAS.475.2344V .

gmw_00003942.name: UGC 10214
gmw_00003942.overview: UGC 10214 is a massive spiral galaxy that interacted with a dwarf galaxy approximately 250 million years ago [arXiv:1811.12417]. This minor merger produced a perturbed galactic disk, a giant tidal tail, and a recently enhanced star formation rate [arXiv:1811.12417].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 10214 as a massive minor-merger spiral whose dwarf interaction produced a perturbed disk, giant tidal tail, normal molecular depletion time, and recently enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 10214 (the ``Tadpole Galaxy'') is a prime example of this process in which a dwarf galaxy has interacted with a large spiral galaxy 250 Myr ago and produced a perturbed disk and a giant tidal tail. We use a multiwavelength dataset, partly from new observations ( , HI, and CO) and partly from archival data, in order to study the present and past star formation rate (SFR) and its relation to the gas and stellar mass at a spatial resolution down to 4 kpc. UGC 10214 is a very massive (stellar mass = 1.28 10^ 11 ) galaxy with a low gas fraction ( / = 0.24), a high molecular gas fraction ( / = 0.4) and a modest SFR (2 -- 5 yr ^ 1 ).

The global SFR compared to its stellar mass places UGC 10214 on the galaxy main sequence (MS). The comparison of the molecular gas mass and current SFR gives a molecular gas depletion time of about 2 Gyr (based on ), comparable to those of normal spiral galaxies. Both from a comparison of the \ emission, tracing the current SFR, and far-ultraviolet (FUV) emission, tracing the recent SFR during the past tens of Myr, as well as from spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting with CIGALE, we find that the SFR has increased by a factor of about 2 -- 3 during the recent past.

This increase is particularly noticeable in the centre of the galaxy where a pronounced peak of the \ emission is visible. A pixel-to-pixel comparison of the SFR, molecular gas mass and stellar mass shows that the central region has had a depressed FUV-traced SFR, both compared to the molecular gas and the stellar mass, whereas the traced SFR shows a normal level.

gmw_00003943.name: J0306+1853
gmw_00003943.overview: J0306+1853 is a radio source that appears unresolved and point-like in JVLA L-band and C-band observations [arXiv:2109.08156]. Its steep spectral index suggests the presence of a compact, sub-arcsecond active galactic nucleus jet structure spanning less than 20 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2109.08156].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0306+1853 as unresolved at JVLA L and C bands but radio powerful with a steep spectrum, implying a compact sub-arcsecond AGN jet structure.

quotes.quote: J0306+1853 is unresolved in the L and C bands. It corresponds to a radio size smaller than 20 kpc; however, the spectral index is 0.87 0.06, suggesting the presence of a sub-arcsecond structure. The lack of an extended jet structure could be a result of the too low brightness due to the high inverse Compton losses.

The source \ was not observed with a VLBI array, and in JVLA images it shows a point-like image. However, its radio power and the relatively steep spectral index (0.87) suggests that a sub-arcsecond AGN jet structure is also present in this source.

gmw_00003944.name: IC 1860
gmw_00003944.overview: IC 1860 is the central dominant galaxy of its galaxy group [arXiv:1001.1630, arXiv:2111.02430]. It hosts a central radio source that is slightly elongated in the northeast and southwest directions [arXiv:1001.1630, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: IC 1860 is presented as the dominant galaxy of its group, located at the diffuse X-ray peak, with a central radio point source plus faint extensions and offset X-ray structure.

quotes.quote:
IC 1860 is the dominant galaxy of the IC 1860 group, which is itself part of Abell S301. The dominant galaxy of Abell S301 according to Hudson01 is the Seyfert 2 galaxy IC 1859, which is 6.5 arcminutes distant from IC 1860. However, XMM-Newton data (Observation 0146510401) shown in Fig. fig:images , IC 1860 sits at the peak of the diffuse X-ray emission, and the position of IC 1859 is off to the west.

The radio data of IC 1860 show a clear point source with faint extensions in the northeast and southwest directions. The X-ray data show an extension towards the southeast.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 1860 as the central dominant galaxy of a 1.3 keV group, with a slightly elongated point-like VLA radio source and X-ray sloshing signatures.

quotes.quote: IC 1860 IC 1860 is CDG of the 1.3 keV group IC 1860 Abell S301; abell1989 . The observed radio emission in the VLA A configuration has a form of point-like Point-like morphology was found by using observations at 1.4 GHz from 2007. radio source slightly elongated in the north-eastern and south-western direction (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a a).

gmw_00003945.name: NGC 499
gmw_00003945.overview: NGC 499 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2111.02430] located in the Pisces cluster [arXiv:2111.02430] that is unusually radio quiet for its X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1001.1630]. It is currently interacting and probably merging with the nearby galaxy NGC 507 [arXiv:2201.04591], which has perturbed its X-ray emission [arXiv:2201.04591].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 499 as the nearby bright group member or small group probably merging with NGC 507 and plausibly perturbing its X-ray sloshing pattern.

quotes.quote: We note that the membership allocation process groups together the galaxies NGC 507 (with a velocity of 4934 \ reporting the NED velocity after heliocentric correction and a Ks magnitude of 8.28 as reported by tully2015 ) and NGC 499 (4399 and Ks magnitude of 8.71). These two are separated in velocity by 535 \ and are the brightest members of the group with a difference in magnitude of m_ 12 =0.43 . A low value of m_ 12 for such an evolved X-ray-bright system is an indication that the system is undergoing a merger.

However, the nearby galaxy group NGC 499, located at a projected distance of 270 kpc, remains a very good candidate. The excess X-ray emission between NGC 507 and NGC 499, the tail of X-ray emission present in the southern extent of NGC 499 (see Fig. fig:507xray ), and the high velocity dispersion measured clearly suggest that the two systems are interacting. The small magnitude difference between the two systems further implies a sufficiently high mass ratio to give rise to the observed perturbations. The spiral pattern observed in the X-ray emission of NGC 507 would then imply that the orbit of NGC 499 is nearly in the plane of the sky.

sources.summary: NGC 499 is identified as unusually radio quiet for its X-ray luminosity, with no radio detection, a possible southeast X-ray depression, and reported tidal interaction with NGC 507.

quotes.quote: NGC 499 lies near NGC 507 in the NGC 507 galaxy Group. There is no radio detection for this galaxy down to a level of 1.18 10^ 4 Jy, identifying NGC 499 as abnormally radio quiet for its X-ray luminosity. The RMS noise in the VLA data is 35.8 Jy/beam, which is fairly typical of the observations presented here (see Table tab:vla ). There appears to be a depression in the X-ray emission to the south-east, visible in Fig. fig:images . However, whether this is the result of a past outburst of AGN activity is not clear.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 499 as the only early-type sample galaxy without detected VLA radio emission, despite a faint NVSS source, and places it in the Pisces cluster.

quotes.quote: NGC 499 NGC 499 is the only one galaxy in our sample of 42 early-type sources without radio emission (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a d) observed in the nucleus or in the extended diffuse region. \ A faint radio source was observed within the 1.4 GHz NVSS survey condon1998 with a flux density of 0.7 mJy at 2 _ RMS brown2011 , which is well above the sensitivity limit of our VLA observations (for more details see Section sec:radio_central ). NGC 499 is a CDG of the NGC 499 group, which is a part of the Pisces cluster kim1995 .

gmw_00003946.name: NGC 741
gmw_00003946.overview: NGC 741 is the brightest elliptical galaxy in a rich fossil group [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256, arXiv:1103.1364] and hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1707.02985, arXiv:2107.02141]. It is interacting with the companion galaxy NGC 742 [arXiv:1103.1364, arXiv:2111.02430], which has shaped its complex radio structure and created a connecting radio bridge [arXiv:1707.02985, arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 741 as the brightest galaxy in a group, with companions, no detected dust, central emission filaments, and a head-tail radio source.

quotes.quote: NGC 741 NGC 741 is the brightest galaxy in a group of galaxies (Zabludoff Mulchaey 1998). There is a small apparent elliptical companion at 8'' (2.7 kpc assuming same distance) which is not cataloged in NED. A second elliptical companion, NGC 742, is located at 48'' (16.2 kpc). No dust is detected in NGC 741. We detect a central peak of emission with low level emission filaments extending 2'' (680 pc) towards the North, East and South.

Macchetto (1996) detected an emission-gas disk from the ground with minor and major diameter of 10.8'' (3.7 kpc) and 22.6'' (7.7 kpc) respectively, and with an average flux density which is significantly below our detection threshold. This disk fills most of the PC . NGC 741 hosts a head-tail radio source (Birkinshaw Davies 1985).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 741 as the central radio galaxy and BCG of a disturbed group, analyzing merger history, X-ray filaments, and candidate AGN-inflated cavities.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of recent deep Chandra and XMM-Newton integrations of NGC 741, which provides an excellent example of a group with multiple concurrent phenomena: both an old central radio galaxy and a spectacular infalling head-tail source, strongly-bent jets, a 100 kpc radio trail, intriguing narrow X-ray filaments, and gas sloshing features. Supported principally by X-ray and radio continuum data, we address the merging history of the group, the nature of the X-ray filaments, the extent of gas stripping from NGC 742, the character of cavities in the group, and the roles of the central AGN and infalling galaxy in heating the intra-group medium.

In this context, NGC 741 is a very interesting and possibly unique system: based on a relatively short X-ray exposure, it has previously been reported to host a ghost cavity, a region of low X-ray surface brightness evacuated by the central AGN. However, the non-thermal radio emission from the relativistic particles lies below the sensitivity of previous observations ( 2008MNRAS.384.1344J ). Furthermore, it is known to host a radio bridge between the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) and a close member galaxy, NGC 742, as discussed in . Also, further extended radio emission around the BCG was detected, but, as we discuss in this work, it originates not from the AGN of the BCG, NGC 741, but from NGC 742, and marks the trajectory during the encounter of the two galaxies.

We find in the normalized residual image (Fig. fig:rel_resid ) a strong decrease in surface brightness ( 25 with respect to the surrounding annulus) about 16 kpc west of NGC 741. This seems to be a very good candidate for a cavity inflated by the NGC 741 AGN, especially because the radio emission seems to roughly overlay the feature seen in the X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 741 as the brightest group galaxy at z=0.019 with an extended X-ray halo and argues its active nucleus likely inflated the ghost cavity.

quotes.quote: The BGG, identified as NGC 741, and its companion galaxy NGC 742 are separated by 45 arcsec (17 kpc), with NGC 742 lying almost exactly to the E of NGC 741, together with a much smaller third companion approximately 9 arcsec (3.4 kpc) to the NE of NGC 741 1995A&A...297...28B . 1998ApJ...496...73M first reported a significant extended X-ray halo in this group, extending to 19.2 arcmin from the centre of NGC 741 (approximately 440 kpc in our adopted cosmology), with a luminosity of 5.3 10^ 41 erg \ s^ 1 (again in our adopted cosmology).

We have examined the energetics of the galaxy group associated with NGC 741. We find that if the cavity seen in the X-ray data was originally inflated by a radio-loud AGN associated with the brightest galaxy, NGC 741, then there is sufficient energy to counteract cooling. This interpretation is consistent with the fact that NGC 741 clearly has an active nucleus at the present time, and this could have been more active in the past.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 741 as the brightest elliptical in a fossil group, interacting with NGC 742 and embedded in a complex radio structure with a long tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 741 NGC 741 is the central and brightest elliptical galaxy of a rich fossil group of galaxies at a redshift of z = 0.019 (Mulchaey Zabludoff 1998; Miles et al. 2004; Forbes et al. 2006). The velocity dispersion of the group is 460 km s ^ 1 (Jetha et al. 2008), making it one of the richest groups in our sample. There are 30 catalogued members of the group, even though the magnitude difference between the brightest (NGC 741) and second brightest (NGC 742) galaxies is almost three magnitudes, separated by only 20^ (17 kpc) in the plane of the sky. However the velocity difference is 400 km s ^ 1 , suggesting that NGC 742 has fallen at high velocity through the group core and is passing close to NGC 741. The two galaxies have long been known to be associated with the bright, extended radio source 4C 05.10 (Birkinshaw Davies 1985).

sources.summary: The paper reports an F475X photometry anomaly for NGC 741, excludes the affected chip, and notes that the usable globular-cluster sample is reduced.

quotes.quote: Close preliminary inspection of the photometry after all the culling steps listed above revealed an anomaly for one of the fields, NGC 741. The SNR values indicated a significant number of bad measurements in F475X for this galaxy. Upon further inspection, we found that one of the two CCD chips used with F475X had produced unrealistically faint magnitudes (see Figure fig:ngc741 ) and thus extremely red colors on one side of the galaxy. In our analysis, we have therefore used only objects from the other, apparently unaffected chip that had produced F475X measurements in line with those from the rest of our galaxy sample. For this galaxy, the GC sample size is therefore roughly a factor of two smaller than originally intended.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 741 as a low-surface-brightness galaxy with uncertain but useful rotation data, using measurements from 15 to 30 despite an unclear turnover radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 741. The relatively low surface brightness of this galaxy results in large uncertainties in the kinematic data. Nonetheless, its six position angle measurements of the rotation curve make it very attractive to model. Outside of 15 some rotation appears on PA 10 and PA 40. Although it is not at all clear that the turnover radius of the rotation curve has been found, all of the data from 15 to the outermost data point at 30 are used.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 0741 as a nucleated core-Sersic galaxy whose inner position angle is strongly twisted and whose break-radius twist coincides with an ellipticity minimum.

quotes.quote: Also evident in Fig. FigV5 , is that despite the twists, the galaxies' orientations at their break radii are fairly aligned with the spheroid's outer orientation except for five galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC NGC 3842, NGC 4589, NGC 5061 and NGC 6849) with inner position angles which are twisted by 27^ from the outer position angle. NGC 0741, NGC 3842 and NGC 6849 show strong isophotal twists at their break radii coincident with local minima in their ellipticities, but for NGC 5061 the strong 30^ twist and the ellipticity minimum which occurs between 3 and 10 may be a result of tidal effects (Tal et al. \ 2009). While the correlation between high twist amplitudes and low isophote ellipticities has already been known for some time (e.g., Galleta 1980; Nieto et al. \ 1992; Rahman Shandarin 2004), these four galaxies have interesting features discussed in the literature. NGC 0741 is a nucleated galaxy (e.g., Dullo Graham 2012), NGC 3842 is a bright cluster galaxy that has an ultramassive black hole ( M_ BH = 9.7 10^ 9 M_ ) with a 1 .2 radius of influence (McConnell et al. \ 2011) whereas the S0 galaxy NGC 6849 has an inner bar which manifests as a peak in the ellipiticity profile (see Dullo Graham 2013).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 741 as the only X-ray-luminous compact elliptical AGN in its sample and later treats it as an outlier in black-hole scaling relations.

quotes.quote: This results in one source: the cE NGC 741, which is a well-studied AGN at X-ray, optical, and radio wavelengths (e.g. Schellenberger2017 ; Kim2019 ) with a BH mass upper limit of log < 8.67 derived from gas dynamics using Hubble Space Telescope /STIS spectroscopy ( Beifiori2009 ; vandenBosch2016 ).
Only one source has an X-ray luminosity indicative of AGN emission, the cE NGC 741, which is a well-studied AGN at several wavelengths.
NGC 741 is a clear outlier in both panels but instead J085431.18+173730.5 falls on top of the scaling relation with the stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 741 as a group CDG whose radio structure was shaped by passage of NGC 742, including a bridge and bent radio tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 741 NGC 741 is the CDG in the group holding the same name. The radio structure, first studied by and , was probably formed by the close passage of the second brightest galaxy in the group, the head-tail radio galaxy NGC 742 with the same redshift as NGC 741 schellenberger2017 . Our analysis, using a new high resolution A configuration total intensity image, confirms the radio emission in the form of a bridge connecting NGC 471 and NGC 742 and a prominent bent radio tail (to the southwest) in the C configuration total intensity image (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a g).

gmw_00003947.name: NGC 1132
gmw_00003947.overview: NGC 1132 is an isolated giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9810458, arXiv:2111.02430] that serves as a prototype fossil-group system [arXiv:1208.4060, arXiv:2106.13255]. It features a group-like X-ray halo [arXiv:astro-ph/9810458], shell-like optical structures [arXiv:1208.4060], and an unresolved central radio source [arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1132 as an isolated elliptical with a group-like X-ray halo and clustered dwarf population, consistent with either a merged or failed group.

quotes.quote: ASCA observations of the first galaxy studied, NGC 1132, reveal an X-ray halo that extends out to at least 250 kpc h _ 100 ^ 1 . The temperature ( 1 keV), metallicity ( 0.25 solar) and luminosity ( 2.5 10 ^ 42 h _ 100 ^ 2 erg s ^ 1 ) of NGC 1132's X-ray halo are comparable to those of poor group halos. The total mass inferred from the X-ray emission, 1.9 ^ +0.8 _ 0.6 10 ^ 13 h _ 100 ^ 1 M _ , is also like that of an X-ray detected group.

Optical imaging uncovers a dwarf galaxy population clustered about NGC 1132 that is consistent in number density and in projected radial distribution with that of an X-ray group. The similarities of NGC 1132 to poor groups in both the X-ray band and at the faint end of the galaxy luminosity function, combined with the deficit of luminous galaxies in the NGC 1132 field, are compatible with the merged group picture. Another possibility is that the NGC 1132 system is a `failed' group ( i.e. , a local overdensity in which other bright galaxies never formed).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1132 as the prototype fossil-group galaxy, measuring its globular clusters and specific frequency while finding shell-like residual structure from surface-brightness analysis.

quotes.quote: In order to have a representative sample we choose: the nearest, NGC 6482 ( z=0.013 ), the prototype, NGC 1132 ( z=0.023 ), and the most massive known to date, ESO 306-017 ( z=0.036 ), shown in Fig. pretty_pictures .

For NGC 1132 we see residuals with an appearance of shells; in order to rule out that these are caused by a bad choice of PA value, we constructed model images changing the PA; we recovered the shell-like structure in all cases.

The main results of this study are: We detected 369, 1410 and 1918 GCs down to magnitude z =24.7, 26.2 and 26.2 in NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively; after completeness correction and assuming a Gaussian GCLF with expected M^0 _g=-7.2 and _ GCLF =1.4 , the number of GCs in each FOV are: 1140 87, 3613 295 and 11577 1046, respectively. The derived values of S_N are: 1.9 0.1 , 3.1 0.3 and 6.3 0.6 for NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively. From analysis of the surface brightness distributions, we found evidence of recent interactions, particularly in ESO 306-017, which shows a tidal feature coincident with the finger reported previously in X-rays, and NGC 1132, which has shell-like structure.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1132 as a famous fossil-group example whose magnitude gap is questioned after comparison with nearby spiral NGC 1126.

quotes.quote: It is worth noting that some famous FGs are excluded from the list. As an example, we discuss the prototype of this category, NGC 1132 Mulchaey1999 , for which we were not able to find m_ 12 . There are indeed information on the fainter galaxies e.g. Dong-woo2018 , but not a clear computation of the magnitude gap.

We check in the SDSS DR16 the magnitudes of both NGC 1132 and NGC 1126: the former has m_r = 12.20 , the latter m_r = 14.01 , leading to a m_ 12 = 1.81 , rejecting it as a genuine FG. However, as we already mentioned, an accurate study of the membership of NGC 1126 to the group of NGC 1132 should be done and errors in SDSS magnitudes can not be excluded (NGC 1126 is flagged with ``unreliable photometry'', as many other bright galaxies, mainly due to an over estimation of the sky around bright objects).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1132 as a fossil-group giant elliptical with unresolved central radio emission and an X-ray shock-front feature linked to merger history.

quotes.quote: NGC 1132 NGC 1132 is a giant elliptical, which resides inside of the well-known fossil group NGC 1132. \ Radio emission from the archival VLA C configuration data (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a e) shows a point source morphology from the unresolved central source. \ The outer contour level ( 5 _ RMS ; see Table tab:results ) coincides with the possible shock front in the X-rays noticed by and could be connected to the merging history of NGC 1132 as suggested by and predicted by the simulations of barnes1989,governato1991 .

gmw_00003948.name: NGC 1550
gmw_00003948.overview: NGC 1550 is a group-dominant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2106.14937, arXiv:0901.4626] and a dispersion-dominated merger remnant [arXiv:2106.14937, arXiv:1306.1124] that hosts a central radio source [arXiv:2005.12950, arXiv:1001.1630]. It features asymmetric radio jets [arXiv:2005.12950, arXiv:2111.02430], old radio lobes [arXiv:2005.12950], and a relaxed, luminous hot X-ray halo [arXiv:2106.14937] showing signs of gas sloshing [arXiv:2005.12950].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1550 as a group-central radio galaxy whose asymmetric jets, old lobes, X-ray sloshing, and recent minor-merger evidence show coupled AGN feedback and gas motions.

quotes.quote: Our GMRT radio images at 235 and 610 MHz show an asymmetric jet lobe structure with a total size of 33 kpc at 235 MHz. The west jet is found to extend twice as far from the nucleus as the east jet, and the high resolution 610 MHz image reveals a kink to the south at the base of the west jet and on the other side a bending of the east jet towards the south while enclosed by the east lobe. The 235 610 MHz spectral index map of the source reveals that both radio lobes present steep spectral index values ranging between about 1.2 and 1.8 indicating the presence of an old electron population.

We find indications of sloshing in NGC 1550 from the observed arc-shaped structure in the X-ray residual map. We find that the overall radiative age estimate derived from the radio observations ( 33 Myr) is in reasonable agreement with the range of dynamical timescale estimates derived from the X-ray data (20 50 Myr), and is somewhat shorter than the shortest timescale estimate for sloshing motions (40 80 Myr).

We conclude that the observed radio/X-ray structure in NGC 1550 is most likely the combination of sloshing motions with effects of buoyant forces from jet/lobe growth in the IGM. Although NGC 1550 has been considered a relaxed system hosting a decaying radio source, we find evidence that it has undergone a recent minor merger.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 1550 as a group-dominant, fossil-like merger remnant with luminous X-ray gas, a relaxed large-scale hot halo, and central elongation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1550 is an exciting counter-example of NGC 4636. This group-dominant galaxy (at D=51.1 Mpc) belongs to one of the most X-ray luminous galaxies within 200 Mpc with L _ X,GAS 10^ 43 erg sec ^ 1 sun2003 . This galaxy has been extensively studied using the Chandra sun2003 , XMM kawaharada2009 , and Suzaku observations sato2010 . Although it does not meet the condition ( m_ 12 > 2 ) for a fossil group sun2003 , this system is close to a fossil-group as a relaxed, merger remnant jones2003, kawaharada2009, sato2010 .

Unlike NGC 4636, the Chandra image (Figure fig4 left) indicates that the hot gas in NGC 1550 is relatively smooth, except in the central region where the Chandra image shows an E-W elongation ( < 10 kpc; see also sun2003, kolokythas2020 ). The XMM observations (Figure fig4 right) show that on a large scale (50 - 100 kpc), the hot halo is smooth and relaxed (see also Figure 3 of kawaharada2009 ). In contrast to NGC 4636, this fossil-like system contains a relaxed hot halo, as expected as an end product of galaxy mergers (e.g., jones2000, khosroshahi2006 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1550 as an X-ray extended elliptical in a group-scale dark halo whose metal distributions support a merger-built central galaxy scenario.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxy NGC 1550 at a redshift of z=0.01239 , identified with an extended X-ray source RX J0419+0225, was observed with XMM-Newton for 31 ks. From the X-ray data and archival near infra-red data of Two Micron All Sky survay, we derive the profiles of components constituting the NGC 1550 system; the gas mass, total mass, metal mass, and galaxy luminosity. The metals (oxygen, silicon, and iron) are extended to 200 kpc from the center, wherein 70 of the K band luminosity is carried by NGC 1550 itself.

Further studies comparing mass densities of metals with those of the other cluster components reveal that the iron (as well as silicon) in the ICM traces very well the total gravitating mass, whereas the stellar component is significantly more concentrated to within several tens kpc of the NGC 1550 nucleus. Thus, in the central region, the amount of metals is significantly depleted for the luminous galaxy light. Among a few possible explanations of this effect, the most likely scenario is that galaxies in this system were initially much more extended than today, and gradually fell to the center and merged into NGC 1550.

sources.summary: NGC 1550 is described as a group-dominant galaxy with unusual two-peaked radio morphology, X-ray elongation aligned with the radio axis, and an X-ray filament tracing the eastern lobe.

quotes.quote: NGC 1550, also known as NGC 1551, is the dominant galaxy of the NGC 1550 group. The source exhibits an unusual radio morphology with two peaks: one approximately centered on NGC 1550 and the other offset 45 arcsec to the west. The group is unusually X-ray luminous for its temperature and velocity dispersion. The X-ray emission is elliptical, with the semi major axis in the same direction as the axis of the radio source Sun03 . A filament of X-ray emission traces the eastern radio lobe, wrapping around the southern part.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 1550 among dispersion-dominated galaxies with high central dispersion, uses long-slit data, and finds its black-hole mass change remains within errors.

quotes.quote: Five galaxies are dispersion-dominated with rotational velocity not more than 50 \ and no obvious rotation axis: NGC 1407, NGC 1550, NGC 4472, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516. In this group, NGC 1550, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516 have the highest , although none are higher than NGC 4751. In NGC 1550 and NGC 5516 increases towards the centre, while in NGC 5328 it decreases.

In the case of NGC 1407 and NGC 4472, the data go out to only a fraction of R_e (around 0.5 and 0.25 R_e respectively) but still contribute to an appreciable change in , while in NGC 1550 the change in \ is within the error although the data extend out to R_e (Table tab:res ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1550 as a group CDG with asymmetric small-scale radio jets, a hotspot-like feature, and a corresponding X-ray cavity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1550* NGC 1550 is the CDG of the group of galaxies having the same name. The radio emission observed within our new high resolution A configuration observation revealed asymmetric small-scale jets with a physical extent of 4 kpc. At the lowest contour level created at 5 _ RMS , we find a small hotspot-like feature at the distance of 6 kpc.

gmw_00003949.name: NGC 3091
gmw_00003949.overview: NGC 3091 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0606165] that dominates the poor Hickson Compact Group 42 [arXiv:2111.02430, arXiv:astro-ph/0606165]. The galaxy features a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1306.1124] and exhibits faint, point-like radio emission at its center [arXiv:2111.02430].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3091 has a kinematically decoupled core, overlapping SINFONI datasets that resolve the sphere of influence, and a black-hole mass sensitive to dark matter.

quotes.quote: NGC 3091 displays a signature of a kinematically decoupled core.

There are two sets of SINFONI data for NGC 3091, which overlap in the inner 1.5 arcsec radius. The 100-mas dataset excels in spatial resolution while 250-mas data is better in S/N. To fit we use the 100-mas data out to a radius of 0.65 arcsec, which corresponds to the 6th radial bin in the kinematic maps. With \ of 3.6 10^9 , the radius of the sphere of influence is 0.68 arcsec, meaning that the region within the sphere of influence is fully constrained by the high-resolution data (100-mas).
There are three galaxies whose black hole masses change significantly outside their 1 errors, i.e. NGC 3091, NGC 5328 and NGC 7619.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3091 as a Hickson Compact Group CDG with faint central point-like radio emission and no AB-configuration detection.

quotes.quote: NGC 3091* NGC 3091 is a part of our VLA high resolution project from 2015 and is the CDG of a poor Hickson Compact Group, HCG 42 hickson1982 colbert2001 . The radio morphology, seen in the highest resolution 2 arcsec VLA A configuration data (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_a h), is represented by a faint point source-like radio emission in the center of the galaxy. The AB configuration data with 14 arcsec resolution at the same frequency (1.4 GHz) reveals no detection, maybe because of a lower flux density limit of our VLA observation (see Section sec:radio_central ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC3091 as the giant elliptical dominating HCG 42, notes gas emission centered on the galaxy, and uses its D_n distance as the adopted distance for the group.

quotes.quote: The Hickson Compact Group 42 HCG 42; hic82 is dominated by the giant elliptical galaxy NGC3091 (E3) and by the galaxies HGC 42b (SB0), HCG 42c (E2) and HCG 42d (E2).

Further analysis by pon96 and mul98 showed that HCG 42 has two components in the gas emission, one centered around NGC 3091 galaxy, with L _ X 10^ 41 h ^ 2 erg s ^ 1 and a second, more extended, that could be associated to the global potential well of the group mul98,mul03 . The assumed distance to HCG 42, shown in Table 1 (col 1), is based on the distance to the galaxy NGC 3091, obtained by the D _ n method fab89 .

gmw_00003950.name: z_ abs =0.239 DLA ( 5 ) toward PKS 0952+179
gmw_00003950.overview: The absorbing galaxy associated with the z_abs = 0.239 damped Lyman-alpha system toward PKS 0952+179 remains unconfirmed [arXiv:0801.0447]. Two nearby edge-on dwarf low surface brightness galaxies are candidates for this system, which exhibits multiple velocity components and weak 6284 DIB absorption [arXiv:0801.0447].

sources.summary: The paper says this absorbing galaxy is unconfirmed, has two nearby dwarf low surface brightness candidates, shows multiple velocity components, and has weak 6284 DIB absorption.

quotes.quote: The absorbing galaxy giving rise to the z_ abs =0.239 DLA ( 5 ) toward PKS 0952+179 has not been confirmed. However, two candidates lie within <4.6 h_ 70 ^ 1 kpc raot03 . The authors note that these two galaxies appear to be nearly edge-on and are classified as dwarf low surface brightness galaxies.

The DLA appears to have multiple components around the central line at z=0.239 raot00 . The adopted hydrogen column density is measured from the central feature. However, the velocity structure suggests a possible clustering.

The four DLAs toward AO 0235+164 ( z=0.524 ), Q0738+313 ( z=0.091 ), PKS 0952+179 ( z=0.239 ), and PKS 1127--145 ( z=0.313 ) are underabundant in their 6284 DIB strengths by factors of 4-10 times expected from the Galactic DIB-- N( ) relation.

gmw_00003951.name: SDSS J103024.95+551622.7
gmw_00003951.overview: SDSS J103024.95+551622.7 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.435 [arXiv:1811.02147]. It hosts a double-lobed radio source with a projected lobe separation of 116 kpc and displays diffuse emission characteristic of Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxies [arXiv:1811.02147].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J103024.95+551622.7 as a rare NLSy1 galaxy hosting a 116 kpc double-lobed FR II-like radio source with classical-radio-galaxy jet power.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the discovery of a rare large double lobed radio source with its radio core associated with a NLSy1 galaxy SDSS J103024.95+551622.7 at z = 0.435. The lobe separation is 116 kpc which is the second largest known projected size among NLSy1 radio sources. This finding is based on the analysis of 1.4 GHz data from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-centimeters (FIRST) archives. Along with the core and edge-brightened lobes we detected significant (30 ) fraction of clear diffuse emission showing typical back-flow from FR II radio galaxy lobes. For the source, we estimated a jet power of 3 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 suggesting that its jet power is similar to that of classical radio galaxies.

gmw_00003952.name: PKS 0301-243
gmw_00003952.overview: PKS 0301-243 is a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:1706.02049] that hosts an active galactic nucleus with an extended southwest-pointing radio jet [arXiv:2310.10206]. The source exhibits log-normal gamma-ray flux variability [arXiv:1901.10246] with a reported quasi-periodic modulation of approximately 2.1 to 2.3 years [arXiv:1706.02049, arXiv:2206.07614].

sources.summary: The paper reevaluates PKS 0301-243 as a blazar with reported high-energy QPV, finding log-normal flux variability and possible local-significance QPV, but treating its PSD as the exception.

quotes.quote: At high energies (HE; E>100 MeV) five blazars (PG 1553+113, PKS 2155-304, PKS 0426-380, PKS 0537-441 and PKS 0301-243) have been reported to show year-like quasi-periodic variations (QPVs) with significance >3 . As these findings are based on few cycles only, care needs to be taken to properly account for random variations which can produce intervals of seemingly periodic behaviour. We present results of an updated timing analysis for six blazars (adding PKS 0447-439 to the above), utilizing suitable methods to evaluate their longterm variability properties and to search for QPVs in their light curves. We generate ray light curves covering almost ten years, study their timing properties and search for QPVs using the Lomb-Scargle Periodogram and the Wavelet Z-transform.

(1) Comparing their probability density functions (PDFs), all sources (except PG 1553+113) exhibit a clear deviation from a Gaussian distribution, but are consistent with being log-normal, suggesting that the underlying variability is of a non-linear, multiplicative nature. (2) Apart from PKS 0301-243 the power spectral density (PSD) for all investigated blazars is close to flicker noise (power-law slope 1 ). (3) Possible QPVs with a local significance 3 are found in all light curves (apart from PKS 0426-380 and PKS 0537-441), with observed periods between (1.7-2.8) yr. The evidence is strongly reduced, however, if evaluated in terms of a global significance.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0301 243 as a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar and reports a 2.1-year gamma-ray quasi-periodic modulation, speculating it may indicate a binary supermassive black hole.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The position of the target is located at right ascension (R.A.) = 03^h 03.442^m , declination (decl.) = 24^h 07.192^m (J2000; l=214.621, b=-60.177 ).

quotes.quote: We report a nominally high-confidence ray quasi-periodic modulation in the blazar PKS 0301 243. For this target, we analyze its Fermi LAT Pass 8 data covering from 2008 August to 2017 May.

A quasi-periodicity with a period of 2.1 0.3 yr appears at the significance level of 5 , although it should be noted that this putative quasi-period variability is seen in a data set barely four times longer. We speculate that this ray quasi-periodic modulation might be evidence of a binary supermassive black hole.

PKS 0301 243 is a high-synchrotron-peaked blazar (HSP) with its synchrotron peak frequency 10^ 15 \ Hz abr , and its redshift is 0.266 pita . The High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.) has detected TeV photons from this source abr . The events are collected between 2008 August 4 and 2017 May 19 (Modified Julian Date, MJD: 54,682.66 -- 57,892.66) in the energy range from 100 MeV to 500 GeV, and in a square region of interest (ROI) of 20^ 20^ centered at the position of PKS 0301 243.

sources.summary: The paper says 0301 243 has MOJAVE-monitored superluminal motion, unresolved high-frequency core-jet emission, a 30 mas TANAMI jet, and H.E.S.S. VHE detection.

quotes.quote: 0301 243: The source has been monitored by MOJAVE at 15 GHz between 2010 and 2012 . Superluminal apparent speeds up to 2.3 0.5 were detected lister19 . Both the 15 GHz MOJAVE and 22 and 43 GHz whittier observations show an unresolved core and an extended jet towards the southwest. We recover a similar structure with an extended jet reaching 30 mas. The source has been part of the Fermi Bright Source List abdo09 , and has been detected by H.E.S.S. hess0301 with a 9.4 significance and a flux of 1.4 of that of the Crab Nebula.

sources.summary: The paper reports PKS 0301-243 below the 0.01 p-value level, with an 821-day period higher than literature values and a distinctive sinusoidal component with a second harmonic.

quotes.quote: Two sources, PKS 0301-243 and RX J0521.7+2112, are below a p_ value of 0.01, with a period of 821 days ( 2.3 years) and 1136 days ( 3.1 years), respectively. Both values are higher and not compatible with those on literature, where periods of 2 and 2.8 years are found in the high significance level ( >3 ), respectively. RX J0521.7+2112 does not show a linear trend and it is dominated by an auto-regressive component of second order with _1 = 0.38 and _2 = 0.24 . PKS 0301-243 shows a special sinusoidal behaviour, a principal periodic component of 821 days ( 2.3 years) with a second harmonic. The result on this source is shown in Figure fig:mcmcfits_harm .

sources.summary: PKS 0301--243 showed weak overall optical behavior, but selected shorter intervals had strong color-magnitude correlations across about one magnitude of flux variation.

quotes.quote: PKS 0301--243: The source was observed during 145 nights in 2009--2012. The observed trend in the entire dataset is rather weak, although during the selected shorter time intervals strong CM correlations can be noted, as shown on the additional Figure 0301 . The flux varied by 1 mag, and the optical spectral index clustered within a relatively narrow range 0.5 _ BR 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports first-time intra-night optical variability measurements for this source, finding no detected INOV across three observing epochs.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0303.4 2407: This source was first observed on 26 December 2016 and again on 01 and 02 February 2018. No INOV was detected in this source on all the three epochs. The observations reported here are the first time measurements for INOV.

gmw_00003953.name: 3C 220.1
gmw_00003953.overview: 3C 220.1 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.62 [arXiv:astro-ph/0105194]. It is situated within a cluster-like environment that exhibits extended thermal X-ray emission, while its central core displays one-sided radio jet emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0105194, arXiv:astro-ph/0511606].

sources.summary: Chandra separates 3C 220.1 into thermal cluster gas and a bright unresolved radio-core component, confirming both point-like and extended X-ray emission and suggesting direct AGN-related X-rays.

quotes.quote: We report results of an 18 ks exposure with the ACIS instrument on Chandra of the powerful z = 0.62 radio galaxy 3C 220.1. The X-ray emission separates into cluster gas of emission-weighted kT 5 keV and 0.7-12 keV luminosity (to a radius of 45 arcsec) 5.6 10^ 44 ergs s ^ 1 , and unresolved emission (coincident with the radio core). While the extended X-ray emission is clearly thermal in nature, a straightforward cooling-flow model, even in conjunction with a point-source component, is a poor fit to the radial profile of the X-ray emission.

The inference from ROSAT of both point-like and extended X-ray emission in 3C 220.1 (Hardcastle et al. 1998) is verified in a dramatic way with Chandra , visible by eye in the image and confirmed by detailed analysis of the radial profile (Fig. profile ). The radial profile is centrally spiked, fitting well the PRF in the inner bins, and extended emission is detected out to a radius of 45 arcsec.

The new high-resolution radio data for the source (kindly provided by Guy Pooley; Fig. profile ) reveal one-sided jet emission with a jet to counter-jet ratio of > 4 , so that it is possible we are seeing the X-ray emission from the vicinity of the AGN directly.

sources.summary: The paper notes likely cluster-like extended emission and finds a flatter core spectral index than earlier work, while fluxes and luminosities agree after cosmology corrections.

quotes.quote: 3C 220.1 : the source shows extended emission which is likely to be cluster-like in origin dmw01 . The spectral index we found is somewhat flatter than that found by dmw01 , who found that intrinsic absorption was required. When our results are compared with the Worrall et al. fit without intrinsic absorption then the two slopes are in good agreement. Moreover, after taking into account the different cosmological parameters, fluxes and luminosities are in agreement.

gmw_00003954.name: NGC 4102
gmw_00003954.overview: NGC 4102 is a weakly barred Sb spiral galaxy [arXiv:0807.0422, arXiv:astro-ph/0104087] located approximately 17 Mpc away [arXiv:0807.0422, arXiv:0801.4382]. It hosts a composite nucleus containing a LINER [arXiv:1008.1800], an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.4382], and intense starburst activity confined to a rotating 300 pc ring [arXiv:1008.1800].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4102 as a nearby Sb galaxy with a hard nuclear point source, reflection-dominated X-ray spectrum, radio and infrared counterparts, and AGN-like luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4102: This is a Sb galaxy at 17 Mpc, classified optically as a HII nucleus. detected a hard point source in its nucleus with extended soft emission (Fig. fig:n31n41 ). The spectrum of the nuclear point source is a power-law with 2 and no intrinsic absorption. However, a strong Fe K- line is detected with EW=2.5 keV. Even though the error on the EW is large, owing to the low S/N of the spectrum, the EW is inconsistent with the ( 300 eV) value observed in unabsorbed AGNs. Thus the spectrum is that of a reflection-dominated source.
Thus the spectrum is that of a reflection-dominated source. The image and the spectrum are similar to those of Seyfert 2 galaxies . The nucleus is also a FIRST radio source.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4102 as an Sb galaxy with segregated hard and soft nuclear X-ray emission, reflection features, strong star formation, and compelling AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 4102 This is an Sb galaxy, classified as H 2 in hfs97-3 and as H 2 /LINER in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED), at a distance of 17 Mpc t88 . The \ observation of this galaxy was presented by dsgs05 and also by tg07 . There are about 350 counts in the x-ray image of the nucleus. As a hardness ratio map (Fig. fig:n4102reg ) shows, the hard and soft emission are well segregated into a fairly hard (HR =-0.20 0.08 ), point-like source with extended, very soft (HR =-0.88^ +0.03 _ 0.06 ) emission to the west of it.

Finally, the combination of a point-like hard source, soft extended circumnuclear emission, and an Fe K line with a large EW, is one often seen in type 2 AGNs. There is undeniably strong star formation occurring in the nucleus of NGC 4102, and we cannot rule out the extremely large SFR implied if all of the observed emission is imputed to star formation alone. Nevertheless, the evidence is strong that there is an AGN in NGC 4102.

sources.summary: The paper agrees with a previous NGC 4102 classification but uses deeper imaging to tentatively assign the outer light to a spheroid and revise the Type II.o break analysis.

quotes.quote: Our classification of the profile is identical to that of pohlen-trujillo , except that our deeper INT-WFC image allows us to examine the shape of the isophotes outside the second break radius better. Since these show some sign of becoming rounder at larger radii, as might be expected from a spheroid rather than an extension of the (inclined) disk, we tentatively classify the it as III-s(?), and do not attempt to fit an exponential to the outermost part of the profile. We do, however, differ from Pohlen Trujillo in our analysis of the Type II.o part of the profile.

We fit the short, nearly flat region from 27 -35 , which as it happens is the region just inside the radius of the outer ring; Pohlen Trujillo fit a steeper region outside the outer ring (which we consider part of the transition region). Consequently, we have rather different values for h_ i and the break radius. Since our break radius is based on fitting a larger range of the profile, on both sides of the outer ring, and since the resulting break radius (42.9 ) is closer to the radius of the outer ring (36 ), we consider our analysis more correct.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4102 has complex spectra requiring thermal and narrow-line components, with small continuum reflection but a large iron-K equivalent width.

quotes.quote: The spectra of NGC 4102 are complex, and cannot be fit with the base-line model alone. By including two apec models, we obtain a better fit with ^2/d.o.f = 348.15/271 . Further, adding three narrow emission lines other than the neutral iron-K line largely improves the fit ( ^2/d.o.f = 299.3/265 ). Each line component improves the fit by ^2 > 7 for two degrees of freedom (center energy and normalization).

We obtain the line energies of 1.36 0.02 keV, 6.75^ +0.03 _ 0.04 keV, and 7.11^ +0.18 _ 0.10 keV, which possibly correspond to Mg XI, slightly blue-shifted Fe XXV, and Fe K lines, respectively. The reflection strength derived from the continuum fit is small, R_ torus < 0.14 , whereas the observed equivalent width of the iron-K line is large ( 900 eV).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4102 as a composite LINER/starburst galaxy whose nuclear star formation is confined to a rotating 300 pc ring with heavy extinction, low neon abundance, and intense central mass concentration.

quotes.quote: The composite galaxy NGC 4102 hosts a LINER nucleus and a starburst. We mapped NGC 4102 in the 12.8 \ line of [ NeII ] , using the echelon spectrometer TEXES on the NASA IRTF, to obtain a data cube with 1.5 \ spatial, and 25 km s^ 1 spectral, resolution. Combining near-infrared, radio and the [ NeII ] data shows that the extinction to the starburst is substantial, more than 2 magnitudes at K band, and that the neon abundance is less than half solar. We find that the star formation in the nuclear region is confined to a rotating ring or disk of 4.3 \ ( 300 pc) diameter, inside the Inner Lindblad Resonance. This region is an intense concentration of mass, with a dynamical mass 3 10^9 M_ , and of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4102 as a genuinely weakly barred galaxy with a central lens-like structure, strong mid-infrared concentration, and unusually high colors for an SAB system.

quotes.quote: For this population -- excepting NGC 4102 This galaxy shows a peculiar structure, with a central lens-like body or fat oval of moderate length ( D_ bar / D_ 25 0.2 ) surrounded by an external pseudo-ring probably associated with a Lindblad resonance. NGC 4102 is thus a genuine weakly barred galaxy, and not a SB. However, such a dynamical structure is still efficient to drive inward mass transfer. We also point to the very strong concentration of its mid-infrared emission (see Table tab:tab_photom ), a property common to early-type barred spirals (Fig. fig:cfrac15_type ). -, the mid-infrared color is remarkably constant around a value of 1 (ranging from 0.7 to 1.2).
Above that threshold, SB galaxies strongly dominate (the SAB galaxy with high colors is NGC 4102) and the F_ 15 /F_7 ratio follows the increase of F_ 25 /F_ 12 .

sources.summary: The paper concludes NGC 4102 is starburst-dominated but contains a weak Seyfert 2 component detected mainly through line broadening.

quotes.quote: NGC 4102 is a Sb galaxy (Sandage Bedke 1994). For Ho et. al. (1997a) it is a \ region, although its UV spectrum does not resemble that of a starburst galaxy (Kinney et al. 1993). The spectrum published by Ho et al. (1995) rather indicates a ``transition object''. Our blue spectrum shows a line obviously narrower than the \ lines, indicating that the spectrum is ``composite''.

gmw_00003955.name: RGG 1
gmw_00003955.overview: RGG 1 is classified as an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1908.00020]. It features a disky outer component and a subdominant, roughly Gaussian inner component that is classified as a pseudobulge or nuclear disk [arXiv:1908.00020].

sources.summary: RGG 1 is modeled as an S0 galaxy with a disky outer component and a subdominant Gaussian inner component classified as a pseudobulge or nuclear disk.

quotes.quote: RGG 1 (Figure fig:RGG1_Model ) is a S0 galaxy that contains a disky outer component ( n_ outer 0.8 ) with a half-light radius of 1.6 kpc. The inner Sersic component has a half-light radius of 0.7 kpc with a roughly Gaussian profile ( n_ inner 0.3 ). The disk component has an axis ratio of 0.7 and the inner component has an axis ratio of 0.5. The inner Sersic component is subdominant to the disk at all radii, which may indicate a nuclear disk as opposed to a more classical bulge component. With these factors in mind we classify RGG 1 as pseudobulge galaxy.

gmw_00003956.name: RGG 32
gmw_00003956.overview: RGG 32 is classified as an Sa spiral galaxy [arXiv:1908.00020]. It features a dim ring or spiral structure in its disk, a round dominant inner classical bulge, and an outer disk component [arXiv:1908.00020].

sources.summary: RGG 32 is an Sa galaxy with dim ring or spiral disk structure, a round dominant inner classical bulge, and an outer disk fit by a low-Sersic component.

quotes.quote: RGG 32 (Figure fig:RGG32_Model ) is an Sa galaxy with a dim ring/spiral structure in the disk, most readily seen in the residual panel of Figure fig:RGG32_Model . The bulge component has a Sersic index of n 1.6 and a half-light radius of 0.29 kpc. While the Sersic index of this component is less than 2 it has a round profile and dominates over the disk brightness at inner radii, we therefore classify it as a classical bulge. The disk component has a Sersic index of n 0.75 with a half-light radius of 2.03 kpc.

gmw_00003957.name: RGG 48
gmw_00003957.overview: RGG 48 is a disk-dominated spiral galaxy containing an active galactic nucleus and a central pseudobulge [arXiv:1908.00020]. This pseudobulge is surrounded by a partially obscured ring, a barred spiral structure, and an asymmetric, slightly offset exponential disk [arXiv:1908.00020].
