sources.summary: RGG 48 is a structured disk-dominated spiral with an obscured ring, barred spiral features, a pseudobulge-like inner component, and an asymmetric offset exponential disk.

quotes.quote: RGG 48 (Figure fig:RGG48_Model ) is a disk dominated spiral galaxy with a great deal of structure and star-forming regions. Most clearly seen in the residuals of Figure fig:RGG48_Model there is a partially obscured ring around the small central bulge and AGN. This is accompanied by an asymmetric/obscured, barred spiral which is embedded in an asymmetric disk. While these features are readily picked out in the residual image they are actually quite dim and GALFIT has difficulty converging on a model which takes into account more than the inner bulge and the disk.

We find the inner component has a Sersic index of n 0.6 and a half-light radius of 0.3 kpc. The low Sersic index, flat profile and presence of features such a the stellar ring allow this component to be confidently classified as a pseudobulge. The outer component has a Sersic index of n 0.3 with half-light radius of 2.12 kpc, fitting the classic description of an exponential disk. The disk is interesting as it is quite asymmetric and slightly offset from the pseudobulge component.

gmw_00003958.name: NGC2276
gmw_00003958.overview: NGC 2276 is an SABc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1207.1684] with a lopsided optical structure [arXiv:1207.1684] and a high star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0604549]. It exhibits a compressed western edge and an eastern gas tail [arXiv:1811.06335], driven by both tidal interactions with NGC 2300 [arXiv:1207.1684, arXiv:2106.06315] and ram pressure from the intragroup medium [arXiv:1811.06335, arXiv:2106.06315].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 2276 in X-rays, finding hot gas structures and no clear shock, with distortions most likely driven by tidal interaction with NGC 2300.

quotes.quote: NGC 2276 is an SABc galaxy with a lopsided optical structure and a slight extension towards NGC 2300 at the centre of the galaxy group. One of the spiral arms seems to follow this extension. Star forming regions are somewhat shifted to the western and southwestern side of the galaxy, where a distinct and sharp edge is visible. Radio observations by Hummel Beck ( ) showed a steeper gradient in the total radio emission and an associated polarized radio ridge in this part of the galaxy, with the magnetic field parallel to the ridge, as well as a slightly polarized radio tail extending to the east on the other side of the galaxy.

NGC 2276 shows a short tail of hot gas extending to the east. In contrast to the Chandra data analysed by Rasmussen et al. ( ), no strong gradient on the western side of the disk is visible (Fig. 2276xfig ). The higher resolution image of NGC 2276 (Fig. 2276xfig ) shows, in contrast to the corresponding map in Rasmussen et al. ( ), numerous 3 patches of emission north and west of the galaxy, which is clear evidence of soft X-ray emission of low surface-brightness, that might not have been detected by Chandra, owing to its lower sensitivity.

In NGC 2276, the visible distortions are likely produced by a tidal interaction with NGC 2300, although the observed polarized radio ridge may also be the result of the combined effects of the rotation of the galaxy and its movement through the IGM, as suggested by an increase (though within errors) in the temperature of hot gas in the area of the polarized radio ridge.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra data for NGC 2276, finding 19 discrete X-ray sources, 11 ULXs, disturbed ram-pressure morphology, and east-west subgalactic regions used for scaling-relation tests.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present results from Chandra observations of the X-ray starburst galaxies NGC 3310 and NGC 2276. We detect 27 discrete sources in NGC 3310, and 19 discrete sources in NGC 2276 with luminosities above 1.0 10^ 38 \ erg \ s^ 1 . The majority of the sources have photon indices of 1.7 2.0 , typical for X-ray binaries. Both galaxies have large numbers of ultra-luminous X-ray sources (ULXs; sources with L(0.3-10.0 \ keV)>10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 ), 14 for NGC 3310 concentrated on the circumnuclear star-forming ring and north spiral arm and 11 for NGC 2276 with the brighter ones on the west side of the galaxy which is compressed due to harassment by the intra-group medium it is moving into.

NGC 2276 has been extensively studied in the X-rays. Chandra observations have shown that it hosts 16 ULXs wolter11,wolter15 . Its diffuse X-ray morphology shows several similarities to its optical morphology with the shock-like feature along the west side and a faint tail to the east side of NGC 2276 rasmussen06,wolter15 and has temperatures of kT 0.3-0.8 \ keV and luminosities of L(0.3-2.0 \ keV)=1.9 18.0 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 for the main body and the faint tail of the galaxy. wolter15 using hydrodynamic simulations found that, though the periapsis passage of NGC 2276 and NGC 2300 85 Myr ago helped to produce tidal arms and thicken the gaseous disk, these effects are marginal compared to the effects from ram-pressure and viscous stripping of the galaxy by the IGM.

sources.summary: The paper maps molecular gas and depletion time across interacting spiral NGC 2276, linking its asymmetric star formation, short depletion times, tidal effects, and ram pressure to its pre-merger environment.

quotes.quote: Simulations by Wolter15 show that ram pressure alone could explain the morphology and the lack of some HI gas in NGC 2276. Despite its exceptional SFR, the distribution of NGC 2276's molecular gas reservoir has not previously been mapped at high spatial resolution. Spatial variations in _ depl could indicate if tidal forces and/or ram pressure have an impact on the ISM physics and _ depl as such in NGC 2276. This letter presents observations of H _2 gas (as traced by CO emission) at sub-kpc scales and spatially resolved measurements of _ depl in NGC 2276 for the first time.
The NGC 2276 data from the slit show a decrease in _ depl from 3 Gyr to 10 Myr from NE toward SW, while the center exhibits a _ depl of about 0.4 Gyr.

For NGC 2276, we find Q=-0.9 , which is significantly higher than the typical value for isolated galaxies ( Q=-5.2 0.8 ) and on the high end of isolated galaxy pairs ( Q=-2.3 1.2 , ArgudoFernandez15 ). As well as galactic-scale tides, our new observations also show evidences for ram pressure affecting NGC 2276. First, the scale-length of the ionized gas on the SW side of NGC 2276's disk is significantly shorter (up to 1-2 kpc) than the stellar emission scale-length (Fig. fig:Fig_3 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra point sources in NGC 2276, finding 17 sources, six new ULXs, diffuse hot gas, and interaction signatures from the group environment.

quotes.quote: We present results for X-ray point sources in the Sc galaxy NGC 2276, obtained by analyzing Chandra data. The galaxy is known to be very active in many wavelengths, possibly due to gravitational interaction with the central elliptical of the group, NGC 2300. However, previous XMM-Newton observations resulted in the detection of only one bright ULX and extended hot gas emission. We present here the X-ray population in NGC 2276 which comprises 17 sources. We found that 6 of them are new ULX sources in this spiral galaxy resolved for the first time by Chandra.

Spatially resolved spectroscopy shows that the data are consistent with intragroup gas being pressurized at the leading western edge of NGC 2276 due to the galaxy moving supersonically through the IGM at a velocity of 850 km/s.
We find that a large number of ULXs are present, which makes NGC 2276 one of the richest enviroment of this still enigmatic bright sources.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2276 as a starbursting spiral whose disturbed gas, western shock-like edge, X-ray tail, and H i deficiency indicate ram-pressure interaction with the NGC 2300 intragroup medium.

quotes.quote: To test this assumption, we obtained Chandra X-ray data of the starbursting spiral NGC 2276 in the NGC 2300 group of galaxies, a candidate for a strong galaxy interaction with hot intragroup gas. The data reveal a shock-like feature along the western edge of the galaxy and a low--surface-brightness tail extending to the east, similar to the morphology seen in other wavebands. Spatially resolved spectroscopy shows that the data are consistent with intragroup gas being pressurized at the leading western edge of NGC 2276 due to the galaxy moving supersonically through the intragroup medium at a velocity 850 km s ^ 1 . Detailed modelling of the gravitational potential of NGC 2276 shows that the resulting ram-pressure could significantly affect the morphology of the outer gas disc but is probably insufficient to strip large amounts of cold gas from the disc.

Evidence supporting this interpretation is the high star formation rate of NGC 2276 ( 5 M _ yr ^ 1 ; davi97 ), with much of this starburst activity occurring along the western edge, as expected if an interaction with the IGM has triggered some of the star formation via ram pressure compression of molecular gas. Also, similarly to many of the Virgo cluster spirals showing signs of ICM interactions caya90 , there is evidence that NGC 2276 is deficient in H i compared to spirals in the field.

sources.summary: NGC2276 is discussed as a comparison galaxy whose optical disturbance, loose one-sided spiral arms, and molecular-gas depletion gradients may reflect both ram pressure and tidal effects.

quotes.quote: In the observational study Wolter2015 , the galaxy NGC2276 was analysed and the source of morphological disturbance was investigated. At optical wavelengths, the galaxy exhibits a similar pattern to our sample, with loose spiral arms extended to one side of the galaxy. The authors concluded from estimation of the gravitational and hydrodynamical interaction strengths and from comparison with simulations that the galaxy will be most strongly affected by ram-pressure stripping.

In the study of Tomicic2018 , evidence of tidal interaction on NGC2276 was observed in the large scale gradient in the molecular gas depletion time across the disk of the galaxy. Such variations in the star formation efficiency of the molecular gas resulting from both tidal and ram-pressure interaction may also play a role in shaping the morphology of NGC2276.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2276, also called Arp 25, as a strongly interacting companion whose velocity and entropy bridge suggest interaction between its halo gas and the group medium.

quotes.quote: NGC 2276 (Arp 25) is considered a strongly interacting object (Gruendl et al. 1993; Hummel Beck 1995), which supports our suggestion on the supersonic interaction. The line-of-sight velocity difference between NGC 2276 and NGC 2300 amounts to 503 7 km s ^ 1 , which is supersonic with respect to the sound speed for the IGM temperature of 0.7 keV. In general, late-type spiral galaxies are considered to be currently infalling and moving on radial orbits (Biviano Katgert 2004). There is an entropy bridge (shown in green color in Fig. cf:1 ) toward the second galaxy (NGC 2276), suggesting absence of a large line-of-sight separation between the two. This implies that gas in the halo of the spiral galaxy is interacting with IGM of the group.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 2276 as a group starburst galaxy with a radio and X-ray gas tail, bow shock, tidal interaction, and ram-pressure-driven disturbance.

quotes.quote: NGC 2276 has a gas tail likely from RPS, though it is also tidally interacting with NGC 2300. The stripped tail is apparent in the radio continuum ( 1.4 GHz , davis1997 ) and at X-ray wavelengths rasmussen2006,wolter2015 . NGC 2276 also shows a bow shock front opposite to the tail, with elevated radio continuum emission, H emission, and a large number of bright X-ray sources along the leading edge davis1997,wolter2015 . rasmussen2006 and wolter2015 conclude that ram pressure (along with viscous effects) is responsible for both the disturbed morphology and high star formation rate in NGC 2276.

gmw_00003959.name: JO200
gmw_00003959.overview: JO200 is a galaxy with a generally uniform spiral morphology featuring three spiral arms [arXiv:2010.09733]. It is a ram pressure stripped galaxy where the unwound tails and similar gas and stellar velocities suggest a nearly edge-on stripping scenario [arXiv:2010.09733].

sources.summary: JO200 is described as an edge-on stripped comparison case with uniform spiral morphology, similar gas and stellar velocities, and unwound tails matching an edge-on stripping simulation.

quotes.quote: JO200 shows a generally uniform spiral morphology, with three spiral arms extending below the disk in the image.

In the case of JO200, the gas velocities are similar to the stellar velocities, with some differences in the central gradient. The stripped ionised gas appears to continue rotating with similar velocity to the galaxy disk.

The spiral arms in both the simulated and observed galaxies show a similar amount of unwinding with similar pitch angles (JO200: inner=22.0 outer=30.5, Edge-on sim: inner=15.6 outer=30.5) in the unwound tails at the lower edge of the galaxies. The resemblance suggests that the unwinding pattern of JO200 may be well described by a nearly edge-on stripping scenario.

gmw_00003960.name: JO194
gmw_00003960.overview: JO194 is a galaxy likely on a radial orbit crossing the dense core of its cluster [arXiv:2010.09733]. It exhibits unwinding spiral arms with outer pitch angles more than double those of the inner disk [arXiv:2010.09733], and it features a ram pressure stripped tail where star formation accounts for about 21% of the emission [arXiv:1811.00823].

sources.summary: JO194 has SF+Composite tail diagnostics, only about 20-30% pure-SF diffuse emission, a strong OI-diagram SF component, the highest total SFR outlier, and 4-5% tail SFR.

quotes.quote: For the majority of galaxies, the NII DD and and SII DD generally show an excellent agreement, and they indicate star formation as the predominant ionization mechanism in the tails (in JO113, JO141, JO160, JO171, JO175, JO201, JO204, JO206, JO49, JO60, JO95, JW100, JW39 plus JO147 and JO194 from SII DD) or a SF+Composite origin (NII DD for JO194, and especially for JO147).

SF still accounts for more than the half of the tail emission in JO175 and JO60 (66 and 58 , respectively), and only for about 20-30 in JO135 (29 ) , JO204 (36 ) and JO194 (21 ). Four of our galaxies have a particularly strong SF component according to the OI DD ( 40-60 in JO206, JO49, JW39 and JO194). Our jellyfishes form in total between 1.5 and 6 M_ yr^ 1 , with two outliers being JO194 with 8.3 M_ yr^ 1 and JO95 with 0.4 M_ yr^ 1 (Table 3). In another 5 galaxies the tail SFR is about 4-5 of the total (JO60, JO147, JO175, JO95, JO194), while for the remaining 5 galaxies it is at the 1-2 level (JO113, JO141, JO135, JO49, JO160).

sources.summary: JO194 is presented as a strong unwinding case on a likely radial orbit, with large outer-to-inner pitch-angle changes and spatial offsets between older and younger stellar contours.

quotes.quote: In particular, there are several galaxies (most notably JO201, JO194 and JO85) occupying the lower envelope of the trumpet-shape distribution of galaxies in phase-space close to the cluster centre, which is an indication that they could be crossing the dense core of the ICM at high speeds on radial orbits along our line of sight on first infall see Bellhouse2017,Jaffe2018 .

For some galaxies in the stripped sample, the variation between inner and outer disk pitch angles is not much greater than in the control sample, however in a couple of stronger cases, notably JO194, JW39 and JO175, the outer disc pitch angles can be more than double the inner disc pitch angles.

In the upper left of JO194, the green and blue contours show a distinct spatial offset of around 5kpc, which traces the motion of the unwinding arm. The green contour of stars which formed around 300Myr earlier, is more tightly wound with the disc of the galaxy, whilst the blue contour of younger stars lies further extended from the disc, around 6^ steeper in pitch angle.

gmw_00003961.name: ID53
gmw_00003961.overview: ID53 is a young, high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.77 [arXiv:2111.14855, arXiv:2305.15346]. It is characterized by strong Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2111.14855], low stellar metallicity [arXiv:2111.14855, arXiv:2305.15346], and two spatially resolved continuum components [arXiv:2111.14855].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MUSE 53 as a special case where the spectrum determines the best-fit parameters, while adding the surface-brightness profile tightens constraints.

quotes.quote: In the case of MUSE 53, the parameters are constrained entirely by spectrum, which can be expected from their posterior distributions given by spectrum (Figure fig-lnliksp-53 ) that are much sharper than those by surface brightness profile (Figure fig-lnliksbp-53 ). Although the surface brightness profile does not alter the best-fit parameter values determined by spectrum, it still makes the parameter constraints tighter. This is clearly seen in the total posterior distributions (Figure fig-lnliktot-53 ) in comparison with the spectrum posterior distributions (Figure fig-lnliksp-53 ).

However, we present posterior distributions for spectrum ( L _ sp ) and surface brightness profile ( L _ SBP ) for MUSE 53 because it is a special case of its best-fit parameter set being determined solely by spectrum. Nevertheless, parameter constraints become tighter by including surface brightness profile, which is revealed by comparing Figures fig-lnliktot-53 and fig-lnliksp-53 . The best-fit model spectrum and surface brightness profile determined with ( L _ tot ) for the seven galaxies are also shown in Figures fig-bestfit-tot-82 , fig-bestfit-tot-6905 , fig-bestfit-tot-1343 , fig-bestfit-tot-53 , fig-bestfit-tot-171 , fig-bestfit-tot-547 , and fig-bestfit-tot-364 .

sources.summary: The paper presents ID53 as a young z=4.77 starburst with strong Ly emission, resolved components, low stellar metallicity, and possible high ionizing-photon escape or complex star formation history.

quotes.quote: We present a case study of ID53 at z=4.77 , the UV-brightest (but L ^ ) star-forming galaxy at z>3 in the MUSE eXtremely Deep Field with a mass of 10^9 M _ . In addition to very strong Lyman- (Ly ) emission, we clearly detect the (stellar) continuum and an N v P-Cygni feature, interstellar absorption, fine-structure emission and nebular C iv emission lines in the 140 hr spectrum. Continuum emission from two spatially resolved components in Hubble Space Telescope data are blended in the MUSE data, but we show that the nebular C iv emission originates from a subcomponent of the galaxy.

Models with a young age and low metallicity (log _ 10 (age/yr)=6.5-7.6 and [ Z/H ] =-2.15 to -1.15) are preferred, but the details depend on the assumed SFH. The intrinsic H luminosity of the best-fit models is an order of magnitude higher than the H luminosity inferred from Spitzer /IRAC data, which either suggests a high escape fraction of ionising photons, a high relative attenuation of nebular to stellar dust, or a complex SFH. The metallicity appears lower than the metallicity in more massive galaxies at z=3-5 , consistent with the scenario according to which younger galaxies have lower metallicities.

sources.summary: The paper uses the z=4.77 background galaxy ID53, the continuum-brightest z>3 MXDF galaxy, to measure Ly transmission and compare IGM absorption with foreground galaxy density.

quotes.quote: The background galaxy, ID53 at z=4.77 Matthee22 , is the continuum-brightest galaxy at z>3 in the MXDF while it has a typical L ^ _ UV luminosity. The deep spectrum and the available infrared photometry allowed detailed modeling of the young stellar population and dust attenuation, yielding a good fit with the first estimate of the stellar metallicity in such a high-redshift galaxy Matthee22 . The accurate SED fit simultaneously provides the intrinsic spectrum in the _0 =1026-1216 region without attenuation through the intergalactic medium e.g.
We use this high density of redshifts to construct a three dimensional map of the galaxy density around the sight-line to the galaxy ID53, enabling us to directly compare density and HI transmission.

gmw_00003962.name: NGC 210
gmw_00003962.overview: NGC 210 is a regular barred spiral galaxy with well-developed, symmetrical spiral arms [arXiv:0803.0109]. Although it appears optically regular, its outer neutral hydrogen structure reveals a long, co-rotating gas tail that provides clear evidence of a recent merger [arXiv:0803.0109].

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 210 as optically regular but H I-disturbed, with a long co-rotating gas tail interpreted as recent merger evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 210 is a good example of a galaxy that shows no indications of accretion or interaction in the optical, but where the \ data give clear evidence for a recent merging event. Fig. \ f_interactions (middle left panel) shows that NGC 210 is a regular barred galaxy with well developed, symmetrical spiral arms. In the region of the optical disk, the \ contours indicate a regular structure. In the outer parts, however, the \ shows a long tail extending to the SE and containing about 6 10^8 . This is 10 of the total \ mass of NGC 210.

gmw_00003963.name: NGC 2985
gmw_00003963.overview: NGC 2985 is a nearby early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:0810.3503] featuring tightly wound, bisymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:0810.3503, arXiv:astro-ph/0509708] and a regular stellar velocity field [arXiv:0705.4162]. It exhibits an asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:0803.0109], likely caused by a tidal interaction with its companion galaxy NGC 3027 [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:0803.0109].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2985 as a regular early-type spiral control galaxy, noting a companion interaction, symmetric stellar rotation, weak ionised emission, and regular gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 2985 is an early-type spiral galaxy, with regular gas and stellar distributions. It is the control for NGC 4151. The disk orientation is determined by an inclination of i=40 and a major-axis position angle of PA=180 . report a tidal interaction with its companion NGC 3027.

The stellar velocity field of NGC 2985 shows a regular and symmetric pattern, consistent with motions in an axisymmetric gravitational potential (Fig.  pair4 ). The PA of the kinematic major-axis (-2 ) is aligned with the photometric major-axis of the outer disc. The velocity dispersion rises inwards, with 100 km s ^ 1 at 15 from the centre and 150 km s ^ 1 in the inner 5 . There is very little emission from the ionised gas \ and \ in NGC 2985 (Fig.  pair4 ). The emission lines flux peaks in the inner 2 with very little flux outside. The \ (Fig.  kinHb2 ) and \ (Fig.  pair4 ) kinematics are similar: the velocity fields are regular, showing the same overall symmetry as the stellar velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2985 as a nearby bright spiral whose bisymmetric arms, rotation curve, surface-density profile, and disk diagnostics favor a baryon-dominated inner optical disk over a submaximal halo-heavy model.

quotes.quote: NGC 2985 is a nearby (D = 18 Mpc) bright spiral galaxy. An image taken from DSS, which is reproduced in Fig. fig7 , shows that it has developed well defined bisymmetric spiral arms. have measured its rotation curve and the velocity dispersions of the stars in two slit positions so that the three dimensional velocity dispersion components are available. Moreover, they have imaged the galaxy and derived its surface density profile which is shown in Fig. fig8 .

In Fig. fig10 I show a submaximal disk model for the rotation curve of NGC 2985. The fit to the rotation curve, which includes now a dark matter halo contributing as much as the disk, is of the same quality as in the case of a maximum disk, but both the predicted number of spiral arms and the Q parameters as well as the implied mass-to-light ratio indicate that this not viable model for the disk of NGC 2985.
As an example of a high surface brightness spiral I have analyzed NGC 2985 along these lines and found that it seems to be dominated in the inner part, where the optical disk resides, by baryons.

sources.summary: The paper presents long-slit spectroscopy of the Sab spiral NGC 2985, derives its stellar velocity ellipsoid, and finds disc heating broadly consistent with gradual heating models.

quotes.quote: Here we present follow-up observations of the morphologically similar Sab galaxy NGC 2985, still only the second galaxy for which this ratio has been determined outside of the solar neighbourhood. The result is consistent with simple disc heating models which predict ratios of _z / _R less than one.

We have applied the analysis described above to the Sab galaxy NGC 2985. It is a typical multi-armed spiral with an inclination of 36 ^ (Grosbol, 1985). Its distance, using an Hubble constant of 75, is 18 Mpc and the amplitude of the inclination corrected HI rotation curve is about 250 km/s (WHISP database), see table galpar .

The slightly rounder velocity ellipsoid found in NGC 2985 may reflect a genuine difference in the internal heating mechanisms. Since both galaxies are multi-armed type spiral galaxies but the Sab galaxy NGC 2985 is of slightly earlier type than NGC 488, which is an Sb galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2985 as an inclined Sab galaxy with tightly wound two-armed structure, measured stellar rotation, and a known velocity-dispersion ellipsoid.

quotes.quote: The Sab galaxy NGC 2985 is another example of an early type galaxy in our sample. This galaxy has an inclination of about 36 (Grosbol 1985), and distance of 18 Mpc with Hubble constant of 75 km/s/Mpc. The spiral pattern in NGC 2985 is similar to that in galaxy NGC 488. NGC 2985 has a regular two-armed spiral pattern outlined by tightly wound narrow arms, as can be seen on the left panel of Figure 12.

We build equilibrium models of NGC 2985 based on measurements by Gerssen et al. (2000). Figure 13 shows the observed rotation curve of the stellar disk of NGC 2985 obtained with long-slit absorption spectra along the major axis of the galaxy. The dashed line in Figure 13 is our best-fit to the observational data used in the numerical simulations.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 2985 as an asymmetric spiral whose H I distribution, regular velocity field, and likely interaction with NGC 3027 produced lopsided structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2985 (UGC 5253) Fig. \ f_n2985 shows the total \ map (contours) for NGC 2985 (the galaxy on the right) and its surroundings overlaid on a DSS image. NGC 2985 is a spiral galaxy at a distance of 18 Mpc. A number of features can be noted that are relevant here. First, the \ distribution is very asymmetric. This may be due to an interaction with NGC 3027, the galaxy 20 ' (120 kpc) east of NGC 2985 and of similar redshift as NGC 2985 (velocity difference 250 ).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2985's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, rings, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2985 (III-d; (R')SA(rs)ab) : The central part of the disk (out to 43 ) has a tightly wound multi-armed structure, followed by another weaker spiral which extends out to 107 (11 kpc). From there on out the spiral arms are more tenuous, forming a pseudo-ring near 68 (7 kpc). The image quality does not let us trace the profile reliably further out than 312 (32 kpc), but the profile is clearly Type III. Since the ellipticity at 150 -200 is only marginally higher than the ellipticity further inside, and since spiral structure is clearly visible beyond the break radius, we classify the profile as III-d.

sources.summary: The paper flags UGC05253 as a SPARC galaxy with elevated dark-matter asphericity significance and as a heavy dark-matter disk candidate for future small-scale structure detection.

quotes.quote: The five SPARC galaxies with the highest dark matter asphericity significance are NGC5005, UGCA444, UGC11914, NGC7793 and UGC05253, with ^2_ dof =0.48, 0.49, 0.61, 0.59, 0.48 respectively; ^2_ dof is defined (see Sec.  sparc ) such that ^2_ dof 1 would be a robust indicator of asphericity. The five galaxies with the highest percentage of negative values are NGC2683, F563-1, UGC11914, NGC6015, NGC7793 with 30,18,21,20,33 of negative values respectively. Currently, the error bars on the rotation curves are too high for these to be significant evidence of non-spherically symmetric dark matter.

Using the HIDM model fit from , we identified four galaxies that are compatible with a particularly heavy dark mater disk built up through DM accretion in the baryonic disk: UGC11557, NGC7793, UGC06614 and UGC05253. These are displayed in Fig. 4 where it can be seen that NGC7793 and UGC05253 may be good candidates for future detection of small-scale structure in the dark matter distribution using the asphericity diagnostic, if measurement accuracy can be sufficiently improved.

gmw_00003964.name: NGC 3027
gmw_00003964.overview: NGC 3027 is a galaxy located approximately 120 kiloparsecs east of NGC 2985 [arXiv:0803.0109]. It exhibits an asymmetric distribution of neutral hydrogen, and its past passage likely caused a tidal displacement of NGC 2985's outer disk [arXiv:0803.0109].

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 3027 as a similar-redshift neighbor of NGC 2985 with asymmetric H I whose passage likely displaced NGC 2985s outer disk.

quotes.quote: This may be due to an interaction with NGC 3027, the galaxy 20 ' (120 kpc) east of NGC 2985 and of similar redshift as NGC 2985 (velocity difference 250 ). NGC 3027 also has an asymmetric \ distribution. Interestingly, the velocity field of NGC 2985 is, overall, fairly regular and dominated by differential rotation (Fig. \ f_n2985 , right panel). It looks as if the passage of NGC 3027 has caused a tidal displacement of the outer disk of NGC 2985 with respect to the inner disk without destroying it and making NGC 2985 very lopsided in appearance.

gmw_00003965.name: BzK-16000
gmw_00003965.overview: BzK-16000 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.52 [arXiv:1404.7393]. Observations of its molecular gas reveal a large reservoir of hydrogen gas and suggest sub-thermal excitation of carbon monoxide at the J=2 transition [arXiv:1005.4965].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO(1-0) detection in BzK-16000, finding the largest molecular gas mass among the three BzK galaxies and evidence for sub-thermal CO excitation by J=2.

quotes.quote: We find H _2 gas masses of 8.3 1.9 10^ 10 \ M_ , 5.6 1.4 10^ 10 \ M_ and 1.23 0.34 10^ 11 \ M_ for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively, assuming a conversion _ CO =3.6 \ M_ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 .

We find brightness temperature ratios between the CO 2-1 and CO 1-0 emission lines of 0.80_ 0.22 ^ +0.35 , 1.22_ 0.36 ^ +0.61 and 0.41_ 0.13 ^ +0.23 for BzK-4171, BzK-21000 and BzK-16000, respectively. At the depth of our observations it is not possible to discern between thermodynamic equilibrium or sub-thermal excitation of the molecular gas at J=2 . However, the low temperature ratio found for BzK-16000 suggests sub-thermal excitation of CO already at J=2 .

Interestingly, in the case of BzK-16000, our measurements suggest that the CO emission is sub-thermal ( r_ 21 <1 ) already at J=2 , resembling what appears to be the case for the inner disk of the Milky Way galaxy Fixsen1999 . Our estimate of r_ 21 0.4 differs from the LTE at the 2 level. Measurements of higher J transitions will help to study this particular galaxy in more detail.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLA CO(1-0) observations of BzK-16000, including D plus C array data, redshifted line coverage, and imaging choices.

quotes.quote: CO(1--0) observations of BzK-4171 and BzK-16000 were obtained in the D, and D plus C array configurations, respectively. D-array observations of both sources were performed during 2010 March and 2010 April (VLA project: AC975). C-array observations of BzK-16000 were done during 2012 April (VLA project 12A-264). At the redshifts z_ CO(2-1) =1.4652 and z_ CO(2-1) =1.5249 Daddi2010a , the CO(1--0) line is shifted to 46.7594 GHz and 45.6537 GHz for BzK-4171 and BzK-16000, respectively. The calibrators and correlator configuration used for the D-array data were the same as those used to observe BzK-21000.

gmw_00003966.name: MRK 1216
gmw_00003966.overview: MRK 1216 is an isolated, compact elliptical relic galaxy [arXiv:1701.05197, arXiv:1902.02938] containing a 13-billion-year-old stellar population [arXiv:1711.09983, arXiv:2302.05453] and a highly concentrated dark matter halo [arXiv:1902.02938, arXiv:2110.13931]. The galaxy features an extended hot X-ray atmosphere [arXiv:1711.09983] and is considered a fossil group [arXiv:2110.13931].

sources.summary: The paper confirms Mrk 1216 as a massive relic galaxy with compact early-type structure, old stellar populations, high velocity dispersion, and a less extreme relic state than NGC 1277.

quotes.quote: We confirm two new local massive relic galaxies, i.e. untouched survivors of the early universe massive population: Mrk 1216 and PGC 032873. Both show early and peaked formation events within very short timescales ( < 1 Gyr) and thus old mean mass-weighted ages ( 13 Gyr). Their star formation histories remain virtually unchanged out to several effective radii, even when considering the steeper IMF values inferred out to 3 effective radii.

Mrk 1216 is an isolated galaxy, with only two closer galaxies at 1 Mpc distance, that has been analyzed by Yildirim2015 . The authors showed that it is a compact, fast rotating, oblate early-type galaxy with no signs of substructure. Its peaked velocity dispersion is indicative of a high concentration of mass in its center, as proven by the large black hole mass they derived with dynamical models.

Mrk 1216, instead, seems to be at the other end: it is the less outlier in all the scaling relations, it presents a larger size and it built up its mass in a slightly larger timescale (by 1 Gyr difference), showing a later accretion history.

sources.summary: The paper treats MRK 1216 as a high-concentration fossil-group comparison object, contrasting its halo mass, concentration, central cooling, and black hole properties with NGC 1600.

quotes.quote: This puts it in similar company with two other known outliers: NGC 6482 and MRK 1216; whose concentrations are c_ 200 =30.4 4.3 and c_ 200 =32.2 7.1 respectively ( Buote2017, Buote2019 ). NGC 1600 has a lower _ 200 than either NGC 6482 and MRK 1216, but it also has a larger mass, compared to M _ 200 5 10^ 12 M _ for NGC 6482 and MRK 1216. This follows the trend that _ 200 decreases as M _ 200 increases as predicted by CDM models ( Navarro96,Macci2007 ). Fossil group For both NGC 6482 and MRK 1216, the high concentration is associated with the fact that both systems are considered fossil groups.

A direct comparison to one of the previously mentioned systems, MRK 1216, provides some useful insight. Both MRK 1216 and NGC 1600 have t_ /t_ ff < 20 within their centers. While the region extends to roughly the scale radius _s = 12 kpc for MRK 1216, the region of low t_ /t_ ff is within the central 3--5 kpc for NGC 1600, much lower then the calculated scale radius of _s = 15 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra X-ray data to show Mrk 1216 is a compact elliptical relic with an exceptionally concentrated dark-matter halo and relaxed hot plasma.

quotes.quote: The Extremely High Dark Matter Halo Concentration of the Relic Compact Elliptical Galaxy Mrk 1216 David A. \ Buote and Aaron J. \ Barth Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of California at Irvine, 4129 Frederick Reines Hall, Irvine, CA 92697-4575; buote@uci.edu Accepted for Publication in The Astrophysical Journal Spatially compact stellar profiles and old stellar populations have established compact elliptical galaxies (CEGs) as local analogs of the high-redshift ``red nuggets'' thought to represent the progenitors of today's early-type galaxies (ETGs).

The high value of c_ 200 we measure implies an unusually early formation time that firmly establishes the relic nature of the DM halo in . The highly concentrated DM halo leads to a higher DM fraction and smaller total mass slope at 1R_e compared to nearby normal ETGs. In addition, the highly concentrated total mass profile of \ cannot be described by MOND without adding DM, and it deviates substantially from the Radial Acceleration Relation.
Finally, other than its compact stellar size, the stellar, gas, and DM properties of \ are remarkably similar to those of the nearby fossil group NGC 6482.

sources.summary: The paper presents MRK 1216 as an isolated compact relic galaxy with an extended hot atmosphere, short cooling time, old stars, radio-mechanical AGN feedback, and high X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: We find that the hot atmosphere surrounding MRK 1216 extends far beyond the stellar population and has an 0.5--7 keV X-ray luminosity of L_ X =(7.0 0.2) 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , which is similar to the nearby X-ray bright giant ellipticals. The hot gas has a short central cooling time of 50 Myr and the galaxy has a 13 Gyr old stellar population. The presence of an X-ray atmosphere with a short nominal cooling time and the lack of young stars indicate the presence of a sustained heating source, which prevented star formation since the dissipative origin of the galaxy 13 Gyrs ago. The central temperature peak and the presence of radio emission in the core of the galaxy indicate that the heating source is radio-mechanical AGN feedback.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 1216 as an observed compact massive relic benchmark, comparing its old stellar age and kinematics with MOND collapse models.

quotes.quote: Our analysis focuses on the collapse dynamics of isolated non-rotating post-Big-Bang gas clouds, revealing kinematic and density profiles comparable to observed compact massive relic galaxies like NGC 1277, Mrk 1216, and PGC 032873.

This is similar to compact massive relics ( 2014ApJ...780L..20T, 2017MNRAS.467.1929F, 2024MNRAS.527.8793S ) where NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 are found to have formed their stars around 10 Gyr ago.

Here we study the kinematical properties of one of the models from 2022MNRAS.516.1081E of comparable mass with the observed compact massive relic galaxies NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 2014ApJ...780L..20T,2017MNRAS.467.1929F listed in Table tab:values and find the model to be very similar to these observed compact massive relic galaxies. These compact massive relics are found to have their average stellar ages similar to ETGs of comparable mass in a high-density environment as found by 2005ApJ...621..673T , where a galaxy with M_ * = 10^ 11 M_ would have formed 10 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 1216 as one of three fully characterized local massive compact relic galaxies with old stars and morphology, kinematics, and density profiles resembling red nuggets.

quotes.quote: So far, only three relics have been found and were fully characterised in the local Universe: NGC1277 Trujillo+14, Martin-Navarro+15_IMF_relic, Beasley+18 , Mrk 1216, and PGC 032873 Ferre-Mateu+17 . These three objects all show a very quick (time-scales <1 Gyr) high- z star formation and thus are consistent with having stars with a very old mean mass-weighted age ( 13 Gyr, almost as old as the Universe). Moreover, their morphology, kinematics, and density profiles perfectly resemble those of z > 2 red nuggets Ferre-Mateu+17 .

gmw_00003967.name: NGC 6482
gmw_00003967.overview: NGC 6482 is a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1809.01251, arXiv:1701.08772] and the brightest member of a nearby fossil group [arXiv:1809.01251, arXiv:2201.05080]. It features a highly concentrated dark matter halo [arXiv:1611.08600, arXiv:2110.13931], patchy dust [arXiv:1701.08772], and complex stellar kinematics that suggest a major-merger origin [arXiv:1809.01251, arXiv:1403.4627].

sources.summary: NGC 6482 is studied as a massive fossil-group BCG with a young, metal-rich central population, shallow metallicity gradient, and evidence favoring major-merger formation.

quotes.quote: We present long-slit spectroscopic observations along the major, minor, and diagonal axes of NGC 6482 and NGC 7556, which are the BCGs of two nearby FGs. The measurements include spatially resolved stellar kinematics and radial profiles of line-strength indices, which we converted into stellar population parameters using single stellar-population models. NGC 6482 and NGC 7556 are very massive ( M_ 10^ 11.5 ) and large ( D_ 25 50 kpc) galaxies. They host a centrally concentrated stellar population, which is significantly younger and more metal rich than the rest of the galaxy.

The age gradients of both galaxies are somewhat larger than those of the other FG BCGs studied so far, whereas their metallicity gradients are similarly negative and shallow. Moreover, they have negligible gradients of element abundance ratio. The measured metallicity gradients are less steep than those predicted for massive galaxies that formed monolithically and evolved without experiencing any major merger. We conclude that the observed FGs formed through major mergers rather than being failed groups that lacked bright satellite galaxies from the beginning.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 6482 as a slow rotator with photometric-kinematic misalignment, rising-profile triaxial signatures, and complicated kinematics associated with merger-remnant interpretations.

quotes.quote: All of the five galaxies, and particularly NGC 6482, also show evidence of misalignment between their photometric and kinematic PAs.
NGC 6482 specifically, highlighted in Figure Fig:Misalignment , is misaligned by 60^ .

In the same way, we have argued that the SRs with rising profiles also show clear signs of triaxiality (as typified by NGC 5982 and NGC 6482). They generally show some evidence of a central LV component that transitions to slow disk-like rotation. In addition, the PA tends to be misaligned with the photometric axis in the central regions. NGC 6482 particularly shows strong kinematic misalignment of between 20^ and 50^ out to at least 2 R_e . Based on their complicated kinematics, both galaxies have been put forward as recent merger remnants Statler1991, Oosterloo1994, DelBurgo2008 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6482 as the nearest fossil-group galaxy, finding a red globular-cluster system, high X-ray gas metallicity, low specific frequency, and a standard surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: The main results of this study are: We detected 369, 1410 and 1918 GCs down to magnitude z =24.7, 26.2 and 26.2 in NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively; after completeness correction and assuming a Gaussian GCLF with expected M^0 _g=-7.2 and _ GCLF =1.4 , the number of GCs in each FOV are: 1140 87, 3613 295 and 11577 1046, respectively. The mean g z colors are: 1.26, 1.11 and 1.20 for NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively. The mean color for NGC 6482 is unusually red; it is interesting that this is also the FG with the highest X-ray gas metallicity.

The derived values of S_N are: 1.9 0.1 , 3.1 0.3 and 6.3 0.6 for NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively. While NGC 6482 is well-described by a standard \ profile, the two brighter galaxies are better fitted by core- \ models.

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 6482 as a nearby fossil group, measuring an unusually high halo concentration and favoring weaker adiabatic-contraction models to ease concentration and baryon-fraction tensions.

quotes.quote: Using a modest \ ACIS-S observation for the nearby fossil group NGC 6482, khos04a presented intriguing evidence for an extremely high concentration value (c_ 200 60, M_ 200 4 10^ 12 M_ ) compared to the mean \ M relation 7 , e.g., dutt14a , from which they inferred a very early formation time ( z 5 ).

We measure c_ 200 =32.2 7.1 and M_ 200 =(4.5 0.6) 10^ 12 M_ for the fiducial model. For a halo of this mass, the value we measure for c_ 200 exceeds the expected value of c_ 200 =7.1 for relaxed \ halos dutt14a by 3.5 with respect to the statistical observational error, and by 6 considering the intrinsic scatter in the \ M relation. This measurement situates \ as the most extreme outlier known for a fossil system.

We argue the X-ray analysis favors the ad hoc ``Forced Quenching'' AC model of dutt15a , since not only does it have the advantages of producing smaller c_ 200 and f_ b,200 than the un-contracted models, but it also yields the largest of the AC models (equal to the un-contracted models) which agrees best with the stellar mass predicted by the SPS models.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6482 as a fossil-group elliptical whose patchy warm ionized gas may trace cooling flows connected to its hot X-ray halo and dust reservoir.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6482, the gas is patchy and its velocity structure may hint at cooling flows as the dominant origin scenario e.g., see fabian94 . NGC 6482 is known to be a fossil group system; it is a single elliptical galaxy with a group-like X-ray halo and in which all other group members are at least two optical magnitudes fainter see mulchaey00,khosroshahi04 . This galaxy is also known to harbor a patchy dust reservoir alamomartinez12 . We do not have the spatial resolution to study the detailed morphology of the gas, but if the origin of the warm ionized gas is indeed via cooling flows, then we would expect to see a connection with the hot X-ray-emitting halo gas.
Future comparisons of deep narrow-band and X-ray imaging could help us test the cooling flow origin hypothesis in MASSIVE galaxies like NGC 6482.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 6482 as an early fossil-group mass-profile case with an NFW-like concentrated profile, no central cool-core drop, and radio signs of recent AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Possibly the first mass profiles of FGs to be computed were those of NGC 6482 Khosroshahi2004 , RX J1416.4+2315 Khosroshahi2006 , and ESO 3060170 Sun2004 . The first two systems shows a mass profile well described by an NFW, with a high central concentration that was interpreted as a sign of early formation. Later works were focused on deeper studies of individual FGs and found controversial results: Khosroshahi2004, Khosroshahi2006 found no central drop in NGC 6482 and RX J1416.4+2315.

It is interesting to note that Miraghaei2014 studied three of the cited clusters (NGC 6482, RX J1416.4+2315, and ESO 3060170) using radio observations, finding signs of recent AGN activity only in the first two. However, the AGN power computed was not sufficient to remove CCs from these clusters.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6482 as a fossil-group comparison, citing its high halo concentration, lower halo mass relative to NGC 1600, and possible billion-solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: This puts it in similar company with two other known outliers: NGC 6482 and MRK 1216; whose concentrations are c_ 200 =30.4 4.3 and c_ 200 =32.2 7.1 respectively ( Buote2017, Buote2019 ). NGC 1600 has a lower _ 200 than either NGC 6482 and MRK 1216, but it also has a larger mass, compared to M _ 200 5 10^ 12 M _ for NGC 6482 and MRK 1216. This follows the trend that _ 200 decreases as M _ 200 increases as predicted by CDM models ( Navarro96,Macci2007 ). Fossil group For both NGC 6482 and MRK 1216, the high concentration is associated with the fact that both systems are considered fossil groups.
NGC 6482 may contain a SMBH with M_ BH 10^9 M _ based upon simulations ( Raouf2016 ), although find no significant effect when including a SMBH of this mass in their model fits.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 6482 as the brightest fossil-group galaxy, with Core-halo ionized gas, regular stellar rotation, abundant warm gas, cooling-flow interpretation, and possible merger or AGN-feedback signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 6482 (see Fig. Figure_AB9 ): This galaxy is the brightest object of its fossil group Corsini2018 . The ionised gas in our image has a Core-halo morphology. The object was studied in the context of the MASSIVE survey Ma2014 , were found a regular rotation pattern in the stellar component, well aligned with the photometric axis. They found that it is one of the galaxies with more warm gas from the sample, extended up to 16 kpc, in contrast to the ionised gas that goes up to 4 kpc. They associated it to cooling flows that may be connected to the X-ray emission in the system Pandya2017 .

gmw_00003968.name: J0759+2750
gmw_00003968.overview: J0759+2750 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a star-forming and active galactic nucleus composite emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its resolved low-frequency and 10 GHz radio emission is partly aligned with a tidal structure visible in the host galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0759+2750 as a radio AGN with resolved low-frequency and 10 GHz emission partly aligned with tidal structure.

quotes.quote: J0759+2750 is a radio AGN hosted by a SF-AGN composite emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by both LoTSS and at 10 GHz, but unresolved by RACS, FIRST, and VLASS (Figure fig:0759 ). The eastern extension to the 144 MHz emission is similar to the tidal feature visible in the host galaxy in that direction but does not overlap perfectly with that feature. The 144 MHz extension is slightly broader in width than the tidal feature. The 10 GHz morphology shows both a diffuse, non-linear component and an unresolved component, perhaps indicative of both nuclear SF and AGN activity.

gmw_00003969.name: J0843+3549
gmw_00003969.overview: J0843+3549 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a Seyfert emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is resolved into multiple components, showing an extreme change in position angle from kiloparsec to sub-kiloparsec scales [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0843+3549 as a Seyfert radio AGN with multi-component emission and evidence for a precessing radio jet.

quotes.quote: J0843+3549 is a radio AGN hosted by a Seyfert AGN emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by LoTSS, FIRST, and VLASS, and shows multiple components at 10 GHz (Figure fig:0843 ). The extended feature to the compact emission in each map has a varying deconvolved position angle (PA) from 131.5 5.1 at 144 MHz to 18 15 at 10 GHz. This extreme PA change from kpc- to sub-kpc-scales shows that

gmw_00003970.name: J0851+4050
gmw_00003970.overview: J0851+4050 is a possible radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The galaxy exhibits visible tidal features, with resolved 144 MHz radio emission oriented perpendicular to these structures [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0851+4050 as a possible LINER-hosted radio AGN with marginal 144 MHz resolution and radio-excess constraints.

quotes.quote: J0851+4050 is a possible radio AGN hosted by LINER emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is marginally resolved by LoTSS but unresolved at 10 GHz, and is a non-detection in FIRST and VLASS (Figure fig:0851 ). The resolved emission at 144 MHz is perpendicular to the tidal features visible in the host galaxy. Because it is a non-detection at 1.4 GHz, the radio-based SFR we calculated in Section sec:radio_excess is an upper limit. Given this constraint, it still falls in the radio excess region of Figure fig:radio_origins .

gmw_00003971.name: J0916+4542
gmw_00003971.overview: J0916+4542 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a star-forming active galactic nucleus composite emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The galaxy features diffuse 144 MHz radio emission that extends along its east-west tidal structures [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0916+4542 as a composite-host radio AGN with diffuse 144 MHz emission along tidal features and a 10 GHz detection.

quotes.quote: J0916+4542 is a radio AGN hosted by a SF-AGN composite emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by LoTSS and at 10 GHz, and unresolved by FIRST (Figure fig:0916 ). The diffuse emission at 144 MHz is approximately extended along the east-west tidal features of the host galaxy. The radio source located 32 to the southwest does not appear to be associated with the host SPM, and may either be associated with SDSS J091649.73+454130.4 or may be an FRII-like lobe associated with WISEA J091653.20+454128.7. Without a detection at 3 GHz, the lower limit to the spectral index is _3^ 10 >-0.91 .

gmw_00003972.name: J1015+3914
gmw_00003972.overview: J1015+3914 is a star-formation-dominated radio source hosted by a star-forming emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its diffuse 10 GHz radio emission indicates recent star formation activity, and its resolved low-frequency features do not spatially coincide with the host galaxy's visible tidal features [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1015+3914 as a star-formation dominated radio source with resolved low-frequency and 10 GHz emission.

quotes.quote: J1015+3914 is a SF-dominated radio hosted by a SF emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by LoTSS and at 10 GHz, marginally resolved by VLASS, and unresolved by FIRST (Figure fig:1015 ). The resolved features at 144 MHz to the northeast are not spatially coincident with the visible tidal features of the host galaxy. The diffuse morphology of the 10 GHz radio emission is highly indicative of recent SF activity. The radio spectrum is steep with _3^ 10 =-0.64 0.10 .

gmw_00003973.name: J1018+3613
gmw_00003973.overview: J1018+3613 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a Seyfert emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is resolved by LoTSS but remains unresolved by FIRST, as well as at 3 GHz and 10 GHz [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1018+3613 as a Seyfert radio AGN with LoTSS-resolved emission but compact FIRST, 3 GHz, and 10 GHz detections.

quotes.quote: J1018+3613 is a radio AGN hosted by a Seyfert AGN emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved only by LoTSS, and is unresolved by FIRST, and at 3 GHz and 10 GHz (Figure fig:1018 ). The extended feature to the southeast in the LoTSS map is not spatially coincident with the southeast tidal structure visible in the host galaxy.

gmw_00003974.name: J1041+1105
gmw_00003974.overview: J1041+1105 is a radio source hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The radio emission is likely dominated by star formation and is spatially coincident with its host galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1041+1105 as a likely star-formation dominated LINER-hosted radio source with uncertain low-frequency morphology.

quotes.quote: J1041+1105 is likely a SF-dominated radio source hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by RACS, unresolved at 10 GHz, and is a non-detection by both FIRST and VLASS (Figure fig:1041 ). The morphology at 888 MHz is difficult to gauge, as the local image RMS is 470 Jy. When considering this constraint, only the southern radio component shown in the RACS panel of Figure fig:1041 , produced using an image RMS equal to the nominal survey sensitivity of 250 Jy, remains. Indeed, this southern component is spatially coincident with the host galaxy, while the northern and diffuse components are likely artifacts.

gmw_00003975.name: J1113+2714
gmw_00003975.overview: J1113+2714 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a star-forming emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is unresolved and spatially coincident with the optical nucleus of the host galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1113+2714 as a star-forming host radio AGN with compact nuclear emission and limited spectral-index constraints.

quotes.quote: J1113+2714 is a radio AGN hosted by a SF emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is unresolved by RACS, FIRST and at 10 GHz, and is a non-detection by VLASS (Figure fig:1113 ). The unresolved emission is spatially coincident with the optical nucleus of the host galaxy. Without a detection at 3 GHz, the lower limit to the spectral index is _3^ 10 >-1.42 . More sensitive observations are needed to determine if its radio spectrum is steep or flat and to determine if the broadband radio spectrum is better fit by a simple or curved power law.

gmw_00003976.name: J1135+2953
gmw_00003976.overview: J1135+2953 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a star-forming emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The galaxy exhibits a resolved morphology at 10 GHz with inversion symmetry, likely indicating the presence of a bipolar jet [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1135+2953 as a radio AGN with resolved morphology, excess radio luminosity, and a likely bipolar jet.

quotes.quote: J1135+2953 is a radio AGN hosted by a SF emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by LoTSS, VLASS, and at 10 GHz, and unresolved by FIRST (Figure fig:1135 ). The extension to the northwest in the LoTSS map is similar in PA to the tidal feature visible in the host galaxy, though the two features do not perfectly align. The emission becomes compact at 1.4 GHz, but clearly has a resolved morphology at 10 GHz. The morphology at 10 GHz is inversion symmetric, and the 1.4 GHz emission shows excess radio luminosity over SF predictors, likely indicating that this is a radio AGN with a bipolar jet.

gmw_00003977.name: J1304+6520
gmw_00003977.overview: J1304+6520 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2309.03252]. The object is hosted by a Seyfert emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1304+6520 as a radio-loud Seyfert AGN with resolved LoTSS and 10 GHz emission but unusual morphology.

quotes.quote: J1304+6520 is a radio AGN hosted by a Seyfert AGN emission-line galaxy. The radio emission of

gmw_00003978.name: J1433+3444
gmw_00003978.overview: J1433+3444 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The galaxy exhibits a radio morphology with an FRI-like structure [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1433+3444 as a LINER-hosted radio AGN with the samples strongest AGN-like radio morphology and an FRI-like structure.

quotes.quote: J1433+3444 is a radio AGN hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy.

gmw_00003979.name: J1445+5134
gmw_00003979.overview: J1445+5134 is a radio source hosted by a star-formation and active galactic nucleus composite emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is resolved at some frequencies, showing a steep, curved spectrum and extended components that do not align with the host galaxy's tidal features [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1445+5134 as a star-formation dominated composite-host radio source with resolved radio emission and curved steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: J1445+5134 is a SF-dominated radio source hosted by a SF-AGN composite emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by LoTSS and at 3 and 10 GHz, and unresolved by FIRST (Figure fig:1445 .) The extended components in the LoTSS map are not spatially coincident with any of the tidal features visible in the host galaxy's morphology. Even though the GHz-frequency radio emission is SF-dominated, the diffuse, host-galaxy-like morphology that is standard for such sources is not observed in J1445+5134. The spectral index is steep with _3^ 10 =-1.46 0.04 . The broadband radio spectrum is well fit by a curved power law, and shows evidence of curvature ( q=-0.18 0.02 ) with a peak frequency at 155 MHz.

gmw_00003980.name: J1511+0417
gmw_00003980.overview: J1511+0417 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is unresolved by FIRST and features multiple components at 3 GHz and 10 GHz [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1511+0417 as a LINER-hosted radio AGN and candidate dual AGN with two GHz components aligned with optical nuclei.

quotes.quote: J1511+0417 is a radio AGN hosted by a LINER emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is unresolved by FIRST, has multiple components at 3 GHz and 10 GHz, and is a non-detection by RACS (Figure fig:1511+0417 ). Like

gmw_00003981.name: J1511+2309
gmw_00003981.overview: J1511+2309 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. Its radio emission is resolved at 888 MHz and 10 GHz, showing a north-south extension at lower frequencies [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1511+2309 as a radio AGN with resolved low-frequency and 10 GHz emission and a curved, flattening radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: J1511+2309 is a radio AGN hosted by an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is resolved by RACS and at 10 GHz, and unresolved by FIRST and at 3 GHz (Figure fig:1511+2309 ). We discuss the choice of morphological classification at 1.4 GHz in Section sec:radio_morph . At 888 MHz (RACS), the source is extended in a north-south direction, with a marginal extension in the east-west direction. The synthesized beam of RACS is too large (25 ) to study if these extended features are associated with any tidal structures visible in the host galaxy.

gmw_00003982.name: J1517+0409
gmw_00003982.overview: J1517+0409 is an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. It hosts a possible radio active galactic nucleus that is detected only at 10 GHz and is co-spatial with the optical nucleus [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1517+0409 as a possible radio AGN detected only at 10 GHz and co-spatial with the optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: J1517+0409 is a possible radio AGN hosted by an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy. The radio emission is only detected at 10 GHz, unresolved, and co-spatial with the optical nucleus of the host galaxy (Figure fig:1517 ). We found no evidence for emission at the 3 level or greater in any of the RACS, FIRST, or VLASS maps centered on the 10 GHz position of the radio source. Because it is a non-detection at 1.4 GHz, the radio-based SFR we calculated in Section sec:radio_excess is an upper limit. Given this constraint, it still falls in the radio excess region of Figure fig:radio_origins .

gmw_00003983.name: J1617+2512
gmw_00003983.overview: J1617+2512 is a star-formation-dominated radio source hosted by a star-formation-active galactic nucleus composite emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The host galaxy exhibits visible tidal features, and its radio emission is resolved at higher frequencies [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1617+2512 as a star-formation dominated composite-host radio source with resolved higher-frequency morphology and a GPS-like spectrum.

quotes.quote: J1617+2512 is a SF-dominated radio source hosted by a SF-AGN composite emission-line galaxy. The source is marginally resolved by RACS, unresolved by FIRST, and resolved by VLASS and at 10 GHz (Figure fig:1617 ). The RACS emission is generally diffuse but the synthesized beam is too large (25 ) to confidently assess if this diffuse nature is spatially coincident with the tidal features visible in the host galaxy. The source is extended to the southwest in the VLASS map, and marginally extended along a roughly northwest-southeast direction at 10 GHz. Although it is a SF-dominated radio source, we do not see the typical diffuse emission morphology indicative of this at 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00003984.name: J1655+2639
gmw_00003984.overview: J1655+2639 is a radio active galactic nucleus hosted by an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2309.03252]. The galaxy exhibits eastern and southern tidal features, with radio emission extending along these structures and displaying a jet-like morphology [arXiv:2309.03252].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1655+2639 as a clear radio AGN with emission along tidal features and jet-like 3 and 10 GHz morphology.

quotes.quote: J1655+2639 is a radio AGN hosted by an optically-quiescent emission-line galaxy. The source is marginally resolved by RACS and FIRST, and resolved at 3 and 10 GHz (Figure fig:1655 ). The 888 MHz emission is extended along the eastern and southern tidal features visible in the host galaxy. These 888 MHz features are slightly exaggerated in Figure fig:1655 , as the image was produced assuming the nominal image RMS for RACS of 250 Jy, but the local image RMS is actually 500 Jy The 1.4 GHz emission is also extended along the eastern tidal feature, although the dominant component to the radio emission is compact. Our observations at 3 and 10 GHz show the jet-like morphology of this radio AGN at a PA of 140 .

gmw_00003985.name: BZBJ2031+1219
gmw_00003985.overview: BZBJ2031+1219 is a blazar that exhibits an almost completely featureless spectrum [arXiv:1502.07121]. It has a tentative redshift of z=1.215 [arXiv:1502.07121], and a nearby companion source located 2.9 arcseconds from its core may affect polarization measurements [arXiv:2012.00008].

sources.summary: The paper describes this blazar as nearly featureless, tentatively detects Mg II at z=1.215, detects an intervening absorber, and suggests a high state dilutes thermal emission lines.

quotes.quote: BZBJ2031+1219 (PKS 2029+121) This blazar is almost completely featureless. We only report a tentative detection of the Mg II doublet at the redshift z=1.215 , consistent with that by . In addition, we detect the intervening absorber seen by at z=1.116 , which features the Mg II , 2796,2803 doublet and the [ Mg I 2852 ] line, which gives more robustness to our redshift determination. Since the main emission lines should fall in regions relatively free of sky lines, this might suggest that we have caught BZBJ2031+1218 in a high state, during which the thermal emission is overwhelmed by the non-thermal one. If this is the case, the emission lines are diluted by the synchrotron emission below the detection threshold of our spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper notes J2031+1219 has a nearby Gaia source close to the core that could affect polarization, so the nucleus was measured with a fixed aperture.

quotes.quote: J2031+1219: There is a nearby source located 2.9 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data. It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 3 arcsec aperture.

gmw_00003986.name: BZBJ2134 0153
gmw_00003986.overview: BZBJ2134 0153 is a blazar [arXiv:1502.07121, arXiv:2212.05279] at a redshift of approximately 1.28 [arXiv:1502.07121, arXiv:2212.05279] that is considered a candidate supermassive black hole binary [arXiv:2212.05279]. The object features a well-collimated radio jet that follows a swinging, potentially helical trajectory [arXiv:astro-ph/0111279].

sources.summary: The paper detects three emission lines and z=1.286 for this blazar, finds several upper limits or nondetections, and argues luminosity, WISE colors, and possible Ly-alpha favor FSRQ classification.

quotes.quote: BZBJ2134 0153 (4C 02.81) This blazar shows three emission lines, namely, [ O II ] , [ O III ] 5007 and H . We found a redshift of z=1.286 0.001 , consistent with that reported by . For the H and [ O III ] 4959 features we could set only upper limits. However, the [ O III ] doublet falls in a region highly contaminated by sky lines. It is interesting to note that the H lower limit is about eight times smaller than the H value, despite the ratio between the two oscillator strengths being only 5 .

Concerning BZBJ2134 0153, its classification is ambiguous, since it was considered an FSRQ in the 1LAC catalog Abdo10d and a BL Lac in the 2FGL one Nolan+12 . Its ray luminosity of 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and its WISE colors (see Massaro+11 ) better place this object in the FSRQ class. In addition, given the steep NIR-to-UV slope, if the redshift of this object were z 2 , a strong Ly- line would have been detected, definitely placing BZBJ2134 0153 in the FSRQ class.

sources.summary: The paper finds 2131 021 has VLBI, MERLIN, and combined-image components forming a collimated swinging trajectory that may indicate helical motion and early saturation.

quotes.quote: 2131 021 : we have determined for this source a PA=36 , although in the VLA image different structures can be considered the end of the VLBI jet. In order to determine the misalignment we have taken into account the arcsecond-scale component closest to the core, which presumably will be reached first. The VLBI image (Fig. 2131e ) shows a component in the East direction extended for about 20 mas. In the MERLIN image (Fig. 2131m ) a second component is present toward the South at about 280 mas from the core. The combined image (Fig. ) shows a jet extended for about 30 mas in the SE direction, and a series of components extending up to 350 mas from the core.

The jet appears well collimated, and seems to follow a swinging trajectory, indicating a helical motion. The jet seems collimated and the amplitude of the oscillation constant. If the new VLBA data confirm this behaviour, it would imply that the saturation takes place in the first tens of mas.

sources.summary: The paper applies the surfing effect to PKS 2131--021, treating it as a blazar SMBHB candidate and finding pulsar-timing periodicities near the expected GW frequency.

quotes.quote: An interesting recent study on the blazar PKS 2131--021 opens the possibility for an application of the surfing effect . In fact, according to the data analysis of the blazar PKS 2131--021 radio emission, it can be identified as an SMBHB candidate located at a redshift z=1.285 , with an observed orbital period of 1760.4^ +5.3 _ 5.3 d and a non-red-shifted chirp mass 5.4 10^9 M_ (see ref. ). Adopting the most recent values for cosmological constants determined by the Planck collaboration , the luminosity distance of the SMBHB candidate is D=9.2 Gpc, while the angular frequency and the amplitude of the expected GW emission are =8.262^ +0.025 _ 0.025 10^ 8 Hz and A 2.4 10^ 15 , respectively.

In both cases (see Figures fig:narrow4 and fig:wide4 ), the LS algorithm allows finding the periodicity with the highest normalized amplitude associated with an angular frequency even closer to =8.262^ +0.025 _ 0.025 10^ 8 Hz. Specifically, it is found =8.147 10^ 8 Hz in the case of the narrowband dataset, and =8.205 10^ 8 Hz in the case of the wideband dataset. Even if these values are so close to the angular frequency of the GW emission expected for the SMBHB candidate PKS 2131--021, this is not enough for claiming its detection.

gmw_00003987.name: BZBJ2243 2544
gmw_00003987.overview: BZBJ2243 2544 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of approximately z=0.780 [arXiv:1502.07121] or z=0.774 [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171]. It features a red synchrotron-dominated spectral energy distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171], high optical polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171], and four detected emission lines [arXiv:1502.07121].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 2240 260 as a BL Lac object with a red synchrotron-dominated SED and high optical polarization, but finds little NIR variability during these observations.

quotes.quote: PKS 2240 260: A BL Lac object at z=0.774 . It has a redder optical/NIR SED ( B-K=4.43 from FWW, and is fitted by WWF with a strong synchrotron component). High optical polarisation has been observed in this source ( 15.1 , Wills et al. \ (1992)), and only weak, if any, emission lines have been seen. This object was seen to exhibit optical intra-day variability by Heidt Wagner (1996).

Secondly, the analysis of PKS 2240 260 is compromised by comparison star 2. Since both the Q-S2 and S1-S2 curves show the same variability, it is clear that the origin of this variability is with this star, and not the BL Lac. However, even the Q-S1 light-curve shows little variability, with the _T values being less than half those of PKS 2243 123, indicating that the source is most likely not varying.

sources.summary: The paper reports four emission lines in this blazar, derives z=0.780, limits H, and treats its observed equivalent width as borderline for classification.

quotes.quote: BZBJ2243 2544 (PKS 2240 260) This blazar shows four emission lines, namely [ O II ] , the [ O III ] doublet and H . The redshift determined for this object is z=0.780 0.002 , consistent with . For the H feature only an upper limit could be set. Again, we note that this lower limit is more than ten times lower than the H value, despite the ratio between the two oscillator strengths is just a factor of 5 . The detected emission lines are displayed in Fig. fig4 .
A third source in our sample shows border-line (observed) EW (BZBJ2243 2544, EW = 4.4 0.5 ).

gmw_00003988.name: Bo o tes II
gmw_00003988.overview: Bootes II is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2302.03708] that is embedded within the Sagittarius stream [arXiv:2312.07279]. It has a systemic velocity of 130.4 km/s [arXiv:2302.03708] and contains at least one associated RR Lyrae variable star [arXiv:2312.07279].

sources.summary: The paper presents Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy for Bo o tes II, finds its largest member-star sample, revises velocity, metallicity, and dispersion, and assesses dark-matter content.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Boo II & June 18, 2021 & 59383.80 & 13:57:56.000 & 12:50:50.00 ... Property & Description & Aquarius II & Bo o tes II & Units _ 2000 & Right Ascension & 338.4813 0.005 a & 209.5141 0.005 b & deg _ 2000 & Declination & 9.3274 0.005 a & 12.8553 0.006 b & deg

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a chemical and kinematic analysis of two ultra-faint dwarf galaxies (UFDs), Aquarius II (Aqu II) and Bo o tes II (Boo II), using Magellan/IMACS spectroscopy. We present the largest sample of member stars for Boo II (12), and the largest sample of red-giant-branch members with metallicity measurements for Aqu II (8). In both UFDs, over 80 of targets selected based on Gaia proper motions turned out to be spectroscopic members. In order to maximize the accuracy of stellar kinematic measurements, we remove the identified binary stars and RR Lyrae variables.

Similarly for Boo II, we measure a systemic velocity of 130.4^ +1.4 _ 1.1 km s ^ 1 , more than 10 km s ^ 1 different from the literature, a metallicity almost 1 dex smaller at [ Fe/H ] = 2.71^ +0.11 _ 0.10 , and a velocity dispersion 3 times smaller at _ v_ hel = 2.9^ +1.6 _ 1.2 km s ^ 1 .

For Boo II, we estimate a dynamical half-mass of 3.1^ +4.4 _ 2.1 10^5 M_ , and a mass-to-light ratio of 460^ +1000 _ 440 M_ /L_ . Boo II is therefore consistent with a large dark matter content as well, but we cannot confirm at high confidence that it is dark matter dominated because of the large uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper finds one previously known RRL associated with Bootes II and argues it is likely a true member despite the galaxy being embedded in the Sgr stream.

quotes.quote: Finally, Bootes II is embedded in the Sgr stream (Fig. fig:styx ). We found only one RRL associated with this star, which was previously known sesar14 . The star is located well within 1 R_h from the center of Bootes II. This RRL is brighter than the bulk of RRL in the Sgr stream and the proper motion also separates well from Sgr stars (Fig. fig:Sgr ). Thus, in this case, we are confident to believe this RRL is indeed a member of the UFD.

gmw_00003989.name: ALFA ZOA J1952+1428
gmw_00003989.overview: ALFA ZOA J1952+1428 is a nearby dwarf galaxy discovered at low Galactic latitude through its neutral hydrogen emission [arXiv:1107.4343]. It has a low luminosity, blue colors, and a low neutral hydrogen mass, indicating it is not an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1107.4343].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALFA ZOA J1952+1428 as a nearby H I-discovered dwarf candidate with an optical counterpart, small H I extent, blue colors, and possible blue compact dwarf properties.

quotes.quote: The Arecibo L-Band Feed Array Zone of Avoidance (ALFA ZOA) Survey has discovered a nearby galaxy, ALFA ZOA J1952+1428, at a heliocentric velocity of +279 km s^ 1 . The galaxy was discovered at low Galactic latitude by 21-cm emission from neutral hydrogen (H i ). We have obtained follow-up observations with the EVLA and the 0.9-m SARA optical telescope. The H i distribution overlaps an uncataloged, potential optical counterpart. The H i linear size is 1.4 kpc at our adopted distance of D = 7 Mpc, but the distance estimate is uncertain as Hubble's law is unreliable at low recessional velocities.

ALFA ZOA J1952+1428 appears to be a dwarf galaxy judging from its Gaussian H i profile and low H i mass. At a distance of 7 Mpc, M_ HI = 10^ 7.0 M _ , which is significantly lower than the gaseous content of spiral-type galaxies (Roberts and Haynes 1994). Also, its low luminosity ( L_ B = 10^ 7.5 L _ at 7 Mpc), H i content, and blue colors are strong evidence that it is not an early-type galaxy.

gmw_00003990.name: J1042+1641
gmw_00003990.overview: J1042+1641 is a dust-reddened, radio-quiet quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:2211.03866, arXiv:2209.03381]. It is quadruply lensed by a foreground galaxy [arXiv:2211.03866, arXiv:2209.03381] and exhibits heavy X-ray absorption [arXiv:2209.03381], strong gas outflows [arXiv:1811.07902, arXiv:1906.00064], and an anomalously high black-hole-to-host-galaxy mass ratio [arXiv:1811.07902, arXiv:2209.03381].

sources.summary: The paper confirms WISE J104222.11 + 164115.3 as a z=2.521 extremely luminous IR galaxy, reporting likely strong lensing, a high black-hole-to-galaxy mass ratio, outflowing gas, and absorbed AGN emission.

quotes.quote: We present a near-infrared (NIR) spectrum of WISE J104222.11 + 164115.3, an extremely red dust-obscured galaxy (DOG), which has been observed with the Long-slit Intermediate Resolution Infrared Spectrograph (LIRIS) on the 4.2m William Hershel Telescope. This object was selected as a hyper-luminous DOG candidate at z 2 by combining the optical and IR photometric data based on the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE), although its redshift had not yet been confirmed. Based on the LIRIS observation, we confirmed its redshift of 2.521 and total IR luminosity of (L_ IR /L_ ) = 14.57 , which satisfies the criterion for an extremely luminous IR galaxy (ELIRG).

Very recently, it has been reported that this object is an anomalous gravitationally lensed quasar based on near-IR high-resolution imaging data obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope. Its magnification factor has also been estimated with some uncertainty (i.e., = 53--122).

We find that the M_ BH /M_ ratio (i.e., 0.0140--0.0204) is significantly higher than the local relation, following a sequence of unobscured quasars instead of obscured objects (e.g., submillimeter galaxies) at the same redshift. Moreover, the LIRIS spectrum shows strongly blueshifted oxygen lines with an outflowing velocity of 1100 km/s, and our Swift X-ray observation also supports that this source is an absorbed AGN with an intrinsic column density of N_ H ^ int = 4.9 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .
