These results imply that WISE J104222.11 + 164115.3 is in a blow-out phase at the end of the buried rapid black hole growth.

sources.summary: The paper reports W2M J1042+1641 as a quadruply lensed, dust-reddened QSO at z=2.517 with a strong flux-ratio anomaly, absorption and variability, outflows, and a massive host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of a gravitationally lensed dust-reddened QSO at z=2.517 , identified in a survey for QSOs by infrared selection. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals a quadruply lensed system in a cusp configuration, with a maximum image separation of 1.8 . We find that compared to the central image of the cusp, the neighboring brightest image is anomalous by a factor of 7-10 , which is the largest flux anomaly measured to date in a lensed QSO.

Among the sources was W2M J104222.11+164115.3 The source name is shortened to W2M J1042+1641 hereafter. , whose infrared luminosity, based on WISE photometry, L_ IR 10^ 14 L_ , was more luminous than any other known radio-quiet QSO and implied extreme properties suggestive of gravitational lensing. The source is undetected in FIRST, implying that its 20 cm flux density is below 1 mJy. There are also multi-epoch Swift /X-ray Telescope (XRT) observations of this object revealing moderately high absorption ( N_H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) and exhibiting variability Matsuoka18 .

Consistent with previous red QSO samples, this source exhibits outflows in its spectrum as well as morphological properties suggestive of it being in a merger-driven transitional phase. We find a host-galaxy stellar mass of M_ /M_ =11.4 , which is higher than the local M_ BH vs. M_ relation, but consistent with other high redshift QSOs.

sources.summary: The paper treats WISE J104222.11+164115.3 as an extreme dust-obscured galaxy in a blowout phase, with blueshifted oxygen lines and a potentially overmassive black hole.

quotes.quote: A contender for the most extreme system is currently the very dust--obscured WISE J104222.11+164115.3, at redshift z = 2.52 Matsuoka:2018 . This galaxy has strongly blueshifted oxygen lines corresponding to an outflow velocity 1100 km s^ 1 , close to the prediction in Equation vout . A luminous accretion disc is required to launch this, and from Equation mmax the claimed SMBH mass M = 10^ 11 requires it to have a spin a 0.7 and accrete prograde at the time of launch. We note that some caution is in order with this estimate, as the nature of the blowout phase may affect the reliability of the usual virial mass estimators.

This evolution fits that of the system WISE J104222.11+164115.3 Matsuoka:2018 discussed above. This appears to be in the blowout phase, which would normally require its SMBH mass M 10^ 11 to be close to M_ . But even for a gas--rich galaxy the required value 1208 km s^ 1 is extremely high, so it is likely instead that M is significantly greater than M_ , as expected if it has evolved from regions 2 or 3 into region 1.

sources.summary: J1042+1641 is identified as a possible line-profile exception; the paper finds its compact high-J CO emission hard to reconcile with disk rotation and estimates a short radiative blow-out timescale.

quotes.quote: J1042+1641 is a possible exception, although this may be explained by the signal-to-noise ratio of the data. To measure the line width, we fit the line profiles with a single Gaussian, taking into account the noise in each channel.

For the two red quasars with resolved high-J CO line emission, the sizes of 70 pc would suggest an unfeasibly large enclosed mass of 10^ 10 . This could be reconciled for J1042+1641 if the CO emission was from a Keplerian disc seen edge-on, although this is not consistent with the Gaussian shape of the line profile.

Two red quasars in our sample have measured sizes of their CO emission from lens modelling in this work (Figs. fig:0414_cont and fig:1042_line ). From Eq. eq:timescale and the estimated outflow contribution, we estimate an outflow timescale of 0.1 Myr for MG J0414+0534 and J1042+1641. This is within the expected timescale of a radiatively-driven blow-out Ishibashi:2017,Ishibashi:2018 .

sources.summary: 2MASS J10422211+1641151 is a reddened quadruply lensed z=2.517 radio-quiet quasar with heavy X-ray absorption, a lensing foreground galaxy, and an unusually high black-hole-to-host-galaxy mass ratio.

quotes.quote: 2MASS J10422211+1641151, hereafter , is a rare example of a strongly lensed quasar that is heavily reddened in the optical, implying strong obscuration towards the central engine ( Glikman21 ).

The quasar in , at z = 2.517 , is radio-quiet (undetected in the FIRST survey), and is lensed by an early-type foreground galaxy at z_ lens = 0.599 , producing a `cusp' configuration for the four quasar images with an Einstein ring of 0.9 '' ( Glikman21 ; broadly similar to \ RX J0911 + 0551, Bade97 , and RX J1131-1231, Sluse03 ). \ is technically a short-axis cusp ( Saha03 ), and so is more similar to the configuration of RX J0911 + 0551. Based on the \ and \ line widths, argue for the presence of a very massive black hole with M_ BH /M_ 9.6-10 , leading them to suggest the mass ratio of the black hole and its host galaxy is anomalously high ( M_ BH /M_ gal 0.01-0.02 , based on the galaxy mass inferred from modelling the optical--IR SED with appropriate AGN/host galaxy templates) when compared to other obscured systems at similar redshifts (which typically have M_ BH /M_ gal 0.004 ; Alexander08, Wu18 ). \ is also detected as a bright X-ray source. Based on a series of snapshot observations taken with the \ (hereafter ; SWIFT ), report that the X-ray spectrum implies fairly heavy obscuration, as expected based on its classification as a red quasar, with an absorbing column density of N_ H 5 10^ 23 .

sources.summary: The paper assigns larger uncertainties to J1042 because of close, high-contrast quasar images and notes small warm-dust flux-ratio differences in this higher-redshift lens.

quotes.quote: For the system J1042, we adopt different uncertainties. This system has a pair of images with a flux ratio ranging from about 10:1 in F560W to 4:1 in F2550W, and a separation of only 0 5, which is smaller than the PSF FWHM (0 591, 0 803) in F1800W and F2550W respectively. A potential concern is that if the PSF properties vary systematically with brightness e.g. the brighter-fatter effect, argyriou_brighter-fatter_2023 over this dynamic range, measuring the fluxes with a fixed PSF for all four images may yield a systematic bias. To check this, we performed a fit to the real F2550W data allowing the PSF to vary for each image, and found no trend between the inferred jitter sigma and the image brightness.

For some of the higher redshift lenses (e.g., J0659, and J1042), small and statistically insignificant differences in the flux ratios between the reddest filter and the inferred warm dust appear, at the 1 level.

gmw_00003991.name: [ HB89 ] 0906+015
gmw_00003991.overview: [ HB89 ] 0906+015 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of 1.018 [arXiv:1005.2407]. The source exhibits strong submillimeter and gamma-ray variability [arXiv:1205.1929], with an optical-UV bump likely dominated by thermal emission from its accretion disk [arXiv:1005.2407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies [ HB89 ] 0906+015 as a Herschel-detected blazar, a broad-line flat-spectrum quasar at redshift 1.018 with WMAP/Fermi detections and a double-humped SED.

quotes.quote: Both H-ATLAS J090910.1+012135 ( [ HB89 ] 0906+015) and H-ATLAS J090940.3+015957 (PKS 0907+022) are known blazars. The former is a flat-spectrum quasar with strong broad emission lines at a measured redshift of 1.018 (Falomo et al. 1994).

The blazar [ HB89 ] 0906+015 was detected by the WMAP satellite (source 215 in the NEWPS5yr catalog; Massardi et al. 2009) and also by the Fermi/Large Area Telescope (LAT) (Abdo et al. 2010; note that in the previous bright gamma-Ray source list the ray source was identified with PKS 0907+022). The shape of its SED exhibits the typical double hump distribution, attributable to synchrotron and inverse Compton, respectively.

Since [ HB89 ] 0906+015 has strong broad emission lines, its optical-UV flux is most likely dominated by the thermal emission produced by the accretion disk, with only a minor contribution from the beamed non-thermal continuum (Ghisellini et al. 2010b). There are indeed clear indications of an optical-UV bump.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J090910.1+012135 as a blazar whose Herschel flux changes and Fermi variability confirm non-thermal, jet-related submillimeter emission.

quotes.quote: The blazar J090910.1+012135 happened to be observed twice by Herschel , on 2009 Nov 22 and 2010 May 30, and showed a strong variability, thus providing a direct confirmation of the non-thermal, jet-related nature of its sub-mm emission. Its 250, 350 and 500 m flux densities increased from 160, 194 and 266 mJy, respectively, at the first epoch, to 258, 338 and 425 mJy at the second epoch. This source was also found to be variable also in rays: its Fermi LAT variability index is 159.5.

gmw_00003992.name: PKS 0907+022
gmw_00003992.overview: PKS 0907+022 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1005.2407]. It has an estimated photometric redshift of 1.575 and is modeled as a low-synchrotron-peaked source [arXiv:1005.2407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0907+022 as a Herschel-detected BL Lac blazar, estimates its photometric redshift, models its synchrotron peak, and classifies it as likely low-synchrotron-peaked.

quotes.quote: Both H-ATLAS J090910.1+012135 ( [ HB89 ] 0906+015) and H-ATLAS J090940.3+015957 (PKS 0907+022) are known blazars. The former is a flat-spectrum quasar with strong broad emission lines at a measured redshift of 1.018 (Falomo et al. 1994). The latter is a BL Lac with an estimated photometric redshift of 1.575 (Richard et al. 2004); a Keck spectrum obtained by E. Barton and J. Cooke does not show any clearly identifiable line.

For PKS 0907+022, since no X-ray nor ray measurements are available, we could only use the data fitting method for the synchrotron peak. In both cases, the available data from different instruments are non-simultaneous. As for all blazars, they may therefore by affected by variability, which causes additional uncertainty in the derived spectral parameters. This adds to the uncertainties in estimates of spectral parameters. Based on the best-fit model values of _p^s , both blazars can be classified as LSPs; however, an ISP classification cannot be ruled out. The Herschel detection of two blazars in the SDP area is interesting in a number of ways. First of all, it confirms that the H-ATLAS survey can play an important role in determining properties of blazars in a crucially important spectral region. With an area of 550 deg ^2 , the full H-ATLAS should yield a sample of 80 blazars, the first statistically significant sample selected at sub-mm wavelengths. The large area is of crucial importance in this context, because the blazar counts are expected to be relatively flat. The H-ATLAS survey will extend by about one order of magnitude downwards in flux the blazar number counts from the Planck survey, which is predicted to have a (95 reliability) detection limit of 430 mJy at 545 GHz (Leach et al. 2008). The number of detected blazars is consistent with the predictions of the De Zotti et al. (2005) model which yields 0.15 blazars \ deg ^ 2 brighter than 50 mJy at 500 m, i.e., 2.4 objects in the 16 deg ^ 2 SDP area. An observational estimate of the blazar surface density at 5 GHz was obtained by Padovani et al. (2007; see their Figs. 4 and 6). Taking into account the steepening at frequencies approaching the synchrotron peak, the effective 5 GHz to 500 m spectral index yielded by the model is 0.2 . The 500 m flux limit of 50 mJy then corresponds to 130 mJy at 5 GHz; our results are then in very good agreement with the corresponding blazar surface density found by Padovani et al. (2007) and the relatively flat spectral index assumed by the de Zotti et al. model. If this result is confirmed by data of the full H-ATLAS blazar sample, future models will need to explain in addition evidence of a substantial spectral curvature downwards from cm to mm wavelengths (Waldram et al. 2007; Sadler et al. 2008). The blazar catalog of Massaro et al. (2009) lists three more blazars in the SDP field: PKS0858-004 with S_ 1.4 GHz =325 mJy, 1RXS J085920.6+0047 with S_ 1.4 GHz =39 mJy, and 1RXS J085749.8+0135 with S_ 1.4 GHz =89 mJy. None of them has a plausible counterpart among the H-ATLAS sources. In the case of PKS0858-004, this implies that the effective 1.4 GHz to 500 m spectral index has to be steeper than 0.3, i.e., steeper than the effective indices of the two detected blazars (0.13 and 0.25), but still within the range observed for these sources. Based on the best-fit model estimate of the synchrotron peak frequency, _p^s , both [ HB89 ] 0906+015 and PKS 0907+022 fall in the low frequency synchrotron peaked (LSP) category.

gmw_00003993.name: Kiso 5639
gmw_00003993.overview: Kiso 5639 is a tadpole dwarf galaxy featuring a slowly rotating disk and a massive, star-forming head [arXiv:1605.02822]. This head star-forming region is accompanied by an older tail, wispy filaments of young stars, and H-alpha emission shells [arXiv:1605.02822].

sources.summary: The paper studies Kiso 5639 as a tadpole dwarf galaxy whose metal-poor accretion likely triggered a massive star-forming head, older tail regions, filaments, shells, and elevated star formation.

quotes.quote: The tadpole galaxy Kiso 5639 has a slowly rotating disk with a drop in metallicity at its star-forming head, suggesting that star formation was triggered by the accretion of metal-poor gas. We present multi-wavelength HST WFC3 images of UV through I band plus H to search for peripheral emission and determine the properties of various regions. The head has a mass in young stars of 10^6 M_ and an ionization rate of 6.4 10^ 51 s ^ 1 , equivalent to 2100 O9-type stars. There are four older star-forming regions in the tail, and an underlying disk with a photometric age of 1 Gyr.

Wispy filaments of H emission and young stars extend away from the galaxy. Shells and holes in the head H ii region could be from winds and supernovae. Gravity from the disk should limit the expansion of the H ii region, although hot gas might escape through the holes. The star formation surface density determined from H in the head is compared to that expected from likely pre-existing and accreted gas. Unless the surface density of the accreted gas is a factor of 3 or more larger than what was in the galaxy before, the star formation rate has to exceed the usual Kennicutt-Schmidt rate by a factor of 5 .

gmw_00003994.name: SDSSJ1048+1228
gmw_00003994.overview: SDSSJ1048+1228 is a LINER galaxy that exhibited a mid-infrared outburst peaking in May 2020 [arXiv:2307.16667]. This transient event is consistent with a dust echo from a supermassive black hole accretion, and its spatial position matches the neutrino event IceCube-200109A [arXiv:2307.16667].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ1048+1228 as a MIRONG outburst coincident with IceCube-200109A, with high infrared luminosity suggesting a dust echo from transient SMBH accretion.

quotes.quote: They are SDSSJ104832.79+122857.2 (hereafter SDSSJ1048+1228) and SDSSJ164938.77+262515.3 (SDSSJ1649+2625), matching with IceCube-200109A and IceCube-200530A, respectively (see their information in Table twoobj ). Specifically, the MIR outburst in SDSSJ1048+1228 peaks on 2020 May 8 (MJD=58977) in WISE W2 band, which is four months ahead of IceCube-200109A while IceCube-200530A arrives about 2 months earlier than the IR peak time of SDSSJ1649+2625 on 2020 August 7 (MJD=59068). Spatially, the two neutrinos are 159 and 143 arcmins away from the two galaxies, yet they are consistent with each other within the 90 location uncertainties.

Following the measurements performed on the entire MIRONG sample Jiang2021apjs , we have fitted the MIR emission of SDSSJ1048+1228 and SDSSJ1649+2625 with blackbody model and found a peak luminosity of 10^ 42.94 and 10^ 43.29 , respectively. Such high IR luminosities almost exclude the supernova scenario and suggest that they are instead due to dust echoes of transient accretions onto SMBHs Jiang2019,Jiang2021apjs . Additionally, the integrated energies radiated in IR of the two sources are 10^ 50.72 and 10^ 51.02 erg as of the end of 2022, which are already comparable to the most energetic supernova (e.g., SN2016aps, Nicholl2020 ), but are rather typical for transient accretions such as TDEs vV2021c . Furthermore, both galaxies exhibit little or weak AGN activities according to their pre-outburst SDSS spectra (Figure spec ), and have been classified as a LINER and a composite galaxy in the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich (BPT) diagram Baldwin1981 , respectively.

gmw_00003995.name: SDSSJ1649+2625
gmw_00003995.overview: SDSSJ1649+2625 is a composite galaxy that historically exhibited little or weak active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2307.16667]. It recently underwent a mid-infrared outburst, likely representing a dust echo from transient accretion onto its supermassive black hole [arXiv:2307.16667].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ1649+2625 as a MIRONG outburst coincident with IceCube-200530A, with high infrared luminosity suggesting a dust echo from transient SMBH accretion.

quotes.quote: They are SDSSJ104832.79+122857.2 (hereafter SDSSJ1048+1228) and SDSSJ164938.77+262515.3 (SDSSJ1649+2625), matching with IceCube-200109A and IceCube-200530A, respectively (see their information in Table twoobj ). Specifically, the MIR outburst in SDSSJ1048+1228 peaks on 2020 May 8 (MJD=58977) in WISE W2 band, which is four months ahead of IceCube-200109A while IceCube-200530A arrives about 2 months earlier than the IR peak time of SDSSJ1649+2625 on 2020 August 7 (MJD=59068). Spatially, the two neutrinos are 159 and 143 arcmins away from the two galaxies, yet they are consistent with each other within the 90 location uncertainties.

Following the measurements performed on the entire MIRONG sample Jiang2021apjs , we have fitted the MIR emission of SDSSJ1048+1228 and SDSSJ1649+2625 with blackbody model and found a peak luminosity of 10^ 42.94 and 10^ 43.29 , respectively. Such high IR luminosities almost exclude the supernova scenario and suggest that they are instead due to dust echoes of transient accretions onto SMBHs Jiang2019,Jiang2021apjs . Additionally, the integrated energies radiated in IR of the two sources are 10^ 50.72 and 10^ 51.02 erg as of the end of 2022, which are already comparable to the most energetic supernova (e.g., SN2016aps, Nicholl2020 ), but are rather typical for transient accretions such as TDEs vV2021c . Furthermore, both galaxies exhibit little or weak AGN activities according to their pre-outburst SDSS spectra (Figure spec ), and have been classified as a LINER and a composite galaxy in the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich (BPT) diagram Baldwin1981 , respectively.

gmw_00003996.name: Beaver radio galaxy
gmw_00003996.overview: The Beaver radio galaxy is a tailed radio galaxy associated with the host galaxy 2MASX J17131603+6347378 [arXiv:0909.5198]. Its long radio tail extends to almost 1 Mpc at a wavelength of 85 cm, showing a steepening spectral index that indicates severe energy losses [arXiv:0909.5198].

sources.summary: The Beaver is analyzed as a nearly 1 Mpc tailed radio galaxy whose steepening spectrum indicates radiative aging and possible particle supply to Abell 2255’s halo.

quotes.quote: During the analysis of our multi-frequency observations, we studied the physical properties of the Beaver radio galaxy because its long tail gave us the possibility to test ageing models of the radiating electrons. Spectral index analysis One of the most interesting properties of the Beaver is that it doubles the length of its tail to almost 1 Mpc between 25 cm and 85 cm (see Fig. comparisonbeaver ). This suggests very steep values for the ending part of the tail, which is an indication of severe energy losses suffered by the relativistic particles. In Figs. beaverspix2585 and beaverspix852 , we show the spectral index maps of the Beaver, between 25 cm and 85 cm, and between 85 cm and 2 m. The spectral index distribution clearly confirms that we are dealing with a tailed radio galaxy, since severely steepens going from the head of the source towards the end of the tail.

The Beaver radio galaxy (1712+638, J2000), whose tail extends to almost 1 Mpc at 85 cm wavelength, provides a clear example of such radio sources. The long tail of the Beaver may suggest that the host galaxy (2MASX J17131603+6347378) has traveled from the central regions of the cluster to its actual location without any substantial deviation from its original direction, at least on the plane of the sky. Projection effects could make the tail appear shorter, but knowing the real extent of the tail as well as the 3-dimensional location of the Beaver with respect to the other cluster structures is challenging.

gmw_00003997.name: FCC 277
gmw_00003997.overview: FCC 277 is a boxy lenticular or early-type galaxy located in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1303.1210, arXiv:1906.08187]. It features a nucleated core with a central velocity dispersion drop [arXiv:1303.1210, arXiv:1906.08187] and hosts an old, metal-rich nuclear star cluster with a minor young stellar population [arXiv:2104.06412].

sources.summary: FCC 277 is studied as a nucleated Fornax elliptical with a non-rotating NSC, nuclear disk signatures, varied stellar populations, and merger or gas-dissipation influence.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present our study of the NSC in FCC 277, a nucleated elliptical galaxy in the Fornax cluster. We use a combination of adaptive optics assisted near-infrared integral field spectroscopy, Hubble Space Telescope imaging, and literature long slit data. We show that while the NSC does not appear to rotate within our detection limit of 6 , rotation is detected at larger radii, where the isophotes appear to be disky, suggesting the presence of a nuclear disk. We also observe a distinct central velocity dispersion drop that is indicative of a dynamically cold rotating sub-system.

Following the results of orbit-based dynamical modelling, co-rotating as well as counter-rotating stellar orbits are simultaneously needed to reproduce the observed kinematics. We find evidence for varying stellar populations, with the NSC and nuclear disk hosting younger and more metal rich stars than the main body of the galaxy. We argue that gas dissipation and some level of merging have likely played an important role in the formation of the nucleus of this intermediate-mass galaxy.

sources.summary: FCC 277 is described as a boxy lenticular intermediate infaller with a bulge-disc kinematic structure, nuclear dispersion dip, bluer nuclear colours, and discy line-strength features.

quotes.quote: FCC 277 (NGC 1428) -- Fig. fig:FCC277map FCC 277 is a boxy lenticular galaxy located on the eastern side of the cluster at a distance of R_ proj =0.8^ from FCC 213 (NGC 1399) in the transition from high to low-density regions. FCC 277 is 2 mag fainter than FCC 276, which is the other ETG in this area, and their distance differs by 1 Mpc Blakeslee2009 . Differently from FCC 276, FCC 277 is an intermediate infaller. The stellar kinematics is consistent with a bulge of 90 km s ^ 1 for R 10 arcsec and a disc of v_ max 100 km s ^ 1 and 50 km s ^ 1 at R 30 arcsec.

A dip in the velocity dispersion is observed in the nucleus ( 60 km s ^ 1 for R 2 arcsec). This feature has bluer colours than its surroundings Iodice2018 . The line-strength maps show that H , Mg b , and Fe5015 are larger ( 4 ) in a discy region of 10 arcsec along the major axis, whereas the values of all the indices are smaller outside.

sources.summary: NGC 1428 has boxy isophotes, significant rotation, E-S0 classification, and a central drop likely caused by the wide GMOS slit.

quotes.quote: NGC 1428 = FCC 277 The isophotes of this galaxy are boxy and the flattening is 0.5 . A foreground star, located 1 arcsec in the NE direction from the centre, was removed before performing the photometry. The galaxy rotates with V_ max = 90 , and V_ max / > 1. We classify it as E-S0 and assign it to the E group, though merluzzi1998 classified is as S0. This galaxy was not observed with FORS2, and the drop seen in the central 3 arcsec may be an artifact due to the wide slit used for this GMOS observing run (such an effect is observed in the other galaxies for which a comparison with narrow-slit observations is available).

sources.summary: NGC 1428 has central kinematics obscured by a superimposed star; observable regions show increasing rotation, wave-like RC/VDP features, and weak profile asymmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 1428 The presence of a star superimposed on the nucleus of this galaxy prevents one from obtaining kinematical data in the central 4 . In the observable region, the rotation velocity steadily increases, reaching 70 km s ^ 1 at 2.7 a_e on both sides. Small amplitude waves are present around 15 '' from the center in both the RC and the VDP. A general, though weak, asymmetry is observed in both profiles: on the E side the velocity dispersion is lower and the rotation velocity is higher than on the W side. The increase of the VDP beyond 20 '' in the E side is, on the other hand, not real, as can be inferred from the large uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper places FCC 277 among intermediate-mass galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs, interpreting their stellar populations as evidence for repeated central star formation or young-cluster accretion.

quotes.quote: Galaxies with old, metal-rich NSCs FCC 190, FCC 310, FCC 277, and FCC 249 have similar stellar masses ( 3 - 5 10^9 M_ ). Their NSCs are more metal-rich than the hosts and any GCs. The NSCs and galaxies are characterised by old stellar populations ( > 10 Gyr) and FCC 190 and FCC 277 show a minor young, metal-rich population in its NSC. The high metallicities of the NSCs as well as the extended SFHs in FCC 190, FCC 310, and FCC 277 are in agreement with formation from multiple episodes of in-situ star formation or the repeated accretion of young star clusters.

sources.summary: The paper reuses detailed FCC 277 nuclear kinematics, reporting rotation outside the NSC effective radius, a central dispersion drop, measured NSC ellipticity, and prior counter-rotating orbit modeling.

quotes.quote: The central kinematics of FCC 277 was studies in detail in az13 . The maximum rotation of 25 is reached outside of the NSC's effective radius and the velocity dispersion map has a distinct drop in the centre.

Other four nuclei display a velocity dispersion drop, including FCC 277, for which we presented an orbit-based dynamical model in our pilot study and found that a significant rotation on counter rotating orbits is present.

gmw_00003998.name: CGCG 436 030
gmw_00003998.overview: CGCG 436 030 is a luminous infrared galaxy that appears to be interacting with a fainter companion galaxy to its east [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:1811.09281]. The galaxy features a star-forming clump to the northwest of its nucleus and displays rotational gas kinematics [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: CGCG 436 030 has three soft X-ray peaks, star-forming regions along spiral arms, and an optical bridge to a faint interacting companion not seen in Chandra or MIPS.

quotes.quote: [ 47 ] CGCG 436 030 : This galaxy shows three bright X-ray peaks in the soft band, with only the central one corresponding to a hard-band peak. The other two, placed following the spiral arm structure in the optical images, most likely correspond to star-forming regions. In the DSS image there is a hint of a bridge of material between the galaxy and a fainter galaxy 1 ' to the east, with which it seem to be interacting MirSan1988,ZinLes2000 . This other galaxy is not detected in the Chandra X-ray data nor the MIPS data, and is only visible in near-infrared observations such as the largest-wavelength IRAC channels. Therefore, we have not considered any contribution to the IRAS flux originating from this nearby companion.

sources.summary: CGCG 436 030 is described as a LIRG with a northwest star-forming clump; its gas is weak, not apparently shock heated, and follows rotational kinematics.

quotes.quote: CGCG 436 030 CGCG 436 030 is a LIRG with a star-forming clump to the northwest of the nucleus, as demonstrated in its flux map well modeled in Paper I. In this galaxy, the gas is relatively weak and does not appear to be shocked heated by the nearby star-forming clump. Both and display rotational kinematic signatures that follow each other fairly consistently.

gmw_00003999.name: MCG+07 23 019
gmw_00003999.overview: MCG+07 23 019 is a ring galaxy [arXiv:1810.02371] featuring an elongated main body with double optical components [arXiv:1810.02371] and an obscured nucleus [arXiv:0807.1477, arXiv:1810.02371]. It is classified as a radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.4177] with extended soft X-ray emission that likely traces star formation [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp148 as having uniformly distributed ring emission, a likely companion-related eastern knot, and strong dust associated with the companion's emitting region.

quotes.quote: Arp148: \ emission is uniformly distributed in the ring, with the only knot coinciding with the brightest knot on the K band image. We identify this knot as the nucleus. The \ emitting knot eastward of the ring is most likely associated with the companion. The companion is elongated along the east-west direction, splitting into two objects separated by 7 \ in the B band. The K band and \ fluxes are maximum between the two B band knots.

sources.summary: MCG+07 23 019 is a ring galaxy with double optical components, an obscured nucleus, central Chandra emission, and extended soft X-rays around the nucleus following the oval ring.

quotes.quote: [ 51 ] MCG+07 23 019 : This ring galaxy is composed of an elongated main body with double components separated by 5 " ( 4 kpc) and an oval ring with a diameter of 16" to the west of the main body HatYos2004 . As already suggested by JHKL-band mapping, the nucleus of the galaxy lies between the two optical components and is heavily obscured in optical images JoyHar1987 . Chandra data show clear emission coming from the elongated disk of the galaxy. The X-ray emission is more intense in the centre, and unobscured both in hard and soft bands. There is extended soft X-ray emission around the nucleus, which partly follows the oval ring, most likely tracing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG +07-23-019 as an extended radio source with an FRI-like jet structure and classifies it as a radio AGN from central flattening and outward steepening beyond 1.1.

quotes.quote: MCG +07-23-019 This is an extended radio source at 1.49 GHz, and at 8.44 it presents a jet-like structure similar to FRI; fr74 , whose centre is not associated with the centre of IR emission at 8 m (map not shown here). The values are flat in the centre of radio emission at 1.49 GHz, steepening outwards and exceeding the value of 1.1, which is also seen in the histogram. Based on our classification this is a radio-AGN.

gmw_00004000.name: NGC 6670
gmw_00004000.overview: NGC 6670 is a luminous infrared galaxy merger composed of two closely interacting galaxies, designated as the eastern and western components [arXiv:1211.1674, arXiv:1810.02371]. Both components contribute to the system's infrared flux and are individually resolved in X-rays [arXiv:1211.1674, arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 18329+5950 as a class IIIa luminous infrared merger system with two X-ray-resolved nuclei, wide projected separation, and infrared luminosities consistent with a LIRG.

quotes.quote: IRAS 18329+5950 is a class IIIa system composed of two merging galaxies with estimated nuclear separations of 28^ Arribas04 . The offset in declination ( 5.^ 70 ) of the two galaxies in this system is much smaller than the offset in right ascension ( 27.^ 5 ). In projection, it appears that the nucleus of the eastern component (18329E) is close to the plane of the disc of the western component (18329W). The system is classified as a LIRG in the IRAS Revised Bright Galaxy Sample (hereafter RBGS; Sanders03 ). Using the RBGS fluxes, we calculate luminosities of (L_ FIR / ) = 11.48 (where FIR denotes 40-400 m) and (L_ IR / ) = 11.60 based on the redshift of z=0.029, which corresponds to a distance of 128.5 Mpc Armus09 .

The total exposure time for IRAS 18329+5950 was 37.2 ks, with 9.4 ks usable after background filtering. The left panel of Figure fig: NewtonImage demonstrates that the nuclear regions of the two separate galaxies in IRAS 18329+5950 are resolved in X-rays.

sources.summary: NGC 6670 is described as an interacting merger of eastern and western components, both contributing to IRAS flux but showing different X-ray morphologies and absorption signatures.

quotes.quote: [ 52 ] NGC 6670 : This closely interacting merger is composed of two sources, NGC 6670A (or East) and NGC 6670B (or West), separated 0.5 '. Both galaxies contribute to the IRAS flux ChuSan2017 , with the western component being slightly brighter. X-ray emission from the eastern component is mostly observed in soft band, and concentrated around the nucleus. The western source, however, shows extended diffuse emission, particularly along the plane of the galaxy. The emission near the centre is more intense, both in hard and soft band, at both sides of the nucleus.

gmw_00004001.name: UGC 02369
gmw_00004001.overview: UGC 02369 is a double galaxy system separated by 0.4 arcminutes, where the southern component dominates both the X-ray and infrared emission [arXiv:1810.02371]. This southern companion contains a radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.4177] and exhibits compact X-ray emission from both its nucleus and a spiral-arm star-forming region [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: The paper says the south companion of UGC 02369 shows a radio core plus diffuse emission, lacks mid-infrared AGN evidence, but is classified as a radio AGN from very steep spectral-index values.

quotes.quote: UGC 02369 (S) Only the south companion has been observed at 8.44 GHz. From the radio structure at 8.44 GHz we see a core with diffuse radio emission around it. There is no indication for a weak jet from the radio structure. There is no evidence that this is an AGN based on mid-IR AGN diagnostics.
Yet, the map gives very steep values, above 1.1, suggesting an AGN. Thus we classify this object as radio-AGN.

sources.summary: UGC 02369 is a double system dominated by the southern component in infrared and X-rays, with compact emission from both nucleus and a spiral-arm star-forming region.

quotes.quote: [ 53 ] UGC 02369 : This double system, separated by 0.4 ', is clearly dominated in X-rays by the southern component which, as shown in Table table:IRfrac , is also responsible for 98 of the infrared emission. Due to the negligible contribution to the IRAS flux originating in the northern galaxy, we do not present any results for this component. An X-ray analysis would also not be possible, as only 5 cts are detected for this source. The southern source is compact in X-rays, with emission coming both from the nucleus and from a star forming region in the spiral arm, at south, both in soft and hard bands.

gmw_00004002.name: IRAS F06076 2139
gmw_00004002.overview: IRAS F06076 2139 is a closely interacting double system of two distinct galaxies that are unlikely to merge [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:1811.09281]. The northern component is dominated by star formation [arXiv:1811.09281, arXiv:1509.08468], while the southern nucleus likely hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.02884, arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F06076--2139 as a double system detected by NuSTAR and Chandra, with a probable obscured AGN associated with the southern nucleus.

quotes.quote: IRAS F06076--2139 The source was detected by our 20 ks NuSTAR observation in both the 3-10 and 10-24 keV band and both the North and South nuclei were detected by an archival Chandra observation. IRAS F06076--2139 is a double system and the northern component is the dominant source in the far infrared and soft X-rays Torres-Alba:2018rt . It is probable that the IRAM 30m HCN/HCO ^+ measurement of Privon2015 is dominated by this northern component, as ALMA observations of the 4--3 HCN/HCO ^+ transition find emission only in the northern galaxy (D. Jeff et al. 2020, in preparation ). We combined the Chandra spectrum of the South nucleus, which was the one suspected to host an AGN by , with the NuSTAR spectra.

While our NuSTAR observations do not resolve the pair, it is probable that the obscured AGN is associated with the southern nucleus. Thus the AGN, contributing a small fraction of the total luminosity, may be unassociated with the region showing enhanced HCN emission.

sources.summary: IRAS F06076 2139 is a closely interacting merger dominated by its northern component, while the southern source meets a hardness-ratio AGN criterion despite low counts.

quotes.quote: [ 57 ] IRAS F06076 2139 : This closely interacting merger is clearly dominated by the northern source both in infrared and X-rays. However, with only 10 cts, the southern source meets our HR criterion for AGN selection ( HR=-0.03 0.17 ). The spectrum also shows an increase in flux toward higher energies, despite the significant error bars. Only the hardness ratio is computed as part of the analysis of this source, due to the low number of counts. For the same reason, a radial profile is not provided for this component.

sources.summary: The northern IRAS F06076 nucleus lies in a two-galaxy system unlikely to merge; OSIRIS resolves two continuum peaks and extended, star-formation-dominated gas.

quotes.quote: IRAS F06076 2139N IRAS F06076 is a system of two clearly distinct galaxies with projected nuclear separation of 6.7 kpc from according to HST ACS imaging. Large-scale, optical IFS data from VLT/VIMOS show that at low-intermediate masses and with projected velocities of 550 km s ^ 1 , the two galaxies are unlikely to ever merge . The northern nucleus, which is the one our OSIRIS FOV covers, is dominated by star formation as indicated by optical line ratio and velocity dispersion . Our 35mas data resolves the K band continuum emission into two peaks, and illustrates that the gas is extended over this region. The gas is more concentrated at the continuum peaks, with velocity dispersion 110 km s ^ 1 in this inner 200 pc region.

sources.summary: The paper treats this ULIRG as an interacting two-galaxy system, with star formation dominating the northern galaxy and enhanced line ratios in the southern galaxy.

quotes.quote: IRAS F06076-2139 Arribas08 describe this ULIRG in great detail in their IFS study of ULIRGs. They determined that the system consists of two galaxies that may never merge given their significantly different velocities, though they are interacting as evidenced by a ring of emission Elmegreen06,Arribas08 . Our velocity dispersion and line ratio data imply the northern galaxy is dominated by star formation, with some amount of composite ratios and in the southern galaxy. The southern galaxy in this closely interacting pair has a fairly low 6.2 PAH eqw, which also corresponds to enhanced / in our maps.

gmw_00004003.name: IRAS F16399 0937
gmw_00004003.overview: IRAS F16399 0937 is a closely interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:1810.02371] whose northern source dominates the mid-infrared emission [arXiv:1810.02371]. This star-formation dominated system features a northern ring of star-forming gas [arXiv:1509.08468] and nuclear emission characteristic of a LINER [arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper describes this close-merger pair as mostly star-formation dominated, with a northern ring of star-forming gas and possible shock or outflow-related LINER-like nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16399-0937 This close-merger pair shows region+composite emission and a velocity dispersion distribution dominated by star formation. The emission line ratio map indicates a ring of star forming gas surrounding the northern nucleus. The northern nucleus has much stronger emission line ratios, though very few spaxels are detected in both and . The composite nuclear spectrum of the northern nucleus has / near unity, indicating LINER-like emission. This may be associated with shocks, possibly caused by an outflow.

sources.summary: IRAS F16399 0937 is a closely interacting pair whose northern source dominates MIR emission and shows hard X-ray peaks, nuclear hard counts, and possible absorption.

quotes.quote: [ 67 ] IRAS F16399 0937 : This closely interacting pair is unresolved both in Herschel and MIPS data, though HaaSur2011 derive that most of the MIR emission ( > 90 ) is coming from the northern source. We use this value to correct for the fraction contributed to the IRAS flux by each galaxy. The northern source shows two intense hard X-ray peaks, both corresponding to soft X-ray emitting regions, the more southern of which is the nucleus. The other, as well as the less-intense knots seen in both sources, probably correspond to star forming regions. The spectrum of this source shows an excess at > 4 keV, with a few hard counts coming from the nuclear region.

gmw_00004004.name: IRAS F16164 0746
gmw_00004004.overview: IRAS F16164 0746 is a late-stage coalesced galaxy merger [arXiv:1509.08468, arXiv:2207.08929] featuring a tidal tail [arXiv:2207.08929] and a single nucleus [arXiv:2207.08929]. This nucleus has been classified as a LINER [arXiv:2207.08929] or Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1810.02371], and it exhibits a soft X-ray elongation that may indicate an outflow [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: The paper describes this coalesced merger as AGN-dominated at X-ray and mid-infrared wavelengths, with optical rotation, narrow and broad components, and ambiguous nuclear classifications.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16164-0746 This coalesced merger is AGN dominated in the X-ray and mid-IR Iwasawa09,Iwasawa11,Petric11 . Our optical spectra show rotation and a narrow and a broad component, consistent with star formation and shocks, though our line ratio map shows a composite spectrum. Our data do not have sufficient S/N to detect / where the other line ratios increase towards the nucleus, where more LINER-like ratios dominate Veilleux95 . Our nuclear spectrum is somewhat ambiguous, indicating composite, SB and Seyfert-like spectra with / near unity.

sources.summary: IRAS F16164 0746 meets hardness-ratio and [Ne v] AGN criteria, is optically Seyfert 2, and shows soft X-ray elongation suggestive of an outflow.

quotes.quote: [ 68 ] IRAS F16164 0746 : This source meets two of our AGN selection criteria, the HR and the [ Ne v ] line, and is also classified as an optical Seyfert 2 in YuaKew2010 . The X-ray source is elongated in the soft band, in the direction perpendicular to the disk of the galaxy, which could be interpreted as an outflow. There is also a secondary point source 3 '' from the nucleus, both in soft and hard band, without any obvious overlap with a star-forming knot. With an associated X-ray luminosity in the 2-10 keV range of 3 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , it could be classified as a ULX.

sources.summary: The paper treats IR 16164-0746 as a late-stage merger with a LINER nucleus and tidal tail, modeling its radio SED as a single star-forming region without low-frequency turnover.

quotes.quote: IR 16164-0746 : classified as a late stage merger Stierwalt13 with a single LINER-type nucleus and a tidal-tail Dixon11, Hung15 , its radio SED covering 1.6 decades in frequency (70 MHz-3.0 GHz) is best-fitted with emission from a single star-forming region without low frequency turnover. The synchrotron spectral index is 0.5. The TF is 0.8 . The CIGALE-estimated SFR, L_ dust , AGN fraction are 33.11 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 10.77 L_ , 2.2 , respectively.

gmw_00004005.name: IC 5298
gmw_00004005.overview: IC 5298 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342] classified optically as a Seyfert 2 or LINER [arXiv:1408.4177, arXiv:astro-ph/9811342]. It hosts an absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1810.02371] with a radio structure resembling a Fanaroff-Riley Class I source [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: IC 5298 is identified as a clear absorbed AGN in Chandra and XMM-Newton data, with high column density, Fe-line evidence, [Ne v], and optical S2 classification.

quotes.quote: [ 72 ] IC 5298 : This source is a clear absorbed AGN, seen both in the Chandra spectrum presented in this work, and through XMM-Newton data analysis. Using data from both telescopes, when a photon index of 1.8 is assumed, a column density of N_ H 4 10^ 23 is obtained. There is a hint of a line at 6.4 keV in the Chandra data, with significance lower than 1 . The presence of the line can be confirmed with a significance of 2 when checking XMM-Newton EPIC data, with a fit that is also consistent with the derived absorbing column density. The AGN diagnostics is also confirmed through the presence of the [ Ne v ] line, and the optical S2 classification VeiKim1995 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 5298 as a radio AGN, describing FRI-like 8.44 GHz morphology, a 1.49 GHz tail, outward steepening beyond 1.1, and matching mid-infrared/optical AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: IC 5298 This radio source resembles an FRI radio source fr74 at 8.44 GHz, and has a tail at 1.49 GHz. The distribution of values, flat in the centre steepening outwards ( > 1.1), suggests a radio-AGN. We note that the uncertainties in , in particular the d value is 1.1, but we will keep the radio-AGN classification because there is also an indication from the radio structure. This object is also classified as an AGN based on the mid-IR diagnostics, and is a Seyfert 2 or LINER in the optical.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5298 as a luminous infrared galaxy with intermediate published properties, but its own observations indicate a region-dominated composite spectrum.

quotes.quote: IC 5298 is a LIG (Goldader et al. 1997). Wu et al. (1998a,b) found that its properties are intermediate between \ regions and Liners; it was classified as a Seyfert 2 by Veilleux et al. (1995) although the \ lines are rather weak ( \ = 0.05).

gmw_00004006.name: IRAS F03359+1523
gmw_00004006.overview: IRAS F03359+1523 is a luminous infrared galaxy pair [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:1811.09281] in an early-stage merger [arXiv:1811.09281]. The eastern companion features an intact, edge-on disk [arXiv:1810.02371, arXiv:1811.09281] and is classified as an active galactic nucleus and starburst system [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: IRAS F03359+1523 is a two-source system where only the eastern galaxy is X-ray detected and dominates the MIPS, IRAC, and radio emission.

quotes.quote: [ 80 ] IRAS F03359+1523 : Only one of the two sources present in this system is observed in X-rays, the eastern source, with an elongated morphology that corresponds to the length of the edge-on disk in the optical data. The sources are unresolved in the Herschel data, and only one source is visible in the MIPS data, which is centred at the position of the eastern one. It is not possible to affirm if this is due to lack of resolution, or if the western source has no contribution to the MIPS flux. However, observing the IRAC images from channel 1 through 4 (at 3.6, 4.5 ,5.8 and 8.0 m ), it is possible to see that the eastern source clearly dominates and the western source fades with increasing wavelength. Also, GolJos1997 mention that only one (believed to be the eastern source) is prominent at radio wavelengths.

sources.summary: IRAS F03359+1523 is described as an early-stage merger with an intact edge-on disk; higher-resolution data show gas and continuum consistency plus possible high-dispersion enhanced line ratios.

quotes.quote: IRAS F03359+1523 IRAS F03359+1523 is an early-stage merger with a large, nearly edge-on disk that has yet to be disrupted (as evidenced from the analysis of the 100mas data in Paper I). Here, we zoom into the nucleus of the galaxy with 35mas data. The distribution of the continuum and gas are consistent with the larger scale data. However, the higher-resolution data shows that enhanced / may correlate with a region of high velocity dispersion ( 200 cm s ^ 1 ) even though the gas has not yet reached the our conservative shock threshold.

sources.summary: The paper associates IRAS F03359+1523 with the east companion of a galaxy pair and classifies it as AGN/SB because its extended radio source shows a core-jet-like feature and outward steepening.

quotes.quote: IRAS F03359+1523 This radio source is associated with the east companion of a galaxy pair, while the HST bulge is associated with the west companion. It has an extended radio source at 1.49 GHz, that presents a core-jet-like feature at 8.44 GHz. The radio spectral index at the centre of the source is flat, steepening outwards with values of exceeding 1.1, but due to the uncertainties we will classify this as AGN/SB.

gmw_00004007.name: ESO 550 IG025
gmw_00004007.overview: ESO 550 IG025 is a galaxy system containing two sources separated by 0.3 arcminutes, both of which contribute to the system's infrared flux [arXiv:1810.02371]. The southern component exhibits a flat spectrum and hosts a hard X-ray peak that may represent an ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: ESO 550 IG025 has two IRAS-contributing sources; the southern component has a flat spectrum and a hard X-ray peak west of the nucleus, possibly a ULX.

quotes.quote: [ 81 ] ESO 550 IG025 : Both sources in this system, with a separation of 0.3 ', contribute to the IRAS flux. The southern source has a rather flat spectrum, partly due to the contribution of the hard X-ray peak placed at about 4 '' west of the nucleus. This source cannot be easily interpreted as the X-ray counterpart to any star-forming regions in the galaxy. If it is associated to this galaxy, its X-ray luminosity in the 2-10 keV range is of 3 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , implying it could be classified as a ULX.

gmw_00004008.name: IRAS F17138 1017
gmw_00004008.overview: IRAS F17138 1017 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1709.08307] and obscured starburst [arXiv:2104.06790] that likely represents a post-coalescence merger [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:1509.08468]. Its central regions are dominated by star formation [arXiv:1509.08468] with aligned star-forming regions [arXiv:2104.06790], and it hosted the supernova SN 2015cb [arXiv:1709.08307].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F17138-1017 as an obscured starburst and possible post-coalescence merger, with aligned star-forming regions, radial kinematic component, and dispersion structure tied to star formation.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17138-1017 : This target, classified as an obscured starburst galaxy in Depoy+88 , has been suggested to be a post-coalescence merger Yuan+10,Bellocchi+16 . This galaxy shows a complex nuclear structure dominated by the presence of aligned bright star-forming regions ( 30 ). These regions bias the photometric analysis of the NICMOS image, especially the inclination and centre determination within 0.2<<1 kpc. The kinematic analysis reveals the presence of a radial component aligned with the SF regions ( 35 ).
The dispersion maps display a central peak (i.e. 110 km s ^ 1 ) and regions with lower (i.e. 80 km s ^ 1 ) that are spatially correlated with the intense SF regions.

sources.summary: The paper reports SN 2015cb in IRAS 17138-1017 near its nucleus, follows the host with near-infrared and VLA data, and notes central LIRG background likely blended any radio point source.

quotes.quote: SN 2015cb 2015cb in IRAS 17138-1017 was discovered with GeMS/GSAOI on 2015 March 6; see Fig. fig:iras17138 . Subtraction of a GeMS/GSAOI image from 2013 March 22 revealed a residual point source 1.4 \ North and 0.6 \ East (600 pc projected distance) from the nucleus. WCS matching in theli with a catalogue of 76 sources extracted from a VISTA image yielded R.A. = 17^ h 16^ m 35.84^ s and Decl. = 10^ 20 37.48 , with 0.04 \ and 0.04 \ uncertainty in R.A. and Decl., respectively.
We observed IRAS 17138-1017 at K-band (centred at 22 GHz) with a total bandwidth of 8 GHz, and NGC 3110 at X-band (10 GHz), with 4 GHz of bandwidth, in both cases using full polarization.
In the case of SN 2015cb in IRAS 17138-1017, any point source was most likely blended with the significant background signal in the central regions of the LIRG; see Fig. fig:iras17138_vla .

sources.summary: IRAS F17138 1017 has a flat, hardening X-ray spectrum, meets the hardness-ratio AGN criterion, and shows soft X-ray deficit likely caused by absorption.

quotes.quote: [ 85 ] IRAS F17138 1017 : This source has a rather flat spectrum in X-rays, with flux slightly increasing towards higher energies. It meets the HR criterion for AGN selection, though no [ Ne v ] line is observed. Fitting with an absorbed AGN model, fixing a spectral index of 1.8, a low column density of N_ H 2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 is obtained. Morphologically, Chandra data shows a soft X-ray deficit at the optical centre of the galaxy, which could be caused by absorption. The hard X-ray image does not show a clear peak of emission, but a rather homogeneous flux around a larger circular region.

sources.summary: The paper describes this coalesced merger as centrally star-forming, with shock-like velocity dispersion and line ratios in its outskirts and no AGN indication.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17138-1017 Our observations of this coalesced merger are consistent with the IFS observations of Monreal10 . The central regions of the galaxy are dominated by star formation, with an underlying shock-like velocity dispersion component. In the outer portions of the galaxy, our line ratios are also consistent with a dominant contribution from shocks both in line ratio and velocity dispersion. There is no indication of any AGN activity at any wavelength, with a very high 6.2 PAH eqw of 0.68 Stierwalt13 .

sources.summary: The paper says the IRAS 17138 1017 maps lack enough resolution for morphology, may have off-nuclear silicate absorption, and do not securely locate the nucleus.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17138 1017 The limited spatial resolution of the emission maps does not allow us to identify any morphological details in this galaxy. The maximum of the silicate absorption may not be located at the nucleus. Note however that the near-infrared AAH06s and the mid-infrared Tanio2008 morphologies do not allow us to determine the position of the nucleus.

gmw_00004009.name: NGC 7592
gmw_00004009.overview: NGC 7592 is a luminous infrared galaxy merger [arXiv:1110.3046] consisting of a closely interacting triple system with tidal tails [arXiv:1110.3046, arXiv:1810.02371]. The system features an eastern starbursting component and a compact western component containing a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1110.3046, arXiv:0708.2645].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7592 as an early-stage LIRG merger with tidal tails, PAH emission from the eastern starburst, H2 from the western Seyfert component, and ionized-gas star formation in the overlap region.

quotes.quote: NGC 7592 The two merging galaxies in this LIRG system L _ IR /L _ =10^ 11.33 , San03 have a projected separation of 14 ( 7 kpc), a NIR luminosity ratio of 1:1, and show tidal tails in the NIR light. Fig. multi_NGC7592 highlights the details of this system at multiple wavelengths. Like NGC 4676, the PAH and H _2 emissions have a different distribution, with the PAH emission primarily originating from the eastern starbursting component and the H _2 from the western component, which has a Seyfert 2 nucleus Lai03 . We find an additional concentration in the overlap region between the two nuclei (with an offset of 12 towards south) in the NIR light, FUV light, HI emission and in several mid-IR features (main PAH features, [ NeII ] and [ NeIII ] ).

No significant H _2 emission is found at this position. The PAH interband strength and [ NeIII ] / [ NeII ] ratios indicate that most of the emission from this intermediate region originates from ionized gas (see Fig. PAH_scatter ), which is likely due to star-formation as we find a strong emission peak in the FUV and H Lai03 at this position as well.

sources.summary: NGC 7592 is a close triple system with unresolved [Ne v] emission, a likely western Seyfert 2 origin, and a western nuclear 6-7 keV excess.

quotes.quote: [ 105 ] NGC 7592 : This source is a closely interacting triple system, formed by two main infrared and X-ray sources (East and West) and a smaller, southern source. This third source, seen in the optical SDSS images, is undetected in X-rays, and also does not contribute to the IRAS flux. There is unresolved detection of the [ Ne v ] line for this triple source that meets our AGN selection criterion. However, as the western source is classified as an optical Seyfert 2, it is likely that it is the origin of the infrared line. The spectrum of the western source shows an excess between 6-7 keV, originating in the nucleus, that can be fitted as a Gaussian line with an energy of 6.7_ 0.3 ^ 0.1 keV.

sources.summary: The paper images NGC 7592 with NICMOS, notes a complex circumnuclear region and possible missing eastern nucleus, and identifies a compact western Seyfert 2 nucleus.

quotes.quote: We acquired NICMOS images of NGC 7592 in one pointing, as both nuclei in principle fit into the field of view of the NIC2 aperture (see Figure f:seventyfiveninetytwovk ). However, this observation was affected by the NICMOS aperture location offset, discussed in ss:obsreduc , which placed the second circumnuclear region right on the edge of the NIC2 aperture. Although the image covers most of the circumnuclear region, this region shows a very complex morphology and there is a chance that the true eastern nucleus (NGC 7592 E) is located outside the field of view. In contrast, the western nucleus (NGC 7592 W) is clearly defined and compact, and was previously identified by raf92 to be of Seyfert 2 type.

gmw_00004010.name: UGC 02238
gmw_00004010.overview: UGC 02238 is a galaxy in a post-merger stage, characterized by a highly disturbed disk and tidal tails [arXiv:1810.02371]. Its diffuse X-ray emission features three main peaks near the nucleus, which likely originate from intense starburst regions [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: UGC 02238 shows diffuse emission, off-nuclear hard X-ray peaks, starburst-dominated X-rays, and a disturbed disk with tidal tails consistent with a post-merger stage.

quotes.quote: [ 123 ] UGC 02238 : This source presents a rather diffuse emission, showing three main X-ray peaks near the nucleus, only one of which (the westernmost) is also peaked in the 2-7 keV band. However, as shown by the contours over the IRAC channel 1 image, this region is outside of the nucleus of the galaxy. We consider this emission to most likely originate from different intense starburst regions, given how there is no clear hard-band central peak. Optical and infrared imaging data show a highly disturbed disk and tidal tails, and classify this galaxy as a post-merger stage e.g. SmiHer1996, LarSan2016 , which is consistent with the described X-ray morphology.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 2238 with literature CO data, explains the beam mismatch and extended CO distribution, and therefore regards its HCN/CO ratio as a lower limit.

quotes.quote: We used literature L'_ CO of UGC 2238 measured with the National Radio Astronomy Observatory (NRAO) 12 m telescope 1991ApJ...370..158S . The beam of the NRAO 12 m telescope ( _ PB 55 ) is much larger than the LMT beam, and the past CO imaging study reveals that the CO emission is distributed inside and outside the LMT beam R_ CO = 20 5; 2014ApJS..214....1U . Hence, we consider L'_ HCN /L'_ CO of UGC 2238 as a lower limit.

gmw_00004011.name: MCG 01 60 022
gmw_00004011.overview: MCG 01 60 022 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits diffuse soft X-ray emission surrounding its central absorbed peak [arXiv:1810.02371]. The galaxy is located near MCG 01 60 021, with which it interacts via long, thin tidal bridges [arXiv:1810.02371].

sources.summary: MCG 01 60 022 lies near interacting galaxies connected by tidal bridges and itself shows diffuse soft emission around a central absorbed AGN.

quotes.quote: [ 136 ] MCG 01 60 022 : This source is near galaxies MCG 01 60 021 and Mrk 0399, at 4.4 ' interacting with the former e.g. DopPer2002 , connected through thin and long tidal bridges. Both nearby galaxies are undetected in the Chandra data, and are detected together as another IRAS source, resolved from MCG 01 60 022 DiaCha2010 . This source presents diffuse soft band emission surrounding the central X-ray peak, which has its origin in an absorbed AGN. This sources meets the HR X-ray criterion, and spectral fitting of an absorbed power-law with a fixed photon index of 1.8 yields a an absorbing column density of N_ H 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00004012.name: NGC 4064
gmw_00004012.overview: NGC 4064 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511081] in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0803.3638] featuring a central bar, open spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:astro-ph/0511081], and perturbed dust lanes [arXiv:astro-ph/0511081]. It exhibits compact circumnuclear star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243] and gas deficiency [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:astro-ph/0511081], likely caused by a combination of gas stripping and a gravitational interaction or minor merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:astro-ph/0511081].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4064 is a Virgo truncated/compact low-rotator whose stellar-kinematics distance is much larger than its HI estimate, implying environmental disturbance and interactions affect gas velocities.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

NGC 4064 and NGC 4424 are particularly interesting because both galaxies have been regarded as possible foreground galaxies. Moreover, both galaxies, together with NGC 4694, belong to the class of Rubin's ``low rotator" galaxies, although for the case of NGC 4064 Rubin's H velocities are much smaller than our ionized gas velocity measurements (Cort es 2006). Solanes's distance estimations indicate a distance of 9.8 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4064, and 4.1 0.2 Mpc for NGC 4424 1 errors in stark contrast with our estimations of 18.0 _ 0.8 ^ 1.3 Mpc, and 15.2 1.9 Mpc respectively. The discrepancies are of a factor 1.8 for NGC 4064 and 3.7 for NGC 4424, which are statistically significant. In the case of the other ``low rotator", NGC 4694, we determine a distance of 13.4 _ 1.0 ^ 1.3 Mpc.

Clearly, NGC 4064 and NGC 4424 belong to the Virgo cluster. NGC 4694 might be affected by the dark matter bias in our models, so its distance should be considered a lower limit. All these galaxies present truncated H disks (Koopmann Kenney 2004), and their gas kinematics are peculiar (see Cort es 2006), indicating that they are heavily disturbed by the environment. They all show evidence for strong gravitational interactions or mergers, which may account for their low gas velocities.

sources.summary: NGC 4064 has compact circumnuclear HII emission, high central H surface brightness, noncircular kinematics, open spiral arms and a bar, with dust and HI deficiency pointing to minor merger or tidal effects plus stripping.

quotes.quote: These include two Virgo galaxies with the most unusual H morphologies of our sample: NGC 4424 and NGC 4064, which have compact H emission from several circumnuclear HII complexes, as shown in Figure stt . The H surface brightnesses of these two galaxies within 0.1r _ 24 0.8 kpc are among the highest in the Virgo and isolated samples (see also Figure haclasscomp ), and most of the H emission originates in HII complexes with luminosities of L _ H =5 10 ^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . H major axis spectra show low line-of-sight velocities inconsistent with circular motions in the plane suggested by the outer optical isophotes (Rubin et al. 1999).

NGC 4064 has a more regular R morphology than NGC 4424. It shows very open spiral arms and a bar in the center. The peculiar dust morphology also suggests a recent minor merger or tidal interaction (Cortes Kenney, in prep.). Strongly HI-deficient with no gas detected in their outer disks, these galaxies probably have experienced ICM-ISM stripping in addition to mergers.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4064 as an HI-deficient Virgo barred spiral with central star formation, disturbed gas and dust, and evidence for gas stripping plus a gravitational encounter.

quotes.quote: NGC 4064 NGC 4064 is a peculiar SB galaxy located 8.8 (2.5 Mpc) from M87, in the outskirts of the Virgo cluster, and is among the most distant known cluster members. The optical image shows perturbed dust lanes and a central bar ending in very open spiral arms (Fig. n4064rgb ). H imaging by Koopmann (2001) shows that the H emission is confined to the central kpc. The H surface brightness in this central region is among the highest in the Virgo Cluster (Koopmann Kenney 2004). NGC 4064 has been reported to have low H line-of-sight velocities for its luminosity with V_ max 40 (Rubin 1999), but has a HI linewidth of 163 (Helou 1984) consistent with its luminosity.

NGC 4064, case for some type of gravitational interaction plus gas stripping In the case of NGC 4064, there is evidence for some type of gravitational interaction plus gas stripping, although the detailed scenario is not as clear as in NGC 4424.

We think the observed properties of NGC 4064 indicate a combination of gas stripping, either by an ICM-ISM or ISM-ISM interaction, and a gravitational encounter, either a minor merger or tidal interaction or direct collision.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4064 as a barred spiral with asymmetric minor-axis ionized-gas motions and slower stellar rotation along the minor axis.

quotes.quote: The asymmetric velocity profiles observed along the minor axis of NGC 4064 and NGC 4189 can be explained as due to the presence of a bar.

The ionized-gas velocity profile measured along the optical minor axis of NGC 4064 and NGC 4189 is strongly asymmetric. The gas velocity rises to a maximum and it remains almost constant out to the last observed point on the receding side. On the contrary, it drops to zero at large radii on the approaching side.

Non-zero velocities are measured along the minor axis of NGC 4064, NGC 4189, and NGC 4941 for the stellar component too. The stars rotate slower than gas and have a larger velocity dispersion ( 80 ) in NGC 4064 and NGC 4189.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4064 as an HI-deficient Virgo-outskirts spiral with S0/S0-a-like morphology, low inner rotation, and likely merger-related gas depletion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4064 This galaxy has a very large high surface brightness inner disk or flattened bulge with little trace of spiral arms. NGC 4064 may lie between S0 and S0/a.
The very low inner rotation RWK99 suggests that it is a merger remnant (see NGC 4424 below), which may have been responsible for its deficiency.
The deficiency of NGC 4064 is sufficiently high that a correction of 0.25 will still keep it among the 3 cases using both DEF _1 and DEF _2 criteria.

gmw_00004013.name: NGC 4305
gmw_00004013.overview: NGC 4305 is a spiral galaxy located in the direction of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367]. It is considered a background galaxy situated at a distance of approximately 40 Mpc, and its gas deficiency is likely tidal in origin due to the presence of a major tidal companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4305 as a background HI-deficient galaxy whose blue color and major tidal companion support a tidal origin for its gas deficiency.

quotes.quote: NGC 4305 This galaxy, at 40 Mpc and with a large radial velocity, is almost certainly a background galaxy. It has a major tidal companion (Table 8) and a blue color for its luminosity, assuming it is indeed far behind Virgo, confirming the tidal origin of its gas deficiency.

Finally, two galaxies ( 4356 and NGC 4492 ) in the direction of the cluster have low but non negligible probability of being in or near the cluster, while the last two are definitely foreground ( NGC 4424 ) and background ( NGC 4305 ) galaxies.

gmw_00004014.name: NGC 4307
gmw_00004014.overview: NGC 4307 is a spiral galaxy that has a high probability of being located near the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367]. It features a highly deficient, centrally concentrated neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:0909.3615] and may have historically interacted with the neighboring galaxy NGC 4356 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4307 has a very small, centrally concentrated H I disk, strong gas deficiency, modest asymmetries, and a still-rising position-velocity diagram.

quotes.quote: NGC 4307 The gas disk of NGC 4307 has very small dimensions and is found only deep within the optical disk, in agreement with the strong gas-deficiency estimated for this galaxy. The deficiency parameter (eq. [ def ] ) measured from our VLA flux (see Table toribio_tab4 ) shows a pretty good consistency with the value of 1.41 one can infer from the observed flux provided by Ken08. We remark that this new value is also in agreement with the extremely deficient status reported in previous works (e.g. Sol02 ; Gav05 ).

The 0 th-order moment map shows that the maximum of the 21 cm emission is displaced SE with respect to the optical center, in a direction nearly perpendicular to the major axis, with an excess of emission at the approaching side. This asymmetry shows up also in the global profile, which has a peaked appearance indicative of centrally concentrated gas. The PV diagram along the major axis is still rising at the edge of the measured distribution, as if the gas was rotating almost as a pure solid body.

sources.summary: The paper determines that low surface brightness streaks near NGC 4307 are reduction artifacts from asteroid masking and notes a background spiral in the field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4307 The two parallel low surface brightness streaks east of NGC 4307 are image reduction artifacts. On the night of April 28 2019, asteroid 326 Tamara passed through the field coincidentally as we were imaging it. The asteroid was identified as an unwanted bright source and masked automatically in the frames contributing to the final stacked images. The mask was evidently not wide enough, leaving residuals on either side of the asteroid's track. We also note NGC 4307A, a background face-on spiral galaxy immediately south of NGC 4507.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4307 has a significant chance of lying near Virgo, but may instead be interacting with NGC 4356 in the background group.

quotes.quote: The closest one, NGC 4307 , has one chance in two of being located near the cluster and 40 probability of being within the cluster itself.

NGC 4307 The origin of the deficiency in this galaxy is difficult to ascertain, as it has 41 (48 ) probability of lying within the sphere centered on the Virgo cluster with radius its 1 (2.5) times the virial radius. Its proximity with NGC 4356 suggests that it may have interacted in the past with that galaxy, and if it is indeed in the background of Virgo, as suggested by its Tully-Fisher distance, it would be blue for its luminosity, confirming the interaction hypothesis with NGC 4356 .

gmw_00004015.name: NGC 4356
gmw_00004015.overview: NGC 4356 is a nearly edge-on galaxy with a very small neutral hydrogen distribution compared to its optical dimensions [arXiv:0909.3615]. It is an HI-deficient galaxy that may have interacted in the past with the nearby galaxy NGC 4307 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4356 resembles NGC 4307, with a very small H I distribution, steeply rising PV diagram, and low-S/N flux differences.

quotes.quote: NGC 4356 and VCC 740 The properties of the neutral hydrogen in NGC 4356 resemble those of NGC 4307. This is a nearly edge-on galaxy that like the former one, and even more strongly so, has a very small gas distribution compared with its optical dimensions. The PV diagram along the major axis also rises steeply and shows no signs of turning over, as in the H rotation curve measured by Gav99.

There is similarly a misalignment between the distribution and motion of the atomic gas and the optical disk. As in the former case, this offset is less than half the synthesized beam size and, hence, relatively small and not necessarily indicative of ram-pressure pushing. For this galaxy, our VLA linewidth and flux values are somewhat smaller that the most recent estimates by Ken08.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4356 as an HI-deficient galaxy with uncertain Virgo proximity and possible past interaction with nearby NGC 4307.

quotes.quote: However, the furthest one, NGC 4356 , has one chance in 5 of belonging to the Virgo cluster and less than one chance in four of being within 2.5 virial radii.

NGC 4356 The origin of the deficiency in this galaxy is difficult to ascertain, as it has 19 (23 ) probability of lying within the sphere centered on the Virgo cluster with radius its 1 (2.5) times the virial radius. Its proximity with NGC 4307 suggests that it may have interacted in the past with that galaxy, as confirmed by its blue color for its luminosity, assuming it lies indeed in the background of Virgo.

gmw_00004016.name: NGC 4492
gmw_00004016.overview: NGC 4492 is a spiral galaxy likely located in the background of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367]. It is severely deficient in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:0909.3615], which is distributed asymmetrically and displaced from the optical center [arXiv:0909.3615].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 4492's weak H I, finding severe gas deficiency, asymmetric and displaced neutral hydrogen, and no reliable rotation-curve fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4492 In spite of the strong image-degrading effects of the Sun for the second half of the observations of NGC 4492, we succeed in detecting its signal at a quality level comparable with the previous single-dish observations carried out by HG86 and Hof89 --and not too different from the one achieved by the ALFALFA measurements, which have a S/N of 4.6. In this galaxy, the detected neutral hydrogen is located within the optical radius and shows an important elongation in the SE-NW direction, almost perpendicularly to the galaxy major axis. The distribution is strongly asymmetric, with the peak of the emission shifted some 30 to the East from the optical center of the galaxy.

With this caveat in mind, our VLA measurements assign a new deficiency parameter of 1.21 to NGC 4492, which indicates that the gas content for this galaxy could be less than 6 of the expectation value. Thus, the velocity map of NGC 4492 reveals a possible displacement of the dynamical center from the light center (consistent with that observed in the 0 th-order map), while on the PV diagram along the major axis most of the emission is found coming from the receding side. The low S/N of the datacubes, the small size of the disk with respect to the beam size, as well as a not too favorable orientation of this galaxy, prevent any attempt to fit a rotation curve model to the velocity data.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4492 is probably a background Virgo-outskirts galaxy with low cluster-proximity probability and possible tidal encounters in a background group.

quotes.quote: The third one, NGC 4492 , has only one chance in 9 of being within 2.5 virial radii of the Virgo cluster. This low probability is not surprising, given that it is located close to the infall/expansion zone of Figure proj .

NGC 4492 This galaxy is probably in the background of the Virgo cluster, in which case it would be blue for its luminosity and morphological type. It may lie in a group that could include NGC 4307 and NGC 4356 and thus be suffering tidal encounters with these and/or other members of the group.

gmw_00004017.name: NGC 4580
gmw_00004017.overview: NGC 4580 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a truncated star-forming disk with a symmetric star-forming ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:0803.3638]. It may be undergoing gas stripping outside the Virgo Cluster core [arXiv:0803.3638, arXiv:astro-ph/0401367] and possibly interacting with NGC 4586 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: NGC 4580 is grouped with severely truncated Virgo disks, but it retains a mostly regular stellar disk with spiral arms and a symmetric star-forming ring near the truncation radius.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 4580, NGC 4405, and IC 3392, have severely truncated H disks with enhanced star formation at the H truncation radius, but appear fairly normal inside the truncation radius. These galaxies were assigned `mixed' Hubble-type classifications of Sc/Sa and Sc/S0 by Binggeli, Sandage, Tammann (1985; hereafter BST) because of their peculiar morphology. The R images (Figure stmix ) show that the stellar disks beyond the edge of the star-forming disk are mostly regular and featureless, except for NGC 4580, which has spiral arms. NGC 4580 and IC 3392 have symmetric rings of star formation near the truncation radius, containing a large fraction of the H emission. NGC 4405 has a knottier and asymmetric HII region distribution, with an arc or partial ring to the southwest.

H major axis spectra of all three galaxies show regular, rising rotation curves for the ionized gas (Rubin, Waterman, Kenney 1999). The truncated H distribution, combined with the regular stellar isophotes and regular gas kinematics, strongly suggest that these galaxies are victims of ICM-ISM stripping.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4580’s stellar-kinematics and HI distances agree, but its truncated/normal star formation suggests ICM-ISM stripping outside the Virgo core, perhaps in the M49 environment.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.
From inspection of this figure, we conclude that NGC 4293, NGC 4450, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4651 have SKB distances that agree within the errors with those of Solanes.

NGC 4580 has a ``Truncated/Normal" star formation class, so it is likely to have been affected by ICM--ISM stripping. However it couldn't have crossed the core according to the simple cluster model, and the age of stellar population in outer disk reveals that it must have been stripped outside the cluster core (Crowl Kenney 2008). This galaxy is closer (in the plane of the sky) to M49 than M87, so perhaps it is bound to the M49 subcluster rather than the M87 subcluster, and affected by the M49 environment.
