sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4580 likely lies near but outside Virgo, may be losing HI by ram pressure, and may interact with NGC 4586.

quotes.quote: Similarly, NGC 4580 and NGC 4586 are at a projected separation of 311 kpc with a velocity difference of 254 km s^ 1 , so they too may constitute an interacting pair.

NGC 4580 This galaxy probably lies near but not in the Virgo cluster, and may possibly be bouncing out of the cluster, therefore losing its neutral gas by ram pressure stripping. It may be interacting with NGC 4586 , and is somewhat blue for its luminosity, especially if it lies behind Virgo.

gmw_00004018.name: NGC 4586
gmw_00004018.overview: NGC 4586 is a galaxy that features a boxy, peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382] and exhibits non-circular gas motions driven by a bar or a triaxial bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. Located near the Virgo cluster, it may be interacting with the galaxy NGC 4580 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 4586's central gas velocity gradients on both axes as non-circular disk-plane motion driven by a triaxial bulge or bar, and notes its boxy bulge.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586 the gas velocity rises almost linearly in the inner regions of both the major (where gas rotates faster than stars) and minor axes. Non-zero gas velocities along the minor axis are confined in the nuclear regions. Such gas kinematics have been explained as being due to non-circular motion induced by a triaxial potential.

Non-circular gas motions in a triaxial bulge or in a bar Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis in the bulge-dominated region of NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586, and a velocity gradient is observed along their major axis too. We interpret the observed kinematics as due to non-circular gas motion in the principal plane of the disk caused by bulge triaxiality or due to the action of a bar.
Both NGC 4224 and NGC 4586 have a boxy/peanut-shape bulge (Panel 76, CAG).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4586 probably lies near Virgo, may be losing neutral gas after bouncing out, and may be interacting with NGC 4580.

quotes.quote: Similarly, NGC 4580 and NGC 4586 are at a projected separation of 311 kpc with a velocity difference of 254 km s^ 1 , so they too may constitute an interacting pair.

NGC 4586 This galaxy probably lies near the Virgo cluster and may possibly be bouncing out of the cluster, therefore losing its neutral gas by ram pressure stripping. It may be interacting with NGC 4580 , and is blue for its luminosity, especially if it lies within the Virgo cluster.

gmw_00004019.name: NGC 4606
gmw_00004019.overview: NGC 4606 is an Sa spiral galaxy [arXiv:0803.3181] located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0803.3638, arXiv:astro-ph/0401367]. Classified as a truncated or compact galaxy [arXiv:0803.3638], it exhibits features suggesting gravitational interactions [arXiv:0803.3638] and is likely tidally perturbed by its companion, NGC 4607 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401367].

sources.summary: The paper reassesses whether NGC 4606 violates M60 compact-group isolation, concluding recent photometry makes it too faint to affect the group status.

quotes.quote: The neighboring galaxy NGC 4606 appears (with 80-90 confidence) to be too faint to affect the isolated status of the CG.

The M60 CG had been missed by , because NGC 4606 , an Sa galaxy lying at 1.98 Hickson circle radii from the group center, was only 2.4 mag fainter than M60 in the B band. Even after a crude extrapolation to R magnitudes for the different morphological types of the two galaxies, NGC 4606 was still slightly less than 3 mag fainter than M60, so NGC 4606 caused the CG to fail Hickson's isolation criterion. When I discovered this CG Mamon89 , I noticed that more accurate B band photometry indicated that NGC 4606 was 2.88 mag fainter in B than M60, which, after the crude correction for morphological types, suggested that NGC 4606 was at least 3 mag fainter than M60 in the R band. I therefore argued that NGC 4606 did not affect the isolated status of the CG, and concluded that the M60 CG was the nearest HCG-like group.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4606 as a truncated/compact Virgo spiral whose SKB distance exceeds the HI estimate, with environmental effects and gravitational-interaction signatures likely reducing its linewidth.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

NGC 4351 and NGC 4606 Consider now the cases of NGC 4351, and NGC 4606. Both galaxies belong to Rubin's sample but they were not classified as ``low rotators". Solanes's estimation gives distances of 11.2 1.3 Mpc, and 12.7 0.3 Mpc respectively which again differ from our results of 20.3 _ 1.2 ^ 0.8 Mpc, and 19.9 _ 0.5 ^ 1.0 Mpc. In NGC 4351 the discrepancy, could be due to truncation of the HI disk via ICM-ISM stripping, a very likely effect on this galaxy considering its high relative velocity with respect to the cluster mean ( 1200 ). NGC 4606 has a star formation class ``Truncated/Compact", same as NGC 4064, and NGC 4424, and as these galaxies it has features that suggest gravitational interactions (Cort es 2006), so again its small line-width could be due to the action of environmental effects.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4606 as a likely Virgo member with a moderately close major companion and probable tidal perturbation despite modest tidal signs.

quotes.quote: NGC 4305 and NGC 4606 have moderately close major companions.

NGC 4606 This galaxy is a very likely member of the Virgo cluster. It is likely to be tidally perturbed by its companion NGC 4607 (Table 8), as attested by its blue color for its luminosity assuming it indeed lies in Virgo, although it shows only modest signs of such tides.

gmw_00004020.name: Reticulum II
gmw_00004020.overview: Reticulum II is an old, metal-poor ultrafaint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1711.04696]. Located approximately 30 kpc away, it has a half-light radius of about 32 pc and an absolute magnitude of -2.7 [arXiv:1711.04696].

sources.summary: The paper selects Reticulum II as an old, metal-poor ultrafaint dSph with a large J-factor, reports its discovery, distance, location, size, luminosity, and stellar-density fit.

quotes.quote: In this work, We select three dSphs, namely Willman 1 (SDSS J1049+5103), Reticulum II (DES J0335.6-5403) and Triangulum II, in our analysis. These old and metal-poor stellar sources have almost the largest J-factors among known dSphs.

Reticulum II was discovered from the recent optical imaging data collected during the first year of the Dark Energy Survey (DES). It is 30 kpc away and is located at (l, b)=( 266.30^ , -49.74^ ) with a half-light radius r_h 32 pc and an absolute magnitude M_V 2.7 . The stellar density of Reticulum II and fitting result are shown in Fig. fig:zy2 . We take the exponential profile plus background to fit the data, with parameters I_ 0 =1.67 10^5 stars/kpc ^2 , r_ =0.023 kpc, and background I_ bkg =3570 stars/kpc ^2 .

gmw_00004021.name: Triangulum II
gmw_00004021.overview: Triangulum II is an old, metal-poor ultrafaint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1711.04696]. Discovered in the Panoramic Survey Telescope And Rapid Response System 3 imaging data, it is located at a heliocentric distance of 30 kpc and has a half-light radius of approximately 34 pc [arXiv:1711.04696].

sources.summary: The paper selects Triangulum II as an old, metal-poor ultrafaint dSph with a large J-factor, giving its survey discovery, location, distance, size, luminosity, and density profile.

quotes.quote: In this work, We select three dSphs, namely Willman 1 (SDSS J1049+5103), Reticulum II (DES J0335.6-5403) and Triangulum II, in our analysis. These old and metal-poor stellar sources have almost the largest J-factors among known dSphs.

Triangulum II was discovered from the Panoramic Survey Telescope And Rapid Response System 3 imaging data. It is located at (l, b)=( 140.90^ , -23.82^ ), with a heliocentric distance of 30 kpc, a half-light radius r_h 34 pc, and an absolute magnitude M_V 1.8 . The stellar density of Triangulum II is depicted in Fig. fig:zy3 . We also adopt the exponential model plus background to fit the data with I_ 0 =5.9 10^4 stars/kpc ^2 , r_ =0.021 kpc, and I_ bkg =2726 stars/kpc ^2 .

gmw_00004022.name: SDSS J030831.67-005008.6
gmw_00004022.overview: SDSS J030831.67-005008.6 is a blue-cloud star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1901.05508]. It is characterized as a low-metallicity galaxy and serves as the host of the transient KISS15s [arXiv:1901.05508].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J030831.67-005008.6 as the host of KISS15s and describes it as a blue-cloud, low-metallicity star-forming galaxy with measured distance and offset.

quotes.quote: Because KISS15s is located on the edge of the extended structure of SDSS J030831.67-005008.6, we identify SDSS J030831.67-005008.6 as the host galaxy of KISS15s. Follow-up spectroscopy for KISS15s (described in Section obs:sepc ) confirms that the redshift of KISS15s is consistent with the redshift of this galaxy. A luminosity distance ( d_ L ) and distance modulus (DM) of the host galaxy corrected for the Virgo infall + Great Attractor + Shapley supercluster local flow is d_ L = 156 Mpc and DM = m-M = 35.97 mag, respectively d_ L and m-M were taken from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) . The angular-spatial size scale is 0.720 kpc/ '' ; thus, the galactocentric offset of KISS15s is 2.23 kpc.

The host galaxy g and band absolute magnitude is M_g = -17.01 0.02 and M_r = -17.18 0.02 mag, and the u- color is 1.46 0.13 mag (corrected for Galactic extinction), which are typical values of a ``blue cloud'' star forming galaxy e.g., sch14 . The effective radius of the exponential profile model r_e is 4.3'' 0.2'' (3.1 0.1 kpc) in the band. Figure fig:bpt_diagram shows a BPT diagram bal81 of SDSS J030831.67-005008.6, where the Galactic extinction corrected line intensities are calculated from SDSS spectroscopy pipeline outputs ( spZline ). Based on the BPT classification, this galaxy is a low-metallicity star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00004023.name: VVV-WIT-04
gmw_00004023.overview: VVV-WIT-04 is an extragalactic source identified as the near-infrared counterpart of the radio source PMN J1515-5559 [arXiv:1909.07516]. Its highly variable K_s band light curve is consistent with the behavior of an optically violent variable quasar [arXiv:1909.07516].

sources.summary: The paper argues that VVV-WIT-04 is the near-infrared counterpart of PMN J1515-5559, with variability consistent with an OVV quasar and alternatives disfavored.

quotes.quote: We suggest that VVV-WIT-04 has an extragalactic origin as the near-IR counterpart of PMN J1515-5559. The K_ s band light-curve of VVV-WIT-04 is highly variable and consistent with that of an Optically Violent Variable (OVV) quasar. The variability in the near-IR can be interpreted as the redshifted optical variability. Residuals to the proper motion varies with the magnitude suggesting contamination by a blended source. Alternative scenarios, including a transient event such as a nova or supernova, or even a binary microlensing event are not in agreement with the available data.

gmw_00004024.name: PKS 1229-021
gmw_00004024.overview: PKS 1229-021 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1907.03442] that exhibits a knotty, asymmetric core-jet-lobe morphology [arXiv:1907.03442, arXiv:1710.04250]. Its jet displays a considerable bend [arXiv:1710.04250], and a distinct morphological difference between millimeter and centimeter wavelengths suggests a likely jet realignment [arXiv:2305.05173].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 1229 02 as an FSRQ with asymmetric core-jet-lobe morphology, weak Doppler boosting, and insufficient beaming to explain gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 1229 02 with a knotty and asymmetric radio morphology was identified as the optical and radio counterpart of a ray source. In this paper, we study the properties, e.g. morphology, opacity, polarization and kinematics of the jet in PKS 1229 02 using radio interferometry. With our results, we find that the knotty and asymmetric morphology of this source may probably shaped by the interaction between its anterograde jet and the nonuniform dense ambient medium. By reproducing a Spectral Energy Distribution of PKS 1229 02 with the obtained kinematic parameters, we find that the relativistic beaming effect in PKS 1229 02 is not strong enough to produce the reported ray emission, i.e. PKS 1229 02 may not be a ray AGN.

On kpc-scale, the asymmetric structure of PKS 1229 02 is clearly revealed by our results, as the integral flux density of the anterograde jet is a few times higher than that of the retrograde jet. The source is edge-brightened on kpc-scale, i.e. the jet components at the far-end of the source (e.g. W1, W2, E1) tend to have higher integral flux density than the jet components at the middle of the source (e.g. W3, W4, W5, E2).
As we see, T_ b of the VLBI core is at the order of magnitude of 10 ^ 10 K or 10 ^ 11 K, almost the same as the T_ eq , suggesting that PKS 1229 02 is not affected by major Doppler boosting.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 1229-021 as an X-ray jet source, resolving cannonball-like knots, combining unresolved BCD knots, and ruling out IC/CMB at 6.1 sigma.

quotes.quote: PKS 1229-021 PKS 1229-021 was previously observed and modeled as an IC/CMB X-ray jet by , where we use their reported HST and Chandra densities in our SED (Figure fig:jet_SEDs ). The radio images shown in Figure fig:1229 images show four well-defined 'cannonball'-like knots downstream of the core. Interestingly, similar periodic knot structures are seen in all six of the jets discussed in this paper. As shown in Figure fig:1229 images , knots B, C, and D are not separately resolved by ALMA and Chandra ; thus we combined the data for these knots, which are labeled as knot BCD. Past this combined feature, we see the jet is considerably bent, implying a change of jet direction.

Combining the individual band results using the inverse normal method, we find the IC/CMB model is ruled out at the 14.3 , 6.1 , 4.7 , and >15.2 levels for PKS 1136-135, PKS 1229-021, PKS 1354+195, and PKS 2209+080 respectively.

sources.summary: SDSS J123200.01-022404.7 shows different millimeter and centimeter morphologies: ALMA finds western jet knots, VLA images show central and eastern emission, suggesting likely jet realignment.

quotes.quote: SDSS J123200.01-022404.7 (4C -02.55) is a quasar at a redshift of 1.05 observed in band 6 szakacs21 . Comparing with FIRST (1.4 GHz) and VLASS (2--4 GHz) imaging, there is a clear morphological difference between mm and cm wavelengths. While ALMA shows four jet knots to the west of the location of the quasar, the VLA image reveals a bright central emission and a fainter source (a jet knot or lobe) to the east of the quasar. This feature falls outside the ALMA field of view. Given the distinct spatial resolutions of each image (i.e. all the structure detectable by ALMA is within a FIRST survey synthesized beam), it is impossible to determine whether this morphology is related to significant jet realignment (i.e. the closest jet component goes from being that in the eastern side to the one in the western side), nevertheless this is the most likely scenario.

gmw_00004025.name: PKS 2209+080
gmw_00004025.overview: PKS 2209+080 is a quasar host galaxy that features an irregular tail structure, which suggests a possible recent merger [arXiv:1710.04250]. The system hosts a straight, highly knotty jet with a southern hot spot resolved into two components [arXiv:1710.04250].

sources.summary: The paper reports new HST and ALMA observations of PKS 2209+080, resolving its knotty straight jet, merger-like host tail, and UV upturns inconsistent with IC/CMB.

quotes.quote: PKS 2209+080 PKS 2209+080 was previously observed and modeled as an IC/CMB X-ray jet by , where we used their X-ray flux density in our SED. As shown in Figure fig:2209 images , PKS 2209+080 has a very knotty and straight jet with the southern hot spot showing two resolved components. Our ALMA and HST observations resolve all of the identified radio knots. As can be seen in both HST images, the host galaxy has an irregular tail structure suggestive of a possible recent merger. Only the southern hot spot is detected in the infrared as shown in the lower-middle panel of Figure fig:2209 images .

The only knot reported to show X-ray emission by was knot E. In Figure fig:2209 knot seds we show the SEDs of knots A through D which are upstream of knot E with data shown as squares and synchrotron model fits as solid lines. In knots A through C there is an upturn in the SED at 600 nm which lies above a single power-law fit for the synchrotron spectrum.

gmw_00004026.name: NGC 7241
gmw_00004026.overview: NGC 7241 is an edge-on disk galaxy with a mass comparable to the Milky Way [arXiv:2112.07029]. It exhibits strong off-plane star formation and may be undergoing a complex interaction with a nearby dwarf companion [arXiv:2112.07029].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7241 as a Milky-Way-mass edge-on disk with off-plane star formation, a dwarf companion, and a blue stellar stream tracing possible interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7241 is a galaxy that is transitioning from the low-mass, with no warm-hot corona, to the high-mass scenario (like the MW), with a spatially extended HI gas disk Leaman2015 . This edge-on disk galaxy has a strong off-plane star formation that can be identified in the UV images from Galex. Interestingly, this galaxy shows no morphological evidence of recent interaction, but HI observations by Giovanelli1984 showed evidence of the presence of a possible dwarf companion in the field.

Analysis of the connection between the so-called "companion" and the NGC 7241 was not conclusive. The authors also studied the companion's SFR and observed that it is one order of magnitude higher than expected in a normal galaxy of its mass. This result, the absence of strong morphology perturbations, and enhanced SF in the NGC 7241 disk pointed towards an unknown but potentially complex interaction history.

gmw_00004027.name: SDSS J123708.91+142253.2
gmw_00004027.overview: SDSS J123708.91+142253.2 is the nearest bright galaxy with a known redshift to the giant low surface brightness galaxy Malin 1 [arXiv:1005.5294]. It is a candidate massive intruder that may have undergone a head-on collision with Malin 1 approximately 1 Gyr ago [arXiv:1005.5294].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J123708.91+142253.2 as the nearest bright redshift-known galaxy and a possible massive intruder in a past head-on collision with Malin 1.

quotes.quote: The nearest bright galaxy with known redshift is SDSS J123708.91+142253.2 with V_ hel = 24907 27 (Table 1). The galaxy is located at the projected distance of 3.9 arcmin or 350 kpc far from Malin 1 and has an apparent magnitude g( SDSS )=18.6 . The velocity difference between this galaxy and Malin 1 is rather small --- 132 29 km/s. We can use parameters of the collisional model presented by Mapelli et al. (2008) to check the galaxy SDSS J123708.91+142253.2 as a candidate for being a possible intruder.

Our simple velocity estimate for the intruder is in agreement with the data presented here. Moreover, current location of the galaxy SDSS J123708.91+142253.2 maintains the idea for the closest passage to happen 1 Gyr ago that is also in accordance with the model by Mapelli et al. (2008).

gmw_00004028.name: J 0401-0540
gmw_00004028.overview: The active galactic nucleus J 0401-0540 exhibits a disc-like line profile with a weaker blue peak than red peak [arXiv:1211.4033]. It maintains a stable soft X-ray count rate and has a radio flux upper limit of 1.35 mJy [arXiv:1211.4033].

sources.summary: The paper finds J 0401-0540 has a disc-like line profile with an unusually weaker blue peak, stable long-term soft X-ray count rate, and only an NVSS radio upper limit.

quotes.quote: We note that for J 0401-0540 , the model fits well the red peak while it shows a slight excess at the blue peak. In fact, unlike the others, the observed J 0401-0540 line profile possesses a slightly weaker blue peak than red peak, which cannot be perfectly fit by a circular disc model, and a model with a non-axisymmetric perturbation is probably required e.g., an elliptical disc; see Eracleous1995 .
The RASS-derived count rate (0.078 s ^ 1 ) of J 0401-0540 appears to be consistent with the \ observation (0.072 s ^ 1 ).

For the one source (J 0401-0540 ) that is not covered by the FIRST survey, we checked the NRAO VLA Sky Survey (NVSS; Condon1998 ) catalog and determined its flux upper limit to be 1.35 mJy, which is three times the rms noise of the NVSS.

gmw_00004029.name: J 0850+4451
gmw_00004029.overview: J 0850+4451 is a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar located at a low redshift of z=0.5422 [arXiv:1811.04174, arXiv:1211.4033]. The system is heavily obscured in the X-ray band [arXiv:1211.4033] and appears to be deficient in hot dust [arXiv:1811.04174].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0850+4451 as a low-redshift LoBAL quasar whose broad absorption outflow has wavelength-dependent partial covering and negligible host contamination near He I absorption.

quotes.quote: Extrapolation of the SimBAL spectral synthesis model of the HST COS UV spectrum from SDSS J0850+4451 reported by leighly18 to non-simultaneous rest-frame optical and near-infrared spectra reveals evidence that the covering fraction has wavelength dependence, and is a factor of 2.5 times higher in the UV than in the optical and near-infrared bands. The difference in covering fraction can be explained if the outflow consists of clumps that are small and either structured or clustered relative to the projected size of the UV continuum emission region, and have a more diffuse distribution on size scales comparable to the near-infrared continuum emission region size. The lower covering fraction over the larger physical area results in a reduction of the measured total column density by a factor of 1.6 compared with the UV-only solution.

The low redshift ( z=0.5422 ) LoBAL quasar SDSS J085053.12 + 445122.5, hereafter referred to as SDSS J0850+4451, was discovered to have a He 1 * 3889 absorption line in its SDSS spectrum e.g., luo13 . We obtained Gemini GNIRS and LBT LUCI near-infrared spectroscopic observations and identified the presence of a deep He 1 * 10830 absorption line ( observations below). SDSS J0850 + 4451 was detected by GALEX , indicating that it was bright enough to be observed by HST using COS.

The WISE points were excluded because, compared to the Richards continuum, the torus in SDSS J0850+4451 is considerably less bright, suggesting that this object is deficient in hot dust hao10,hao11,lyu17 . Also shown in the top panel of Fig. sed_fitting_fig is the decomposition of the SED with components indicated in the figure legend. This solution has reddening E(B - V) = 0.0 , meaning that the solution hit the lower bound of allowed values. The galaxy contribution at = 10830 \ is 0.09 0.03 . The power-law index is 1.53 , which is not unusual for quasars krawczyk15 . The residuals to the fit are shown in the lower panel of the figure, where these are defined as = (data model) / model. The model fit is not particularly good, with reduced ^2 = 50 , but much of this is likely to be due to the simplicity of the model; variability may play a role as well (see Fig. fig15 ). These results show that the contribution of the host galaxy emission to the continuum under the He 1 * 10830 absorption line is negligible. t 1.0 Image Analysis of the SDSS J0850+4451 Host Galaxy Coincidentally, an HST program PI: Canalizo, ``The Nature of Low-Ionization BAL QSOs'', program number 11557 made WFC3 observations in the near-infrared of SDSS J0850+4451; the information about this observation is found in Table observations .

sources.summary: The paper describes J 0850+4451 as heavily X-ray obscured and identifies it as a LoBAL quasar, the first reported disc-like emitter that is also a BAL quasar.

quotes.quote: J 0850+4451 has only three counts within the source aperture, preventing spectral modeling. However, these three counts are all hard X-ray photons, in the 4--5 keV range (corresponding to 6.2--7.7 keV in the rest frame), suggesting that this source is heavily obscured. To obtain a first-order estimate of the level of obscuration, we used the fakeit routine in XSPEC to simulate observed spectra assuming an absorbed power-law model with =1.8 and different N_ H,int values. A value of N_ H,int 7 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 is required to generate three photons with energies above 4 keV and almost no other lower-energy photons.

The SDSS spectrum of J 0850+4451 shows a broad absorption line on the blue side of Mg ii   2799 with a minimum outflow velocity of v_ min 1300  km s ^ 1 and a velocity width of FWHM 2600  km s ^ 1 , indicating that it is a low-ionization broad absorption line (LoBAL) quasar e.g., Boroson1992,Trump2006,Gibson2009 . The Mg ii 2799 line is shown in Figure  fig-bal a; We fit two Gaussian profiles to the broad emission line in the continuum-subtracted spectrum, and then used one Gaussian to model the remaining absorption feature. The SDSS spectrum also shows probable broad absorption features in He i 3188 and He i 3889 , as have been observed in some other BAL quasars (e.g., Boksenberg1977 ; Leighly2011 ).

gmw_00004030.name: J 2125-0813
gmw_00004030.overview: J 2125-0813 is a radio-quiet quasar that exhibits double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines and optical Fe II emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0607566]. The galaxy displays long-term X-ray variability [arXiv:1211.4033], and its integrated H-beta line flux dropped by over 40 percent between 2001 and 2008 [arXiv:1211.4033].

sources.summary: The paper reports stable disc-model parameters but declining H-line flux for J 2125-0813, plus long-term X-ray variability and no significant Fe K excess in improved spectra.

quotes.quote: We have obtained additional optical spectra for J 2125-0813 with the Low-Resolution Spectrograph Hill1998 on the Hobby-Eberly Telescope (HET; Ramsey1998 ) in 2006 Jun and 2008 Jun, contemporaneous of the \ or XMM-Newton observations. The disc-like H line profiles in these spectra are similar to that in the SDSS spectrum taken in 2001, and the corresponding disc-model parameters do not change. However, the integrated H line flux dropped by 40 in 2006 and 44 in 2008 compared to the 2001 value. A comparison of the SDSS and HET spectra of J 2125-0813 is shown in Figure  fig-het .

For J 2125-0813 , the count rate observed by \ (0.056 s ^ 1 ) dropped by a factor of about two over a 20 year time-scale compared to that expected from the RASS count rate (0.122 s ^ 1 ). Additional \ and XMM-Newton observations of J 2125-0813 also indicate long-term variability, as presented in Section 4.3 below.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J2125-0813 as a radio-quiet quasar whose FeII and Balmer lines are modeled with a shared elliptical accretion-disk profile.

quotes.quote: We report on the radio quiet quasar SDSS J2125-0813 which obviously emits optical FeII emission lines and double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines. Using the accretion disk model for double-peaked broad low-ionization emission lines, we reproduce the composite line spectra at the optical band between 4100 and 5600 . Broad FeII emission lines can be fit simultaneously with the broad H and MgI lines, such that all broad lines have an elliptical disk profile with the same disk parameters. This results indicates that the optical FeII emission lines originate from the accretion disk near the central black hole which produces the double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines.

Furthermore, we find that the object has dimensionless accretion rate m 0.4-0.6 which is much larger than accretion rate for ADAF mode, and that the energy budget of the accretion disk is enough to power the double-peaked broad Balmer emission lines.

gmw_00004031.name: J 2148+0010
gmw_00004031.overview: J 2148+0010 is a luminous radio-quiet active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1211.4033]. The source is moderately obscured by a neutral intrinsic absorber and is likely X-ray weak [arXiv:1211.4033].

sources.summary: The paper treats J 2148+0010 as a luminous radio-quiet AGN with limited X-ray counts, moderate intrinsic absorption, an unusually flat simple power-law fit, and likely X-ray weakness.

quotes.quote: However, due to the small number of source counts for J 2148+0010 , we fixed its photon index at =1.8 , a typical value for luminous radio-quiet AGNs e.g., Reeves1997,Just2007,Scott2011 , and we also adopted the C statistic (cstat) in XSPEC.

J 1500+1210 shows a small amount of absorption, although with a large uncertainty, and J 2148+0010 is moderately obscured [ N_ H,int =(2.5_ 1.0 ^ +1.2 ) 10^ 22  cm ^ 2 ] . If we adopt a simple power-law model for J 2148+0010 and allow its photon index to be a free parameter, the resulting value is an unusually flat 0.7 , also indicating likely obscuration. We note that a neutral intrinsic absorber was assumed in this fitting.

Our eight sources reside at the high-luminosity end, and their _ OX parameters follow the _ OX - L_ 2500  relation (within 1 ) except for J 0850+4451 and J 2148+0010 , which are likely X-ray weak due to the previously noted absorption (see Section 4).

gmw_00004032.name: dSph1
gmw_00004032.overview: dSph1 is a low surface brightness dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate [arXiv:1609.00224]. It is projected within the halo of the bright elliptical galaxy NGC 3193, with an almost circular shape [arXiv:1609.00224].

sources.summary: The paper identifies dSph1 as an LSB dSph candidate projected in the halo of NGC 3193, with nearly circular modeling and UDG-like physical size if at NGC 3193 or HCG 44.

quotes.quote: In the particular case of the object within the halo of NGC 3193 (hereafter dSph1; top panels in Figure dSphs ), we first modeled the bright elliptical galaxy, the halo of which covers the whole frame, and subtracted it from the original images. In the galaxy-subtracted images, we modeled the light of the bright star located next to the elliptical. This model was later subtracted from the original images, and this star-subtracted images were used to obtain the final model of the elliptical galaxy. The images over which we worked to obtain the profiles of the LSB object were the star-galaxy-subtracted ones. To model dSph1, we masked all the objects in the images and we used a fixed center, ellipticity and position angle, keeping the ellipticity almost circular ( =0.05 ).

If we assume that this LSB object is located at the distance of NGC 3193 ( D=33.6 Mpc, 2013AJ....146...86T ), we have a physical scale of 0.163 kpc arcsec ^ 1 and the total projected radius of this galaxy would be 4.2 kpc. If this galaxy were at the distance of HCG 44 (we will assume a mean distance of D=23.6 Mpc, (m-M)=31.86 , from those of NGC 3185 and NGC 3189/3190 shown in Table distancias ) the physical scale would be 0.115 kpc arcsec ^ 1 and the radius would translate into 3.0 kpc. The projected separation between dSph1 and NGC 3193 is 1.4 arcmin (13.7 kpc at the distance of NGC 3193).

gmw_00004033.name: dSph2
gmw_00004033.overview: dSph2 is an extremely faint, low surface brightness dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate located within the Hickson Compact Group 44 region [arXiv:1609.00224]. If it is a member of this group, its effective radius is comparable to ultra-diffuse galaxies [arXiv:1609.00224].

sources.summary: The paper identifies dSph2 as an extremely faint LSB dSph candidate in HCG 44 whose possible radius, effective radius, and nearby LSB structure make it UDG-like and potentially tidal.

quotes.quote: After performing several tests, and due to the extremely low surface brightness of the galaxy (S/N=0.5 per binned pixel in the inner zone of the g' profile), we considered fixed circular isophotes to obtain the brightness profile of the dSph galaxy located within the HCG 44 region (hereafter dSph2; lower panels of Figure dSphs ). As in the case of dSph1, we worked on sky-subtracted images in which we masked all the extended and unresolved objects, and we built the curve of growth in order to correct the initially assumed sky value. In this case, the curve of growth in the g' filter stabilizes at r_ eq 54 arcsec ( _ g' 30 mag arcsec ^ 2 ). If the galaxy were located at the distance of the HCG 44, this total equivalent radius would translate into =6.2 kpc. In that case, this would imply that this object would be a large LSB galaxy.

Similarly to dSph1, the r_ eff of dSph2 would be in agreement to those reported for Coma's UDGs and would be significantly larger than those of LG dSphs (Figure mueff_dSphs ), if it were a member of HCG 44. In addition, it would not follow Virgo's RS. The projected separation between dSph2 and NGC 3185 is 5.5 arcmin, while the separation between dSph2 and NGC 3189/3190 is 5.6 arcmin.

gmw_00004034.name: G1
gmw_00004034.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy G1, also known as RXCJ 0014.3-3022 NW A, is located in a merging cluster environment [arXiv:0804.0299, arXiv:2303.10210]. It exhibits twisted outer isophotes and is surrounded by diffuse stellar emission that extends up to 100 kpc from its center [arXiv:0804.0299].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RXCJ 0014.3-3022 NW A as one of seven BCGs with diffuse light; its twisted outer isophotes support a real bridge in the merging cluster.

quotes.quote: Diffuse stellar emission is robustly detected around all the seven BCGs in the three X-ray selected clusters, and its distribution and B-R colour down to 26 R- (observed frame) are investigated. The diffuse light around BCGs extends up to 100 kpc from their centres. At this distance, we expect that on average 30--40 of the detected emission (including galaxies) is due to the intracluster light (ICL), as from the statistical results obtained from 683 optically selected clusters of galaxies at 0.2 z 0.3 (Zibetti et al. 2005).

One visual pair of BCGs lies to the north-west with respect to the X-ray centroid (i.e., RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW A and B). The projected separation of the two BCGs is 185 kpc . Lack of spectroscopy for RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B does not permit the physical nature of the pair to be established, but RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW is associated with an X-ray feature connecting the two subcomponents of the ICM.

In particular, RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW suggests strong tidal forces in action: a stellar ``plume'' stretches out of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B along the bridge of diffuse emission connecting the two BCG main bodies - as defined by their effective radii - (see Fig. FigBR1a ). In absence of spectroscopic evidence of interaction, the stellar plume stretching out of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B and the twisted outer isophotes of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW A support the hypothesis that the stellar bridge between these BCGs is not a projection effect.

sources.summary: The lens model treats G1 as one of the three brightest external-clump galaxies, optimizing its mass contribution and ellipticity while using nearby multiple images to improve the Abell 2744 mass reconstruction.

quotes.quote: Due to the lack of secure multiple image systems around the massive structures in the northwestern region of the cluster prior to the JWST/NIRCam imaging, the Bergamini2022 reference LM-model included a simple description of the mass contribution from three external clumps, associated with the three brightest galaxies (G1, G2, and G3), as singular isothermal sphere (SIS) profiles. The identification of 29 (3) multiple images around the galaxies G1-G2 (G3) allows now for a more accurate total mass reconstruction of these infalling structures, ^ ENV , which is decomposed into the following mass contributions: ^ ENV = _ G1-G2 ^ halo + _ G3 ^ halo + _ G1 + _ G2 + _ G3 The first two terms correspond to the cluster component that is modeled as two cluster-scale non-truncated elliptical dPIEs: one associated with the galaxies G1 and G2 and allowed to move within a large square region containing the two galaxies (see table:inout_lensing , corresponding to a region of about 270 kpc 180 kpc ), and a second one centered on the position of the galaxy G3. The parameters describing the mass contribution (i.e., the velocity dispersion and cut radius) of the three galaxies G1, G2 and G3 are individually optimized, within large flat priors. In addition, the ellipticity parameters of G1 and G2 are left free to vary (see table:inout_lensing ).
An even higher precision of _ rms ^ G1,G2 =0.20 is found in reproducing the observed positions of the 29 multiple images forming around G1 and G2.

gmw_00004035.name: G2
gmw_00004035.overview: The galaxy G2, also designated RXCJ 0014.3-3022 NW B, is a brightest cluster galaxy located in a merging cluster environment [arXiv:0804.0299, arXiv:2303.10210]. It exhibits a stellar plume stretching along a diffuse light bridge, which suggests the presence of strong tidal forces [arXiv:0804.0299].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RXCJ 0014.3-3022 NW B as a BCG in the merging cluster; a stellar plume from it helps support a physical diffuse-light bridge.

quotes.quote: Diffuse stellar emission is robustly detected around all the seven BCGs in the three X-ray selected clusters, and its distribution and B-R colour down to 26 R- (observed frame) are investigated. The diffuse light around BCGs extends up to 100 kpc from their centres. At this distance, we expect that on average 30--40 of the detected emission (including galaxies) is due to the intracluster light (ICL), as from the statistical results obtained from 683 optically selected clusters of galaxies at 0.2 z 0.3 (Zibetti et al. 2005).

One visual pair of BCGs lies to the north-west with respect to the X-ray centroid (i.e., RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW A and B). The projected separation of the two BCGs is 185 kpc . Lack of spectroscopy for RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B does not permit the physical nature of the pair to be established, but RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW is associated with an X-ray feature connecting the two subcomponents of the ICM.

In particular, RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW suggests strong tidal forces in action: a stellar ``plume'' stretches out of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B along the bridge of diffuse emission connecting the two BCG main bodies - as defined by their effective radii - (see Fig. FigBR1a ). In absence of spectroscopic evidence of interaction, the stellar plume stretching out of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW B and the twisted outer isophotes of RXCJ 0014.3 3022 NW A support the hypothesis that the stellar bridge between these BCGs is not a projection effect.

sources.summary: The lens model treats G2 as one of the three brightest external-clump galaxies, optimizing its mass contribution and ellipticity while using nearby multiple images to improve the Abell 2744 mass reconstruction.

quotes.quote: Due to the lack of secure multiple image systems around the massive structures in the northwestern region of the cluster prior to the JWST/NIRCam imaging, the Bergamini2022 reference LM-model included a simple description of the mass contribution from three external clumps, associated with the three brightest galaxies (G1, G2, and G3), as singular isothermal sphere (SIS) profiles. The identification of 29 (3) multiple images around the galaxies G1-G2 (G3) allows now for a more accurate total mass reconstruction of these infalling structures, ^ ENV , which is decomposed into the following mass contributions: ^ ENV = _ G1-G2 ^ halo + _ G3 ^ halo + _ G1 + _ G2 + _ G3 The first two terms correspond to the cluster component that is modeled as two cluster-scale non-truncated elliptical dPIEs: one associated with the galaxies G1 and G2 and allowed to move within a large square region containing the two galaxies (see table:inout_lensing , corresponding to a region of about 270 kpc 180 kpc ), and a second one centered on the position of the galaxy G3. The parameters describing the mass contribution (i.e., the velocity dispersion and cut radius) of the three galaxies G1, G2 and G3 are individually optimized, within large flat priors. In addition, the ellipticity parameters of G1 and G2 are left free to vary (see table:inout_lensing ).
An even higher precision of _ rms ^ G1,G2 =0.20 is found in reproducing the observed positions of the 29 multiple images forming around G1 and G2.

gmw_00004036.name: G3
gmw_00004036.overview: G3 is a massive galaxy located in a cluster environment [arXiv:2303.10210]. It acts as a gravitational lens, deflecting light from a background galaxy into three observed multiple images [arXiv:2303.10210].

sources.summary: The lens model treats G3 as one of three bright external-clump galaxies, optimizing its mass contribution and noting lower local image-reproduction precision around its massive clump.

quotes.quote: Due to the lack of secure multiple image systems around the massive structures in the northwestern region of the cluster prior to the JWST/NIRCam imaging, the Bergamini2022 reference LM-model included a simple description of the mass contribution from three external clumps, associated with the three brightest galaxies (G1, G2, and G3), as singular isothermal sphere (SIS) profiles. The identification of 29 (3) multiple images around the galaxies G1-G2 (G3) allows now for a more accurate total mass reconstruction of these infalling structures, ^ ENV , which is decomposed into the following mass contributions: ^ ENV = _ G1-G2 ^ halo + _ G3 ^ halo + _ G1 + _ G2 + _ G3 The first two terms correspond to the cluster component that is modeled as two cluster-scale non-truncated elliptical dPIEs: one associated with the galaxies G1 and G2 and allowed to move within a large square region containing the two galaxies (see table:inout_lensing , corresponding to a region of about 270 kpc 180 kpc ), and a second one centered on the position of the galaxy G3. The parameters describing the mass contribution (i.e., the velocity dispersion and cut radius) of the three galaxies G1, G2 and G3 are individually optimized, within large flat priors. In addition, the ellipticity parameters of G1 and G2 are left free to vary (see table:inout_lensing ).

In contrast, the \ precision decreases to _ rms ^ G3 =0.73 around galaxy G3. Here, only three non-spectroscopic images (700.a, 700.b, and 700.) of a single background galaxy are included in the lens model. We notice that the deflecting contribution of the massive clump surrounding G3 is mainly driven by its influence on the many multiple images observed relatively far from its center, i.e. in the cluster core.

gmw_00004037.name: WISEA J033429
gmw_00004037.overview: WISEA J033429 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits strong soft excess emission in the soft X-ray band below 1 keV [arXiv:2305.00991]. The source is also characterized by intrinsic X-ray weakness, with an X-ray weakness factor of 20.1 [arXiv:2305.00991].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J033429 as the sample source with the strongest relative soft excess and intrinsic X-ray weakness, favoring a reflection-dominated explanation.

quotes.quote: Soft Excess Emission We fit the 2-10 keV energy band in the observed frame with an absorbed powerlaw at the Galactic column density values ( 0.7-10 keV for SDSS J020435 and WISEA J033429 due to inadequate data bins in the 2-10 keV band). We then include the 0.3-2 keV band data without re-fitting to illustrate the soft excess emission in our sources. We show the data/model ratio plots in Figure fig:sx_strength . All spectra show strong excess emission in the soft X-ray band below 1 keV.

Most of our higher-redshift sources show good consistency with the _ OX L_ 2500 relation of radio-quiet type 1 AGNs in Strateva+05 except for WISEA J033429. This object is significantly X-ray weak, with _ OX = _ OX,obs _ OX,exp =-0.50 from the relation, corresponding to an X-ray weakness f_ weak =10^ _ OX /0.3838 =20.1 . The X-ray weakness indicates the \ of WISEA J033429 estimated from the X-rays is underestimated, which is consistent with the discrepancy between the X-ray and the optical Eddington ratios ( _ Edd,X =-1.60 ; _ Edd,opt =-0.55 ) shown Table tab:optical-uv-xray . However, as this source also shows the strongest soft excess emission relative to the continuum ( 1 and 2 ), the X-weakness is likely to be intrinsic.

gmw_00004038.name: PG1543+489
gmw_00004038.overview: PG1543+489 is a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.00991] that shows no signs of variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0006315]. Its far-infrared emission is dominated by thermal radiation [arXiv:astro-ph/0006315], and it hosts a rapidly spinning supermassive black hole [arXiv:2305.00991].

sources.summary: The paper singles out PG1543+489 as the sample object with a blurred iron line, weak soft excess, very soft continuum, and evidence for a rapidly spinning black hole.

quotes.quote: PG1543+489 is the only source in our sample that shows a blurred iron line feature. By modeling the emission line with the relativistic line emission model relline , we would be able to probe the innermost region of the accretion disk. The results indicate a fast-spinning BH ( a_*>0.97 ). The emissivity profile shows a steep powerlaw ( q_ in >6 ) within a relatively small radius ( R_ br =2.1_ 0.3 ^ +0.7 ). This scenario indicates a strong illumination from the primary source onto the innermost disk region, where the relativistic effect can play a significant role in the outgoing reflection spectrum.

PG 1543+489 is the outlier in our sample. The soft excess strength is weak (lowest 1 , 2 , and 3 in the sample), while it shows a very soft powerlaw continuum. It is also one of the most massive and luminous sources in our sample and the NLS1s in GW+20.

sources.summary: The paper says PG 1543+489 has a steep sub-mm/far-IR slope indicating thermal far-IR emission, shows no variability, and has an observed IR SED broader than the fitted parabola.

quotes.quote: Two sources in the sample, PG 1543+489 and 3C 405, have a sub-mm/far-IR spectral index larger than 2.5. Their far-IR emission is therefore dominated by thermal radiation.
Six sources (PG 1718+481, PG 1100+772, 3C 405, PG 1543+489, B2 1721+34, and PG 2214+139) show no sign of variability.
The parabola is too narrow to satisfy the observed IR SED in a few cases, e.g., in 3C 405 and PG 1543+489.

gmw_00004039.name: SDSS J024651
gmw_00004039.overview: SDSS J024651 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.00991] classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2305.00991]. X-ray observations of the galaxy reveal a degeneracy between its soft-excess flux and its power-law continuum component [arXiv:2305.00991].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J024651 to illustrate degeneracy between the soft-excess and power-law continuum components, finding two statistically similar spectral solutions.

quotes.quote: To better illustrate the degeneracy, we plot the constraints on the flux of the bbody component from 0.5-10 keV and the powerlaw photon index for SDSS J024651 in Figure fig:e1346_cont . Although the confidence levels shown with the contours are consistent with the uncertainties in Table tab:baseline , the ``banana-shaped" contours clearly show the degeneracy between the soft excess flux and the photon index. Within 99 confidence level, two possible solutions are found to explain the data with similarly good fits : (1) =2.6 and F^ bb _ 0.5-10 keV =-13.2 (2) =2.0 and F^ bb _ 0.5-10 keV =-12.4 .

gmw_00004040.name: E1346+266
gmw_00004040.overview: E1346+266 is a Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of 0.917 [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2305.00991]. It features a compact core with faint diffuse emission [arXiv:1801.03519], a rapidly spinning supermassive black hole [arXiv:2305.00991], and a steep radio spectrum [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The most distant sample source is reclassified as steep-spectrum, appears compact with faint diffuse emission, and has properties reminiscent of high-star-formation sources.

quotes.quote: J1348+2622 SDSS J134834.28+262205.9 is another bona-fide F source, which we reclassify as steep-spectrum. It is the most distant source of the sample (z = 0.917), and in the map of Fig. fig:J1348p2622 it appears as a compact source with a deconvolved core size of 3.4 1.3 kpc and some faint diffuse emission toward South and East. The spectral indexes are all rather steep, of the order of unity, and the brightness temperature is low, 700 K. These characteristics seem to recall those of sources with high star formation rates. However, this is in contrast with the result of Caccianiga15 , who did not find any sign of strong starbursts using WISE colors.

sources.summary: The paper reports that E1346+266 provides stringent reflection-model constraints, indicating high black-hole spin and the only constrained inner-disk electron-density measurement in the sample.

quotes.quote: The most stringent constraints come from E1346+266 and PG 1543+489. Both sources indicate a rapidly spinning BH ( a_*>0.94 and a_*>0.81 , respectively).

The only source with constrained measurements of n_ e is E1346+266, which has the largest mass and highest redshift among our sources. In this source, about 95 of the energy in the disk is released to the corona, and the radiation-pressure dominated region is <2R_ s to explain the inferred high disk density.

gmw_00004041.name: SDSS J144800.15 + 404311.7
gmw_00004041.overview: SDSS J144800.15 + 404311.7 is a broad-absorption line quasar hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2402.07855]. Its asymmetric hydrogen-beta emission line profile is modeled as emission from an accretion disk viewed at a low angle [arXiv:2402.07855].

sources.summary: The paper reports an IRTF spectrum of SDSS J144800.15 + 404311.7 and models its unusual Balmer-line profile as face-on accretion-disk emission.

quotes.quote: The left inset panel shows the H line, which has a unusual shape in this object. It is not symmetric; it has an extended blue wing and a steeply rising blue side, with a gradual decline toward longer wavelengths. While red wings have been observed in Balmer lines from AGNs previously e.g., lamura09 , the steep blue side is rare. This profile is an example of line emission from an accretion disk viewed at a low angle from the normal e.g., Fig. \ 2 of ch89 .

In irtf we modeled the H line from SDSS J144800.15 + 404311.7. We found that it was adequately modeled using a diskline with an outer radius of 9500 gravitational radii. leighly22 estimated a log black hole mass of 8.55, corresponding to an outer radius equal to 0.16 parsec.

gmw_00004042.name: 26W20
gmw_00004042.overview: 26W20 is an elliptical radio galaxy located in the Abell 754 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9804197]. It features a variable, non-thermal X-ray core with BL Lac-like properties [arXiv:astro-ph/9804197], weak optical emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/9804197, arXiv:astro-ph/0212408], and a single jet or tail structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0212408].

sources.summary: The paper describes 26W20 as an Abell 754 radio galaxy with BL Lac-like core emission, variable nonthermal X-rays, elliptical optical morphology, weak emission lines, and a possible beamed jet.

quotes.quote: 26W20 , a radio galaxy located at a projected distance of 2.2 Mpc from the center of Abell 754 (z=0.054) exhibits core emission properties similar to those of a BL Lac object. New radio observations and VLA archival data show parsec scale features in the core ( L_ 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 ) with a flat spectrum. From observations with ROSAT, we demonstrate that the X-ray core emission (L_ x =3 10^ 43 ergs s ^ 1 ) is non-thermal and variable. An 18 increase in the X-ray luminosity was observed in two observations separated by five days. The optical morphology is that of a large, normal elliptical galaxy ( m_ v =15.6) and the optical spectrum lacks strong emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper considers 26W20 as a possible source of Abell 754 hard X-ray excess, describing it as a tailed radio galaxy with BL Lac-like X-ray properties.

quotes.quote: However, we cannot ignore the presence of the radio galaxy 26W20 in the field of view of the PDS, located at a distance of about 27 ' from the pointing, that could be responsible for the hard excess in A754. This source, discovered in the Westerbork radio survey by Harris (1980), shows a X-ray bright core similar to that of a BL Lac object but with weak emission lines and this makes 26W20 different from a typical BL Lac object. The source is preferred to be classified as tailed radio galaxy for the presence of a single jet or tail and being member of a small group of galaxies.

gmw_00004043.name: zC-488879
gmw_00004043.overview: Located at a redshift of approximately 1.47, zC-488879 is a star-forming main-sequence galaxy [arXiv:2302.00030]. Its carbon monoxide emission reveals a regularly rotating disk with a smooth spatial distribution, though the emission peaks of its different transitions vary across the galaxy [arXiv:2302.00030].

sources.summary: The paper presents zC-488879 as a z=1.47 main-sequence galaxy with smooth CO emission, differing CO line peaks, and regularly rotating disks in both CO transitions.

quotes.quote: The galaxy zC-488879 at z 1.47 Figure fig:Mosaic2 provides an overview of the emission-line data of zC-488879 at z 1.47 . Similarly to zC-400569, the CO distribution is smooth and does not show the large clumps that are often seen in rest-frame UV and H images of galaxies at cosmic noon e.g., Zanella2019 . The spatial distributions of CO( 3-2 ) and CO( 2-1 ) emission are in overall agreement, but the CO( 2-1 ) emission peaks near the galaxy center while the CO( 3-2 ) emission peaks toward the south. This may be possibly related to varying CO line excitation conditions in the galaxy. Both CO( 2-1 ) and CO( 3-2 ) lines indicate regularly rotating disks, as shown by velocity maps and PV diagrams.

gmw_00004044.name: Leo I
gmw_00004044.overview: Leo I is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:0708.2661]. Photometric observations reveal that the galaxy has an ellipsoidal projected spatial structure with core and tidal radii of 6.21 and 11.70 arcminutes, respectively [arXiv:0708.2661].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS crowded-field photometry to map Leo I, finding an ellipsoidal projected structure, King-model radii, no clear tidal debris, and dark-matter-dominated mass-to-light estimates.

quotes.quote: Improved photometry of SDSS crowded field images: Structure and dark matter content in the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Leo I V. Smol i Max-Planck-Institut f ur Astronomie, K onigstuhl 17, Heidelberg, D-69117, Germany D. Zucker Institute of Astronomy, University of Cambridge, Madingley Road, Cambridge CB3 0HA, UK E. F. Bell, M. G. Coleman, H. W. Rix, E. Schinnerer Max-Planck-Institut f ur Astronomie, K onigstuhl 17, Heidelberg, D-69117, Germany Z . Ivezi Department of Astronomy, University of Washington, Box 351580, Seattle, WA 98195-1580, USA A. Kniazev South African Astronomical Observatory, Observatory Road, Cape Town, South Africa We explore how well crowded field point-source photometry can be accomplished with Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data. For this purpose, we present a photometric pipeline based on \ schechter93 , and tuned for analyzing crowded-field images from the SDSS. Using Monte Carlo simulations we show that the completeness of source extraction is above 80 to an i band AB magnitude of 21 and a stellar surface density of 200 arcmin ^ 2 .

We apply our pipeline to an area of 3.55 \ around the dwarf spheroidal galaxy ( ) . Using the resulting multi-band ( , , i ) photometry we construct a high signal-to-noise star-count map of , utilizing an optimized filter in color-magnitude space. This filter reduces the foreground contamination by 80 and enhances the central stellar surface density contrast of the dwarf by a factor of 4 , making this study the deepest wide-field study of the \ \ based on accurate CCD photometry. We find that the projected spatial structure of \ is ellipsoidal. The best fitting empirical King model to the stellar-surface density profile yields core and tidal radii of (6.21 0.95)' and (11.70 0.87)' , respectively.

gmw_00004045.name: NGC 7796
gmw_00004045.overview: NGC 7796 is an old, massive, and isolated elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1411.7183, arXiv:1809.08964] that features a bright X-ray halo [arXiv:1809.08964]. It has a confirmed dwarf companion galaxy, NGC7796-DW1, which likely accreted cold gas from NGC 7796 to trigger its own star formation [arXiv:1809.08964].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7796 as an isolated elliptical, analyzing its globular cluster system, photometry, dynamics, dark-matter constraints, old stellar population, and dwarf companion with tidal features.

quotes.quote: We investigate the globular cluster system of the isolated elliptical NGC 7796, present new photometry of the galaxy, and use published kinematical data to constrain the dark matter content. Deep images in B and R, obtained with the VIsible MultiObject Spectrograph (VIMOS) at the VLT, form the data base. We performed photometry with DAOPHOT and constructed a spherical photometric model. We present isotropic and anisotropic Jeans-models and give a morphological description of the companion dwarf galaxy. The globular cluster system has about 2000 members, so it is not as rich as those of giant ellipticals in galaxy clusters with a comparable stellar mass, but richer than many cluster systems of other isolated ellipticals.

NGC 7796 is an old, massive isolated elliptical galaxy with no indications of later major star formation events as seen frequently in other isolated ellipticals. Its relatively rich globular cluster system shows that isolation does not always mean a poor cluster system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7796 as an isolated, old elliptical with a normal group-like globular-cluster, photometric, and X-ray profile, and as the likely source of cold gas accreted by its dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 7796 is an IE reda04 . With respect to richness of the globular cluster system, photometric properties, and X-ray properties, it resembles a normal, old elliptical galaxy in a group or cluster richtler15 , in contrast to many other IEs that show signatures of recent interactions and/or younger stellar populations salinas15 . vader94 list two companions, but only NGC7796-1 (we respect its nature as a dwarf galaxy and hereafter use NGC7796-DW1) can be confirmed as a real companion galaxy (see the remarks below) and is classified as dE2.

Therefore, excluding a merger, only one viable scenario remains: accretion of cold gas from the inner gaseous halo of NGC 7796, a galaxy this is old and X-ray bright. osullivan07 gives, using XMM, an abundance profile that shows nearly solar abundance in the central region and then declines modestly, but with uncertainties which still permit almost solar abundance at larger radii.

A plausible reservoir of gas with appropriately high metallicity is the gas of the X-ray halo in NGC7796. We therefore conclude that star formation in our dwarf has been triggered by accretion of cold gas that coexists with the hot X-ray gas.

gmw_00004046.name: NGC 7796-1
gmw_00004046.overview: NGC 7796-1 is an early-type dwarf companion galaxy [arXiv:1809.08964, arXiv:1411.7183] that exhibits very boxy isophotes, tidal tails, and multiple nuclei [arXiv:1411.7183]. Its main body is old and metal-poor [arXiv:1809.08964], but it contains younger, bluer central regions [arXiv:1809.08964] and an extended, ring-like emission structure [arXiv:1809.08964].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC7796-DW1 as an early-type transition dwarf with a tidal tail, boxy isophotes, young central regions, extended emission, and evidence for cold gas accretion.

quotes.quote: The isolated elliptical (IE) NGC 7796 is accompanied by an interesting early-type dwarf galaxy, named NGC7796-DW1. It exhibits a tidal tail, very boxy isophotes, and multiple nuclei or regions (A, B, and C) that are bluer than the bulk population of the galaxy, indicating a younger age.

We use the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) at the VLT to investigate NGC 7796-DW1. We extract characteristic spectra to which we apply the STARLIGHT population synthesis software to obtain ages and metallicities of the various population components of the galaxy. The galaxy's main body is old and metal-poor. A surprising result is the extended line emission in the galaxy, forming a ring-like structure with a projected diameter of 2.2 kpc.
The dwarf-dwarf merger scenario is excluded because of the missing metal-rich merger component. A viable alternative is gas accretion from a reservoir of cold, metal-rich gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7796-1 as a dwarf companion with tidal tails, multiple nuclei, boxy isophotes, large effective radius, and properties suggestive of a dwarf-dwarf merger.

quotes.quote: The dwarf companion galaxy NGC 7796-1 exhibits tidal tails, multiple nuclei, and very boxy isophotes. NGC 7796 is an old, massive isolated elliptical galaxy with no indications of later major star formation events as seen frequently in other isolated ellipticals. Its relatively rich globular cluster system shows that isolation does not always mean a poor cluster system. The properties of the dwarf companion might indicate a dwarf-dwarf merger.

We do not know for certain whether NGC7796-1 is a merger product, but its properties are very suggestive of this. In particular, its large effective radius might indicate the case (i) of cloet14 , where a massive progenitor grows with only a few infalling satellites. Future spectroscopic work will lend more clarity. If so, NGC7796 provides an even more convincing example than amorisco14 of the scale-free character of this process. Regarding brightness and the blue nucleus, NGC 7796-1 shows some similarity to NGC 205 with its blue nucleus and recent star formation monaco09 .

gmw_00004047.name: Sagittarius dwarf irregular galaxy
gmw_00004047.overview: The Sagittarius dwarf irregular galaxy is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy and a member of the NGC 7793 subgroup [arXiv:1211.0137]. It contains mixed-age stellar populations ranging from 10 million to several billion years old, dominated by intermediate-age stars [arXiv:1211.0137].

sources.summary: The paper derives SDIG’s resolved-star formation history, finding mixed-age stellar populations, centrally concentrated young stars, intermediate-age dominance, low metallicity, and recent enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: We study the resolved stellar populations and derive the star formation history of the SDIG, a gas-rich dwarf galaxy member of the NGC7793 subgroup in the Sculptor group of galaxies. We construct a color-magnitude diagram using archival observations from the HST/ACS in order to examine the stellar content of SDIG, as well as the spatial distribution of stars selected within different stellar evolutionary phases. We derive the star formation history of SDIG using a maximum-likelihood fit to the color-magnitude diagram.

The color-magnitude diagram shows that SDIG contains stars from 10 Myr to several Gyr old, as revealed from the main sequence, blue loop, luminous asymptotic giant branch, and red giant branch stars. The young stars with ages less than 250 Myr show a stellar spatial distribution confined to the central regions of SDIG, and additionally the young main sequence stars exhibit an off-center density peak. The intermediate-age and older stars as traced by the red giant branch stars are more spatially extended. SDIG is dominated by intermediate-age stars with an average age of 6.4 ^ +1.6 _ 1.4 Gyr. The average metallicity inferred from the CMD modelling is [ M/H ] 1.5 dex.

sources.summary: The paper studies extremely metal-poor stars in Sculptor, confirming five with [Fe/H] below -3 and finding their abundance patterns more similar to Milky Way halo stars than Sculptor's metal-rich population.

quotes.quote: We present abundances for seven stars in the (extremely) low-metallicity tail of the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy, from spectra taken with X-shooter on the ESO VLT. Targets were selected from the Ca II triplet (CaT) survey of the Dwarf Abundances and Radial Velocities Team (DART) using the latest calibration. Of the seven extremely metal-poor candidates, five stars are confirmed to be extremely metal-poor (i.e., [ Fe/H ] <-3 dex), with [ Fe/H ] =-3.47 0.07 for our most metal-poor star.

None of the seven stars is found to be carbon-rich. We estimate a 2--13 possibility of this being a pure chance effect, which could indicate a lower fraction of carbon-rich extremely metal-poor stars in Sculptor compared to the Milky Way halo. The [ /Fe ] ratios show a range from +0.5 to --0.5, a larger variation than seen in Galactic samples although typically consistent within 1--2 .

Overall, we conclude that the CaT lines are a successful metallicity indicator down to the extremely metal-poor regime and that the extremely metal-poor stars in the Sculptor dwarf galaxy are chemically more similar to their Milky Way halo equivalents than the more metal-rich population of stars.

sources.summary: The paper identifies new distant Sculptor members, extends the dwarf to 10 half-light radii, and interprets its outer metal-poor population and density-profile kink as evidence for tidal outskirts.

quotes.quote: A Bayesian algorithm is used to find new candidate members in the extreme outskirts of the Sculptor dwarf galaxy. Precise metallicities and radial velocities for two distant stars are measured from their spectra taken with the Gemini South GMOS spectrograph. The radial velocity, proper motion and metallicity of these targets are consistent with Sculptor membership. As a result, the known boundary of the Sculptor dwarf extends now out to an elliptical distance of 10 half-light radii, which corresponds to a projected physical distance of 3 kpc.

As reported in earlier work, the overall distribution of radial velocities and metallicities indicate the presence of a more spatially and kinematically dispersed metal-poor population that surrounds the more concentrated and colder metal-rich stars. Sculptor's density profile shows a "kink" in its logarithmic slope at a projected distance of 25 arcmin (620 pc), which we interpret as evidence that Galactic tides have helped to populate the distant outskirts of the dwarf. We discuss further ways to test and validate this tidal interpretation for the origin of these distant stars.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates claimed X-ray binaries in the Sculptor dwarf galaxy, describing it as old and metal-poor and concluding no robust associated X-ray sources remain.

quotes.quote: And we show that a suggested population of quiescent X-ray binaries in the Sculptor Dwarf Galaxy is mostly (perhaps entirely) background galaxies.

The Sculptor dwarf galaxy is old > 10 Gyrs, Monkiewicz99,Mapelli09 and metal-poor Mateo98 , with a total mass of possibly 1.4 10^7 \ Queloz95 , of which roughly 2 10^6 \ is stars. A satellite of the Milky Way, it is 86 5 kpc away Pietrzynski08 . Maccarone05 used 126 ks of Chandra time (in 21 exposures) to identify X-ray sources towards the Sculptor dwarf galaxy. They identified five moderately faint ( L_X 10^ 33 10^ 35 ergs s ^ 1 ) X-ray sources, and three slightly fainter sources, with optical sources, which they argue are on Sculptor's giant and horizontal branches.
Since we have no robust identifications of X-ray sources associated with the Sculptor dwarf, we do not attempt to calculate the X-ray emissivity of the Sculptor dwarf galaxy in this work.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sculptor's dwarf-spheroidal annihilation limits with Draco and Coma Berenices, showing how nuisance-parameter profiling changes its relative constraining power.

quotes.quote: To understand the impact of profiling over the selected nuisance parameters, in fig.  fig:sv_limits , we show the limits for Draco (left panel) and Sculptor (right panel). When profiling over the factor uncertainties only, case 2 , the limits weaken with respect to case 1 as expected, with the degradation depending on the width of the _ 10 J distribution. If, additionally, we profile also over background uncertainties, case 4 , in the case of the single dSph the limits further degrades. However, it is important to note that this degradation is not the same for all dSphs and that the ranking of the most constraining dSphs can be in principle altered by this procedure. This is indeed what we see happening when comparing Draco with, for example, Sculptor. In the case of profiling over factor only, Draco still provides stronger limits than Sculptor because the variation of the factor of the latter is not enough to drive the limits further down. On the other hand, when profiling also over background, at low masses Draco becomes less constraining. The reason is than in the first bin the deficit is less significative than for Sculptor, and the background distribution makes possible to accommodate for larger DM contributions.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sagittarius dwarf irregular galaxy as an abundance-matching test case, stating CDM overpredicts its rotation velocity while DGT matches the observed curve with a 3.28 K thermal bath.

quotes.quote: Sagittarius dwarf galaxy (SDIG) A classic example of the abundance matching mechanism limitation is the prediction of the rotation curve of Sagittarius dwarf irregular galaxy (SDIG) ferr12 , its rotation curve is shown in Fig. sagittarius . Dwarf Sagittarius has a measured mass is only 2.6 10^5 M_ sun . The M_ 200 =10^ 10 M_ sum condition of the abundance matching mechanism (solid black curve), predict a maximum velocity of rotation about ten times higher than the observed. Fig. sagittarius also shows the rotation curve (solid red curve) predicted by DGT, and it is seen to agree well with the observed curve. DGT predict a thermal bath temperature for Sagittarius of 3.28 K.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sculptor as a modeled dwarf spheroidal where lithium data show a Spite plateau, adopted metallicity distributions are metal-poor, and the model fit is good.

quotes.quote: Observations of extragalactic objects have confirmed the existence of the Spite plateau in the globular cluster M54 ( Mucciarelli2014 ), in Sculptor dwarf galaxy ( Hill2019 ) and in Centauri ( Monaco2010 ).

From the theoretical point of view we have modeled several dSphs: Sculptor, Fornax, Carina, Draco, Ursa Minor, Sextans and Sagittarius, plus two UfDs: Reticulum II and Bootes I. For all these galaxies we have adopted the metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] ) distribution functions (MDF) from SAGA database. All these objects are on average metal-poor as the stars in the Galactic halo, and their [ Fe/H ] extends from -5.0 to -1.0 dex.
The fit to the observational data in some cases is not perfect and it is mostly due to the lack of observational points, but in other cases such as Carina, Draco and Sculptor the fit is very good.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor as a nearby dwarf-galaxy test case for HubPUG, recovering a proper motion consistent with literature and dominated by Gaia stellar-PM uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Our MW satellite targets, Sculptor and Draco, have been chosen to verify the accuracy of this method for nearby fields. Sculptor and Draco are classical dwarfs ( M_ 10^7 M_ ) located at similar heliocentric distances (86 and 76 kpc; mcconnachie2012 ) and have been a prime focus of PM experiments, dating back to the mid-1990s ( sculptorFirstPM for Sculptor; dracoFirstPM for Draco). In the last two decades, the precision of these measurements has been immensely advanced with the use of \ data e.g., piatek+2006, pryor+2015, Sohn+2017, Massari+18, Massari+20 , observations from the Subaru Suprime-Cam for Draco, casetiidinescu+2016 , and with \ stellar PM measurements .

For Sculptor, we find the values _ ^* _ ^ * _ Sculptor = 0.0775 ^ 0.0180 _ 0.0190 0.1184 ^ 0.0148 _ 0.0196 mas yr^ 1 . These values agree within approximately 2.7 ( _ ^* ) and 0.6 ( _ ) with and within 0.3 and 1.7 with , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper studies APOGEE near-infrared abundances for Sculptor dwarf galaxy stars, modeling Scl chemical evolution, radial gradients, and a top-light IMF.

quotes.quote: In this work, we investigate up to 13 elements in 43 giant stars of the Sculptor dwarf galaxy (Scl) using high-signal-to-noise-ratio near-infrared (NIR) APOGEE spectra. Thanks to the strong feature lines in the NIR, we were able to determine high-resolution O, Si, and Al abundances for a large group of sample stars for the first time in Scl. By comparing the [ /Fe ] (i.e., O, Mg, Si, Ca, and Ti) of the stars in Scl, Sagittarius, and the MW, we confirm the general trend that less massive galaxies tend to show lower [ /Fe ] . The low [ Al/Fe ] ( 0.5 ) in Scl demonstrates the value of this ratio as a discriminator with which to identify stars born in dwarf galaxies (from MW field stars). A chemical-evolution model suggests that Scl has a top-light initial mass function (IMF), with a high-mass IMF power index of 2.7 , and a minimum Type Ia supernovae delay time of 100 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper uses RRd stars to derive Sculptor distance and metallicity, reporting individual RRd-based measurements, about 1.8 percent distance accuracy, and 0.2 dex metallicity dispersion.

quotes.quote: We measure the distances and metallicities of four globular clusters (IC 4499, M15, M3, and M68) and a dwarf galaxy (Sculptor) based on RRd stars with Gaia data. Figure 2 shows the derived metallicities and distance moduli. The different colored dots indicate individual metallicity and distance modulus determinations based on RRd stars in M15 (red), IC 4499 (black), Sculptor (green), M3 (magenta), and M68 (blue).

The mean values of RRd stars in five targets are shown as open circles with 1 internal errors. For dwarf galaxy Sculptor, the distance accuracy is about 1.8 , and the metallicity dispersion is 0.2 dex.

sources.summary: The paper compares Sculptor Dwarf stellar abundance scaling with the Milky Way, finding a similar form but a lower iron breakpoint and steeper decline tied to its enrichment history.

quotes.quote: Figure fig9 shows the same data for [ Mg/Fe ] vs [ Fe/H ] as in Figure fig2 but including data for the Sculptor Dwarf and the LMC bar and disk. The figure shows the Sculptor Dwarf (red circles), the LMC (blue circles) and the Milky Way (grey circles). The Sculptor stars exhibit same behaviour as the Milky Way stars, but with a lower [ Fe/H ] breakpoint and steeper drop. This is to be expected, as there would have been fewer core-collapse supernovae in the Sculptor Dwarf before the Type 1a supernovae started enriching iron, compared to the Milky Way.
The scaling behaviour in each galaxy is similar in form, suggesting a universal process, but one where the break point and ``zero point'' depend on the galaxy, as described by 1993ASPC...48..727W .

sources.summary: The paper finds two point sources in ESO349-G031, a CMD with upper-AGB and main-sequence populations, and places this IBm galaxy 570 kpc from NGC300.

quotes.quote: ESO349-G031 Only two point sources, one centered on the galaxy and one at 25 or 390 pc projected distance (at D = 3.2 Mpc) to the south-east are detected. The CMD also has clear upper-AGB and main sequence population karachentsev2006 . This IBm galaxy is situated 570 200 kpc (3D) from NGC300.

gmw_00004048.name: MACS0940
gmw_00004048.overview: MACS0940 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha halo galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.03 [arXiv:1909.01692]. Gravitationally lensed by a foreground cluster, its multiple images form an almost complete Einstein ring that exhibits strong line-profile variations [arXiv:1909.01692].

sources.summary: The paper presents MACS0940 as a newly discovered z=4.03 lensed Lyman-alpha halo whose multiple images form an almost complete Einstein ring and show strong line-profile variations.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a detailed analysis of such spatial and spectral variations in two highly magnified LAHs: a z=3.5 halo, previously presented by Patricio , found in galaxy cluster SMACS J2031.8-2046 (hereafter SMACS2031) and another newly discovered z=4.03 halo behind the lensing cluster MACS J0940.9+0744 (hereafter MACS0940). All distances are physical. We adopt a cold dark matter cosmology with _ = 0.7 , _m = 0.3 and H_0=70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . The two selected Lyman- emitters were known to be at z>3.5 , highly magnified by galaxy clusters SMACS2031 Richard15 and MACS0940 Nicha .

The MACS0940 galaxy produces four multiple images (labeled 1.1 to 1.4, Fig. fig:pixels ) forming a spectacular, almost-complete, Einstein ring of 10" radius in Lyman- (Fig. fig:pixels ), covering 80 arcsec ^2 in the image plane. We end up with 130 and 123 source plane bins for SMACS2031 and MACS0940 respectively. We have characterised the Lyman- line properties in the haloes out to 10 kiloparsec (kpc) ( 2.1 r_h ) in SMACS2031 and 10 kpc ( 2.5 r_h ) in MACS0940. MACS0940 presents stronger variations, with 60 km s ^ 1 in peak shift and 60 km s ^ 1 in FWHM.

gmw_00004049.name: G1
gmw_00004049.overview: G1 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.017 [arXiv:2311.10815]. It is identified as the most likely host galaxy of the fast radio burst FRB 20220610A and shows signs of tidal interaction with neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2311.10815].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the most likely host of FRB 20220610A, at z=1.017, with high PATH association probability, star-forming stellar population, and measured sky position.

quotes.quote: We label these sources numerically as 1-7, where G1 is the source coincident with the FRB ( 68 confidence) and is clearly detected in both HST filters. G2-G7 follow clockwise from G1 (Figure fig:imaging ). We note that all sources are clearly extended with respect to the instrumental PSF, indicating that they are galaxies, except for G7, which is impossible to distinguish from a point-like source. We note that G3 is not detected in the F606W imaging, signifying that it is comparatively redder. We further note the presence of faint extended emission between G1 and G5 in both filters, possibly indicating a stream or sign of tidal interaction.

G1, the closest galaxy to the FRB, has P(O x) = 0.87 . G6 is the next closest in proximity and has P(O x) = 0.11 . All remaining galaxies have P(O x) < 0.016 . We thus conclude that G1 is the most likely host of FRB 20220610A and present the P(O x) for each of the seven galaxies in Table tab:host/gal_props . With Source Extractor , we derive a position for G1 of R.A.= 23 24 17.573 , decl.= 33 30 49.725 (J2000) in the F160W filter.

The best-fit model for G1 has a stellar mass of log(M _* /M _ ) = 9.69 0.11, a mass-weighted age of t_ m =2.60^ +0.61 _ 0.91 Gyr, and a star formation rate integrated over the past 100 Myr of SFR _ 0-100 Myr =1.67^ +2.41 _ 0.95 M _ yr ^ 1 (68 uncertainties).

gmw_00004050.name: G2
