gmw_00004050.overview: G2 is an extended galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.017, determined from its [O II] doublet emission [arXiv:2311.10815]. Photometric modeling of the galaxy in the F606W filter reveals a structure that is best fit by two distinct components [arXiv:2311.10815].

sources.summary: The paper treats G3 as an extended source that is redder than the others, absent in F606W, possibly at z 0.5-2.7, but too weak for further inference.

quotes.quote: G2-G7 follow clockwise from G1 (Figure fig:imaging ). We note that all sources are clearly extended with respect to the instrumental PSF, indicating that they are galaxies, except for G7, which is impossible to distinguish from a point-like source. We note that G3 is not detected in the F606W imaging, signifying that it is comparatively redder.

The lack of detection of G3 in F606W, coupled with the detection in F160W, is intriguing in the context of a high-redshift origin. Indeed, if we interpret the red color to be attributed to the 4000 \ break, this results in a redshift range of z 0.5-2.7 . However, with present data we cannot rule out the possibility that the reddening is due to a combination of dust extinction or an older stellar population at a different redshift.

sources.summary: The paper resolves G2 as an extended galaxy near the FRB field, establishes its redshift at z=1.017 from [O II], and models it with two F606W components.

quotes.quote: G2-G7 follow clockwise from G1 (Figure fig:imaging ). We note that all sources are clearly extended with respect to the instrumental PSF, indicating that they are galaxies, except for G7, which is impossible to distinguish from a point-like source. However, based on the [ O II ] doublet, we establish the redshifts of G1 and G2 at z=1.017 .

We then fit the remaining galaxies in each filter with single S ersic profiles, with the exception of G2 in the F606W image which is similarly modeled as two components to improve the residual. We present the results from the Galfit analysis, including the values for n and r_e , in Table tab:host/gal_props .

gmw_00004051.name: G6
gmw_00004051.overview: The G6 system, which consists of components A, B, and C, is a high-redshift system with a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:2210.04680, arXiv:2311.10815]. Its morphology is suggestive of two galaxies interacting or merging, featuring a total stellar mass of about 10^10 solar masses and a star formation rate of 0.4 solar masses per year [arXiv:2210.04680].

sources.summary: The paper identifies components A, B and C as the FRB host system, measuring a shared redshift, blue colors, mass, star formation, and likely interaction or merger morphology.

quotes.quote: Simultaneous optical and infrared spectroscopy of components A, B and C (Figure 2) with the Very Large Telescope (VLT) resulted in the detection of multiple emission lines, which yield a redshift for the host system of . A complete summary of properties of the host can be found in Table 1. While the complex has a total stellar mass and star formation history comparable to that of nearby FRB hosts , the morphology is markedly different. The total mass is measured to be approximately 10^ 10 solar masses and we estimate the current star formation rate to be 0.4 solar masses per year . The presence of two bright clumps A and C (separated by a distance of 16 kpc), and the diffuse feature B between them, is suggestive of two galaxies interacting or merging.

The spectra in Figure fig:vlt_spec indicate that components A and C are consistent in redshift to 0.01 , while component B is blueshifted by an amount equivalent to a velocity difference of just 150 km s ^ 1 . With a projected separation of 16 kpc between compact components A and C, it is conceivable that they represent the two distinct nuclei of a merging or interacting system, while B is the gaseous tail linking them and currently drawn out towards the observer. The measured (R-K_ s ) colors for the individual clumps, as well as for the host system as a whole ( 1.70 0.18 ) are unusually blue compared to those for field galaxies at redshifts around 1 .

sources.summary: The paper finds G6 is the second most likely FRB host, with probable z~1 emission, four-band photometry, and Prospector estimates of redshift and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: G1, the closest galaxy to the FRB, has P(O x) = 0.87 . G6 is the next closest in proximity and has P(O x) = 0.11 . All remaining galaxies have P(O x) < 0.016 . We note in the less probable case (by a factor of 8 ) that G6 is the true host, the photometric redshift of G6 is consistent within uncertainties with the spectroscopic redshift of G1 (see Section sec:SPP-modeling ). Moreover, follow-up spectroscopy of G6 indicates a redshift consistent with z 1 (see below).

However, for G6, the combination of HST data and detections recovered from ground-based imaging results in four photometric measurements (of the nine bands), enabling a Prospector fit to estimate its redshift (and additionally, derive its stellar population properties). This is particularly relevant as it is the next most likely host of FRB 20220610A ( P(O x) = 0.11 ). We initialize the G6 model using the four detections and five upper limits with redshift as a free, uniform prior between z=0 and z=3 . The best-fit model suggests a galaxy with redshift z=0.72^ +0.92 _ 0.39 and log(M _* /M _ ) =8.71^ +0.58 _ 0.57 .

gmw_00004052.name: [ PCB2012 ]  2882
gmw_00004052.overview: [PCB2012] 2882 is a gravitationally lensed spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.99 [arXiv:1509.09204]. It is a star-forming galaxy with a high dust mass that likely accreted in the dense phases of its interstellar medium [arXiv:1509.09204].

sources.summary: The paper identifies [PCB2012] 2882 as the NOEMA counterpart to a 2 mm source, modeling it as a lensed z=0.99 spiral with high star formation and dust mass.

quotes.quote: In this paper we show that the NOEMA observations associate the 2 mm source with [ PCB2012 ]  2882 [ PCB2012 ] 2882 is the NED-searchable name for this source. , source number 2882 in the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) Cluster Lensing and Supernova (CLASH) catalog of MACS J1149.6+2223. This source, hereafter referred to as , is a gravitationally lensed spiral galaxy at z=0.99 . We combine the GISMO 2 mm and NOEMA 1.3 mm fluxes with other (rest frame) UV to far-IR observations to construct the full spectral energy distribution (SED) of this galaxy, and derive its star formation history, and stellar and interstellar dust content.

The current star formation rate of the galaxy is 54 ^ 1   , and its dust mass is about 5 10^7   , where is the lensing magnification factor for this source, which has a mean value of 2.7. The inferred dust mass is higher than the maximum dust mass that can be produced by core collapse supernovae (CCSN) and evolved AGB stars. As with many other star forming galaxies, most of the dust mass in \ must have been accreted in the dense phases of the ISM.

gmw_00004053.name: G1
gmw_00004053.overview: The galaxy G1 is located at a redshift of approximately 0.78 [arXiv:1607.00382]. It is potentially part of a small galaxy group with up to four spectroscopically identified members [arXiv:1607.00382].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the strongest nearby perturber of the lens field, measuring it at z about 0.78 and requiring explicit multi-plane treatment in lens models.

quotes.quote: previously reported a redshift z = 0.4188 for G3 as well as Morgan2005 , and z = 0.7818 for G1. Those measurements are statistically compatible with ours. The most important perturber (see Sect.  sec:model ), the galaxy G1, lies in the background of the lens at z 0.78 . It is potentially part of a small galaxy group of up to 4 spectroscopically identified members, including the two nearby galaxies G2 and G5.

The largest flexion shift is found for G1 for which we get _3 x( G1 ) 8 10^ 4 arcseconds. This motivates the explicit inclusion of this galaxy in all the lens models of presented in the companion Paper IV (Wong et al., submitted).

gmw_00004054.name: G3
gmw_00004054.overview: G3 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.419 [arXiv:1607.00382]. It acts as the second most important source of perturbation to the local gravitational potential, producing a flexion shift large enough to be included in lens models [arXiv:1607.00382].

sources.summary: The paper measures G3 near z=0.419 and describes it as the second most important perturbing galaxy, with a flexion shift large enough for lens-model tests.

quotes.quote: previously reported a redshift z = 0.4188 for G3 as well as Morgan2005 , and z = 0.7818 for G1. Those measurements are statistically compatible with ours.
The galaxy G3, at z 0.419, and located at 8 6 W-N-W from , is the second most important source of perturbation of the gravitational potential after G1 (Sect.  sec:model ).
The two galaxies G3 and G4 are also found to have flexion shifts close to or above 10^ 4 arcseconds such that they are also included in one of the lens models presented in Paper IV.

gmw_00004055.name: G4
gmw_00004055.overview: The galaxy G4 is located approximately 9 arcseconds northwest of a central lensing galaxy [arXiv:1607.00382]. It resides at a redshift of 0.4568 within a larger group of galaxies in the direct lens environment [arXiv:1607.00382].

sources.summary: The paper places G4 in the direct lens environment at the lens redshift and finds its flexion shift significant enough for inclusion in lens-model systematic tests.

quotes.quote: The galaxy G4 located 9 N-W of the lensing galaxy, is in the direct environment of the lens, and part of a larger group of galaxies at the lens redshift (see Sect.  subsec:groups ).

The redshifts of the five brightest galaxies that fall within 12 of the lens (G1 - G5, with i [ 19.9, 22.1 ] mag), are measured to be z_ G1 =0.7821, z_ G2 =0.7806, z_ G3 =0.4191, z_ G4 =0.4568, z_ G5 =0.7792 , with a typical random uncertainty of _z( ran) 0.0002 , and a possible systematic uncertainty _z( sys) 0.0004 .
The two galaxies G3 and G4 are also found to have flexion shifts close to or above 10^ 4 arcseconds such that they are also included in one of the lens models presented in Paper IV.

gmw_00004056.name: HS0822+3542
gmw_00004056.overview: HS0822+3542 is a metal-poor compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1605.06152, arXiv:2206.04709] that is carbon-enhanced for its low oxygen abundance [arXiv:1605.06152]. The galaxy consists of a blue clump where major star formation occurs, accompanied by a diffuse, elongated tail [arXiv:2206.04709].

sources.summary: The paper treats J082555 as the strongest C/O outlier in its compact dwarf sample, remeasuring its oxygen abundance with MMT spectra and finding it carbon-enhanced for its low metallicity.

quotes.quote: The most extreme outlier from the G95 relationship in Figure fig6a is J082555. We further investigated this target with respect to the C/O relationship by remeasuring the dependent variable: 12+log(O/H). For this purpose, we obtained follow up MMT optical spectra, as described in Appendix A3 .

At the low redshift of J082555, the [ ion O 2 ] 3727,3729 lines are not included in the SDSS spectrum, which prompted further investigation. We find that the SDSS spectrophotometry is consistent with our MMT observations, confirming the low oxygen abundance of J082555. At this oxygen abundance, J082555 appears to be significantly carbon enhanced relative to other metal-poor dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes HS0822+3542 as a blue-clump EMPG with a diffuse tail, major star formation in the clump, weak velocity gradient, and high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: 4 HS0822+3542 : HS0822+3542 consists of a blue clump (cyan arrow) and a very diffuse EMPG tail elongated from southeast to northwest (white arrow) . The H map indicates that the major star formation occurs in the blue clump. We find a very weak velocity gradient of 5 km s ^ 1 in the blue clump from north to south. HS0822+3542 shows a high velocity dispersion of 20 km s ^ 1 , which is comparable to the previous observations Moiseev2010 .

gmw_00004057.name: J2115 1734
gmw_00004057.overview: J2115 1734 is a local dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2405.01823] classified as an extremely metal-poor galaxy [arXiv:2206.04709]. The galaxy consists of a star-forming blue clump and a redshifted northeastern tail [arXiv:2206.04709].

sources.summary: The paper presents J2115 1734 as an EMPG with a blue clump and redshifted northeastern tail, high dispersion from two components, and a weak clump velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: 6 J2115 1734 : J2115 1734 consists of a blue clump (cyan arrow) and a north-east EMPG tail (white arrow) . The majority of star formation is likely to occur in the blue clump. The EMPG tail is redshifted by 40 km s ^ 1 with respect to the blue clump. Although the observed velocity field continuously changes between the blue clump and the EMPG tail, J2115 1734 shows a relatively high velocity dispersion of 40 km s ^ 1 originating from 2 different velocity components. The blue clump itself has a weak velocity gradient of 20 km s ^ 1 from northeast to southwest with a velocity dispersion of 30 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses J2115-1734 as a local dwarf-galaxy comparison, showing flux ratios, probability distributions, typical y+ values, and modified MCMC analyses.

quotes.quote: The calculated flux ratios for one out of the 68 local galaxies, J2115-1734, are presented in Table tab:flux .

The probability distribution of one of the local galaxies, J2115-1734, is also shown in Figure fig:mcmc . The y^ + value for J2115-1734 is comparable to the typical values of 0.08, indicating that the limited number of the input emission lines is not the cause of the large y^ + in the high- z galaxies.
We also conduct the modified MCMC analyses in the same manner for one of the local dwarf galaxies, J2115-1734 (Figure fig:mcmc_2 ).

gmw_00004058.name: AGC 242019
gmw_00004058.overview: AGC 242019 is a gas-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy [arXiv:2211.07215, arXiv:2208.12495] that is believed to host a cuspy dark matter halo [arXiv:2201.12852]. The galaxy has a neutral hydrogen disk with a thickness comparable to that of dwarf irregular galaxies [arXiv:2208.12495].

sources.summary: The paper examines AGC 242019 as a UDG believed to host a cuspy halo, finding tension with NLG at the innermost point and disagreement with MOND.

quotes.quote: AGC 242019 AGC 242019 is a UDG that is believed to host a cuspy dark matter halo . As we already discussed, NLG does not lead to cuspy effective dark matter halos. Therefore, it is natural to expect that there would be conflict between NLG and observational data for AGC 242019. The observed gas and star surface densities of AGC 242019 are shown in the middle panel of the first row in Fig. AGC114905 .

For NLG, the large ^2/ dof =14.17 is due to the innermost data point close to the center; indeed, ignoring this data point and repeating the MCMC simulations, we find ^2/ dof = 3.78 . The effective dark matter distribution is shown in the middle panel of the third row. Finally, let us mention that the MONDian rotation curve is shown in the middle panel of the second row. Clearly, MOND's rotation curve disagrees with the observational data of AGC 242019 .

sources.summary: The paper includes AGC 242019 as an HI-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy whose low-density regions make the KS law deviate while the extended-Schmidt law remains unbroken.

quotes.quote: To study the ES law in volume densities, we select a sample of 11 spiral galaxies, 13 dwarf galaxies and one HI-rich UDG AGC 242019, all of which have available data of atomic gas, molecular gas, stellar mass, and SFR.

As AGC 242019 has high S/N HI 21 cm observations from the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) with rotation curve and dark matter halo profile well measured by , we include it in our sample too. We also ignore the molecular gas in AGC 242019 because UDGs have little molecular gas Wang2020 .

For AGC 242019, the median offsets from surface-density and volumetric KS law reaches as high as 0.64 dex and 0.59 dex, respectively. The median offsets of these objects in the KS law reduce when we convert the surface densities to volume densities, indicating that the projection effects do contribute to the breakdown of surface-density KS law at low-density end to some level. However, based on our results, the deviation of low-density regions from KS law is not entirely caused by the projection effect. Most of the large deviations occur at regions with larger _ gas (compared to _ SFR _ gas ) but much smaller _ star (compared to _ SFR _ star ). Unlike the KS law, surface-density and volumetric ES law holds unbroken in dwarf galaxies and even AGC 242019, implying that the stellar mass density plays an significant role in regulating the star formation process in low-density environments.

sources.summary: The paper models AGC 242019 as a gas-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy, measuring its H I scale height and arguing its disk thickness resembles dwarf irregulars.

quotes.quote: In this study, we measure the radial profile of the H i scale height ( h_ g ) and flaring angle ( h_ g /R ) of AGC 242019 through the joint Poisson-Boltzmann equation based on its well spatially-resolved HI gas maps. The mean H i scale height of AGC 242019 is h_ g 537.15 89.4 pc, and the mean flaring angle is h_ g /R 0.19 0.03 . As a comparison, we also derive the disk thickness for a sample of 14 dwarf irregulars. It is found that the H i disk of AGC 242019 has comparable thickness to dwarfs. This suggests that AGC 242019 is unlikely to experience much stronger stellar feedback than dwarf galaxies, which otherwise leads to a thicker disk for this galaxy.

gmw_00004059.name: SMM J02396
gmw_00004059.overview: SMM J02396 is a high-redshift ring galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.062 [arXiv:0910.5267]. The galaxy is gravitationally lensed by the cluster Abell 370 with a magnification factor of 2.5, and it has been detected in CO(3-2) emission [arXiv:0910.5267].

sources.summary: The paper detects SMM J02396 at 350 microns, describes it as a lensed ring galaxy with CO emission, and derives modest intrinsic far-infrared luminosity and star formation rate.

quotes.quote: SMM J02396 was detected in CO(3--2) emission by greve05 using the IRAM Interferometer. The source is a ring galaxy identified by soucail99 from HST images. They measured the redshift of z = 1.062 and determined the magnification factor by the cluster Abell 370 to be = 2.5 . smail02 refer to the source as J02399-0134, rather than J02396-0134 as it has come to be labeled.

gmw_00004060.name: RX J0911.4
gmw_00004060.overview: RX J0911.4 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:0910.5267] that is classified as a mini-broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0910.5267]. The system is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.8 [arXiv:0910.5267].

sources.summary: The paper treats RX J0911.4 as a strongly lensed, radio-quiet mini-BAL quasar detected in CO and at 350 microns, with derived dust and star-formation properties.

quotes.quote: RX J0911.4 is a ROSAT source identified as a mini-broad absorption line quasar by bade97 . The quasar is strongly lensed ( =22 ) by a galaxy at z = 0.8 kneib00 . RX0911.4 was detected in CO(3--2) at the Owens Valley Radio Observatory by hainline04 . The quasar is radio quiet.

gmw_00004061.name: SDSS0338
gmw_00004061.overview: SDSS0338 is a high-redshift quasar at z ~ 5 [arXiv:0707.3335, arXiv:0910.5267] located in a dense environment near another fainter quasar [arXiv:1304.3726]. Its host galaxy is a submillimeter-bright starburst [arXiv:1304.3726] containing a compact molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:0707.3335] but lacking radio continuum detection [arXiv:0910.5267].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO in the z=5.0267 QSO host SDSS J033829.31+002156.3, inferring compact molecular gas, a high gas reservoir, and an elevated black-hole-to-bulge mass ratio.

quotes.quote: CO (5--4) is detected in SDSS J033829.31+002156.3 at z=5.0267, placing it amongst the most distant sources detected in CO. The CO emission is unresolved with a beam size of 1'' , implying that the molecular gas is contained within a compact region, less than 3 kpc in radius. We infer an upper limit on the dynamical mass of the CO emitting region of 3 10^ 10 M_ / (i)^2 .

The comparison with the Black Hole mass inferred from near-IR data suggests that the BH--to--bulge mass ratio in this galaxy is significantly higher than in local galaxies. From the CO luminosity we infer a mass reservoir of molecular gas as high as M(H_2)=2.4 10^ 10 M_ , implying that the molecular gas accounts for a significant fraction of the dynamical mass. When compared to the star formation rate derived from the far-IR luminosity, we infer a very short gas exhaustion timescale ( 10^7 years), comparable to the dynamical timescale.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS0338 as a z=5.03 quasar with CO emission, no radio continuum detection, and a 350 micron flux consistent with earlier measurements.

quotes.quote: SDSS 0338 is a high-redshift ( z = 5.03 ) quasar discovered by fan99 . The CO(5--4) line was detected with the IRAM Interferometer by maiolino07 . The radio continuum has not been detected. This source was previously detected at 350 \ by wang08sharc2 , with S_ 350 m = 17.7 4.4 mJy. Our value of 29 10 mJy is consistent within the uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper presents QSO J0338+0021 as a very luminous z~5 quasar in a dense LBG environment, with a nearby fainter QSO and a submillimeter-bright, strongly star-forming host galaxy.

quotes.quote: Although not taken into account when this QSO field was selected, Djorgovski03 discovered a second fainter QSO (RD657) 3' away from SDSS J0338+0021 with a very similar redshift ( z=5.02 for J0338, z=4.96 for RD657). They argued that this pair marked a possible large-scale structure or protocluster.

Therefore, it is reasonable to assume that the occurrence of QSO pairs on this scale is infrequent. Given the rarity of the QSO phenomenon, a rich environment clearly raises the probability of finding two QSOs in close proximity.

QSO J0338+0021 is also a strong mm/submm source with continuum detections from 350 m to 1.2 mm, and detections of CO(5-4), [ NII ] and [ CII ] line emission summarised in Wu09 , indicating that the host galaxy is undergoing a strong ( 2500 M _ yr ^ 1 ) starburst.

sources.summary: The paper includes J0338+0021 as a z~6 quasar host observed with PdBI/NOEMA, deriving CO-line, dust-continuum, infrared-luminosity, and molecular-gas properties for the host sample.

quotes.quote: J0338+0021 was observed on August 1, and September 28, 2008 (program ID: S052), using the narrow-band 2 mm receiver, and again in July 2013 (program ID: X04D) using WideX. The sources 3C454.3 and MWC349 were observed for bandpass and flux calibration. We observed J0406+066 as amplitude and phase calibrator. The final cube includes 7200 visibilities, corresponding to 9.0 h (5-antenna equivalent).
For the quasars J0338+0021, J2054--0005, and J1319+0950, we instead adopt the values published in wang13 and leipski14 , based on the fits of their Spectral Energy Distributions.

gmw_00004062.name: MG0751
gmw_00004062.overview: MG0751 is a high-redshift quasar that is highly lensed with a magnification factor of 16.6 [arXiv:0910.5267]. The galaxy has been detected in carbon monoxide emission and represents a weak source of 350-micron emission [arXiv:0910.5267].

sources.summary: The paper treats MG0751 as a highly lensed CO-emitting quasar and reports it as the weakest 350 micron detection in the sample.

quotes.quote: MG0751 is a quasar detected in CO(4--3) emission by barvainis02a using the IRAM Interferometer. The quasar is highly lensed, with magnification of 16.6 barvainis02b . This is our weakest detection, with a S/N ratio of only 2.3, weaker than the 450 \ detection.

gmw_00004063.name: HCG90-DW2 and HCG90-DW3
gmw_00004063.overview: HCG90-DW2 and HCG90-DW3 are modeled as a likely close dwarf galaxy pair [arXiv:1608.05082]. Alternatively, they may represent a single, extended dwarf irregular system [arXiv:1608.05082].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCG90-DW4 as the only nucleated dwarf candidate, measuring a compact central object whose properties may indicate a nuclear stellar disc.

quotes.quote: Of the five new dwarf candidates, HCG90-DW4 represents the sole example of a nucleated dwarf galaxy (dE,N; see Fig. fig:dwarf4 ). Its spheroid component has r_ eff 750 800 pc (see Table table:info ). Fitting the central object (DW4n) with a S ersic profile yields an n 0.6 0.7 index, with r_ eff 46 63 pc and a magnitude of M_ U, 0 = 7.42 mag.

The spheroid component of HCG90-DW4 appears to host a central compact object (DW4n) with r_ eff 46 63 pc. However, its properties do not agree with those of a nuclear star cluster, which are generally about 5 10 more compact and brighter than DW4n e.g. georgiev14 . Its size is marginally resolved by our data. This is illustrated by its radial profile in Fig. fig:dwarf4 , which is slightly more extended than a stellar PSF. An alternative explanation is that DW4n could represent a so-called nuclear stellar disc (NSD).

sources.summary: The paper models HCG90-DW2 and HCG90-DW3 as a likely close LSB dwarf pair, while noting an irregular single-dwarf interpretation remains possible.

quotes.quote: For the dwarf pair HCG90-DW2 and HCG90-DW3, a single-component S ersic profile did not result in a stable solution. However, we find that a two-component profile with different centers rapidly converges to a stable solution (see center panel in Fig. fig:dwarf_model ). We thus consider the two components separately. While both components of HCG90-DW2 and -DW3 are detected and fit in both filters, the saturation of a nearby star prohibits a robust estimation of their structural parameters in the I band.

Our results imply that we are looking either at a dwarf pair (potentially in projection) or an extended dwarf irregular system. Given that we do not detect increased stochasticity in our U band image that would indicate star-forming regions, with the current information we consider the dwarf pair scenario most likely.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG90-DW5 as the nearest, brightest, and bluest dwarf candidate near the group core, possibly a tidal dwarf from interacting giants.

quotes.quote: Finally, while contamination of HCG90-DW5 by foreground sources makes the structural parameter estimation of its spheroid component challenging, constructing a good mask for the surrounding objects allowed us to find a stable solution that is consistent across both filters (see Fig. fig:dwarf_model ).

The nearest dwarf to the core of the compact group is HCG90-DW5 with potentially interesting properties. It is the brightest candidate with the bluest (U-I)_0 = 1.14 mag colour index. Given its stellar population properties (see discussion above) and considering its close projected proximity to the three interacting giants, we suggest that this object may be a TDG arising from the tidal debris of the interacting giants. Noting the above, this interpretation is hampered by the contamination of DW5's light profile and will require careful follow-up observations to confirm.

gmw_00004064.name: z = 0.12118 galaxy
aliases_external: SDSS galaxy at z=0.12118 near PG 1424+240
gmw_00004064.overview: The z = 0.12118 galaxy is a 1.4L* system with an extended morphology and an absorption-line dominated spectrum [arXiv:2001.10558]. Its u-r color is consistent with a late-type E/S0 or Sa classification, and it has a low star formation rate constrained to 0.05 solar masses per year [arXiv:2001.10558].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=0.12118 SDSS galaxy as a 1.4L* system near the absorber, estimating its virial radius, low star formation, and possible CGM connection.

quotes.quote: The z = 0.12118 galaxy identified by SDSS as closest to the absorber is a 1.4L^* galaxy at an impact parameter of 200 kpc, and a velocity offset, v = -11 11 with reference to the absorber redshift. Using the scaling relation R_ vir = 250 (L/L^*)^ 0.2 kpc ( prochaska_probing_2011 ), we estimate a halo virial radius of R_ vir = 267 kpc for this galaxy. The luminosity scaling relationship of stocke_absorption-line_2014 , log R _ vir = 2.257 + 0.318C + 0.018 C^ 2 0.005 C^ 3 , where C = log (L/L*), also yields a comparable halo radius of R_ vir = 201 kpc for this galaxy, meaning that the absorber is seemingly located at the fringes of the virial halo of the galaxy.

This galaxy has an extended morphology, with an absorption line dominated spectrum (shown in Figure ), suggesting little ongoing star formation. The u - = 2.88 color of the galaxy is also consistent with with being late-type (E/S0, or Sa, strateva_color_2001 ). From the absence of emission-line flux at H- , the star formation rate in the galaxy is constrained to 0.05 M _ yr ^ 1 ( kennicutt_global_1998 ).

gmw_00004065.name: Galaxy 14924
gmw_00004065.overview: Galaxy 14924 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate observed at a redshift of approximately 9.9 [arXiv:2211.02651, arXiv:2304.06429]. It is characterized by an exceptionally high stellar mass of approximately 10.9 solar masses in log scale [arXiv:2211.02651, arXiv:2210.14915].

sources.summary: The paper treats Galaxy 14924 as an extreme JWST galaxy with stellar mass log10 10.9 at redshift about 9.9, requiring enhanced star-formation efficiency and modified small-scale matter power to ease tension.

quotes.quote: Our analysis uses extreme value statistics (EVS) and considers two of the most extremal galaxies observed so far by JWST: CEERS-1749 of stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/ ) = 9.6 0.2 at redshift z = 16 0.6 and Galaxy 14924 of stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/ ) = 10.9 0.3 at redshift z = 9.9 0.5 .

The left panels show these ranges for CEERS-1749 and the right panels for Galaxy 14924. We find that a significantly enhanced star formation efficiency and a modification over the CDM matter power spectrum at scales k_c = O (10)h ^ 1 would be required to alleviate the tension below the 2 level.

We have performed a careful analysis of whether a modified matter power spectrum could explain the existence of two of the most extreme galaxies observed so far by JWST, CEERS-1749 and Galaxy 14924. Considering power-law modifications to the matter power spectrum and extreme value statistics, we have estimated the distribution of the heaviest galaxies expected to be seen by JWST above a given redshift. We have found that both a high star formation efficiency and a modification of the matter power spectrum at the smallest scales allowed by the Lyman- and UV luminosity function constraints are needed for this distribution to be compatible with the observed stellar mass, in particular for Galaxy 14924.

sources.summary: The paper uses Galaxy 14924 as the heaviest high-redshift JWST candidate, comparing its stellar mass and redshift with cosmic-string loop-seeded galaxy predictions.

quotes.quote: We compare the predicted stellar mass of the largest loop-seeded galaxy to the two extreme galaxies detected by JWST: The heaviest hight-redshift galaxy candidate: Galaxy 14924, which has stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/M_ )=10.9 0.3 and inferred redshift z = 9.9 0.5 .

We illustrate the 1 range of stellar masses of the two extreme galaxies by the gray region with black vertical lines. We can find that for N=N_0 , cosmic string loops with G 10^ 9 are able to seed enough high-redshift halos to explain the extreme JWST galaxies.
An extreme value statistic analysis shows that we can explain the heaviest and earliest high-redshift galaxy candidates detected by JWST given a string tension as low as G 10^ 9 .

sources.summary: The paper includes ID 14924 as an extremely massive high-redshift JWST candidate, finding a strong discrepancy with simulated galaxy masses in the CDM framework.

quotes.quote: Labbe_2022 identified candidates with M_ * > 10^ 10 M_ over 7 < z_ phot < 11 from which the two most massive are ID 14924 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 10.93 at z_ phot = 9.92 and ID 38094 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 11.16 at z_ phot = 7.56 (see their figure 3).
For the TNG runs, ID 1514 located at z_ phot = 9.85_ 0.12 ^ +0.18 and ID 14924 at z_ phot = 9.92 are compared with simulated galaxies at z=10.00 .
The stellar mass of ID 14924 is 74 times higher than the most massive simulated galaxy at z = 9.99 .

gmw_00004066.name: UGC 6510
gmw_00004066.overview: UGC 6510 is a bright, face-on spiral galaxy classified as SAB(rs) [arXiv:1111.2509, arXiv:1308.5545]. The galaxy is characterized by multiple arms, high H-alpha luminosity [arXiv:1308.5545], and it hosted the supernova SN 2006V in its outermost region [arXiv:1111.2509, arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: UGC 6510 is a bright face-on, multi-armed H-alpha luminous galaxy whose outer SN site was not on an H II region; 14 regions constrained its gradient.

quotes.quote: UGC 6510 is a bright, face-on galaxy that is formed by multiple arms and characterized by a significant H luminosity. SN 2006V occurred in the outermost part of the galaxy and not on a detectable H ii region. We observed this host with both the VLT and the NOT. The NOT--ALFOSC slit was placed in order to catch the flux of the brightest H ii region close to the SN position (see the top and center-left panels of Fig. sn06V ). The VLT--FORS2 slit was positioned at the exact SN position and through the host center (see the top and center-right panels of Fig. sn06V ).

A total of 14 H ii regions were investigated, allowing for a precise determination of the metallicity gradient (see the bottom panel of Fig. sn06V ). The extrapolated metallicity at the distance of SN 2006V is 12 + log(O/H) = 8.35 dex.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 6510 as the SAB(rs) host galaxy of SN 2006V, gives the supernova offset from its center, and derives a redshift-based distance.

quotes.quote: \ was discovered on 4.7 February 2006 UT chen06 in the SAB(rs) galaxy UGC 6510 during the course of the Taiwan Supernova Survey. With J2000.0 coordinates = 11^ h 31^ m 30.01^ s , = -01^ 17 52 2 , the SN was positioned 32 1 West and 40 4 North from the host galaxy center (see Fig. gal06V ).

According to the Nasa Extragalactic Database (NED), UGC 6510 has a heliocentric redshift z=0.0158 . From the narrow H emission line measured in the optical spectra presented below, we measure a value of z=0.0157 0.0013 (the error corresponds to an uncertainty of 400 km s ^ 1 ). Adopting a Hubble constant of H _0 = 73.8 2.4 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 riess11 our measured redshift from the spectra of SN 2006V corresponds to a distance of 72.7 5.0 Mpc.

gmw_00004067.name: UGC 11057
gmw_00004067.overview: UGC 11057 is a strongly tilted Scd spiral galaxy [arXiv:1308.5545, arXiv:1111.2509] located at a distance of approximately 46.2 Mpc [arXiv:1111.2509]. The galaxy exhibits bright H-alpha emission in its outer arms [arXiv:1308.5545] and hosted the supernova SN 2006au [arXiv:1111.2509].

sources.summary: UGC 11057 is a strongly tilted spiral with bright outer-arm H-alpha emission; the SN coincided with a bright H II region and the outer gradient flattened.

quotes.quote: UGC 11057 is a spiral galaxy that appears strongly tilted (i = 66 deg) on the sky (see the top-right panel of Fig. sn06au ). The brightest H luminosity comes from the outer part of the arms that form this host. A bright, saturated foreground star affected the quality of the continuum-subtracted H image, leaving some residuals that we manually removed.

SN 2006au exploded in spatial coincidence with a bright and extended H ii region (see the top panels of Fig. sn06au ), onto which we placed the slit, at r_ SN /R_ 25 = 0.83. A total of eight metallicity measurements were obtained and from the computed gradient we obtained 12 + log(O/H) = 8.49 dex at the SN position, consistent with the single-measurement value at the exact SN position (see the bottom panel of Fig. sn06au ). Similar to the host of OGLE-2003-NOOS-005, UGC 11057 shows a gradient that tends to flatten in the outer regions.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 11057 as the Scd host galaxy of SN 2006au, derives a redshift-based distance, and uses host-galaxy absorption to estimate reddening.

quotes.quote: \ was discovered on 7.2 March 2006 UT in the Scd galaxy UGC 11057 by the Tenagra Observatory Supernova Search trondal06 . With J2000.0 coordinates = 17^ h 57^ m 13.56^ s , = 12^ 11 03 2 , this object was located 17 0 West and 18 2 North from the center of the host galaxy (see Fig. gal06au ).

NED lists a heliocentric redshift to UGC 11057 of z = 0.0099 , which is in agreement with the value measured from our spectra of z = 0.0098 0.0013 . Our measured redshift to UGC 11057 corresponds to a distance of 46.2 3.2 Mpc, where we again adopted the above-mentioned cosmology and peculiar motions.
Conspicuous Na i D absorption lines at the redshift of UGC 11057 are detected in the first spectrum of SN 2006au.

gmw_00004068.name: VDESJ0143-5545
gmw_00004068.overview: VDESJ0143-5545 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.23 [arXiv:1701.04852]. It exhibits a very blue z - Y color index due to the presence of a strong Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1701.04852].

sources.summary: The paper reports VDESJ0143-5545 as a z=6.23 quasar candidate confirmed with NTT spectroscopy, with strong Ly emission, blue z-Y color, and model-dependent reddening.

quotes.quote: VDESJ0143-5545 (z = 6.23) J0143 5545 was followed up with the NTT and found to have a very strong emission feature at 8820 suggestive of a quasar with z 6.3. This object was well fit by the model with the highest level of reddening, E(B-V) = 0.100, at z = 6.1. This object has a very blue z - Y of -0.61 due to the presence of the very strong Ly emission line in the z filter. When the reddening fit was repeated without using the blended WISE data the object was best fit by a model with E(B-V) = 0.025 suggesting that the W1 and W2 fluxes are effected by a nearby source.

gmw_00004069.name: VDESJ0323-4701
gmw_00004069.overview: VDESJ0323-4701 is a high-redshift quasar located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 6.25 [arXiv:1701.04852]. It exhibits a very red i - z color of 3.52 and is best fit by a non-reddened quasar model [arXiv:1701.04852].

sources.summary: The paper reports VDESJ0323-4701 as a GMOS-confirmed z=6.25 quasar with red i-z color, a non-reddened best-fit model, and a small corrected near-zone size.

quotes.quote: VDESJ0323-4701 (z = 6.25) VDESJ0323 4701 was the lowest ranked candidate followed up. Spectroscopic observations with GMOS revealed a quasar at z 6.25. This object was very red with i - z = 3.52 and was best fit by a non reddened quasar model with a slightly lower redshift of 6.10 than the spectroscopic one. The measured corrected near zone size of 2.8 proper Mpc which could indicate above averge IGM density of a young age for this quasar.

gmw_00004070.name: VDESJ0330-4025
gmw_00004070.overview: VDESJ0330-4025 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z = 6.25 [arXiv:1701.04852]. It exhibits a relatively small corrected near zone size of 2.5 proper Mpc, which may indicate a young age or an above-average surrounding intergalactic medium density [arXiv:1701.04852].

sources.summary: The paper describes VDESJ0330-4025 as a z=6.25 quasar with a small corrected near zone, possibly indicating youth or above-average surrounding IGM density.

quotes.quote: The four new zones that we measure at 6.1 < z < 6.3 span a large range from 3 to 9 Mpc. Two, VDES J0330-4025 and VDES J0323-4701, have relatively small corrected near zone sizes of 3 Mpc which could indicate that these two quasars are younger than the average quasar at this epoch and have relatively small lifetime ( 10^6 - 10^7 years) and the ionized HII regions have not reached their maximum size due to the time taken for the ionizing radiation fronts to expand into the surrounding HI region.

VDESJ0330-4025 (z = 6.25) The measured corrected near zone size of 2.5 proper Mpc which could indicate above averge IGM density of a young age for this quasar. VDESJ0330-4025 lies within 10 deg on the sky of the other quasar with a small near zone; VDESJ0323-4701 which also has a redshift of 2.5 so they could lie in a correlated region of the Universe with above average IGM over density.

gmw_00004071.name: VDESJ2250-5015
gmw_00004071.overview: VDESJ2250-5015 is a high-redshift quasar with a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of 6.00 [arXiv:1701.04852]. It exhibits red colors, which are likely caused by reddening from dust [arXiv:1701.04852].

sources.summary: The paper reports VDESJ2250-5015 as a z=6.00 NTT-confirmed quasar, the brightest source in its sample, with red colors and best-fit reddening.

quotes.quote: VDESJ2250 5015 (z = 6.00 This object was ranked fourth by the selection code with a good fit to a model with E(B V) = 0.05 and a predicted redshift of 6.0. Follow up spectroscopy with the NTT gives a redshift of 6.00. This source was the brightest in our sample with z = 20.11. VDESJ2250-5015 has a fairly red i - z colour of 2.52 and has a very red Y - J colour of 0.80. The reddening fit was repeated with the redshift fixed at the calculated one and without using the WISE data as the close proximity of another source might be influencing this.
This resulted in a model with more reddening (E(B-V) = 0.1) being chosen as the best fit. The red Y - J colour of this object is probably due to the reddening.

gmw_00004072.name: VDESJ2315-0023
gmw_00004072.overview: VDESJ2315-0023 is a high-redshift quasar located in the Stripe 82 region [arXiv:1701.04852]. Spectroscopic follow-up observations of the object confirmed its redshift to be 6.117 [arXiv:1701.04852].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VDESJ2315-0023 as a known Stripe 82 quasar recovered by the selection, with spectroscopic redshift z=6.117 slightly above its photometric estimate.

quotes.quote: VDESJ2315-0023 (z = 6.12) The seventh mostly likely ranked candidate was a known quasar (SDSS J231546.57-002358.1) from the SDSS survey for quasars in stripe 82 Jiang2008 . Their spectropscopic follow up found it to have z = 6.117 which is slightly higher than our photometric estimate of 6.0.
Two are recovered by the selection criterion, VDES0454-4448 (z = 6.10) from R15 and SDSSJ2315+0023 from Jiang et al, 2008 as discussed in section IndObjects .

gmw_00004073.name: J0454--1016a
gmw_00004073.overview: J0454--1016a is a radio galaxy located in the A 521 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433]. It is situated 1.5 arcminutes from a radio relic, and its lobes may be undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0454--1016a as the most powerful A 521 radio galaxy near the relic, possibly supplying fossil plasma or losing lobes through ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: fig:relic2 also shows that the cluster radio galaxy A (J0454--1016a, the most powerful source in A 521 ) is located only 1.5^ away from the relic (in the plane of the sky), and a faint bridge of radio emission connects the two sources.

Another important requirement in this scenario is the presence of an active radio galaxy in the proximity of the relic. This constraint is satisfied in A 521 , where the radio galaxy J0454--1016a is located only 1.5 ^ from the relic, i.e. a projected distance of 350 kpc. A previous cycle of activity of this radio galaxy could have provided the fossil radio plasma in the ICM, revived by the shock compression.

Ram pressure stripping Finally, another appealing possibility is that the relic in A 521 is the result of ram pressure stripping of the radio lobes of J0454 1016a (a) by group merger in the southern cluster region, or (b) by the infalling of J0454 1016a itself through G11.

gmw_00004074.name: J0454--1006
gmw_00004074.overview: J0454--1006 is a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy candidate located in the cluster A 521 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433]. It exhibits a tailed morphology that suggests interaction between its radio plasma and the external medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0454--1006 as an extended wide-angle-tail radio galaxy candidate in A 521, with red-sequence color, intermediate radio power, and tailed morphology suggesting environmental interaction.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, no redshift information is available for the two optical counterparts, whose apparent magnitude is similar to that of the brightest cluster galaxies. Their (B I) colour, derived from the SuperCOSMOS catalogue, is in very good agreement with the red sequence of the elliptical galaxies of the cluster, i.e. <B-I> 2.6 (see Fig.15 in F03). In particular, for J0454--1006 (B--I)=2.47 and for J0453--0957 (B--I)=2.64, therefore they might be part of A 521 , despite the large distance from the cluster centre. If we assume that they are located at the average cluster distance, their total radio power is logP _ 610 MHz = 25.26 0.08 and logP _ 610 MHz = 24.95 0.08 for the wide--angle--tail J0454--1006 and the source J0453--0957 respectively.

These values are consistent with their radio structure, typical of intermediate power radio galaxies. The tailed morphology of J0454--1006 is suggestive of interaction between the radio plasma and the external medium.

gmw_00004075.name: J0453--0957
gmw_00004075.overview: J0453--0957 is an extended radio galaxy candidate located outside the virial radius of the galaxy cluster A 521 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433]. It exhibits an undisturbed morphology and has a red-sequence color consistent with being an intermediate-power elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510433].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0453--0957 as an extended radio galaxy candidate near A 521, with red-sequence color, intermediate radio power, and an apparently undisturbed morphology outside the virial radius.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, no redshift information is available for the two optical counterparts, whose apparent magnitude is similar to that of the brightest cluster galaxies. Their (B I) colour, derived from the SuperCOSMOS catalogue, is in very good agreement with the red sequence of the elliptical galaxies of the cluster, i.e. <B-I> 2.6 (see Fig.15 in F03). In particular, for J0454--1006 (B--I)=2.47 and for J0453--0957 (B--I)=2.64, therefore they might be part of A 521 , despite the large distance from the cluster centre. If we assume that they are located at the average cluster distance, their total radio power is logP _ 610 MHz = 25.26 0.08 and logP _ 610 MHz = 24.95 0.08 for the wide--angle--tail J0454--1006 and the source J0453--0957 respectively.
These values are consistent with their radio structure, typical of intermediate power radio galaxies.
On the contrary, the morphology of J0453--0957, which is outside the virial radius, appears undisturbed.

gmw_00004076.name: IRAS 19115−2124
gmw_00004076.overview: IRAS 19115−2124, also known as "the Bird," is a luminous infrared galaxy merger consisting of three interacting components [arXiv:0708.2365, arXiv:1701.00890]. The system features 20-kiloparsec tidal tails [arXiv:1701.00890], and its current star formation is dominated by the smallest, irregular component [arXiv:0708.2365, arXiv:1009.5032].

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 19115-2124 as the Bird, a disturbed LIRG pre-merger with three components: barred-spiral Heart, disturbed Body, and pre-existing irregular Head.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared (NIR) adaptive optics imaging obtained with VLT/NACO and optical spectroscopy from the Southern African Large Telescope (SALT) of a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) IRAS 19115-2124. These data are combined with archival HST imaging and Spitzer imaging and spectroscopy, allowing us to study this disturbed interacting/merging galaxy, dubbed the Bird, in extraordinary detail. In particular, the data reveal a triple system where the LIRG phenomenon is dominated by the smallest of the components.

We have studied in detail the spectacular luminous infrared galaxy IRAS 19115-2124, which we have dubbed the Bird. Using near-diffraction limited adaptive optics imaging in the K-band (VLT/NACO), and matching space-based optical imaging (HST/ACS), combined with ground-based optical spectroscopy (SALT/RSS), we find the Bird to be a pre-merger of three components. Two of the components have unambiguously identifiable galaxy nuclei. One has the quite regular composition of a barred spiral disk galaxy (Heart) while the other (Body) has a more disturbed nuclear region, with hints of both spiral/arm structure and a surface brightness profile intermediate to that of disks and ellipticals. The third component (Head) appears to be an irregular galaxy -- our kinematical and dynamical data, however, strongly suggest that it is not merely a tidal dwarf borne out of the interaction of the other two, but rather a pre-existing less massive galaxy intimately involved in the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 19115-2124 as a local interacting luminous infrared merger whose off-nuclear starburst, multiple nuclei, gas kinematics, and component histories trace galaxy transformation.

quotes.quote: IRAS 19115-2124: the Bird The observed target, IRAS 19115-2124, also known as ESO 593-IG008, is at a distance of 200 Mpc ( z 0.05 ). Its infrared luminosity is L / L_ = 11.9 just shy of a ULIRG classification. henceforth V08 Vaisanen2008 presented a detailed study of the system based on near-infrared (NIR) K band adaptive optics (AO) imaging with 0.1 spatial resolution, optical long-slit spectroscopy, and archival optical HST and mid-infrared (MIR) Spitzer imaging. The star formation rate (SFR) of the entire system was measured to be 190 M _ yr ^ 1 from the far-IR SED.

The two main nuclei, as revealed by the NIR observations, are at a 1.5 kpc projected (1.5 ) distance from each other, and the system shows 20 kpc scale tidal tails. A third component, argued to be a satellite galaxy, was also identified from the NIR images, projected 2 kpc North of the two main nuclei. Because of its optical and NIR appearance, we dubbed IRAS 19115-2124 "the Bird" (Fig. bird ). The two primary nuclei in this context are called the Heart and the Body components, while the southern tidal feature is the Tail and the East and West tidal extensions the Wings. The third component north-east of the Heart is the Head of the Bird.

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 19115-2124, the Bird, as a dusty triple encounter where a minor third component dominates the star-formation rate and gas flows appear near nuclei.

quotes.quote: We then present NIR IFU observations of IRAS 19115-2124, aka the Bird, which is an intriguing triple encounter. The third component is a minor one, but, nevertheless, is the source of 3/4 of the SFR of the whole system. Gas inflows and outflows are detected at the locations of the nuclei.

\ (2008) presented NIR AO-imaging and optical spectroscopy of the system IRAS19115-2124. The galaxy, which we dubbed the Bird, was expected to consist of a pair-interaction based on optical HST imaging. The VLT/NACO imaging and SALT spectroscopy, however, suggested a third component.

sources.summary: The authors report that IRAS 19115-2124 contains an unexpected irregular third component, the Bird, which dominates current star formation and pushes the system close to ULIRG luminosity.

quotes.quote: We found an unexpected third component in the supposed pair-interaction of IRAS 19115-2124, dubbed the Bird . Moreover, this third, least massive and irregular component dominates the current star-formation output of the whole system. This is in contrast to the widely held picture that tidal interactions are expected to drive large quantities of gas into the central regions of the interaction resulting in starbursts. While there is strong SF in the central regions as well, it clearly is the smaller ``extra'' component that elevates the Bird into almost a ULIRG-class object. We are in the process of studying this system further with integral field spectroscopy and mid-IR imaging to piece together what has happened in this intriguing system.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 593-IG008 as the Bird merger, models its absorbed star-forming radio SED with a 100 MHz turnover, and derives its synchrotron slope, thermal fraction, SFR, dust luminosity, and AGN fraction.

quotes.quote: ESO 593-IG008 : known as the `Bird' Vaisanen17 , this system is a a well-known galaxy merger Stierwalt13, Hung15 . Its radio SED covering 1.8 decades (70 MHz-4.8 GHz) is fitted with single-component star-forming region synchrotron (including absorption) with a turnover at 100 MHz. Synchrotron spectral index, = 0.87. The computed TF is 0.6 . The CIGALE-estimated SFR, L_ dust , AGN fraction are 51.3 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 11.18 L_ , 3.9 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 19115-2124, the Bird, as a LIRG whose adaptive-optics imaging and multiwavelength data reveal a triple merger with tidal tails and dominant star formation in the head.

quotes.quote: A triple merger The LIRG IRAS 19115−2124 has been dubbed the ``Bird'' on account of its shape, which in NaCo K band images resolves into two wings, a head, body, heart, and extended tail (Fig. f:bird ). By combining this image with data from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and Spitzer satellite, as well as optical longslit spectroscopy with the Southern African Large Telescope, we reach the surprising conclusion that the Bird contains not two, but three galaxies undergoing mutual interaction. The head, heart, and body are each separate galaxies of between 1 and 7 10^ 10 solar masses, with the wings marking tidal tails. Strangely, despite being the least massive of the three galaxies, it is the head which is currently dominating star formation in the Bird.

sources.summary: IRAS F19115-2124 is treated as a close pair with complex structure, projected-radius gradient measurements, and very flat metallicity values that would remain shallow under alternate disk assumptions.

quotes.quote: IRAS F19115-2124 (ESO 593-IG 008) Vaisanen08 analyze the morphology of this close pair in detail using Adaptive Optics K-band images. Because of the complex structure we adopt the projected radii for gradient calculations and make no attempt to separate the pair into two separate gradients. Given the flat values for metallicity seen, however, adapting an inclination and/or two disks would only lead to an even shallower calculated slope in the case of this system.

sources.summary: The paper describes this close pair as possibly triple but kinematically consistent with first pericenter interaction, with a considerably flattened metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: IRAS F19115-2124 (ESO 593-IG 008) Vaisanen08 analyze the morphology of this close pair in detail using Adaptive Optics K-band images and suggest the possibility of a triple-galaxy interaction. Kinematic modelling can be satisfied by an interacting pair, however, and indicates that though this system is classified as a 'close pair', it is actually just entering first pericenter (Josh Barnes, private communication). The metallicity gradient in this system, however, has already flattened considerably Rich12 .

gmw_00004077.name: IRAS 18293-3413
gmw_00004077.overview: IRAS 18293-3413 is a luminous infrared galaxy system in an early- to mid-merger stage [arXiv:1408.0593, arXiv:2207.08929]. The system consists of a northern, star-forming spiral galaxy with suspected leading arms and a southern elliptical companion [arXiv:1408.0593, arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper highlights IRAS 18293-3413 as a LIRG hosting SN 2004ip and showing unusual disk geometry, companion-facing kinematics, and suspected leading spiral arms.

quotes.quote: Leading spiral arms The LIRG IRAS 18293-3413 not only hosted our first AO CCSN discovery SN 2004ip , but also turns out to have unusual dynamics. HST optical images in Figure f:i18293 show significantly more dust in silhouette towards the southwest, consistent with this edge of the galaxy disk being closer to us than the northeast edge. NIR longslit spectroscopy with the IRIS2 instrument on the Anglo-Australian Telescope indicates that the northwest side of the disk, facing the companion galaxy, is approaching us. Putting these two facts together we are led to conclude that the galaxy is turning clockwise in the image, i.e., in the same direction that the spiral arms clearly visible in the inset to Fig. f:i18293 open out. Thus IRAS 18293-3413 is one of just a handful of known or suspected ``leading arm'' spirals.

sources.summary: The paper describes a northern obscured infrared-bright spiral and southern old elliptical component, with star formation and outer shock contribution but no AGN indication.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18293-3413 The IR-bright source in this galaxy is the northern, highly extinguished galaxy. K-band data from NIRC and HST NICMOS show very buried spiral structure Vaisanen08b,Haan11b . The southern galaxy appears elliptical, with our IFS spectra having no detectable emission line gas and a continuum indicative of an old stellar population. The northern galaxy is dominated by star formation with some contribution from shocks in the outskirts of the galaxy. The study of Chandra observations by Iwasawa09,Iwasawa11 shows resolved soft X-ray emission possibly associated with an outflow, but with no indication of an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes IR 18293-3413 as an early- or mid-merger with no X-ray AGN evidence, fitting its radio SED with two star-forming components and deriving steep synchrotron indices.

quotes.quote: IR 18293-3413 : a complex system, classified both as early-merger Haan11 and mid-merger Ricci17 galaxy system with no evidence of AGN activity X-ray spectrum; Risaliti2000 , its radio SED covering 2.1 decades in frequency (70 MHz-8.4 GHz) is best-fitted with two-component model representing two distinct star-forming regions with different ultrarelativistic CRe and one region without any turnover. The synchrotron spectral indices for the two populations are, 1.33 and 1.74, respectively. The turnover frequency is 1.06 GHz. The TF is 3.7 . The CIGALE-estimated SFR, L_ dust , AGN fraction are 15.48 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 11.7 L_ , 2.5 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports a weak ASCA detection of IRAS 18293-3413, finds no AGN evidence, fits the data with a blackbody, and gives its 2-10 keV flux.

quotes.quote: IRAS 18293-3413: This source has been detected at a 5 level. There is no evidence of an AGN contribution. Fitting the data with a blackbody we obtain kT=0.85 keV. The 2-10 keV flux is 9 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

gmw_00004078.name: GASS 3505
gmw_00004078.overview: GASS 3505 is an early-type transition galaxy with the optical morphology and colors of a normal elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0912.1610]. Despite these elliptical characteristics, it possesses an unusually high gas content and displays an external ring-like structure in ultraviolet images [arXiv:0912.1610].

sources.summary: The paper highlights GASS 3505 as an unusually gas-rich early-type transition galaxy with normal elliptical colors, possible disk reaccretion, and an external ring-like ultraviolet structure.

quotes.quote: The stars identify the locations of GASS 3505 (red) and GASS 7050 (green), which are somewhat extreme examples of two opposite types of transition galaxies. The former is an early-type galaxy with unusually high gas content that might be reaccreting a disk (thus moving from the red toward the blue sequence), and the latter is a gas-poor, disk galaxy (which is likely moving in the opposite direction, from the blue to the red sequence.

Also, GASS 3505 (marked with a red star on the diagram), a galaxy that has optical morphology and colours characteristic of a normal elliptical, but a 50 \ mass fraction, is a clear outlier in this plane. More intriguing are red sequence galaxies that might be accreting gas and maybe even regrowing a disk (such as perhaps GASS 3505). Interestingly, also GASS 3505 shows an external ring-like structure in the GALEX images. We have recently obtained VLA data for this galaxy, and will report on it in a separate paper.

gmw_00004079.name: GASS 7050
gmw_00004079.overview: GASS 7050 is a gas-poor disk galaxy [arXiv:0912.1610]. It is a transition galaxy that is likely moving from the blue sequence toward the red sequence [arXiv:0912.1610].

sources.summary: The paper presents GASS 7050 as a gas-poor disk transition galaxy, an outlier for its color, likely moving from the blue sequence toward the red sequence.

quotes.quote: The stars identify the locations of GASS 3505 (red) and GASS 7050 (green), which are somewhat extreme examples of two opposite types of transition galaxies. The former is an early-type galaxy with unusually high gas content that might be reaccreting a disk (thus moving from the red toward the blue sequence), and the latter is a gas-poor, disk galaxy (which is likely moving in the opposite direction, from the blue to the red sequence. The SDSS image and \ spectrum of GASS 7050 can be seen in the right column of the second row of Figure ndet ).

We also notice that GASS 3505 and GASS 7050 stand out as clear outliers in this plot, with a gas content well displaced from the average for their \ colour. Of equal interest are the galaxies with low \ mass fractions, but that are still forming stars. These galaxies are found near the bottom the plots, but shifted to the right ( , GASS 7050, a gas-poor disk galaxy that was not detected in , is indicated by a green star).

gmw_00004080.name: RXJ 0443.8-6141
gmw_00004080.overview: RXJ 0443.8-6141 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.72 [arXiv:1310.6809]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy and is associated with a 0.77 Mpc giant radio source [arXiv:1310.6809].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RXJ 0443.8-6141 as a z=0.72 QSO whose radio morphology is confirmed as a 0.77 Mpc giant radio source and FR II radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: As part of a European Southern Observatory (ESO) key field program to optically identify a sub-sample of 700 ROSAT All Sky Survey (RASS) sources, Danziger et al. (1990) identified RXJ 0443.8-6141 as a QSO with redshift z=0.72 . During the initial radio follow-up of this sub-sample at 4.8 GHz, Anderson (2002) discovered a triple (lobe-core-lobe) radio source possibly associated with RXJ 0443.8-6141. The Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) http://www.narrabri.atnf.csiro.au/observing/users_guide/html/atug.html . data used for this initial discovery was insufficient to show if the lobes were connected to the radio core via jet structures.

We confirm the existence of a GRS associated with , which is located at a redshift of z=0.72 and has a PLE of 0.77 Mpc. We conclude from the radio morphology and radio luminosity that \ is an FR II radio galaxy. This identification is only made possible by using radio observations taken at various frequencies of different resolution. While the lower frequency data enabled accurate measurements of the total integrated flux, and revealed the physical connection between the AGN core and lobes, the higher frequency data enabled the compact hot spots typical of FR II galaxies to be identified.

gmw_00004081.name: WFI 2026--4536
gmw_00004081.overview: WFI 2026--4536 is a quadruply lensed quasar [arXiv:2009.01277, arXiv:1007.3142] at a redshift of approximately 2.22 [arXiv:2009.01277, arXiv:1007.3142]. The system exhibits thermal dust emission [arXiv:2009.01277], microlensing variability [arXiv:1911.06218], and spatially resolved CO line emission that suggests extreme, super-Eddington star formation [arXiv:2009.01277].

sources.summary: The paper observes WFI J2026 4536 with ALMA, detecting thermal dust and spatially resolved CO, and identifies it as the most extreme super-Eddington star-formation case.

quotes.quote: WFI J2026 4536 WFI J2026 4536 is a quasar at z=2.22 Morgan:2004 that is lensed by a galaxy at unknown redshift into four images in a typical fold configuration. There are no radio observations of this system available to characterise its radio properties at 1.4 GHz, but the lack of detection in the Sydney University Molonglo Sky Survey (SUMSS) Source Catalog Mauch:2003 suggests a radio flux density of <12 mJy at 1.4 GHz, assuming a typical synchrotron spectral index of 0.7 . WFI J2026 4536 was observed with ALMA at 350 GHz as part of our ALMA programme, which also observed the CO (10--9) spectral line transition. The ALMA observations detect continuum emission with a flux density of 28.3 mJy and achieve an rms noise level of 30 Jy beam ^ 1 with natural weighting. This flux density is in good agreement with thermal dust emission, based on SED fitting (Fig. fig:seds1 ).

The CLEANed image shows bright emission from the location of the quasar images and extended emission at lower surface brightness. The CO (10--9) line emission is also spatially resolved with a similar morphology to the dust emission.
The peak star formation rate surface densities range from 25 to >4500 , suggesting super-Eddington star formation in the most extreme case (WFI J2026 4536).

sources.summary: The paper treats WFI J2026-4536 as a quadruply imaged quasar with an unknown-redshift lens, probable microlensing, and astrometry mostly consistent with earlier image processing.

quotes.quote: WFI J2026-4536 (f) Morgan2004 discovered this quadruply imaged quasar (RA = 20^ h 26^ m 10^ s .43 and DEC = 45^ 36'27 10 ) during an optical survey using the WFI camera mounted on the MPG/ESO 2.2m telescope operated by the European Southern Observatory (ESO). The redshift of the source is z_ s =2.23 . The one of the lens is unknown, although it is clearly visually detected on high resolution images. No time delay has ever been measured but according to Morgan2004 , the longest one might be of the order of at most a week or two. According to them, the lensed images are likely affected by microlensing.

WFI J2026-4536 (f) The astrometry we obtain, except for the right ascension of the lens ( RA= 0 03), is in agreement, within the error bars, with the results of Morgan2004 who used the same frames but a different image processing technique.

sources.summary: The paper treats WFI 2026 as a narrow-line quad with uncertain lens redshift, deriving a redshift distribution from image separation and finding dark-matter posteriors insensitive to fixed redshift choices.

quotes.quote: We also assume the population mean halo mass for WFI 2026 since the lens redshift used to estimate the central velocity dispersion is very uncertain.

For the third system (WFI 2026), which does not have multi-band photometry from Shajib++18 , we assume a typical velocity dispersion for a massive elliptical galaxy, and derive a probability distribution for the lens redshift from measured quantities such as the source redshift and measured image separation. We give more details regarding this procedure in Appendix app:B . Satellite galaxies and nearby deflectors We model satellite galaxies and other deflectors near the main lens as Singular Isothermal Spheres (SIS), and assume they lie at the lens redshift unless they have measured redshifts that place them elsewhere. We marginalize over the position and Einstein radius of these objects using Gaussian priors on the positions centered on the light centroid with a variance of 0.05 arcseconds. We use a Gaussian prior on the Einstein radius which is estimated from lens model fitting, or in some cases by direct measurements on the central velocity dispersion e.g. Wong++17,Rusu++19 . In the cases of HE0435 and WFI 2033, the nearby galaxy lies at a higher redshift than the main lens plane. The light from the galaxy is therefore subject to lensing by the main deflector, and its true physical location differs from its observed position. We estimate the true physical locations of these objects by sampling the macromodel parameter space using the image positions as constraints, and read out the physical position of background satellite given its observed (lensed) position. We then place the satellite at this derived physical location in the forward model sampling with uncertainties of 0.05 arcseconds. This process significantly speeds up the lensing computations since it does not require the continuous reevaluation of the physical satellite location given its observed position during each lens model computation The physical location of the nearby galaxy needs to be continuously reevaluated because it's observed location depends on the foreground lensing effects from the macromodel, and the parameters describing the macromodel are continuously changing while finding a solution to the lens equation (Equation eqn:raytracing ). . The boost in speed comes at the cost of decoupling the satellite galaxy position from the dark matter parameters in the inference, but we expect the covariance between these quantities will be negligible because the satellite galaxies, even when their locations are corrected for foreground lensing effects, are relatively far from the images, introducing convergence at the main deflector light centroid of < 0.1 in both cases. The default convention in lenstronomy is to place deflectors at their observed angular locations in the universe, but it is now possible (in code versions 0.8.0+) to specify which objects should be treated using the observed (lensed) position instead. We note that the default convention in lensmodel Keeton++97 is to place objects at their observed (lensed) locations during multi-plane ray-tracing. In the case of HE0435, we estimate the angular location without foreground lensing of the satellite to be (-2.37, 2.08) , while for WFI 2033 we obtain (-3.63, -0.08) , for observed (lensed) locations of (-2.911, 2.339) and (-3.965, -0.022) , respectively. These coordinates are with respect to the galaxy light centroid (see Table tab:datasummary ). The angular locations of the lensed background satellites are closer to the mass centroid of the main deflector, just as the physical location of the lensed background quasar is concentric with the mass centroid. The lens-specific priors on satellite galaxies are summarized in Table tab:lenspriors . Lens-specific modeling for RX J0911+0551 and WGD 2038-4008 For system RX J0911, we alter the modeling strategy slightly to increase computational efficiency by allowing the external shear strength _ ext to vary freely while solving for macromodel parameters that fit the observed image positions. For the system WGD 2038, we widen the prior on the power-law slope of the macromodel as the posterior using the default range for _ macro between 1.95 - 2.2 is biased towards higher values of _ macro . For WGD 2038, the posterior peaks at _ macro 2.25 . In this section we present the results of our analysis. We begin in Section ssec:jointinf by showing dark matter halo convergence maps for some of the top-ranked realizations drawn in the forward model. We then display the posterior distributions for a few individual lenses, showing the simultaneous inference of parameters describing the macro lens model and the dark matter hyper-parameters. In Section ssec:mainresults we present the constraints on the abundance of substructure and dark matter warmth for the full sample of 11 quads. Top-ranked realizations and posteriors for individual lenses Minimizing the summary statistic in Equation eqn:summary_stat selects realizations that resemble the observed data as closely as possible. This guarantees that the set of accepted dark matter hyper-parameters yield an accurate approximation of the true posterior distribution for each individual lens with data : p | . For visualization purposes, and to reinforce the fact that the top-ranked realizations look like the data and satisfy S_ lens 0 (Equation eqn:summary_stat ), in Figure fig:best_realizations we display the dark matter halo effective multi-plane convergence maps for some of the top ranked realizations for a subset of quads in our sample. The effective multi-plane convergence is defined as half the divergence of the full deflection field _ effective 1 2 \ . This definition of the multi-plane convergence accounts for the non-linear effects present in multi-plane lensing, and satisfies the single-plane definition of convergence as second derivatives of a lensing potential in the absence of multiple lens planes. To visualize individual realizations of dark matter structure, we define _ effective(halo) _ effective _ macro , where _ macro is the convergence from the lens macromodel, including satellite galaxies and nearby deflectors. In the resulting convergence maps, halos located behind the main lens plane appear sheared tangentially around the Einstein radius due to coupling to the large deflections produced by the macromodel. In Figure fig:best_realizations , we show _ effective(halo) maps of randomly selected realizations of dark matter structure whose corresponding parameters were accepted in the final posterior on the basis of their summary statistic S_ lens . The specific realizations and the corresponding dark matter parameters correspond to a diverse set of substructure populations, warm and cold, which yield similarly good fits to the observed flux ratios satisfying S_ lens 0 . Some models, however, predict flux ratios that match the observed flux ratios more frequently than others. In terms of the Approximate Bayesian Computing algorithm described in Section sec:inference , the frequency with which one dark matter model relative to another predicts observables that resemble the data is a surrogate for the relative likelihood of the models. The probability of accepting a proposed based on the summary statistic in Equation eqn:summary_stat is therefore equal to the likelihood p | (Equation eqn:likelihood ), even though the form of this function is unknown and it is never directly evaluated. The top-ranked realizations for B1422 shown in Figure fig:best_realizations each have a relatively massive dark matter halo, or several smaller ones, located near the top left merging triplet image with (normalized) flux 0.88. This is in agreement with the analysis by Nierenberg++14 , who find that a blob of dark matter near this image brings the model-predicted flux ratios into agreement with a smooth lens model. Although not obvious from examining Figure fig:best_realizations , the underlying macromodels for each accepted realization are unique, with different external shears, power-law slopes, lens ellipticity, etc. We marginalize over different macromodel configurations by simultaneously sampling the macromodel parameters and the dark matter hyper-parameters in the forward model. To illustrate, in Figures fig:f , fig:f , and fig:f we show the posterior distributions for several parameters in the lens macromodel, along with the dark matter hyper-parameters _ sub and for HE0435, WFI 2033, and RX J0911. The system HE0435 generally favors models with low subhalo mass function normalizations (low _ sub ), or a turnover the mass function with higher _ sub . The system WFI 2033 is the opposite, with a posterior favoring CDM-like mass functions with many lens plane subhalos. The system RX J0911 lies somewhere in between, with a peak in the posterior distribution of near 10^ 7 . For each of these systems, in particular WFI 2033, there is a visibly obvious covariance between the overall normalization of the main deflector mass profile b_ macro b_ macro has units of convergence, or projected mass density divided by the critical surface mass density for lensing. , and the parameters _ sub and . This covariance is readily understood: To reproduce the observed image positions, the macromodel responds to the addition of mass in the form of subhalos in main lens plane by decreasing the overall normalization of the main deflector mass profile, and hence these quantities are anti-correlated. Similarly, WDM models correspond to macromodels with larger b_ macro because WDM realizations contain fewer subhalos. Interestingly, there is some structure in the posterior distribution for the lens ellipticity in WFI 2033, and both and _ sub . By simultaneously sampling the lens macromodel and dark matter hyper-parameters, we obtain posterior distributions that account for covariance between and . We do not use lens model priors from more sophisticated lens modeling efforts (e.g. Wong++17,Shajib++18 ) because these analyses did not include substructure in the lens models and therefore do not account for covariances between the macromodel parameters and the dark matter parameters of interest. For the same reason, we do not decouple the lens macromodel parameters from the dark matter hyper-parameters by first sampling the macromodel parameter space that fits the image positions, and using these distributions as priors in the forward modeling. Constraints on the free-streaming length of dark matter For each quad, we obtain a joint likelihood between the macromodel parameters and the dark matter-hyper parameters . We marginalize over the parameters in this 20+ dimensional space to obtain the four-dimensional space of parameters that includes logarithmic slope of the subhalo mass function , the scaling of the line of sight halo mass function _ los , the overall scaling of the subhalo mass function _ sub , and the half-mode mass . We reiterate that these four parameters describe universal properties of dark matter and should therefore be common to all the lenses, while the parameters and the halo mass M_ halo are lens-specific. After marginalizing, we compute the product of the resulting likelihoods and obtain the desired posterior distribution in Equation eqn:posterior , which we display in Figure fig:mainresult . The marginalized constraints on rule out > 10^ 7.8 at 2 , corresponding to thermal relic particle mass of <5.2 \ keV . It is apparent from Figure fig:mainresult that and _ sub are correlated, since halos added by increasing the normalization can be subsequently removed by increasing such that the total amount of lensing substructure remains relatively constant. As a result, the marginalized distribution for the normalization _ sub appears unconstrained from above, as the normalization can be significantly higher in WDM scenarios. With only eight quads we cannot simultaneously measure and _ sub , although our previous forecasts indicate this is possible with more lenses Gilman++18 . The constraints on dark matter warmth in terms of confidence intervals depend on the range of allowed values specified by the prior on _ sub . Similarly, the confidence interval on depends on the lower bound of this parameter that is set by the prior on . As discussed in Section ssec:wdmassumptions , we have chosen the prior on to encompass the region of parameter space where the data can constrain , keeping in mind that the WDM mass concentration relation affects the central densities of subhalos 60 times above (Equation eqn:cmrelation ), and the upper bound of _ sub = 0.1 kpc^ 2 is a conservative choice as most N-body simulations and the galacticus runs predict values below 0.05 kpc^ 2 . In light of these complications, we also quote likelihood ratios which do not depend on the choice of prior. Relative to the peak of the posterior, we obtain likelihood ratios for WDM with = 10^ 8.2 ( = 10^ 8.6 ) of 7:1 (30:1) We remind the reader that the relative heights of the peaks in the posterior somewhat depend on the binning method, or in this case the bandwidth estimator of the KDE.
