Future measurements of their high frequency radio spectral cutoffs would provide a date for the corresponding phase of 3C 129.1, and provide an estimate of the buoyant rise velocity of these old lobes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 129.1 as the second-brightest source in the field, a narrow-angle head-tail radio galaxy with dual jets, detectable polarization, and active synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: With a peak brightness of 142 mJy/beam, 3C 129.1 is the second brightest radio source in the field. It is located in projection close to the cluster center; it is also a narrow angle head-tail radio galaxy but is only slightly resolved in the SRT image.
This radio source also has dual radio jets but it is weaker and smaller than 3C 129, and the radio jets extend over about 2 (e.g. Downes 1984, J a gers 1987, Kassim et al. 1993).

The morphology, the spectral indices, and the spectral curvatures of both 3C 129 and 3C 129.1, indicate that these are currently active sources whose synchrotron emission is dominated by the electron populations with GeV energies injected by the radio jets and accumulated during their entire lives (Murgia et al. 2011).

gmw_00004105.name: 3FGL J0134.5+2638
gmw_00004105.overview: 3FGL J0134.5+2638 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1510.00960]. Its optical spectrum displays a power law continuum with faint absorption lines and an absence of strong emission lines, while its spectral energy distribution exhibits the characteristic two-hump behavior of a blazar [arXiv:1510.00960].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3FGL J0134.5+2638 as a BL Lac object from an Asiago optical spectrum with weak absorption, no strong emission lines, and a two-hump blazar SED.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0134.5+2638 , observed with the Asiago 1.22m telescope, reveals a power law continuum spectrum with faint absorption lines. Absence of emission lines having equivalent width EW 5 leads to a BLL classification. Its SED shows the characteristic two-hump behavior of blazars and it is appreciably well interpolated by a SSC model.

gmw_00004106.name: 3FGL J0339.2--1738
gmw_00004106.overview: The active galaxy 3FGL J0339.2--1738 is associated with an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.0656 [arXiv:1510.00960, arXiv:2302.10080]. Its spectrum reveals an old stellar population [arXiv:1510.00960], a faint [N II] emission line [arXiv:2302.10080], and an optical radiation excess [arXiv:1510.00960].

sources.summary: The paper associates 3FGL J0339.2--1738 with an elliptical galaxy; its spectrum resembles an old stellar population, while its SED has two humps and an optical excess.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0339.2--1738 is associated with an elliptical galaxy, with spectrum available from 6dFGRS.

The resulting spectrum shows the characteristic continuum and absorption lines of an old stellar population, typical of ellipticals. The associated SED is a two-hump distribution with a prominent radiation excess in the optical window.

sources.summary: The spectrum of 4FGL J0339.2 1736 shows host-galaxy absorption at z = 0.0656, a faint [N II] line at the same redshift, and a low nucleus-to-host ratio.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J0339.2 1736 : In our spectrum we clearly detect absorption lines (H _ , Mg I, Ca+Fe, and H _ ) from the host galaxy at z = 0.0656 confirming the redshift proposed by jones2009 from the 6dF Survey spectrum based on Ca II absorption. In addition we also detect a faint emission line at 7015  (EW 1.15  ) attributed to [ N II ]  6584 at the same redshift. The decomposition of the spectrum into an elliptical galaxy and nucleus described by a power-law ( =0.9) yields an N/H = 0.5 (see Fig.  fig:decomposition ).

gmw_00004107.name: 3FGL J1031.0+7440
gmw_00004107.overview: 3FGL J1031.0+7440 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a distance of 569.8 Mpc [arXiv:1510.00960]. Its absolute magnitude places the galaxy on the boundary between a faint quasar and a bright Seyfert 1, where the jet power and thermal emission are comparable [arXiv:1510.00960].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3FGL J1031.0+7440 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy at 569.8 Mpc, near the faint-quasar/bright-Seyfert boundary, with comparable jet and thermal contributions.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J1031.0+7440 was observed in Asiago, with the 1.82m telescope. It shows the prominent emission lines of a Seyfert 1 galaxy, with a full width at half the maximum FWHM ( H ) = 2286 350 km s^ 1 . In a standard CDM cosmology with H_0 = 70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 , _ = 0.7 and _M = 0.3 , its redshift corresponds to a distance of 569.8 Mpc. From an apparent magnitude V = 17.2 , we infer an absolute magnitude M_V = -21.6 , placing this object on the border between faint quasar and bright Seyfert 1 activity. The blazar SED is accompanied by a small radiation excess in the optical domain, suggesting that the jet power and the thermal contribution from the central engine are comparable in this object.

gmw_00004108.name: 3FGL J1315.4+1130
gmw_00004108.overview: 3FGL J1315.4+1130 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1510.00960]. Its spectrum is characterized by a power-law continuum with faint calcium and magnesium absorption features [arXiv:1510.00960].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3FGL J1315.4+1130 as a BL Lac source with a power-law continuum, faint absorption features consistent with Ca II and Mg I, and a two-component blazar SED.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J1315.4+1130 , also detected by the SDSS, is characterized by the BLL power law continuum and by faint absorption lines. Identification of the strongest features as a Ca II doublet is consistent with the presence of an absorption feature at the predicted wavelength of Mg I. Fewer data points are available to reconstruct the SED of this source and we do not appreciate any deviation from a two component blazar SED.

gmw_00004109.name: 3FGL J1412.0+5249
gmw_00004109.overview: 3FGL J1412.0+5249 is a giant elliptical galaxy located within a galaxy cluster [arXiv:1510.00960]. It features a complex spectral energy distribution characterized by a strong optical excess from its bright host galaxy [arXiv:1510.00960].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3FGL J1412.0+5249 as an SDSS-detected giant elliptical in a cluster, with a complex SED dominated by host-galaxy optical excess plus high-energy emission.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J1412.0+5249 is detected by the SDSS and it shows the characteristics of an elliptical galaxy. Its counterpart is actually a giant elliptical located in a galaxy cluster. The associated SED is the most complex of this sample, featuring a strong optical excess, emitted by the bright host galaxy, and a high energy IC component that is hardly reproduced by SSC models.

gmw_00004110.name: WKK 2924
gmw_00004110.overview: WKK 2924 is a galaxy associated with a detected neutral hydrogen signal near a velocity of 3410 km/s [arXiv:0907.4312]. This detection is also observed at the galaxy's position in HIPASS data [arXiv:0907.4312].

sources.summary: The paper treats WKK 2924 as the likely source of an H I signal near 3410 km/s, distinguishing it from nearby WKK 2938.

quotes.quote: WKK 2924/WKK 2938: There are two detections in the beam of WKK 2924: WKK 2938 (L, 34 22 ) at d=7 3 has an optical velocity of 3024 157 km s ^ 1 (FW98) which agrees with the narrow peak at v=2864 km s ^ 1 . WKK 2924 (S8, 58 22 ) is a more likely candidate for the signal at v=3410 km s ^ 1 . HIPASS also shows the latter detection at the position of WKK 2924, while nothing can be seen at the position of WKK 2938.

gmw_00004111.name: WKK 5285
gmw_00004111.overview: WKK 5285 is a galaxy that has been detected in neutral hydrogen observations [arXiv:0907.4312]. These detections occurred within the beams or OFF observations of several neighboring galaxies, including WKK 5534, WKK 5556, and WKK 5297 [arXiv:0907.4312].

sources.summary: WKK 5285 was detected in several nearby beams or OFF observations, with the least-confused profile and alternate H I measurements reported separately.

quotes.quote: WKK 5285: This galaxy has been detected in the beams of three other galaxies: in the OFF observations of WKK 5534 ( d=6 5 ) and of WKK 5556 ( d=2 7 ), as well as in the beam of WKK 5297 (not detected) at d=6 1 . The detection with the smallest distance to the beam centre is listed in Table  cxgadet and shown in Fig.  hiprofile , while the detection in the OFF observation of WKK 5524 is least affected by an RFI at v=5900 km s ^ 1 next to the signal. The other measurements are: v=5631 km s ^ 1 , V_ 50 =353 km s ^ 1 , V_ 20 =383 km s ^ 1 , I=15.65 Jy km s ^ 1 , rms = 5.3 mJy (in the beam of WKK 5297); and v=5635 km s ^ 1 , V_ 50 =357 km s ^ 1 , V_ 20 =396 km s ^ 1 , I=15.17 Jy km s ^ 1 , rms =3.3 mJy (in the OFF observation of WKK 5534).

gmw_00004112.name: WKK 5729
gmw_00004112.overview: WKK 5729 is classified as a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0907.4312]. Due to its weak neutral hydrogen signal, the galaxy is not detectable with HIPASS [arXiv:0907.4312].

sources.summary: The authors infer WKK 5729 as the likely source of repeated similar H I profiles, describing its offsets, late spiral type, and weak HIPASS detectability.

quotes.quote: WKK 5729: The three observations of WKK 5733 ( v_ opt =6215 92 km s ^ 1 , WK99), WKK 5694 ( v_ opt =3412 36 km s ^ 1 , FH95), and WKK 5709 show a very similar profile both in line width and peak flux at v=5729 km s ^ 1 , v=5730 km s ^ 1 , and v=5723 km s ^ 1 , respectively. The left horn appears to come from WKK 5768 (also detected in the beam of WKK 5780), which lies at a distance of d=13 5 , d=17 9 , and d=11 7 , respectively, from the three pointings; the peak flux of this horn varies according to the distance. Due to the similar peak flux and high velocity end of the rest of the profile we conclude that the detected galaxy must lie at a similar distance from these three pointings.

WKK 5729 ( 48 16 , SL) lies at d=6 9 , d=9 1 , and d=5 9 from WKK 5733, WKK 5694, and WKK 5709, respectively. It is a late-type spiral and therefore not visible with 2MASS and DENIS. The signal is too weak to be detectable with HIPASS.

gmw_00004113.name: WKK 5993
gmw_00004113.overview: WKK 5993 is an astronomical object whose observational detection is blended with the signal of the nearby source WKK 5999 [arXiv:0907.4312]. Because of this blending, the low-velocity end of WKK 5993 is undetermined, leaving its derived parameters uncertain [arXiv:0907.4312].

sources.summary: The WKK 5993 observation is described as blended with WKK 5999, leaving its low-velocity end and derived parameters uncertain.

quotes.quote: WKK 5993/WKK 5999: The detection in the observation of WKK 5993 is the blending of two signals. The low-velocity double-horn comes from WKK 5999 (also observed, see Fig.  hiprofile ), while the high-velocity part is due to WKK 5993. The parameters for WKK 5993 in Table  cxgadet have been measured by cutting off the profile of WKK 5999 at v 3350 km s ^ 1 . All the parameters are uncertain since the low-velocity end of WKK 5993 is undetermined.

gmw_00004114.name: WKK 7460
gmw_00004114.overview: WKK 7460 is a Magellanic-type collisional ring galaxy system located approximately 10 Mpc away [arXiv:1508.03620, arXiv:2405.04898]. It features a central late-type spiral galaxy surrounded by a star-forming ring, which was formed by a collision with a dwarf irregular "bullet" galaxy [arXiv:1508.03620, arXiv:2405.04898].

sources.summary: The paper presents Kathryn's Wheel as the nearest known collisional ring galaxy, a Magellanic-type interacting system with a star-forming ring, bullet component, substantial gas, and low mass.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of the closest collisional ring galaxy to the Milky Way. Such rare systems occur due to ``bulls-eye'' encounters between two reasonably matched galaxies. The recessional velocity of about 840 km s ^ 1 is low enough that it was detected in the AAO/UKST Survey for Galactic H emission. The distance is only 10.0 Mpc and the main galaxy shows a full ring of star forming knots, 6.1 kpc in diameter surrounding a quiescent disk. The smaller assumed ``bullet" galaxy also shows vigorous star formation.

The overall system has a physical size of 15 kpc, a total mass of M_ = 6.6 10^9 M _ (stars + H i ), a metallicity of [ O/H ] 0.4 , and a star formation rate of 0.2-0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 , making it a Magellanic-type system. Collisional ring galaxies therefore extend to much lower galaxy masses than commonly assumed. This suggests Kathryn's Wheel is the nearest such system.

sources.summary: The paper associates Kathryn's Wheel with an unassociated gamma-ray source, describing the nearby collisional ring system, its star-forming ring, diffuse radio emission, and possible cosmic-ray or buried-AGN contributions.

quotes.quote: Here we report, for the first time, the association of a collisional ring galaxy system in our Galactic neighborhood (distance 10 Mpc), formed as a consequence of a smaller `bullet' galaxy piercing through a larger galaxy, as the multi-frequency counterpart of an unassociated ray source 4FGL J1647.5 5724. The system, also known as ``Kathryn's Wheel", contains two dwarf irregular galaxies and an edge-on, late-type, spiral galaxy surrounded by a ring of star-forming knots. We utilized observations taken from the Neil Gehrels Swift observatory, Rapid ASKAP Continuum Survey, SuperCOSMOS H Survey, Dark Energy Survey, and VIsible MultiObject Spectrograph at Very Large Telescope to ascertain the association with 4FGL J1647.5 5724 and to explore the connection between the star-forming activities and the observed ray emission. We found that star-formation alone cannot explain the observed ray emission, and additional contribution likely from the pulsars/supernova remnants or buried AGN is required.

``Kathryn's Wheel" is the nearest collisional ring galaxy system and lies within the 95 positional uncertainty region of 4FGL J1647.5 5724. Located in the low-latitude Galactic neighborhood ( |b|=-8^ , distance 10 Mpc), the system includes a dwarf irregular galaxy, LEDA 3080069, acting as the `bullet' that has collided with an edge-on, late-type, spiral galaxy ESO 179 13 e.g., 1987ess..book.....L,2015MNRAS.452.3759P . The system also contains another dwarf irregular galaxy LEDA 3080065 (Figure fig:1 , top left panel). The system has an overall physical size of 15 kpc, a total mass of 6.7 10^9 (H i + stars), and a metallicity of [ O/H ] 0.4 , thus indicating it to be a Magellanic-type system 2015MNRAS.452.3759P .

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 179-IG013, Kathryn's Wheel, as a nearby collisional ring galaxy with perturbed HI tails and first high-resolution HI follow-up.

quotes.quote: ESO 179-IG013: This is an interesting candidate as it appears to have undergone an interaction based on both the optical and the 1 maps (See upper right panels in Fig. fig:mosaic2 ) and is described as collisional ring galaxy by Parker2015 , where they term it ``Kathryn's Wheel". In terms of the galaxy morphology ESO 179-IG013 is classified as SB(s)m. The multiwavelength observations of ESO 179-IG013 by find a ring of star formation based on their H and optical observations (see Fig. 3 in their paper). In addition, identify the system to be the nearest collisional ring to the Milky Way with a systemic velocity of 840 . As part of the 1-RINGS survey, we present for the first time high-resolution 1 follow-up observations for ESO 179-IG013. The 1 velocity map of the system shows clear signs of perturbation with 1 tails extending towards the south-east and north-west ends of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 179 13 as the edge-on spiral member of Kathryn's Wheel, embedded in a star-forming ring but showing little central star formation after the collision.

quotes.quote: Located in the low-latitude Galactic neighborhood ( |b|=-8^ , distance 10 Mpc), the system includes a dwarf irregular galaxy, LEDA 3080069, acting as the `bullet' that has collided with an edge-on, late-type, spiral galaxy ESO 179 13 e.g., 1987ess..book.....L,2015MNRAS.452.3759P .

A complex, diffuse radio morphology was seen overlapping with ``Kathryn's Wheel" though the central galaxy, ESO 179 13, remain undetected down to 0.45 mJy/beam, i.e., 3 detection limit (Figure fig:1 , bottom panel).

The spectacular star-forming ring surrounding the central galaxy, ESO 179 13, is evident, which was also reported by 2015MNRAS.452.3759P . The central galaxy shows little star formation, possibly stripped of gas due to interaction with the `bullet' galaxy LEDA 3080069.

sources.summary: WKK 7460 is identified as the larger member of an interacting system, with its measured profile potentially contaminated by WKK 7463.

quotes.quote: WKK 7460/WKK 7463 is an interacting system with a separation of 1 4 : WKK 7460 ( 198 105 , SL) is the larger component with an optical velocity of 775 36 km s ^ 1 (SH92), while the profile gives v=842 km s ^ 1 . Table  cxgadet gives the full parameters for WKK 7460 only, since the contribution by WKK 7463 ( 82 67 , S) is uncertain. However, considering the types and sizes of the two galaxies as well as the H i velocity as compared to the optical of WKK 7460, we can assume that WKK 7463 contributes to the profile.

gmw_00004115.name: WKK 7465
gmw_00004115.overview: The profile of the galaxy WKK 7465 overlaps with that of WKK 7198, which was detected during its OFF observation [arXiv:0907.4312]. Because of this overlap, the profile of WKK 7465 is truncated, preventing reliable measurements of its line widths and flux [arXiv:0907.4312].

sources.summary: The WKK 7465 profile overlaps with WKK 7198 detected in the OFF observation, preventing reliable width and flux measurements and implying a higher systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: WKK 7465/WKK 7198: WKK 7198 has been detected in the OFF observation of WKK 7465 at d=7 2 , and the profiles overlap. The observation of WKK 7198 (see Fig.  hiprofile ) shows that the profile extends from v 3270 km s ^ 1 to 3540 km s ^ 1 . The profile of WKK 7465 is therefore truncated and no line widths and flux could be derived. The systemic velocity is likely to be higher than the one given.

gmw_00004116.name: 4C +29.70
gmw_00004116.overview: 4C +29.70 is a compact steep-spectrum radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.13069 [arXiv:0903.1899]. Very Large Array observations reveal that the galaxy hosts a highly asymmetric, double radio structure with a projected linear size of 2.45 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0903.1899].

sources.summary: The paper measures stellar absorption features in the CSS radio galaxy 4C +29.70, deriving a velocity dispersion, black-hole mass, redshift, VLA observation, asymmetric double radio structure, and projected size.

quotes.quote: Several stellar absorption features, such as Mg I (5175 ), Ca E band (5269 ) and Na D (5890 ), were detected in the spectra. The stellar velocity dispersion, , of the host galaxy, measured from absorption features is 250 km s^ 1 . If 4C +29.70 follows the M_ BH relation established for nearby galaxies, then its central black hole has a mass of 3.3 10^ 8 M_ .

4C +29.70 is a CSS source of intermediate strength, selected from the 408-MHz Bologna B2.1 catalogue (Saikia et al. \ 2002). Its redshift is z=0.13069 , corresponding to a distance of 476 Mpc ( H_ 0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 and q_ 0 =0.5 ). It was observed in snapshot mode with the Very Large Array (VLA) A-array at 4835 MHz on 1985 February 10 (Saikia et al. \ 2002). The radio structure is highly asymmetric and double, and the total flux density is 263 mJy (Saikia et al. 2002). The largest angular size (LAS) of this source is 1.2 arcsec, corresponding to a projected linear size of 2.45 kpc.

gmw_00004117.name: WISEA J144709.05+724415.5
gmw_00004117.overview: WISEA J144709.05+724415.5 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2009.07270] that is detected as a bright ultraviolet source [arXiv:2302.12527]. It has a star formation rate of 0.6 solar masses per year [arXiv:2009.07270] and hosts a background starburst or H II region [arXiv:2302.12527].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J144709.05+724415.5 as the spiral host of SN 2020faa, measuring host photometry, spectra, mass, star formation rate, and subsolar metallicity.

quotes.quote: obj is positioned on the edge on spiral galaxy WISEA J144709.05+724415.5 which did not have a reported redshift in the NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu catalog, although the CLU catalog CLU has it listed as CLU J144709.1+724414 at the same redshift as our spectroscopy provides below. The sn together with the host galaxy and the field of view is shown in Fig. fig:det . obj was classified as a Type II sn 2020TNSCR.987....1P based on a spectrum obtained on 2020 April 6 with the Liverpool telescope (LT) equipped with the SPRAT (SPectrograph for the Rapid Acquisition of Transients) spectrograph. That spectrum revealed broad H and H in emission, the blue edge being shifted by 9000 km s ^ 1 with respect to the narrow emission line from the galaxy that provided the redshift z=0.041 consistent with CLU as mentioned above.

Host galaxy The results of the SED modeling (Sect. sec:hostsedm ) of the host galaxy is displayed in Fig. fig:host_sed . We obtain a good fit for a galaxy with a mass of 3.2 10 ^ 9 and a star-formation rate of 0.6 per year. This is a relatively regular host galaxy for a Type II sn .

Host galaxy metallicity Using the emission lines from Sect. hostgalaxyspectrum , we can adopt the N2 scale of the calibration using the flux ratio between [ N II ] 6583 and H . We found that 12+ log (O/H) =8.50 0.15 . We also employed another metallicity diagnostic of using [ N II ] , H and [ S II ] lines, in which 12+ log(O/H) =8.40 0.10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J144709.05+724415.5 near SN 2020faa as a GALEX-bright galaxy; spectra trace a background starburst/H II region with young, solar/sub-solar-metallicity line ratios.

quotes.quote: It is located close to a galaxy, WISEA J144709.05+724415.5, also detected by GALEX as a bright UV source.
As we discuss in Sect. sec:host , strong narrow emissions of H I, [ O III ] , and [ S II ] also appear in the spectra at all epochs, originating from a background starburst region.

The resolution in the photometric images is not enough to resolve the region but its emissions are clearly present in the spectra, as can be seen from Fig. fig:fc . To characterise the SN environment, we measured the relative intensity of narrow lines ( [ N II ] 6583 and H , [ O III ] 5007, and H ) and overplotted their ratio on the Baldwin, Phillips Terlevich (BPT) diagram by in the latest six spectra (see Fig. fig:hii ). Here, the solid tracks represent the model grid by for different ages and metallicity. The measured line ratios are mostly consistent with a H II region with an age of 1 Myr and a solar/sub-solar metallicity.

gmw_00004118.name: PG 1630+377
gmw_00004118.overview: PG 1630+377 is a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9903153, arXiv:1307.6799] that features an associated absorption-line system detected in the Lyman series [arXiv:1307.6799]. Spectropolarimetry of the object shows a steep rise in polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/9903153] and a significantly polarized Lyman-alpha line [arXiv:1612.06994].

sources.summary: The paper uses quasar PG 1630+377 as the challenging case for Lyman-edge polarization, comparing observed polarization rises with scattering-skin and outflow models that can resemble or fit the data.

quotes.quote: Especially challenging is the case of PG 1630+377 where the polarization increases from 2 at 1000 to 20 at 700 . Attempts to explain such a steep rise have not been successful (e.g., Blaes Agol 1996; see Koratkar Blaes 1999 for a review). One toy model fitting the data was suggested by Shields, Wobus, Husfeld (1998).

In this case, the predicted behavior of the flux and polarization resembles the data on PG 1630+377 (see Fig. 1). The edge is smeared-out at large inclinations, while it is strongly pronounced at modest inclinations (see Fig. 2). Additional smearing-out may be present due to relativistic effects near a Kerr black hole.
A reasonably good fit to the PG 1630+377 data is obtained for kT 50-100 keV.

sources.summary: PG 1630+377 has an associated absorber detected in Lyman-series and doublet transitions, with Lyman lines reaching zero flux and kinematics distinct between ions.

quotes.quote: PG 1630+377 ( =1.4333) -- This associated absorption-line system is detected in the Lyman series ( ), and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet (Figure fig:pg1630 ). The 1238.821, 1242.804 doublet is covered, but not detected. The is very weak and narrow and does not allow for a proper/definitive determination of partial coverage, as unresolved saturation could yield non-unity coverage fractions. However, the Lyman series down to Ly \ reach zero flux levels, indicating full coverage in the ion. Furthermore, like PG 1444+407 , the kinematics of the appear distinct from the , with significant at v 0 , but no detected absorption at those velocities.

sources.summary: The paper cites PG 1630+377 as a quasar whose HST spectropolarimetry showed strong Ly polarization but no other polarized lines, consistent with Rayleigh scattering though inconclusive.

quotes.quote: One notable example is provided by , who used the Hubble Space Telescope to perform spectropolarimetry of the quasar PG 1630+377. They reported that Ly in this object is significantly polarized with the degree of polarization 7 per cent after correction for unpolarized continuum, whereas no evidence of strong polarization was found in other lines. This is an interesting case because only Ly can be significantly polarized when the main scattering mechanism is Rayleigh. However, their spectral resolution was too insufficient to reveal any polarization structures, which hinders one from drawing a definite conclusion.

gmw_00004119.name: Mrk950
gmw_00004119.overview: Mrk950 is an edge-on spindle galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.0163 [arXiv:1703.00965, arXiv:2312.17417]. It is classified as a post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1703.00965, arXiv:2312.17417] that features a peculiar bar and nucleus [arXiv:1703.00965].

sources.summary: Markarian 950 is the z=0.016328 host of iPTF16fnl, classified as an edge-on spindle galaxy with a peculiar bar/nucleus, stellar mass near 9.7e9 solar masses, and post-starburst age.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of iPTF16fnl is Markarian 950 (Mrk 950) located at z =0.016328 Cabanela1998,Petrosian2007 . Given the edge-on host inclination and the existence of a peculiar bar and nucleus, the galaxy is classified as Sp ( spindle ). The luminosity distance is D _L =66.6 Mpc (distance modulus =  34.12 mag) using H _0 = 69.6 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.29, _ = 0.71 in the reference frame of the 3 K cosmic microwave background CMB; CMB1996ApJ .

The derived K band luminosity of the galaxy is L_K=1.15 10^ 10 . Using the galaxy color u-g=1.5 , we compute a mass to light ratio for the K band, log _ 10 (M/L)=-0.0755 Bell2003ApJS , corresponding to a stellar mass M_ * 9.7 10^ 9 .

The age and metallicity of the host galaxy were determined by fitting a grid of galaxy models from , using stellar evolutionary models from Chabrier2003PASP . The fit was done using the pPXF code 2016arXiv160708538C . The best model was in agreement with a single burst of star formation with an age of 650 300 Myr and a metallicity of Z =0.18 (here, Z=0.2 corresponds to that of the Sun).

sources.summary: Mrk950 is described as the E+A/post-starburst host of iPTF16fnl, with redshift 0.016328, solar metallicity, E(B-V)=0 dereddening, host velocity dispersion, and a young stellar age.

quotes.quote: iPTF16fnl is a relatively faint optical TDE located at the center of an E+A galaxy (Mrk950) at a distance of 66.6 Mpc at redshift 0.016328 2016ATel.9433....1G , 2017ApJ...844...46B , 2018MNRAS.473.1130B . No prior activity in the host galaxy is detected with upper limits of 20-21 mag, whereas the transient is observed with a peak magnitude of 17 mag in the g band.

Since the host galaxy, Mrk950 has the solar metallicity 2017ApJ...844...46B , it is natural to adopt solar abundances as suggested by 2010Ap&SS.328..179G . In our model, we set the He abundance relative to H as a parameter so as to vary it close to its solar value, i.e., 0.1 1998SSRv...85..161G , 2010Ap&SS.328..179G . 2017ApJ...844...46B found the best fit for E(B-V) = 0 after considering the dereddening of the host galaxy.

The host of iPTF16fnl is a post-starburst galaxy with a host velocity dispersion of 89 1 km s^ 1 , which were estimated from the CaII 8544,8664 absorption lines. While the black hole mass inferred from the M_ relation 2013ApJ...764..184M is (2.138 1.87) 10^6 M_ 2017ApJ...844...46B , our model provides the black hole mass to be M_ = (6.73 0.44) 10^5 M_ , which agrees with the previous estimate within its error limit. The age of the host galaxy is 650 300 Myr with the metallicity of Z = 0.18 , which nearly equals the solar metallicity Z_ = 0.2 2017ApJ...844...46B , suggesting that the host galaxy should have more young main-sequence stars.

sources.summary: For iPTF--16fnl, the authors use a late-time VLT/X-shooter spectrum with little TDE contamination, measure a lower velocity dispersion than prior work, and identify it as the fastest-decaying, lowest-mass-black-hole host.

quotes.quote: For iPTF--16fnl, we have obtained a late time spectrum ( 193 days after peak brightness) in which the TDE does not contribute a significant fraction to the total galaxy light on 2016 November 25 (Onori et al. in prep.) with X-shooter Vernet2011 , mounted on UT2 (Kueyen) of the Very Large Telescope (VLT) at Cerro Paranal, Chile. The 1800 s observation (OB ID: 1617353) was performed using an 0 8 slit. The spectral resolution provided by this setup is R = 6200, which yields an instrumental broadening equivalent to = 20 km s ^ 1 at 3900 .

We also note that for iPTF--16fnl there is a discrepancy between our measured value (55 2 km s ^ 1 ) and that of Blago2017 (89 1 km s ^ 1 ), who fit Gaussian lines to the Mg i b and Ca ii triplet simultaneously.
The lowest mass black hole (iPTF--16fnl) hosted the fastest decaying TDE (see Blago2017 ), and the most massive black hole (D3--13) has the slowest decay timescale.

gmw_00004120.name: HCG 95A
gmw_00004120.overview: HCG 95A is an elliptical galaxy that is interacting with HCG 95C [arXiv:astro-ph/0509908]. This interaction has produced two tidal tails in the region connecting the two galaxies, and HCG 95A exhibits hydrogen emission in its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0509908].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 95A as an elliptical galaxy interacting with HCG 95C, lying in a tidal-tail region and showing nuclear H emission.

quotes.quote: By simple eye-inspection of a broad-band image of the group one promptly notices very obvious morphological peculiarities: two tidal tails in the HCG 95A and HCG 95C region, which seems to connect A and C, and a double nucleus in HCG 95C. HCG 95C is most probably an on-going merger of two disk galaxies, also in interaction with HCG 95A (an elliptical galaxy). men94 noted radio and infrared emission as well as morphological distortions in HCG 95B.
vil98 detected H emission in the nuclei of HCG 95A and HCG 95C and in one of the large tidal tails (the eastern tail).

gmw_00004121.name: HCG 95C
gmw_00004121.overview: HCG 95C is an ongoing merger of two disk galaxies that is interacting with the elliptical galaxy HCG 95A [arXiv:astro-ph/0509908]. The galaxy is spectrally classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1509.05779] and features optical shells, a double nucleus, and a mildly disrupted molecular gas structure [arXiv:1509.05779, arXiv:astro-ph/0509908].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 95c as mostly regularly rotating but mildly disrupted, with CO extending into a tidal tail, optical shells, MOHEG emission, high specific star formation, and AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: HCG 95c (Fig fig:hcg95 ) mainly shows regular rotation, but the CO(1--0) emission extends into the tidal tail between HCG 95a and 95c, which is also seen prominently in the 8 m non-stellar emission in the channel map of Fig fig:hcg95 . Shells are also present in the optical light. Spitzer IRAC colors place this galaxy on the edge of the Spitzer IR gap closer to those of dusty spirals, but its H _2 /PAH ratio indicates that it is a MOHEG cluver+13 . Unlike other transition galaxies, this one has a high specific star formation rate.
HCG 95c contains radio emission, and is spectrally classified as an AGN martinez+10 . The molecular gas in HCG 95c is morphologically classified as mildly disrupted.

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 95C as an ongoing merger of two disk galaxies interacting with HCG 95A, associated with infrared emission, nuclear H emission, and tidal-tail activity.

quotes.quote: By simple eye-inspection of a broad-band image of the group one promptly notices very obvious morphological peculiarities: two tidal tails in the HCG 95A and HCG 95C region, which seems to connect A and C, and a double nucleus in HCG 95C. HCG 95C is most probably an on-going merger of two disk galaxies, also in interaction with HCG 95A (an elliptical galaxy). men94 noted radio and infrared emission as well as morphological distortions in HCG 95B.

Reconstructed IRAS frames show infrared emission in this group centered on HCG 95C and enclosing the whole group all96 . An upper limit to the X-ray detection was given by pon96 using ROSAT data. H i was also detected in this group huc00,ver01 . vil98 detected H emission in the nuclei of HCG 95A and HCG 95C and in one of the large tidal tails (the eastern tail). Such tail seems to contain a few tidal dwarf galaxies candidates igl01 .

gmw_00004122.name: HCG 95B
gmw_00004122.overview: HCG 95B is a foreground disk galaxy with a radial velocity of approximately 8000 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0509908]. It exhibits hydrogen emission along its disk and displays morphological distortions likely caused by a nearby dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509908].

sources.summary: The paper treats HCG 95B as a foreground non-member galaxy, confirming its lower radial velocity and noting disk H emission plus morphology likely disturbed by a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: HCG 95B (CGCG 406--067) had H emission detected along its disk igl98 , but at a radial velocity of 8000 km s^ 1 , instead of at the group's velocity. This indicates that this non-member galaxy is in the foreground of the group. Besides the discrepant radial velocity of HCG 95B published by hic92 ( 11637 km s^ 1 ), other similarly erroneous values were published at the RC3 11486 km s^ 1 , dev91 and fou92 ( 11562 km s^ 1 ). However, a spectrum of this galaxy obtained with the 1.52 meter telescope at ESO on November 2002 confirmed the result of igl98 , that its radial velocity is indeed 8000 km s^ 1 and not around 11500 km s^ 1 . According to those authors, the morphological distortions presented by this galaxy would have been caused by a nearby dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00004123.name: GRB 020903 host
gmw_00004123.overview: The GRB 020903 host is a galaxy complex that consists of four interacting components [arXiv:1707.07697]. Spatially resolved observations show that both the gamma-ray burst explosion site and its nearby star-forming regions have comparable sub-solar metallicities [arXiv:1707.07697].

sources.summary: The paper spatially resolves the GRB 020903 host, describing a four-component interacting complex whose explosion site and nearby star-forming regions have comparable sub-solar metallicities.

quotes.quote: GRB 020903 is a long-duration gamma ray burst (LGRB) with a host galaxy close enough and extended enough for spatially-resolved observations, making it one of less than a dozen GRBs where such host studies are possible. GRB 020903 lies in a galaxy host complex that appears to consist of four interacting components. Here we present the results of spatially-resolved spectroscopic observations of the GRB 020903 host.

By taking observations at two different position angles we were able to obtain optical spectra (3600-9000 ) of multiple regions in the galaxy. We confirm redshifts for three regions of the host galaxy that match that of GRB 020903. We measure metallicity of these regions, and find that the explosion site and the nearby star-forming regions both have comparable sub-solar metallicities. We conclude that, in agreement with past spatially-resolved studies of GRBs, the GRB explosion site is representative of the host galaxy as a whole rather than localized in a metal-poor region of the galaxy.

gmw_00004124.name: SDSS J103024.95 + 551622.7
gmw_00004124.overview: SDSS J103024.95 + 551622.7 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy that may represent a young version of a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio galaxy [arXiv:1904.07540]. It features a double-lobed radio structure spanning 100 kpc, a compact core, and possible mid-infrared flux density variability [arXiv:1904.07540].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J103024.95 + 551622.7 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy with double radio lobes, compact core, weak gamma-ray evidence, and possible infrared variability.

quotes.quote: Recently, disc reported the discovery of SDSS J103024.95 + 551622.7, a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy having a 100 kpc scale double-lobed radio structure. Here we analyse archival radio interferometric imaging data taken with the Very Large Array (VLA) at 5 GHz, and with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at 4.3 and 7.6 GHz. Two hotspots and a compact core are detected with the VLA at arcsec scale, while a single milliarcsec-scale compact radio core is seen with the highest resolution VLBA observations.

The Fermi Large Area Telescope did not detect ray emission at the position of this source. In the mid-infrared, the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer satellite light curve, covering more than 7 years and including the most recent data points, hints on flux density variability at 3.4 m. Our findings support the notion that this source is a young version of Fanaroff--Riley type II radio galaxies.

gmw_00004125.name: IRAS 04416+1215
gmw_00004125.overview: IRAS 04416+1215 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1512.02436, arXiv:2202.13839] viewed from an almost face-on orientation [arXiv:2202.13839]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a super-Eddington accretion rate [arXiv:2202.13839, arXiv:2307.16568] and an accretion disk approximately four times larger than standard theoretical predictions [arXiv:2307.16568].

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 04416+1215 as an NLS1 with redward Fe II and H shifts, deriving high virial factors and finding both mass-estimation paths applicable.

quotes.quote: Mrk 493, IRAS 04416+1215 and Mrk 42 have the redward shifted optical Fe ii and H lines with respect to [ O iii ] 5007 . The virialized motions of BLR clouds are assumed to estimate M_ RM in the RM of H for these NLS1s Du14,Wa14 . The redward shifts of H and Fe ii may be the gravitational redshifts. The Fe ii lag is consistent with the H lag for the same source (see Table 3). The Fe ii v_ FWHM is slightly smaller than the H v_ FWHM in IRAS 04416.

A higher mass M_ grav /M_ 9.5 is estimated by equation (7) for IRAS 04416. This larger mass is on the same order of magnitude as the mass estimated by equation (4) for Fe ii when considering its errors. This means that the single broad-line path and the broad-line-to-broad-line comparison path are equally applicable to estimating M_ for IRAS 04416.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 04416+1215 as a high-accreting NLSy1, finding it nearly face-on with narrow lines, soft X-ray excess, ionized outflows, and equatorial-scattering polarization fits.

quotes.quote: Using the FORS2 instrument at the European Southern Observatory's (ESO) Very Large Telescope, We performed spectropolarimetric observations of three NLSy1: Mrk 1044, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, and IRAS 04416+1215. We then modeled the region in the reduced spectra using IRAF spectral fitting procedures and estimated the Stokes parameters and the viewing angles of the three sources. The viewing angles recovered for the three sources indicate that they occupy separate locations in the viewing angle plane, from an almost face-on (IRAS 04416+1215) to an intermediate (SDSS J080101.41+184840.7), to a highly inclined (Mrk 1044) orientation.

recently reported a detailed analysis of the broadband observations of IRAS 04416+1215 ( mag = 16.24, z = 0.089). This source shows a narrow line FWHM = 1670 ; moran_etal_1996 and a very broad [ OIII ] lines FWHM = 1150 ; veron_cetty_2001 . In their work, tortosa_etal_2022 showed that the best-fitting model in the X-ray band is composed of a soft excess, three ionized outflows, neutral absorption, and a reflection component. Prominent soft X-ray excess supports the claim for a super-Eddington accretion rate in this source.

However, for IRAS 04416+1215, which is the source for which we recovered the smallest viewing angle (i.e., 4 ^ o ), we could reconstruct the polarized emission using the model with only the equatorial scattering region.

sources.summary: The paper reports continuum reverberation mapping of IRAS 04416+1215, finding its AGN accretion disk larger than standard Shakura-Sunyaev predictions and about four times the thin-disk model expectation.

quotes.quote: Our analysis on one of the sources- IRAS 04416+1215; based on the broadband observations using the Growth India telescope (GIT), reveals that the size of the accretion disk for this source, calculated by cross-correlating the continuum light curves is larger than expected from the theoretical model. We fit the light curves directly using the thin disk model available in javelin and find that the disk sizes are approximately 4 times larger than expected from the Shakura Sunyaev (SS) disk model. Further studies are needed to understand better the structure and physics of AGN accretion disks and their role in the evolution of galaxies.

Initial results for one of the sources, IRAS 04416+1215, identified as a hyper Eddington accreting source, are presented. Here are the conclusions from this work: The interband continuum lags analysis for IRAS 04416+1215 reveals that the size of the accretion disk is larger than expected from the standard SS disk model. We fit the light curves directly using the thin disk model available in the javelin package, which reveals the disk sizes to be about 4 times larger than the prediction of the theoretical SS disk model.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 04416+1215 in its AGN comparison sample, applying a high-energy cutoff and comparing published Swift and broadband X-ray slopes.

quotes.quote: Three out of these 16 super-Eddington AGNs are part of our Chandra SEAMBHs. present the archival Swift (IRAS 04416+1215, SDSS J074352.02+271239.5) and XMM-Newton (SDSS J100402.61+285535.3) data of these in-common targets. As X-rays can vary, we present the archival data of these three targets as part of the super-Eddington sample and treat them as separate measurements.
For IRAS 04416+1215, we also adopted a high-energy cut-off for the power-law fixed to 44 keV from . An F-test reveals that adding a high-energy cut-off doesn't improve the statistics significantly.

IRAS 04416+1215: finds a photon index of 2.46_ 026 ^ +0.27 in the >2 keV Swift spectrum. A broadband XMM-Newton and NuSTAR study by reveals that the reflected radiation dominates the primary continuum, which exhibits a slope of 1.77.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 04416+1215 as the sample source with the highest Eddington ratio, complex X-ray absorption, soft excess, reprocessed hard emission, and very low coronal temperature.

quotes.quote: IRAS 04416+1215 (z=0.0889) has the highest Eddington ratio in our sample and the highest known Eddington ratio in the local Universe. Its X-ray spectrum shows a multi-phase absorption structure, a prominent soft excess component and a hard X-ray emission dominated by the reprocessed radiation. Moreover, it shows the lowest coronal temperature measured so far by . A comprehensive investigation of the X-ray spectral properties of IRAS 04416+1212 has been carried out in dinosaur .

gmw_00004126.name: J0941+3126
gmw_00004126.overview: J0941+3126 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2009.03583] hosted by the galaxy SDSS J094103.62+312618.7 [arXiv:2009.03583]. The host galaxy has a mid-infrared color consistent with an intermediate disk galaxy [arXiv:2009.03583].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0941+3126 as one of five GRGs, gives its radio position and host, notes high-frequency fluxes, and warns that photometric redshifts make the candidate cautious.

quotes.quote: J0941+3126 This source is also known as B2 0938+31A, and is centred at J2000.0 RA 9 ^ h 41 ^ m 01.24 ^ s DEC +31 ^ 26 ' 32.3 '' Colla1970,Colla1972,Colla1973,Fanti1974 . The source is hosted by SDSS J094103.62+312618.7. The source has a flux density of 20 mJy at 15.2 GHz Waldram2010 , and 7.2 3.3 mJy at 30 GHz Gawronski2010 . Its host has W2-W3>2 , redder than is typical for elliptical galaxies and more consistent with the `intermediate disk galaxy' designation of Jarrett2017 . The host galaxy in this case currently has only photometrically determined redshifts Alam2015,Bilicki2016,Zou2019 , ranged from 0.282 to 0.398.

gmw_00004127.name: J1331+2557
gmw_00004127.overview: J1331+2557 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2009.03583]. Its host galaxy is identified as SDSS J133118.01+235700.4, which features a visible radio core [arXiv:2009.03583].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1331+2557 as a GRG, associates it with an SDSS host, reports archival VLA radio emission and a visible core matched to the host.

quotes.quote: J1331+2557 This source is also known as 7C 1328+2412, and is centred at J2000.0 RA 13 ^ h 31 ^ m 18.12 ^ s DEC +23 ^ 57 ' 07.4 '' Waldram1996 . Its north-east lobe is also known as TXS 1328+242. The host galaxy of this source is identified as SDSS J133118.01+235700.4. We found the source has been observed at 1.4 GHz by the VLA archival public project AG0635 (Fig. fig:AG0635 ). The observation has angular resolution of 19.7 13.7 arcsec, along with source flux density of 172 8 mJy.
The observation shows the source has visible radio core emission cross matched with its host galaxy, and its core flux density is 6 3 mJy.

gmw_00004128.name: J1402+2442
gmw_00004128.overview: J1402+2442 is a giant radio galaxy also known as B2 1400+24 [arXiv:2009.03583]. The host of this source is a close pair of galaxies, though another galaxy is located in close proximity [arXiv:2009.03583].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1402+2442 as a GRG hosted by a close pair of SDSS galaxies, while noting an additional nearby SDSS object as a possible complication.

quotes.quote: J1402+2442 This source is also known as B2 1400+24, and is centred at J2000.0 RA 14 ^ h 02 ^ m 25.87 ^ s DEC +24 ^ 41 ' 53.0 '' Colla1970,Colla1972,Colla1973,Fanti1974 . The host of this source is a close pair of galaxies, SDSS J140224.25+244224.3 and SDSS J140224.31+244226.8. The latter has a photometric redshift z=0.299 0.067 Alam2015 . We note that although we identify the above galaxy pair as the host for this source, SDSS J140225.03+244218.1 is also in close proximity, see Fig. fig:GRG_with_NVSS_contour . This source has a photometric redshift of z=0.208 0.018 Alam2015 .

gmw_00004129.name: J1421+1016
gmw_00004129.overview: J1421+1016 is a giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2009.03583]. Its host galaxy is SDSS J142142.68+101626.2, which is visible in WISE 3.4 micrometer data but not in SDSS-i images [arXiv:2009.03583].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1421+1016 as a GRG previously mentioned but not classified as one, with an SDSS host visible in WISE rather than the shown SDSS-i image.

quotes.quote: J1421+1016 This source is also known as MRC 1419+104, and is centred at J2000.0 RA 14 ^ h 21 ^ m 42.03 ^ s DEC +10 ^ 16 ' 17.3 '' Large1981,Large1991 . This source was mentioned by Amirkhanyan2015 , but not previously identified as a GRG due to differences in the estimation of both the LAS and redshift. This source has host galaxy SDSS J142142.68+101626.2, which is not visible in Fig. fig:GRG_with_NVSS_contour where we show the SDSS-i image, but can be seen clearly in WISE 3.4 m data.

gmw_00004130.name: J1646+3627
gmw_00004130.overview: J1646+3627 is a giant radio galaxy with a high radio luminosity and an ambiguous morphology [arXiv:2009.03583]. Its host galaxy, 2MASX J16464260+3627107, is the brightest cluster galaxy in the cluster GMBCG J251.67741+36.45295 and exhibits a slightly bent radio structure [arXiv:2009.03583].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1646+3627 as a new GRG with ambiguous morphology whose 2MASX host is the brightest cluster galaxy in GMBCG J251.67741+36.45295 and has a slightly bent radio morphology.

quotes.quote: The fifth source, J1646+3627, has an ambiguous morphology. All five sources have comparatively high radio luminosities, with P_ 1.4 W/Hz > 25.1, the mean total radio luminosity of FR II objects as determined by Kozie2011 . Since the host galaxies in each case have W1-W2 < 0.8 and W2-W3 <3.5 , where W1, W2, and W3 are the WISE observed source magnitudes at 3.4 m, 4.6 m and 12 m Cutri2013 , they are likely to be either elliptical or intermediate disk galaxies Jarrett2017 .

J1646+3627 The host galaxy of this source is 2MASX J16464260+3627107. It is the brightest cluster galaxy in the galaxy cluster GMBCG J251.67741+36.45295 Hao2010 and has a slightly bent morphology, see Fig. fig:GRG_with_NVSS_contour . This morphology is consistent with the findings of Garon2019 who used 4304 extended radio sources from RGZ to determine that BCGs have higher probabilities than other cluster members to have slightly bent morphologies.

gmw_00004131.name: SMSS J052915.80--435152.0
gmw_00004131.overview: SMSS J052915.80--435152.0 is a 16th magnitude quasar located at a redshift of z=3.962 [arXiv:2402.15101]. It is currently identified as the most luminous known quasar in the Universe and shows no signs of strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:2402.15101].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMSS J052915.80--435152.0 as the most luminous known quasar, arguing it is unlensed, radio quiet, and powered by an exceptionally massive, rapidly accreting black hole.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the properties of the recently discovered quasar SMSS J052915.80--435152.0, hereafter J0529--4351, which is a 16th magnitude object at redshift z=3.962 (see Fig. image ), and reveal it to be the most luminous quasar currently known in the Universe (see Fig. quasarpop ). Results When quasars appear extremely bright, it may be suspected that their observed brightness is magnified by gravitational lensing from a massive galaxy on the line of sight. Strong lensing causes multiple separate images of a quasar in the sky . Two other quasars with a redshift and apparent brightness similar to J0529-4351 are known to be strongly magnified by lensing, the double-image APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 , with a separation of 0.5 arcsec , and the quadruply imaged B 1422+231 at z=3.62 , with separations up to 1.5 arcsec . Estimated magnification factors for these two objects range from 40 to 100, which implies that these quasars are not intrinsically extreme, but are members of the bulk population .

J0529-4351, in contrast, shows no sign of strong lensing; data from the European Space Agency (ESA) Gaia satellite suggest it to be a point source, in terms of object morphology and astrometric excess noise, which has been used to find dual quasars or lensed quasars that appear unresolved to Gaia (see Figure quasarpop and online methods for more detail). This result makes J0529--4351 the most luminous quasar and by inference, the fastest-growing black hole in the Universe known to date (in terms of mass growth per unit time).

gmw_00004132.name: XMMU J124825.9+083020
gmw_00004132.overview: XMMU J124825.9+083020 is a background BL Lac object located at a redshift of z=0.43 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209500]. Although initially identified as an ultraluminous X-ray source in the nearby galaxy NGC 4698, its optical spectrum and spectral energy distribution confirm its active galactic nucleus classification [arXiv:astro-ph/0209500].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XMMU J124825.9+083020 as a background BL Lac object at z=0.43, not a ULX in NGC 4698, based on optical spectrum and multiwavelength SED.

quotes.quote: Received 5 August 2002; Accepted 20 September 2002 We report the identification of the optical and radio counterparts of the ultraluminous X-ray (ULX) source XMMU J124825.9+083020 (NGC4698-ULX1). The optical spectrum taken with the VLT yields a redshift of z=0.43 , which implies that the ULX is not associated with the nearby galaxy NGC 4698. The spectral energy distribution calculated from the available data indicates that the source is likely to be a BL Lac object.
Classification The absorption features detected in the VLT spectrum clearly places XMMU J124825.9+083020 at z=0.43 . We identify the source as a BL Lac object, for the following reasons.

We argue that the source is most likely a BL Lac object, most likely of the high-frequency peaked category, based on the absence of emission lines, the dilution of the absorption features, the slope of the optical continuum, and the overall shape of the spectral energy distribution.

gmw_00004133.name: ACT-S J020941+001557
gmw_00004133.overview: ACT-S J020941+001557 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.5528 [arXiv:1807.08895]. Spatially resolved observations of its molecular gas reveal a non-axisymmetric distribution that is gravitationally lensed, with a source-plane reconstruction consistent with a rotating disk [arXiv:1807.08895].

sources.summary: The paper maps CO(3-2) in the z=2.5528 lensed DSFG ACT-S J020941+001557, deriving lensing magnification, gas mass, asymmetric molecular gas, and possible disk or merger structure.

quotes.quote: Wollack NASA/Goddard Space Flight Center, Greenbelt, MD 20771, USA We report Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) CO( J = 3 - 2 ) observations of the dusty star-forming galaxy ACT-S J020941+001557 at z = 2.5528 , which was detected as an unresolved source in the Atacama Cosmology Telescope (ACT) equatorial survey. Our spatially resolved spectral line data support the derivation of a gravitational lens model from 37 independent velocity channel maps using a pixel-based algorithm, from which we infer a velocity-dependent magnification factor 7-22 with a luminosity-weighted mean 13 . The resulting source-plane reconstruction is consistent with a rotating disk, although other scenarios cannot be ruled out by our data. After correction for lensing, we derive a line luminosity L^ _ CO(3-2) = (5.53 0.69) 10^ 10 K km s^ 1 pc^ 2 , a cold gas mass M_ gas = (3.86 0.33) 10^ 10 M_ , a dynamical mass M_ dyn sin ^2 i = 3.9^ +1.8 _ 1.5 10^ 10 M_ , and a gas mass fraction f_ gas csc ^2 i = 1.0^ +0.8 _ 0.4 .

We present the first spatially resolved CO( J = 3-2 ) observations of ACT J0209, obtained with NOEMA, and use the pixel-based source reconstruction technique described in tagore2014 and tagore2016 to derive a lens model from the 37 independent velocity channels in our CO data cube. We find that ACT J0209's CO( J = 3-2 ) emission is gravitationally magnified by a factor of 7-22 across the galaxy's velocity profile, with a luminosity-weighted mean 13 consistent with the values found by geach2015 . The source's unexpectedly high line brightness temperature ratio of r_ 3,1 1.6 is most plausibly due to external heating of molecular clouds, although GBT pointing errors may contribute at a modest level; a differential lensing scenario alone is disfavored since the profiles of the GBT CO( J = 1-0 ), NOEMA CO( J = 3-2 ), and ALMA CO( J = 4-3 ) spectra are broadly similar.

The delensed properties of ACT J0209 include a CO( J = 3-2 ) line profile that is still asymmetric across its full FWZI, although less so than the observed spectrum; we conclude that the distribution of molecular gas within the galaxy is not axisymmetric.

gmw_00004134.name: TXS0206 048
gmw_00004134.overview: TXS0206 048 is a quasar at a redshift of approximately 1.1 [arXiv:2310.18406] that is surrounded by a large oxygen-emitting nebula [arXiv:2209.04344, arXiv:2310.18406]. The line-emitting gas in this nebula is highly disturbed over a span of nearly 200 kiloparsecs, with velocity fluctuations consistent with Kolmogorov turbulence [arXiv:2209.04344, arXiv:2310.18406].

sources.summary: The paper finds TXS0206 048 has the largest QSO nebula area, disturbed velocities over nearly 200 kpc, and a second-order VSF consistent with Kolmogorov turbulence.

quotes.quote: Out of the four fields, TXS0206 048 has the largest area. In fact, in terms of the area and the total line emission luminosity of the [ O II ] nebula, TXS0206 048 exceeds the ``Makani" nebula at z=0.459 , the largest [ O II ] nebula detected hitherto Rupke2019 Note that the size and [ O II ] line luminosity of TXS0206 048 reported in Table tab:emission_line_properties were obtained at a surface brightness level of 10^ 19 , significantly lower than the surface brightness threshold of 5 10^ 18 used in . At the level of 5 10^ 18 , the TXS0206 048 [ O II ] nebula has an area 4100 kpc ^2 , slightly smaller than 4900 kpc ^2 covered by ``Makani". . The filtered [ O II ] velocity map of the TXS0206 048 field is shown in the left panel of Figure fig:TXS0206_w_grad , together with its corresponding velocity uncertainty map on the whole 3D mask footprint for comparison.

The observed VSFs of TXS0206 048 The velocity and velocity uncertainty maps of the nebula around TXS0206 048 displayed in Figure fig:TXS0206_w_grad show that the line-emitting gas is highly disturbed with well-determined line-of-sight velocities spanning a wide range from 150 km/s to 300 km/s across the full extent of nearly 200 kpc defined by the narrow stream-like feature toward the northeast and southwest Johnson2022 .
For S_2 of TXS0206 048 obtained using the emission line, we measure a slope of _2=0.72^ +0.12 _ 0.11 .

sources.summary: The paper reuses TXS0206 048 from prior VSF work, notes that only one oxygen line is available at z=1.1, and finds its VSF slope consistent with Kolmogorov turbulence.

quotes.quote: Out of the seven QSO fields, the measurements for four fields (PKS0454 22, J0454 6116, J2135 5316, and TXS0206 048) were presented in Chen2023 . The three newly included fields (PKS0405 123, HE0238 1904, and PKS0552 640) are all part of the MUSE Quasar-field Blind Emitters Survey (MUSEQuBES) program, and we use the MUSE-DEEP datacubes directly downloaded from the ESO phase-3 archive with program IDs 097.A-0089(A) and 094.A-0131(B) PI: J. Schaye; Muzahid2020 .
For TXS0206 048 at z 1.1 , the line is redshifted out of the MUSE spectral window, and therefore only results based on are presented.

Nebulae with _2 consistent with 2/3: the VSF measurements for PKS0405 123 E, HE0238 1904, PKS0552 640 and TXS0206 048 lead to a constrained 2nd-order slope in agreement with the value 2/3. For HE0238 1904 and PKS0552 640, the measurements for both and within the 16 ^ th - 84^ th percentiles are consistent with the Kolmogorov slope. For TXS0206 048, only measurements with are available and the result is consistent with _2=2/3 .

gmw_00004135.name: PKS0454 22
gmw_00004135.overview: PKS0454 22 is a quasar host galaxy surrounded by an extended nebula [arXiv:2209.04344]. This nebula exhibits morpho-kinematic properties suggesting it is primarily composed of interstellar medium stripped during interactions between gas-rich galaxies [arXiv:2209.04344].

sources.summary: The paper measures PKS0454 22 nebular VSFs, finds weaker slope constraints than TXS0206 048, notes varying line ratios, and links its nebula to stripped gas-rich-galaxy material.

quotes.quote: The observed VSFs of PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 For the remaining three fields, PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316, both the and lines are detected in the MUSE cubes. We present the VSF measurements based on both lines, which are shown in Figures fig:PKS0454_o2 fig:J2135_o3 in the Appendix. Constraints on the slopes of the VSFs are summarized in Table tab:slopes . Compared with the results for TXS0206 048, the constraints on the slopes of the VSFs for these three QSO nebulae are weaker.

In particular, while velocity measurements of and for PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 are mostly consistent with each other, there are regions with significantly different values between the two velocity maps, revealing not only that the emission signals are a blend of multiple components along the sightline but also that there exists a large variation in the [ O III ] / [ O II ] line flux ratio between different components.

For PKS0454 22, the morpho-kinematics of the nebula and the continuum sources in the immediate vicinity of the QSO have led to argue that the extended nebula mostly consists of striped ISM through interactions between gas-rich galaxies.

gmw_00004136.name: J0454 6116
gmw_00004136.overview: J0454 6116 is a quasar host galaxy surrounded by a line-emitting nebula [arXiv:2209.04344]. Although two nearby continuum sources are located near the quasar, their velocities are inconsistent with the nebula, suggesting they are not part of a coherent dynamical system [arXiv:2209.04344].

sources.summary: The paper measures J0454 6116 nebular VSFs, finds limited dynamic range and line-ratio variations, excludes inconsistent nearby continuum sources, and reports a relatively flat VSF.

quotes.quote: The observed VSFs of PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 For the remaining three fields, PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316, both the and lines are detected in the MUSE cubes. We present the VSF measurements based on both lines, which are shown in Figures fig:PKS0454_o2 fig:J2135_o3 in the Appendix. Constraints on the slopes of the VSFs are summarized in Table tab:slopes . Compared with the results for TXS0206 048, the constraints on the slopes of the VSFs for these three QSO nebulae are weaker.

In particular, while velocity measurements of and for PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 are mostly consistent with each other, there are regions with significantly different values between the two velocity maps, revealing not only that the emission signals are a blend of multiple components along the sightline but also that there exists a large variation in the [ O III ] / [ O II ] line flux ratio between different components.

However, this scenario of ISM stripping does not seem to be plausible for J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 studied here. For J2135 5316, only two group member galaxies (both far away from the location of the nebula) are found in the QSO field with the current data set (see Table tab:summary_table ). Similarly for the J0454 6116 field, although two continuum sources are found near the QSO, their velocities are inconsistent with the rest of the nebula and are likely not in a coherent dynamical system with the line-emitting gas (see sec:VSF_measurement ). No additional companion continuum sources are found in this field that overlap with the nebula footprint. Interestingly, both J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 exhibit a relatively flat VSF.

gmw_00004137.name: J2135 5316
gmw_00004137.overview: J2135 5316 is a quasar nebula that exhibits an elongated morphology [arXiv:2209.04344]. It resides in a relatively isolated galactic environment with only two neighboring galaxies detected [arXiv:2209.04344].

sources.summary: The paper measures J2135 5316 nebular VSFs, finds limited constraints, elongated morphology, a relatively isolated environment, and interprets its flat VSF as possibly environmental.

quotes.quote: The observed VSFs of PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316 For the remaining three fields, PKS0454 22, J0454 6116 and J2135 5316, both the and lines are detected in the MUSE cubes. We present the VSF measurements based on both lines, which are shown in Figures fig:PKS0454_o2 fig:J2135_o3 in the Appendix. Constraints on the slopes of the VSFs are summarized in Table tab:slopes . Compared with the results for TXS0206 048, the constraints on the slopes of the VSFs for these three QSO nebulae are weaker.
Of the four nebulae studied here, J2135 5316 exhibits an elongated morphology and is most likely affected by such projection effect.

At the same time, recall that the four QSOs reside in a diverse range of galactic environments, with TXS0206 048 in a rich dynamic galaxy group while J2135 5316 in a relatively isolated environment with only two neighboring galaxies found (see Table tab:summary_table ). If galaxy/satellite interactions are a main driver of the turbulent CGM, then a flat VSF found for J2135 5316 may be attributed to the quiescent state of its galactic environment.

gmw_00004138.name: Pegasus V
gmw_00004138.overview: Pegasus V is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located approximately 692 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2204.09068]. It is a distant satellite of Andromeda that contains a sparse red giant branch and a well-populated blue horizontal branch [arXiv:2204.09068].

sources.summary: The paper reports Pegasus V as a newly discovered ultra-faint dwarf satellite of M31, measuring its distance, position, sparse RGB, strong blue HB, and possible reionization-fossil status.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We find a position for Peg V of = 23:18:27.8 0.1 , = 33:21:32 3

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of an ultra-faint dwarf in the constellation of Pegasus. Pegasus V (Peg V) was initially identified in the public imaging data release of the DESI Legacy Imaging Surveys and confirmed with deep imaging from Gemini/GMOS-N. The colour-magnitude diagram shows a sparse red giant branch (RGB) population and a strong over-density of blue horizontal branch stars. We measure a distance to Peg V of D=692^ +33 _ 31 kpc, making it a distant satellite of Andromeda with M_V=-6.3 0.2 and a half-light radius of r_ half =89 41 pc.

We find a position for Peg V of = 23:18:27.8 0.1 , = 33:21:32 3 , a half-light radius of r_ half =0.44^ +0.2 _ 0.1 arcmin, and do not resolve an ellipticity. A visual inspection of the CMD for Peg V presents us with a sparse RGB and a well-populated HB feature. Given its projected distance from Andromeda of D_ proj 245 kpc, the most likely scenario is that Peg V is an M31 satellite.

As blue HB stars are typically considered to trace ancient stellar populations, this could imply Peg V was quenched very early compared to the other M31 dwarfs, making it a good candidate for a reionisation fossil bovill09 .

gmw_00004139.name: IC0512
gmw_00004139.overview: IC0512 is a star-forming spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 27 Mpc [arXiv:2110.04306]. It is the host galaxy of the transient SN 2021fcg, which was discovered on its outskirts at a physical separation of 7.5 kpc from the nucleus [arXiv:2110.04306].

sources.summary: IC0512 is identified as the star-forming spiral host of SN 2021fcg, with the transient offset 7.5 kpc and host redshift and distance used in the analysis.

quotes.quote: SN 2021fcg was discovered by the Zwicky Transient Facility in the star-forming galaxy IC0512 at a distance of 27 Mpc.

Host Galaxy and Extinction SN 2021fcg is located on the outskirts of the star forming spiral galaxy IC0512 at a physical separation of 7.5 kpc (angular separation 53 ) from the nucleus (Figure fig:disc ). The host galaxy has a redshift of z = 0.005384 and a heliocentric velocity of 1614 10 km s ^ 1 Kourkchi2017 . Correcting for the Virgo Infall, Shapley cluster and Great Attractor Mould2000 gives a distance modulus = 32.14 0.15 mag (we use H _ 0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 in this paper) Theureau07 report a Tully-Fisher distance modulus of 31.82 0.41 mag using H _ 0 = 57 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00004140.name: S555
gmw_00004140.overview: S555 is a core-dominated radio source and the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell S555 [arXiv:2403.03974]. Its molecular gas is poorly resolved and concentrated near the radio core [arXiv:2210.14922], showing high-velocity absorption from gas moving toward the galaxy center [arXiv:2210.14922] and signs of ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2403.03974].

sources.summary: S555 has poorly resolved molecular gas concentrated near its radio core, with high-velocity absorption interpreted as gas moving toward the galaxy center.

quotes.quote: S555 's molecular gas is poorly resolved and no velocity structure is discernible. However, the gas is heavily concentrated and centered around 200 pc from the radio core, with weak emission extending out to approximately 3 kpc. The total mass is 5.6 0.2 10^ 8 M _ .

In this case, the properties of the absorption indicate that it is not due to the same population of clouds responsible for the emission. The molecular column density is 130 M _ /pc ^ 2 for the gas causing the emission, so the probability of an alignment is small but not insignificant. However, the actual column density of the absorbing gas is 10 times higher than this, and no molecular emission is found at velocities matched to the absorption lines. Indeed, any absorption which does come from the typical gas clouds would be embedded within the emission -- at least 200 km/s below where the absorption is actually present. The gas is therefore moving towards the galaxy centre at high velocity, and a velocity which is significantly different to the gas seen in emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Abell S555 BCG as a core-dominated radio source with recent central activity, offset asymmetric molecular gas, likely ram-pressure stripping, and redshifted absorption.

quotes.quote: Abell S555 Galaxy and environment The brightest cluster galaxy of Abell S555 lies at the centre of a poorly studied low X-ray luminosity cluster Bohringer2004 . Its strong central radio source is core dominated, and has a significant gamma-ray flux density. Powerful recent activity has been inferred in the galaxy centre due to its radio spectral energy distribution Hogan2015b .

The continuum source and supermassive black hole are therefore offset from the molecular gas in terms of both location and velocity. This indicates a past gravitational disturbance which has led to ram pressure forces stripping the AGN of its surrounding molecular gas.

gmw_00004141.name: Abell 1644
gmw_00004141.overview: Abell 1644 is a brightest cluster galaxy situated at the center of a gravitationally disturbed cluster [arXiv:2403.03974]. Its central radio source is highly variable [arXiv:2403.03974], and it contains molecular gas clumps distributed along an arc that matches X-ray sloshing patterns [arXiv:2210.14922, arXiv:2403.03974].

sources.summary: Abell 1644 contains molecular gas in clumps extending from the galaxy center, with absorption attributed to ordinary clouds associated with the emission.

quotes.quote: Abell 1644 has 1.6 0.3 10^ 9 M _ of molecular gas, the bulk of which is contained in four main clumps distributed along an arc extending out from the galaxy centre. This arc of gas has been spatially matched to X-ray emission, suggesting it has cooled from the hot intracluster medium Baek2022 . Two of the gas clumps are within approximately 200 pc of the radio core, while the others are at radii of approximately 7 and 10 kpc.

The absorption in Abell 1644 is close to the peak of the spatially matched emission. This implies that the absorption is due to a sample of the same clouds responsible for the emission. The velocity dispersion is relatively high, ( = 11 km/s, FWHM = 26 km/s), though from more recent ALMA observations of HCO ^ + , we know this is due to it containing multiple components (Rose et. al, in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper describes the Abell 1644 BCG as a disturbed-cluster central galaxy with variable radio continuum, molecular clumps along X-ray sloshing patterns, and absorption from gas separate from emission.

quotes.quote: Abell 1644 Galaxy and environment The brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 1644 is a complex system at the centre of the brighter of two X-ray peaks in its gravitationally disturbed host cluster Johnson2010 . In recent years the central radio source has been highly variable, with its 15 GHz flux increasing from around 160 mJy in early 2018 to around 240 mJy in early 2021 Rose2022 . Molecular emission Abell 1644's molecular emission has previously been detected in CO(1-0), but in CO(2-1) we are now able to see it with much higher angular resolution and sensitivity.

Based on CO(1-0) emission, Baek2022 identify clumps of molecular gas in an arc along the sloshing patterns detected in X-ray emission. With our CO(2-1) observations, we are able to re-detect all but the farthest of these clumps at higher angular resolution.

gmw_00004142.name: RXC J1603.6+1553
gmw_00004142.overview: RXC J1603.6+1553 is a brightest cluster galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.14922]. It contains molecular gas centered on its radio core that extends across a diameter of approximately 5 kpc with an irregular velocity structure [arXiv:2210.14922].

sources.summary: RXC J1603.6+1553 has radio-core-centered molecular gas with weakly resolved extension, no clear velocity structure, and no detected absorption.

quotes.quote: RXC J1603.6+1553 has 1.0 0.1 10^ 10 M _ of molecular gas, with the brightest emission centred on its radio core. The gas is weakly resolved spatially but appears to extend across a diameter of around 5 kpc. There is no clear velocity structure.

In the three galaxies in our sample which contain emission but no absorption (Fig. fig:maps_of_emitters ), one has a smooth velocity structure (RXC J1350.3+0940), while two are irregular (MACS 1931.8-2634 and RXC J1603.6+1553). Although there is no detectable absorption in these systems, we have provided upper limits in Table tab:gas_densities .

gmw_00004143.name: J2339
gmw_00004143.overview: J2339 is a quasar field that exhibits a bimodal galaxy velocity distribution [arXiv:2403.03983]. This velocity structure suggests the presence of two distinct galaxy groups that may be starting or undergoing a merger event [arXiv:2403.03983].

sources.summary: J2339 is a CUBS quasar field whose galaxy velocities appear bimodal, so the paper reports the group nearer the quasar redshift.

quotes.quote: The velocity dispersion ranges from 150--600 km s ^ 1 (except for J2339), which suggests our initial velocity search range is sufficient.

The velocities of galaxies in the field of J2339 show a bimodal distribution, suggesting there might be two individual galaxy groups perhaps starting or undergoing a merger event. When fitted with two normal distributions, we find two distinct groups of 7 (closer to the quasar redshift) and 6 galaxies with similar velocity dispersion of 350 km s ^ 1 . While 1000 km s ^ 1 ( _ Vel of J2339 fitted with a single Gaussian) is still within a reasonable range for a galaxy group/cluster, 300 km s ^ 1 is more consistent with other groups and better describes the bimodal velocity distribution. We report the group properties of the group closer to the quasar redshift in Table tab:group and plot both velocities in the remaining figures of this paper.

gmw_00004144.name: J0119
gmw_00004144.overview: The field of J0119 contains a galaxy group centered around a quasar [arXiv:2403.03983]. The discovery of six additional nearby galaxies in the field makes J0119 one of the most overdense groups in its sample [arXiv:2403.03983].

sources.summary: J0119 is described as one of the most overdense CUBS quasar groups after additional nearby galaxies were found in supplementary redshift data.

quotes.quote: The only two exceptions are the fields J0114 and J0119. The additional galaxy found in the J0114 field lies at the edge of the velocity distribution and thus increased the velocity dispersion by 30 when included. For the field of J0119, six additional galaxies were found in the LDSS3 FOV near the quasar redshift, which made J0119 one of the most overdense groups in the sample. The estimated velocity dispersion of the galaxy group around J0119 decreases by 20 when including the six additional galaxies.

gmw_00004145.name: J0248
gmw_00004145.overview: J0248 is a quasar host galaxy situated within a galaxy group [arXiv:2403.03983]. This group is characterized by an unusually low velocity dispersion and a potentially underestimated dark matter halo mass [arXiv:2403.03983].

sources.summary: J0248 is singled out as an outlier quasar group with unusually low velocity dispersion and possibly underestimated halo mass in the BH-galaxy-halo comparison.

quotes.quote: The J0248 galaxy group appears to be an outlier of the sample, the velocity dispersion is much smaller than other galaxy groups with a similar number of galaxies, and the halo mass 10^ 12.1 M _ might be underestimated. The observed velocity dispersion may be much smaller by chance, or J0248 could be an outlier in the BH-galaxy-halo relations, but these hypotheses can not be tested without more data on individual quasars. The right panel of Figure fig:mhalo_ms_mhalo shows the stellar mass-halo mass relation. We note that the host galaxy stellar mass and halo mass are derived independently, the former from the M_ BH M_ , bulge relation and the latter from group velocity dispersion.

gmw_00004146.overview: The object 0723+679 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0606659] that hosts a kiloparsec-scale X-ray jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0606659]. X-ray observations of its core reveal a curved continuum [arXiv:astro-ph/0606659] and show evidence of an iron K emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0302265, arXiv:astro-ph/0606659].

sources.summary: The paper models 0723+679 as a powerful radio-loud quasar with a kpc-scale X-ray jet, curved core X-ray continuum, Fe-line evidence, and dedicated XMM/EPIC observations.

quotes.quote: Here we tackle this issue using new, deep \ and \ observations of the cores of three powerful radio loud quasars: 1136--135, 1150+497 ( ), and 0723+679 ( ), in the redshift range z =0.3--0.8. These sources are known from our previous \ snapshot survey to have kpc-scale X-ray jets. In 1136--135 and 1150+497, evidence is found for the presence of diffuse thermal X-ray emission around the cores, on scales of 40--50 kpc and with luminosity L _ 0.3-2 keV 10^ 43 , suggesting thermal emission from the host galaxy or a galaxy group. The X-ray continua of the cores in the three sources are described by an upward-curved (concave) broken power law, with photon indices _ soft 1.8-2.1 and _ hard 1.7 below and above 2 keV, respectively. There is evidence for an unresolved \ line with EW 70 eV in the three quasars.

We observed 0723+679 with \ on April 11, 2005 for a total exposure of 46.9 ks with the EPIC pn, and 46 ks with the MOS. The count rate of the source in 0.3--10 keV is 0.378 0.005 /s with the pn and 0.101 0.002 /s with the MOS. As not enough counts were collected with the RGS ( < 300) for a detailed analysis, here we concentrate on the EPIC and OM data only.

sources.summary: 3C 179 has noisy high-redshift spectral coverage, a weak narrow component with large uncertainty, and boundary Population A/B metrics possibly affected by low signal-to-noise or orientation.

quotes.quote: In 3C 94, PKS 0420-01, and 3C 179, with the highest redshift in the sample, the region is close to the edge of the observed spectrum and thus affected by more noise. This resulted in larger uncertainties in the determination of parameters related to the line profile.
