quotes.quote: An example of a stream in a distant galaxy for which kinematics have been measured and an orbit derived orbit fitting is that of the Umbrella Galaxy (NGC 4651) NGC 4651 . foster2014 followed up the low surface-brightness surface brightness imaging of MD2010 with even deeper imaging from the Subaru/Suprime-Cam instrument. Figure n4651 shows images from this study, which reveal a ``stick'' feature extending out to its terminus at a broad arc to the left of the main galaxy. On the opposite side of the disk (right side of the upper panel in Figure n4651 ), additional shell-like shells features are clearly seen. foster2014 estimated the total stellar mass in the tidal debris to be 4 10^8 M_ , constituting about 1/50th the stellar mass of NGC 4651.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4651 as a normal-background galaxy at about 27 Mpc, far from the Virgo core, with SKB and HI-based distances agreeing within errors.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

On the other hand, NGC 4651 is located at 27 Mpc and is clearly a background galaxy. It is interesting to compare our stellar kinematics-based distance estimates with Solanes's HI-based distance estimates. In figure statdiff , we show the correlation between Solanes's distances and the ratio between the SKB distances and Solanes's, with 3-- error bars. >From inspection of this figure, we conclude that NGC 4293, NGC 4450, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4651 have SKB distances that agree within the errors with those of Solanes.

The case of NGC 4651 is clearer: this galaxy is very far from the core of Virgo (+10.3 _ 1.2 ^ 0.5 Mpc), and its star formation class is ``Normal", indicating that it has not been affected by the ICM medium, so it probably correspond to a background galaxy rather than a Virgo member.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4651 as molecule-poor for a normal galaxy, linking its unusual gas kinematics and tidal features to recently accreted gas.

quotes.quote: Focusing first on the top row of normal galaxies, aside from NGC 4567 (interacting, as discussed in subsec:RHIMS ), NGC 4651 seems to be molecule-poor for an normal galaxy. The kinematic position angles of the \ inside and outside the optical disc of NGC 4651 are different chung09 , and it has stellar and \ tidal features chung09,martinez-delgado10,morales18 , suggesting that much of its \ disc could have been recently accreted. Thus, the molecular gas content likely does not reflect that of an normal galaxy.

gmw_00004179.name: MaNGA galaxy M-8341-09101
gmw_00004179.overview: MaNGA galaxy M-8341-09101 is a spiral galaxy located at a distance of 216.3 Mpc [arXiv:2306.09854]. Although considered a weak-criteria Milky Way twin candidate due to its similar stellar mass and optical radius, its rotation curve and radial oxygen abundance distribution make it more similar to NGC 2903 [arXiv:2306.09854].

sources.summary: The paper considers M-8341-09101 a weak-criteria Milky Way twin candidate, but finds its oxygen abundance distribution makes it more similar to NGC 2903 than to the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: The distance to the MaNGA galaxy M-8341-09101 is 216.3 Mpc. The central oxygen abundance in M-8341-09101 is 12 + log(O/H) _ 0 = 8.63, and the oxygen abundance at the optical radius is 12 + log(O/H) _ R_ 25 = 8.33. The comparison of the radial distribution of the oxygen abundances in the galaxy M-8341-09101 and in the Milky Way is shown in panel (a) of Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh . One can see that the difference between the radial distribution of the oxygen abundances in the two galaxies is significant. The optical radius of M-8341-09101 ( R_ 25 = 13.3 kpc) is close to the radius of the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc).

The stellar mass of M-8341-09101 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.75) is close to that of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). The comparison between the rotation curves in M-8341-09101 and the Milky Way is shown in panel (b) of Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh . Comparison between Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh and Fig. figure:ngc2903--oh suggested that the properties (rotation curve and the radial abundance distribution) of the galaxy M-8341-09101 and NGC 2903 are close to each other, that is, the galaxy M-8341-09101 is more similar to NGC 2903 than to the Milky Way.

gmw_00004180.name: MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703
gmw_00004180.overview: MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703 is a spiral galaxy that was previously suggested to be a Milky Way-like system based on its star formation and chemical evolution histories [arXiv:2306.09854]. However, it is not considered a true Milky Way twin because its radial oxygen abundance distribution differs significantly [arXiv:2306.09854].

sources.summary: The paper uses M-7495-12704 as an AGN-type MaNGA example, mapping BPT-classified spaxels, noting possible northern LIER contamination, and tracing declining central gas velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Panel a of Fig. figure:bpt-definition shows the location of the spaxels with the AGN-like, H ii region-like, and intermediate spectra on the image of the galaxy M-7495-12704, where the circumnuclear region is of the AGN type. The BPT classification of each spectra is color-coded. The spaxels with the AGN-like (intermediate) spectra in the circumnuclear region (center) and in the disc are shown by different colors. The circle marks the kinematic center of the galaxy. Panel b of Fig. figure:bpt-definition shows a map of the observed (non corrected for instrumental profile) gas velocity dispersion.

This seems take place in the galaxy M-7495-12704, the north part of the circumnuclear region may be an adjoining LIER (panel a of Fig. figure:bpt-definition ). Panel of Fig. figure:rg-param-example shows that the observed (non corrected for the instrumental profile) gas velocity dispersion decreases with galactocentric distance up to some radius R_ _ gas and remains approximately constant beyond this radius.

sources.summary: The paper rechecks M-8983-03703, previously labeled Milky-Way-like, and rejects it as a twin because its radial oxygen abundance distribution differs strongly from the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Zhou2023 found that the MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703 is a Milky Way-like galaxy in the context of their star formation and chemical evolution histories. Hence, this galaxy is also considered here. NGC 3521 The galaxy NGC 3521 is an SABb galaxy (morphological type code T = 4.0 0.2). It is included in The H i Nearby Galaxy Survey (THINGS) list Walter2008 . We adopted the following parameters of NGC 3521 used or obtained by the THINGS survey: distance d = 10.7 Mpc; \ optical radius of 4.16 arcmin; physical optical radius R_ 25 = 12.94 kpc Walter2008 ; inclination angle i = 73 ; position angle of the major axis PA = 340 deBlok2008 ; stellar mass M_ = 5.01 10 ^ 10 M _ , or log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.70 Leroy2008 ; and rotation velocity V_ rot = 227 km s ^ 1 deBlok2008,Leroy2008 . The black hole mass in NGC 3521 is log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.85 0.58 Davis2014 . The measurements of the emission lines, which are necessary for the determination of the abundance through the R calibration, are available for only three H ii regions in the NGC 3521 Bresolin1999 . However, NGC 3521 was taken into consideration for two reasons: First, McGaugh2016 noted that NGC 3521 is the closest structural analogue to the Milky Way, having a similar luminosity, scale length, and rotation curve. Second, the measurements of the emission lines R_ 2 and R_ 3 , which allow for estimation of the abundance through the P calibration, are available for ten H ii regions in NGC 3521 Zaritsky1994 . The oxygen abundances determined through the R calibration in three H ii regions from Bresolin1999 are denoted with the triangles in panel (a) of Fig. figure:ngc3521--oh . In order to estimate the oxygen abundances using the oxygen lines R_ 2 and R_ 3 measured by Zaritsky1994 in ten H ii regions in NGC 3521, we obtained the calibration relation (O/H) _ P = f ( R_ 2 ,R_ 3 ) for the upper branch Pilyugin2001,Pilyugin2005 using the calibration data points from Pilyugin2016 and supplemented by the recent measurements of H ii regions in NGC 5457 from Croxall2016 and Esteban2020 lll 12+ (O/H)_P & = & 8.6059 + 0.4143 P & + & (0.2662 - 1.2014 P) X & - & (0.6786 - 0.8121 P) X^2 , where P = R_ 3 /R_ 23 is the excitation parameter, R_ 23 = R_ 3 + R_ 3 , and X = log(1 + R_ 23 ). The oxygen abundances determined through the P calibration in H ii regions from Zaritsky1994 are shown with plus signs in panel (a) of Fig. figure:ngc3521--oh . The radial distribution of those oxygen abundances was approximated by the relation 12+ ( O/H) = 8.786( 0.041) - 0.494( 0.069) R_ g with a scatter of 0.042 dex for the 10 data points. Thus, the values of the optical radii of NGC 3521 ( R_ 25 = 12.9 kpc) and the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc) are close to each other. The value of the stellar mass of the NGC 3521 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.7) is close to the value of the stellar mass of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). The mass of the black hole at the centre of NGC 3521 (log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.85) is close to that in the Milky Way (log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.62). The radial distributions of the oxygen abundances (panel (a) of Fig. figure:ngc3521--oh ) and rotation curves (panel (b) of Fig. figure:ngc3521--oh ) in those galaxies agree satisfactorily with each other. However, in order to make a solid conclusion as to whether the galaxy NGC 3521 is a twin of the Milky Way, the radial distribution of the R calibration-based abundances in NGC 3521 should be established, that is, the measurements of the spectra including emission lines necessary for abundance determinations through the R calibration should be carried out. The distance to NGC 3521 and its optical radius should also be more precise. NGC 4651 The galaxy NGC 4651 is an Sc galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.1 0.6). The inclination angle of NGC 4651 is i = 53 , and the position angle of the major axis is PA = 77 Epinat2008 . The optical radius is 1.99 arcmin RC3 . At the distance of d = 19.0 Mpc Foster2014 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 11.00 kpc. The stellar mass is M_ = 2.61 10 ^ 10 M_ , or log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.42 Leroy2019 (or M_ = 1.7 10 ^ 10 M_ or log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.23 Foster2014 ). The rotation velocity of NGC 4651 is 215 km s ^ 1 Epinat2008 . The radial distribution of the oxygen abundances estimated through the R calibration in H ii regions from Skillman1996 was approximated by the relation 12+ ( O/H) = 8.652( 0.039) - 0.377( 0.083) R_ g with a scatter of 0.039 dex for the six data points (panel (a) in Fig. figure:ngc4651--oh ). The values of the optical radii of NGC 4651 ( R_ 25 = 11.0 kpc) and the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc) are close to each other. The radial distributions of the oxygen abundances (panel (a) of Fig. figure:ngc4651--oh ) and the rotation curves (panel (b) of Fig. figure:ngc4651--oh ) in those galaxies agree satisfactorily to each other. While the rotation velocity of NGC 4651 is rather close to that of the Milky Way, the value of the stellar mass of NGC 4651 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.42) is appreciably lower (by a factor of approximately two, or even more) than the stellar mass of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). If the estimations of the stellar mass of NGC 4651 are correct, then NGC 4651 can be considered a low(-stellar) mass evolutionary analogue of the Milky Way. NGC 2903 The galaxy NGC 2903 is an Sbc spiral galaxy (morphological type code T = 4.0 0.1). Its inclination angle is i = 65 , and the position angle of the major axis is PA = 204 deBlok2008 . The optical radius of NGC 2903 is R_ 25 = 5.87 arcmin Walter2008 . The distance to NGC 2903 is d = 8.9 Mpc Drozdovsky2000 , and it results in a physical optical radius of R_ 25 = 15.21 kpc. The stellar mass of NGC 2903 based on the mean value from Jarrett2019 and Leroy2021 and rescaled to the adopted distance is M_ = 3.33 10 ^ 10 M_ , or log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.52. The mass of the black hole in NGC 2903 is log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 7.06 ^ +0.28 _ 7.06 vandenbosch2016 . The rotation velocity of NGC 2903 is 215 km s ^ 1 deBlok2008 . The abundance gradient traced by the R calibration-based abundances in the H ii regions from the compilation in Pilyugin2014 is 12+ ( O/H) = 8.707( 0.018) - 0.338( 0.035) R_ g with a scatter of 0.047 dex for the 25 data points. The values of the optical radii of NGC 2903 ( R_ 25 = 15.21 kpc) and the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc) are close to each other, and the rotation curves (panel (b) of Fig. figure:ngc2903--oh ) in those galaxies agree satisfactorily to each other. However, the value of the stellar mass of NGC 2903 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.52) is appreciably lower than the stellar mass of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). There is also an appreciable difference between the radial distributions of the oxygen abundances (panel (a) of Fig. figure:ngc2903--oh ) in the galaxies. Thus, NGC 2903 is a Milky Way twin only to some extent. MaNGA galaxy M-8341-09101 The distance to the MaNGA galaxy M-8341-09101 is 216.3 Mpc. The central oxygen abundance in M-8341-09101 is 12 + log(O/H) _ 0 = 8.63, and the oxygen abundance at the optical radius is 12 + log(O/H) _ R_ 25 = 8.33. The comparison of the radial distribution of the oxygen abundances in the galaxy M-8341-09101 and in the Milky Way is shown in panel (a) of Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh . One can see that the difference between the radial distribution of the oxygen abundances in the two galaxies is significant. The optical radius of M-8341-09101 ( R_ 25 = 13.3 kpc) is close to the radius of the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 12.0 kpc). The stellar mass of M-8341-09101 (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.75) is close to that of the Milky Way (log( M_ /M_ ) = 10.716). The comparison between the rotation curves in M-8341-09101 and the Milky Way is shown in panel (b) of Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh . Comparison between Fig. figure:M-8341-09101--oh and Fig. figure:ngc2903--oh suggested that the properties (rotation curve and the radial abundance distribution) of the galaxy M-8341-09101 and NGC 2903 are close to each other, that is, the galaxy M-8341-09101 is more similar to NGC 2903 than to the Milky Way. MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703 Zhou2023 found that the MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703 is a Milky Way-like galaxy in the context of their star formation and chemical evolution histories. Fig. figure:M-8983-03703--oh shows the comparison between the radial oxygen abundance distributions in the MaNGA galaxy M-8983-03703 and the Milky Way. The field-of-view of M-8983-03703 measurement covers the central part of the galaxy only. There is another MaNGA measurement (M-7495-12704) of this galaxy that covers a much larger fraction of the galaxy. The radial oxygen abundance distribution obtained from M-7495-12704 is also shown in Fig. figure:M-8983-03703--oh .

One can see that the difference between the radial distributions of the oxygen abundances in M-8983-03703 (and M-7495-12704) and in the Milky Way is very large. Hence M-8983-03703 cannot be considered as Milky Way twin candidate.

gmw_00004181.name: MaNGA galaxy M-8934-12701
gmw_00004181.overview: MaNGA galaxy M-8934-12701 is an evolutionary Milky Way analogue located at a distance of 240.8 Mpc [arXiv:2306.09854]. While it shares a similar rotation velocity and oxygen abundances with the Milky Way, its optical radius of 18.85 kpc is significantly larger [arXiv:2306.09854].

sources.summary: The paper classifies M-8934-12701 as an evolutionary Milky Way analogue with similar oxygen abundances and rotation velocity, but a much larger optical radius than the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: The MaNGA galaxy M-8934-12701 is at a distance of 240.8 Mpc. The characteristics of M-8934-12701 are the following: the stellar mass is log M_ = 10.75; the rotation velocity is V_ rot = 235 km s ^ 1 ; the optical radius is R_ 25 = 18.85 kpc; the central oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) _ 0 = 8.74; and the oxygen abundance at the optical radius is 12 + log(O/H) _ R_ 25 = 8.28. Fig. figure:m-8934-12701 shows the maps and radial distributions of the properties in the MaNGA galaxy M-8934-12701.

Close examination of Fig. figure:emwa-smwa showed that six out of seven eMWAs are not sMWAs. The values of the stellar mass and rotation velocity in three eMWAs are relatively close to that in the Milky Way, but their optical radii are significantly larger in comparison to the optical radius of the Milky Way ( R_ 25 = 23.18 kpc in NGC 1232, R_ 25 = 27.25 kpc in MGC 6744, and R_ 25 = 18.85 in the MaNGA galaxy M-8934-12701 versus R_ 25 = 12.0 in the Milky Way).

gmw_00004182.name: JO147
gmw_00004182.overview: JO147 is a massive galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus that is undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2309.00449]. Observations of its stripped tail have detected molecular gas characterized by a narrow CO linewidth [arXiv:2309.00449].

sources.summary: The paper describes JO147 as a massive GASP ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with resolved disk rotation, active nucleus, significant molecular gas in its stripped tail, and narrow tail CO linewidth.

quotes.quote: For the massive galaxy A3558 S167 (GASP JO147) the double horn profile indicates that the disk rotation is resolved in velocity. All the galaxies that have a GASP counterpart (i.e. signs of stripped emitting gas) except the low mass A3558 S124 (JO159), have a significant emission of molecular gas, with S/N ranging from 4 to 16. When a signal was detected, the observations reached the desired S/N level using a significantly lower integration time, with respect to what we could predict assuming that cluster galaxies were poor, i.e. having a molecular gas content 3 below the average content of galaxies with a similar stellar mass. This immediately confirms that in most cases our /optical selected sample of candidate ram-pressure stripped galaxies are not deficient. For the APEX pointings centered on the supposed gas tails identified either following the extension or the tail, we obtained a significant detection in 3/8 pointings, namely A3376 S64, A3558 S167 (JO147) and A85 S64 (JO200), shown in Fig. fig:spectra_2 , together with the marginal emission in the tail of A3558 S60 (JO157), which has S/N=2.7. All the other tail pointings have a low S/N (from 1.6 to 2.4) and are characterized by a relatively narrow linewidth. In order to be consistent with our previous works GASPX,Moretti_2020 , we used the measured line fluxes to calculate then the mass using the following equation from WatsonKoda2016 : M_ H_2 M_ = 3.8 10^3 _ 10 4.3 r_ 21 0.7 ^ 1 S_ 21 dv D_L ^2 where _ 10 is the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor expressed in M_ pc^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 Bolatto2013 , r_ 21 is the CO(2-1)-to-CO(1-0) flux ratio, S_ 21 is the CO integrated line flux in Jy and D _L is the luminosity distance in Mpc. As done in GASPX we used _ 10 =4.3 , i.e. the standard Milky Way value corresponding to a conversion factor of 2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 including the helium correction. As for the r_ 21 , we adopted the same value (0.79) that we used in our APEX GASPX and ALMA GASPXXII,Moretti_2020 data analysis for the sake of comparison. To take into account the systematic uncertainty on the factor, we calculated a lower limit on the mass obtained by assuming that its value is 50 lower than the canonical Milky Way factor, while higher values are less probable in massive, metal-rich galaxies Bolatto2013 . Errors calculated in this way are shown in the last column of Tab. tab:results . The molecular gas content of the sample galaxies varies between 10 ^9 and 2 10 ^ 10 in galaxies where the line is measured with sufficient S/N. The molecular gas masses calculated in this way are systematically 5 larger than those calculated using the equation, which explicitly takes into account the dependence on redshift, being M_ H_2 M_ = L'_ CO = 3.25 10^7 S_ CO v _ obs ^ 2 D_L ^2 1+z ^ 3 where S_ CO v is the velocity integrated flux in Jy , _ obs is the observed line frequency at the source redshift z, and D_L is the corresponding luminosity distance. In this formulation the major source of uncertainty comes from the conversion value, which includes both the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor and the r_ 21 line ratio, and is typically assumed to be equal to 5.5 Jachym2014 . These values are, in any case, within the errors of our measurements. This allows a straightforward comparison with the recent determination of the molecular gas content/fraction in Virgo galaxies by , who also uses the IMF to determine galaxy stellar masses. Fig. fig:fh2 shows the position of our targets in the molecular gas fraction versus stellar mass plane, where we indicated with different colors galaxies with (green) and without (magenta) star formation in the tail. The red squares refer to the 4 GASP galaxies undergoing strong RPS analyzed in . It can be immediately seen that 50 of the observed sample of selected galaxies (9/19) with asymmetries lie above the relation found for normal star forming galaxies in the field (blue dashed region from Saintonge2017 ) and in Virgo (grey dots). Among the galaxies closer to the relation for field galaxies, A85 S64 (JO200) possesses a MUSE counterpart, which follows its spiral arms. We covered this emission with a second APEX pointing, which reveals the presence of molecular gas. The morphology of this galaxy closely resembles the one of the JO201 GASP galaxy GASPII , which shows unwinding spiral arms possibly caused by the RPS GASPXXIX . If we include the molecular gas mass in the tail in the global budget, then the point would move even closer to the upper edge of the distribution of field galaxies in the M _ f _ H_2 plane (see Fig. fig:fh2 ). Among the galaxies located below the relation in Fig. fig:fh2 , only one has a clear detection (A3376 S45) indicating that this galaxy is deficient. This galaxy also shows no emission from the tail region. The other galaxies located below the Saintonge2017 relation are characterized by a low signal-to-noise (shaded triangles), irrespective of their stellar mass. One of these, the GASP galaxy JO159, has been classified as an initial stripping phase (Poggianti+, in prep.). It is clear that when the asymmetry is coupled with having SF in the tail, then in 75 (6/8) of the cases with S/N > 3 the content is enhanced within the stellar body (83 = 10/12 if we include the Moretti_2020 galaxies). In particular, galaxies classified by GASP as jellyfishes (red squares and green dots surrounded by a black square in Fig. fig:fh2 ), i.e. those having an ionized gas tail at least as long as the stellar disk and therefore being at their peak stripping phase, show a strongly enhanced molecular gas fraction. Among these, A3558 S167(GASP JO147) is the richest massive galaxy (see also Merluzzi2013 ) and, together with the galaxy JO49, also rich, it hosts an active nucleus, as the other massive jellyfishes in GASP poggianti2017,Peluso+2021 .

Molecular gas in the stripped tails The molecular gas content measured in the pointings covering the expected gaseous tail amount to 10 ^ 9 in all the three sources with S/N > 3, namely A3376 S64, A3558 S167 (JO147) and A85 S64 (JO200). Interestingly, two of these tails belong to very massive GASP galaxies (JO200 and JO147) and exhibit a narrow CO linewidth ( 90 and 130 , respectively), while the third one is a low-mass galaxy showing a broad linewidth.

sources.summary: The paper describes JO159 as a low-mass GASP galaxy in an initial stripping phase, lacking significant molecular emission relative to most GASP counterparts and lying near the field relation.

quotes.quote: All the galaxies that have a GASP counterpart (i.e. signs of stripped emitting gas) except the low mass A3558 S124 (JO159), have a significant emission of molecular gas, with S/N ranging from 4 to 16. When a signal was detected, the observations reached the desired S/N level using a significantly lower integration time, with respect to what we could predict assuming that cluster galaxies were poor, i.e. having a molecular gas content 3 below the average content of galaxies with a similar stellar mass. This immediately confirms that in most cases our /optical selected sample of candidate ram-pressure stripped galaxies are not deficient. For the APEX pointings centered on the supposed gas tails identified either following the extension or the tail, we obtained a significant detection in 3/8 pointings, namely A3376 S64, A3558 S167 (JO147) and A85 S64 (JO200), shown in Fig. fig:spectra_2 , together with the marginal emission in the tail of A3558 S60 (JO157), which has S/N=2.7. All the other tail pointings have a low S/N (from 1.6 to 2.4) and are characterized by a relatively narrow linewidth. In order to be consistent with our previous works GASPX,Moretti_2020 , we used the measured line fluxes to calculate then the mass using the following equation from WatsonKoda2016 : M_ H_2 M_ = 3.8 10^3 _ 10 4.3 r_ 21 0.7 ^ 1 S_ 21 dv D_L ^2 where _ 10 is the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor expressed in M_ pc^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 Bolatto2013 , r_ 21 is the CO(2-1)-to-CO(1-0) flux ratio, S_ 21 is the CO integrated line flux in Jy and D _L is the luminosity distance in Mpc. As done in GASPX we used _ 10 =4.3 , i.e. the standard Milky Way value corresponding to a conversion factor of 2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 including the helium correction. As for the r_ 21 , we adopted the same value (0.79) that we used in our APEX GASPX and ALMA GASPXXII,Moretti_2020 data analysis for the sake of comparison. To take into account the systematic uncertainty on the factor, we calculated a lower limit on the mass obtained by assuming that its value is 50 lower than the canonical Milky Way factor, while higher values are less probable in massive, metal-rich galaxies Bolatto2013 . Errors calculated in this way are shown in the last column of Tab. tab:results . The molecular gas content of the sample galaxies varies between 10 ^9 and 2 10 ^ 10 in galaxies where the line is measured with sufficient S/N. The molecular gas masses calculated in this way are systematically 5 larger than those calculated using the equation, which explicitly takes into account the dependence on redshift, being M_ H_2 M_ = L'_ CO = 3.25 10^7 S_ CO v _ obs ^ 2 D_L ^2 1+z ^ 3 where S_ CO v is the velocity integrated flux in Jy , _ obs is the observed line frequency at the source redshift z, and D_L is the corresponding luminosity distance. In this formulation the major source of uncertainty comes from the conversion value, which includes both the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor and the r_ 21 line ratio, and is typically assumed to be equal to 5.5 Jachym2014 . These values are, in any case, within the errors of our measurements. This allows a straightforward comparison with the recent determination of the molecular gas content/fraction in Virgo galaxies by , who also uses the IMF to determine galaxy stellar masses. Fig. fig:fh2 shows the position of our targets in the molecular gas fraction versus stellar mass plane, where we indicated with different colors galaxies with (green) and without (magenta) star formation in the tail. The red squares refer to the 4 GASP galaxies undergoing strong RPS analyzed in . It can be immediately seen that 50 of the observed sample of selected galaxies (9/19) with asymmetries lie above the relation found for normal star forming galaxies in the field (blue dashed region from Saintonge2017 ) and in Virgo (grey dots). Among the galaxies closer to the relation for field galaxies, A85 S64 (JO200) possesses a MUSE counterpart, which follows its spiral arms. We covered this emission with a second APEX pointing, which reveals the presence of molecular gas. The morphology of this galaxy closely resembles the one of the JO201 GASP galaxy GASPII , which shows unwinding spiral arms possibly caused by the RPS GASPXXIX . If we include the molecular gas mass in the tail in the global budget, then the point would move even closer to the upper edge of the distribution of field galaxies in the M _ f _ H_2 plane (see Fig. fig:fh2 ). Among the galaxies located below the relation in Fig. fig:fh2 , only one has a clear detection (A3376 S45) indicating that this galaxy is deficient. This galaxy also shows no emission from the tail region. The other galaxies located below the Saintonge2017 relation are characterized by a low signal-to-noise (shaded triangles), irrespective of their stellar mass. One of these, the GASP galaxy JO159, has been classified as an initial stripping phase (Poggianti+, in prep.). It is clear that when the asymmetry is coupled with having SF in the tail, then in 75 (6/8) of the cases with S/N > 3 the content is enhanced within the stellar body (83 = 10/12 if we include the Moretti_2020 galaxies). In particular, galaxies classified by GASP as jellyfishes (red squares and green dots surrounded by a black square in Fig. fig:fh2 ), i.e. those having an ionized gas tail at least as long as the stellar disk and therefore being at their peak stripping phase, show a strongly enhanced molecular gas fraction. Among these, A3558 S167(GASP JO147) is the richest massive galaxy (see also Merluzzi2013 ) and, together with the galaxy JO49, also rich, it hosts an active nucleus, as the other massive jellyfishes in GASP poggianti2017,Peluso+2021 . Noticeably, also the GASP galaxy JW108, which has been classified as a truncated disk, i. e. a galaxy where the ionized gas emission comes from a region smaller than the extent of the stellar disk, typical of the latest stage of gas stripping, lies above the relation. In fact, because of the more advanced stage of stripping of this galaxy, most of the gas is probably removed or got converted into , resulting in a very faint emission (below the detection threshold) in this galaxy, while still showing an increased amount of cold molecular gas in its disk (while the detection in the tail has a low S/N). In fact, besides JO159, the only GASP galaxy not lying above the field relation is A3376 S75 (A3376 B 0261) which is a control sample galaxy in GASP and thus shows a pretty normal molecular gas content.

gmw_00004183.name: JO49
gmw_00004183.overview: JO49 is a jellyfish galaxy characterized by an ionized-gas tail that is at least as long as its stellar disk [arXiv:2309.00449]. The galaxy is rich in molecular gas and hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2309.00449].

sources.summary: The paper identifies JO49 as a rich GASP jellyfish galaxy with an ionized-gas tail, active nucleus, and no MeerKAT-derived cold gas measurement in this sample.

quotes.quote: These two galaxies are JO49 in A168 and JO60 in A1991 and have been both classified as jellyfishes by GASP, meaning that they have an ionized gas tail at least as long as the stellar disk. For them, our analysis is limited to the molecular gas content, but cannot include the content. Tab. tab:targets_data contains the list of the observed targets as well as their optical coordinates, spectroscopic redshifts, and stellar masses. The stellar masses have been derived from the existing optical spectroscopy either as fiber spectroscopy from the WINGS/OmegaWINGS spectra, corrected for aperture effects to a total mass, or from the IFU integral within the galaxy disks for the galaxies that possess MUSE data from GASP . When none of these information were available we used the stellar masses derived in using the optical photometry. We also give in Col. 6 of Tab. tab:targets_data the flag indicating whether the galaxy possess a star forming tail or not: we defined as star forming any tail with UV/ or B-band emission (where the comes from the GASP data, when available), while we tagged as non star-forming those asymmetric emission without any evidence of young stars in the tail direction. Tab. tab:targets_data also contains the GASP name, when available, the classification of the galaxy according to the MUSE optical data (Poggianti+, in prep.) and the MeerKAT identification and mass. Finally, the last column refers to the morphology classified according to Yoon+2017 , that we will discuss in Sec. sec:coldgasratio where we relate the properties of the two cold gas phases. = . The last column shows the classification according to Yoon+2017 . The optical maps (2 x 2 ) of all our targets are shown in Fig. fig:maps_sf and Fig. fig:maps_nsf , representing galaxies with and without a star forming tail, respectively. Black circles in Fig. fig:maps_sf and Fig. fig:maps_nsf show the APEX pointings. At the observed frequencies (220 GHz) the FWHM of the APEX primary beam is 28 , which corresponds to scales from 25 to 30 kpc for the different targets. For 8 targets out of the 19 observed we also have a dedicated APEX pointing centred on the supposed emitting gas tail, which is also shown in Fig. fig:maps_sf and Fig. fig:maps_nsf . HI data We used the MeerKAT observations from the MeerKAT Galaxy Cluster Legacy Survey MGCLS; Knowles+2022 . The MGCLS consists of long-track (6–10 hour) observations of 115 galaxy clusters in the Southern Sky using the L-band receiver (900 – 1670 MHz). The observed band was sampled with the 4k mode of the SKARAB correlator with 4096 channels that are 209 kHz ( 44 ) wide in total polarisation. We processed the data over the frequency range, covering clusters A85, A3376 and A3558, and their background, 1300 MHz - 1410 MHz, centred at 1356 MHz. The uv-data were reduced using the standard procedure with the Containerised Automated Radio Astronomical Calibration CARAcal; Jozsa+2020 pipeline https://caracal.readthedocs.io . It is built using a radio interferometry framework, stimela https://github.com/SpheMakh/Stimela makhathini2018 , which is based on container technologies and Python. Various open-source radio interferometry software packages within this framework are available in the same script, such as CASA CASA , WSclean wsclean,wsclean2 , AOFlagger aoflagger , Cubical cubical , SoFiA sofia,sofia2 , PyBDSF pybdsf , MeqTrees meqtrees and Crystalball https://github.com/caracal-pipeline/crystalball . The uv-data were Fourier transformed into a final cube with a pixel size of 6 \ and a field of view (FOV) of 2 deg ^ 2 . We used natural weighting with Briggs robust parameter, = 0.5 and a uv-tapering of 20 \ to optimise the surface brightness sensitivity. The resulting cubes have a rms of = 0.2 mJy/beam. The sidelobes of the synthesised beam were removed by iteratively using SoFiA to produce 3D clean masks and imaging with WSclean while cleaning within the masks down to 0.5 . The restoring Gaussian PSF of each cube has an average FWHM of ( _ maj _ min ) 30 \ 33 \ with a position angle, PA 150 deg. With these data we reach a column density sensitivity of n _ = 2.9 10^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 at 3 assuming a linewidth of 44 . We then explored the final cube using the SoFiA source finder sofia with the smooth and clip (S+C) SoFiA method, searching for emission brighter than 3 . The masses of detected galaxies were calculated using the following: M_ = 2.36 10^ 5 D^ 2 S_ v dv , where the total integrated flux, Svdv is expressed in Jy , and D is the distance to the galaxy in Mpc. We adopt the luminosity distance using the optical velocities of the clusters, i.e. D_L=245, 212, 202 Mpc for A85, A3558 and A3376, respectively. The derived masses are shown in the 8 ^ th column of Tab. tab:targets_data . APEX data The APEX data have been collected during ESO P108 under the program 0108.A-0511A (P. I. Moretti), that was awarded 184 hours of observational time in priority B. Observations have been carried out during the period from August 2021 to December 2021, and the total observational time spent on the requested targets was 88.7 hours. Each source has been observed to the requested noise level limit which is dependent on the galaxy stellar mass, and goes from 0.14 mK for the low mass galaxies to 0.25 mK for the high mass ones. These limits correspond to molecular gas fractions 0.5 dex lower than typical field galaxies Saintonge2017 . We observed the ^ 12 CO(2-1) transition ( _ rest =230.538 GHz), using the nFLASH230 Instrument tuned to the CO line redshifted frequencies for each target. The observations have been performed in a symmetric Wobbler switching mode, with maximum separation between the ON and OFF-beam of 100 arcsec. The spectra calibrated by the APEX on-line calibration pipeline (in antenna temperature scale; T _ A ^ * ), have been checked and analysed using different spectral resolutions, going from 10 to 150 km/s. First-order baselines, defined in a line-free band about 2000 km/s wide, have been subtracted from the spectra. The antenna temperatures have been converted to main-beam brightness temperatures (T _ mb =T _ A ^ * / _ mb ), using _ mb =0.75. As shown in Fig. fig:maps_sf and Fig. fig:maps_nsf , the APEX pointing is encompassing the entire galaxy stellar extent in most cases, and we therefore are confident that with our measurements we are not missing any significant cold gas detection within the stellar disk. For each source and each spectral resolution we derived the peak temperature (main-beam brightness temperature, T_ mb ), the linewidth and the position of the CO line relative to the velocity derived from the optical redshift with a single Gaussian fit, as we are here mostly interested in the integrated line flux. Some of our spectra show, though, hints for a double-horn profile, typical of rotating discs. The extracted CO(2-1) spectra for the central pointing of 14/19 targets with S/N > 3 are shown in Fig. fig:spectra_1 , black lines, with the gaussian fit superimposed in red. The grey shaded region indicates the rms of the measurement, and has been derived from a 2000 km/s wide spectral range, after excluding the 1.5 region centered on the gaussian. . The grey shaded region is the rms of the observations in the plot range, after excluding the line emission. The CO fluxes have been calculated assuming a conversion factor of S _ /T _ mb = 39 Jy beam ^ 1 K ^ 1 for the APEX telescope. The Precipitable Water Vapour (PWV), the on--source time, and the results of the fitting procedure of the observations are given in Tab. tab:results . When multiple observations were present with significantly different conditions we included in the final dataset the ones that, combined, were giving the highest signal-to-noise and we list in this case the median PWV. The fourth column of the table indicates the velocity resolution (channel width) of the fitted spectrum. The errors on the fluxes have been calculated taking into account both the rms of the spectrum and the linewidth (FWHM) determined with the given velocity resolution _V , as Err_ flux = _ rms FWHM FWHM _V To this term we added a 10 error due to the spectral calibration. One of the nineteen target galaxies (A3376 S84) did not show any significant detection at the expected frequency, and we therefore estimated only an upper limit to its CO flux, and to the corresponding molecular gas mass. We calculated these quantities by using the rms of the observation and a linewidth of 300 km/s, which is the value predicted by the Tully-Fisher relation Gnedin+2007 for a galaxy with stellar mass of 10 ^ 11 and seen at 60 deg in the sky-plane. Among the other 18 galaxies 4 have an emission detected with a low S/N ( <3 ). The derived CO velocities of the target galaxies are very close to those inferred from the optical redshift, indicated in Fig. fig:spectra_1 by a blue vertical solid line. The difference between the two velocities are indicated in Col. 8 of Tab. tab:results , and are mostly below the velocity resolution. For the galaxies having a MeerKAT detection we also show the velocity as a dashed line, which is often closer to the molecular gas velocity with respect to the optical stellar one. The largest differences in systemic velocity are found in galaxies that appear nearly face-on. For the massive galaxy A3558 S167 (GASP JO147) the double horn profile indicates that the disk rotation is resolved in velocity. All the galaxies that have a GASP counterpart (i.e. signs of stripped emitting gas) except the low mass A3558 S124 (JO159), have a significant emission of molecular gas, with S/N ranging from 4 to 16. When a signal was detected, the observations reached the desired S/N level using a significantly lower integration time, with respect to what we could predict assuming that cluster galaxies were poor, i.e. having a molecular gas content 3 below the average content of galaxies with a similar stellar mass. This immediately confirms that in most cases our /optical selected sample of candidate ram-pressure stripped galaxies are not deficient. For the APEX pointings centered on the supposed gas tails identified either following the extension or the tail, we obtained a significant detection in 3/8 pointings, namely A3376 S64, A3558 S167 (JO147) and A85 S64 (JO200), shown in Fig. fig:spectra_2 , together with the marginal emission in the tail of A3558 S60 (JO157), which has S/N=2.7. All the other tail pointings have a low S/N (from 1.6 to 2.4) and are characterized by a relatively narrow linewidth. In order to be consistent with our previous works GASPX,Moretti_2020 , we used the measured line fluxes to calculate then the mass using the following equation from WatsonKoda2016 : M_ H_2 M_ = 3.8 10^3 _ 10 4.3 r_ 21 0.7 ^ 1 S_ 21 dv D_L ^2 where _ 10 is the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor expressed in M_ pc^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 Bolatto2013 , r_ 21 is the CO(2-1)-to-CO(1-0) flux ratio, S_ 21 is the CO integrated line flux in Jy and D _L is the luminosity distance in Mpc. As done in GASPX we used _ 10 =4.3 , i.e. the standard Milky Way value corresponding to a conversion factor of 2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 including the helium correction. As for the r_ 21 , we adopted the same value (0.79) that we used in our APEX GASPX and ALMA GASPXXII,Moretti_2020 data analysis for the sake of comparison. To take into account the systematic uncertainty on the factor, we calculated a lower limit on the mass obtained by assuming that its value is 50 lower than the canonical Milky Way factor, while higher values are less probable in massive, metal-rich galaxies Bolatto2013 . Errors calculated in this way are shown in the last column of Tab. tab:results . The molecular gas content of the sample galaxies varies between 10 ^9 and 2 10 ^ 10 in galaxies where the line is measured with sufficient S/N. The molecular gas masses calculated in this way are systematically 5 larger than those calculated using the equation, which explicitly takes into account the dependence on redshift, being M_ H_2 M_ = L'_ CO = 3.25 10^7 S_ CO v _ obs ^ 2 D_L ^2 1+z ^ 3 where S_ CO v is the velocity integrated flux in Jy , _ obs is the observed line frequency at the source redshift z, and D_L is the corresponding luminosity distance. In this formulation the major source of uncertainty comes from the conversion value, which includes both the CO-to- H_2 conversion factor and the r_ 21 line ratio, and is typically assumed to be equal to 5.5 Jachym2014 . These values are, in any case, within the errors of our measurements. This allows a straightforward comparison with the recent determination of the molecular gas content/fraction in Virgo galaxies by , who also uses the IMF to determine galaxy stellar masses. Fig. fig:fh2 shows the position of our targets in the molecular gas fraction versus stellar mass plane, where we indicated with different colors galaxies with (green) and without (magenta) star formation in the tail. The red squares refer to the 4 GASP galaxies undergoing strong RPS analyzed in . It can be immediately seen that 50 of the observed sample of selected galaxies (9/19) with asymmetries lie above the relation found for normal star forming galaxies in the field (blue dashed region from Saintonge2017 ) and in Virgo (grey dots). Among the galaxies closer to the relation for field galaxies, A85 S64 (JO200) possesses a MUSE counterpart, which follows its spiral arms. We covered this emission with a second APEX pointing, which reveals the presence of molecular gas. The morphology of this galaxy closely resembles the one of the JO201 GASP galaxy GASPII , which shows unwinding spiral arms possibly caused by the RPS GASPXXIX . If we include the molecular gas mass in the tail in the global budget, then the point would move even closer to the upper edge of the distribution of field galaxies in the M _ f _ H_2 plane (see Fig. fig:fh2 ). Among the galaxies located below the relation in Fig. fig:fh2 , only one has a clear detection (A3376 S45) indicating that this galaxy is deficient. This galaxy also shows no emission from the tail region. The other galaxies located below the Saintonge2017 relation are characterized by a low signal-to-noise (shaded triangles), irrespective of their stellar mass. One of these, the GASP galaxy JO159, has been classified as an initial stripping phase (Poggianti+, in prep.). It is clear that when the asymmetry is coupled with having SF in the tail, then in 75 (6/8) of the cases with S/N > 3 the content is enhanced within the stellar body (83 = 10/12 if we include the Moretti_2020 galaxies). In particular, galaxies classified by GASP as jellyfishes (red squares and green dots surrounded by a black square in Fig. fig:fh2 ), i.e. those having an ionized gas tail at least as long as the stellar disk and therefore being at their peak stripping phase, show a strongly enhanced molecular gas fraction. Among these, A3558 S167(GASP JO147) is the richest massive galaxy (see also Merluzzi2013 ) and, together with the galaxy JO49, also rich, it hosts an active nucleus, as the other massive jellyfishes in GASP poggianti2017,Peluso+2021 . Noticeably, also the GASP galaxy JW108, which has been classified as a truncated disk, i. e. a galaxy where the ionized gas emission comes from a region smaller than the extent of the stellar disk, typical of the latest stage of gas stripping, lies above the relation. In fact, because of the more advanced stage of stripping of this galaxy, most of the gas is probably removed or got converted into , resulting in a very faint emission (below the detection threshold) in this galaxy, while still showing an increased amount of cold molecular gas in its disk (while the detection in the tail has a low S/N). In fact, besides JO159, the only GASP galaxy not lying above the field relation is A3376 S75 (A3376 B 0261) which is a control sample galaxy in GASP and thus shows a pretty normal molecular gas content. On the other hand, when the asymmetry manifests in galaxies without SF in the tail the content within the stellar body tend to be normal/low in 50 (3/6) of the cases (magenta dots in Fig. fig:fh2 , representing detections with S/N > 3). If we include the low S/N detections, this percentage increases to 67 (6/9). Our result seems to suggest that the different gas proportion and displacement follow a temporal evolution: first the galaxy is hit by the ram pressure and starts developing the asymmetry, possibly losing molecular gas as well (at least the more diffuse component). At the same time, in the regions of the disc compressed by ram pressure the is efficiently converted in molecular gas in the disk, and by the time we see the ionized gas tail the molecular gas fraction of the galaxy is significantly enhanced. This increase in the molecular gas fraction seems to be preserved also in galaxies in advanced stripping stage, as is the case of A3376 JW108. This result is perfectly in agreement with the detection of large cold gas reservoir in post-starburst (PSB) galaxies French+2015,Rowlands+2015,Alatalo+2016 , under the hypothesis that these objects belong to the evolutionary sequence caused by the RPS GASPXXIV,Werle+2022 . In fact, in PSB galaxies the cold gas is thought to be present in a diffuse phase not contributing to the star formation. Molecular gas in the stripped tails The molecular gas content measured in the pointings covering the expected gaseous tail amount to 10 ^ 9 in all the three sources with S/N > 3, namely A3376 S64, A3558 S167 (JO147) and A85 S64 (JO200). Interestingly, two of these tails belong to very massive GASP galaxies (JO200 and JO147) and exhibit a narrow CO linewidth ( 90 and 130 , respectively), while the third one is a low-mass galaxy showing a broad linewidth. All the low S/N tail detections, instead, are related to galaxies with low stellar masses (logM _ =10.1-10.5 ). This result suggests that in our sample, when the ram-pressure is able to produce a long gas tail visible at optical/UV wavelengths, a cold molecular gas counterpart is detectable. In low mass galaxies the amount of cold gas in the tail is proportionally lower implying a more difficult detection. The nFLASH APEX detector, though, can only observe the CO(2-1) line emission, usually associated with the dense gas, while the less dense phase could be more easily stripped and found along the tails. Unfortunately, APEX can not observe this transition, and further data (e.g. ALMA) are needed to evaluate the diffuse molecular gas content in ram-pressure stripped tails. For the majority (16/19) of the galaxies having a molecular gas mass determination from APEX, we also have the cold gas content, as derived from MeerKAT data Two galaxies in the sample are not covered by MeerKAT observations (A169 JO49 and A1991 JO60) .

gmw_00004184.name: SDSS J1237 + 0126
gmw_00004184.overview: SDSS J1237 + 0126 is a quasar that hosts a small, possibly underestimated helium II proximity zone [arXiv:2101.01196]. This small zone size is likely caused by a blended, optically thin hydrogen I system, while strong nitrogen V absorbers may indicate a high-velocity outflow [arXiv:2101.01196].

sources.summary: The paper says SDSS J1237 + 0126 has a small possibly underestimated He II proximity zone, affected by an optically thin H I system and possible outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1237 + 0126 has a blended optically thin H i system at v 500 km s ^ 1 that is likely responsible for its small measured He ii proximity zone. No high-velocity infall is detected. The He ii transmission at 1500 - 2200 km s ^ 1 may belong to the proximity zone. However, provided that our simulations approximately capture the density and velocity field around the quasar host halo, our strict and simple definition of R_ pz ensures a one-to-one comparison to our models (Section sect:models ) that include cases of misestimated proximity zone sizes due to the density field and peculiar velocities, similar to recent work on z_ em 6 H i proximity zones eilers17,eilers20 . The strong N v absorbers at 5312 and 5481 km s ^ 1 may either indicate a much larger proximity zone of SDSS J1237 + 0126 or a high-velocity outflow.

Quasars with more precise redshifts ( _v 400 km s ^ 1 ) have significantly narrower t_ on posteriors, except SDSS J1237 + 0126 whose He ii proximity zone size may have been underestimated (Section sect:proxzones ).

gmw_00004185.name: B3 J2330+3927
gmw_00004185.overview: B3 J2330+3927 is a type II radio galaxy at a redshift of z=3.087 [arXiv:astro-ph/0507489]. It features a flat-spectrum radio core, an asymmetric radio structure with a faint counterjet, and is surrounded by a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:astro-ph/0507489].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B3 J2330+3927 as a z=3.087 type II radio galaxy with a flat-spectrum core, weak counterjet, asymmetric radio structure, and massive gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: We report on 1.7 and 5.0 GHz observations of the z=3.087 radio galaxy B3 J2330+3927, using the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA), and archival 1.4 and 8.4 GHz Very Large Array (VLA) data. Our VLBA data identify a compact, flat spectrum ( _ 1.7 GHz ^ 5 GHz = -0.2 0.1; S_ ^ ) radio component as the core. The VLA images show that the fraction of core emission is very large ( f_c 0.5 at 8.4 GHz), and reveal a previously undetected, very faint counterjet, implying a radio lobe flux density ratio R 11 and a radio lobe distance ratio Q 1.9. Those values are much more common in quasars than in radio galaxies, but the optical/near-IR spectra show a clear type II AGN for B3 J2330+3927, confirming that it is indeed a radio galaxy.

The 113 GHz observations of DB03 with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer revealed CO J=4-3 emission peaking at a position coincident with component a , and at a velocity that closely corresponded to the velocity shift of an associated H I absorber seen in . This strongly suggested that both CO emission and H I absorption originated from the same gas reservoir surrounding the host galaxy. DB03 estimated a molecular hydrogen mass M(H _2 ) = 7 10^ 10 , indicating that the host galaxy must be surrounded by a massive gas reservoir.

gmw_00004186.name: 3C455
gmw_00004186.overview: 3C455 is a radio galaxy historically classified as a quasar, though it has also been interpreted as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0610042]. It features a triple radio structure with a jet [arXiv:0906.1729, arXiv:astro-ph/0610042] embedded in an extended region of low-brightness emission [arXiv:0906.1729].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C455 as a galaxy with significant repolarisation, low rest-frame rotation measure, and extended low-brightness radio emission contributing at lower frequencies.

quotes.quote: In particular, the galaxy 3C455 shows a measured repolarisation greater than the 3 level between 10.45 GHz and 2.64 GHz together with a small value of the RM _ rf (194 rad m ^ 2 ), which is an indication of the influence of a foreground screen, possibly an extended cloud with [ OII ] emission detected by Hes et al. (1996). A plausible mechanism would be the effect of shear layers caused by the interaction between the surface of an expanding source and the surrounding medium (Burn 1966). The source 3C455 was observed with the VLA at 8.35 GHz by Bogers et al. (1994). It shows a triple structure with the indication of a jet joining the core with the south western lobe. The three components are almost aligned along the source major axis extending up to about 4 .

However, 3C455 appears slightly resolved by the NVSS, which has a restoring beam of 45 , suggesting that the three components imaged by Bogers et al. are actually embedded in a more extended region of low brightness emission. This can be seen in the image available in the VLA Low-Frequency Sky Survey (VLSS; Cohen et al. 2007) at 74 MHz, which shows an even more extended structure of about 2 in size. The existence of this extended emission is supported by the source spectral index, which indicates an upturn towards higher flux density above 100 MHz.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 455 as a quasar or narrow-line Seyfert-like object with jet-enhanced narrow-line emission and an alternative host-bulge black-hole-mass estimate.

quotes.quote: The QSO, 3C 455, requires some elaboration. This object has a narrow H (FWHM = 620 km/s), yet the nuclear luminosity is far stronger than a narrow line radio galaxy and its magnitude is more typical of a strong Seyfert 1 galaxy, hence its historical classification as a quasar . Implicit in the first estimate in table 1 is that the object is a narrow line Seyfert 1, but H appears weak compared to [ OIII ] 5007 (H / [ OIII ] =0.2 and H / [ OIII ] >0.33 in narrow line Seyfert1's) because the narrow line region is greatly enhanced by emission line gas that is excited by the jet and is aligned with the radio structure . In support of this interpretation, there is an elongated patch of diffuse emission enveloping the kpc radio jet axis between the two lowest contours of the HST image in with a flux approximately equal to the narrow line flux in the F702W filter . Alternatively, one can interpret the object as a narrow line radio galaxy and M_ bh can be estimated from the galactic host bulge luminosity.

gmw_00004187.name: HUDF-2
gmw_00004187.overview: The high-redshift galaxy HUDF-2 displays an inner disk signature [arXiv:1602.01846] and a highly asymmetric morphology in near-infrared bands [arXiv:1602.01846]. Distinctive surface-brightness bumps suggest the galaxy may have experienced a recent merger event [arXiv:1602.01846].

sources.summary: HUDF-2 shows an inner disk signature, distinctive surface-brightness bumps, possible merger-related low-surface-brightness structure, and residuals requiring a double-Sersic check.

quotes.quote: What we find is that there is an exponential decay for HUDF-2 in the NIR bands between 3 and 8 arcsec, indicating the presence of an inner disk. We will further discuss about it in Section subsec:ongoing .

For the galaxies HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5, a number of quite distinctive surface brightness bumps at magnitude 25 are visible. They are especially strong for the redder bands. In the latter two cases, the association with recent merger events is evident, looking at the visual morphologies in the NIR bands. For the remaining one, this may be also the case, as it looks very asymmetric in the same photometric bands.

This seems to be the case for HUDF-1 and HUDF-2, as their residual images display negative and positive values close to the galaxy centre in perpendicular directions corresponding to the symmetry axes, typical of the presence of a non-subtracted inner galaxy disk (as detected in Section subsec:psf ). Therefore, for these two galaxies, we investigated the residuals coming from the subtraction of a double S ersic fit instead of a single S ersic fit.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HUDF-2 surface-brightness profiles, finding XDF over-subtraction while ABYSS and HUDF12 generally agree in limiting radius with filter-dependent differences.

quotes.quote: We show the surface brightness profile analysis of HUDF-2 in Fig. fig:HUDF2 . For this object we detect a similar over subtraction of the XDF profiles. The XDF F160W surface brightness profile reaches S/N=3 at half the extension of the ABYSS and HUDF12 profiles.

Interestingly, we found that the ABYSS and HUDF12 profile agree remarkably well for this object, reaching the limiting S/N at very similar radius ( R_ lim 13 arcsec, with the exception of F125W and F160W which extends to R 15.5-16 arcsec). In F160W, we observe that the HUDF12 surface brightness profile is 0.3 \ brighter than the ABYSS between R=4 and R=13 arcsec. For the rest of the filters, the ABYSS surface brightness profiles are 0.3 brighter than the HUDF12 ones beyond R>11 arcsec.

gmw_00004188.name: HUDF-3
gmw_00004188.overview: HUDF-3 is an early-type, high-redshift galaxy that exhibits shell-like, low-surface-brightness structures [arXiv:1602.01846]. These features, along with distinctive red-band surface-brightness bumps, are associated with a recent merger event [arXiv:1602.01846].

sources.summary: HUDF-3 has shell-like low-surface-brightness structure, partial WFC3 coverage, distinctive red-band surface-brightness bumps, and merger-related residual features.

quotes.quote: The ubiquity of morphological low surface brightness features displayed by these galaxies is noteworthy (like the shells in HUDF-3 or the fan of stars in HUDF-5).
The only object that was not totally explored using this method is HUDF-3, whose WFC3 images do not cover the whole galaxy (see Fig. fig:mosaicluminanceIR ).

For the galaxies HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5, a number of quite distinctive surface brightness bumps at magnitude 25 are visible. They are especially strong for the redder bands. In the latter two cases, the association with recent merger events is evident, looking at the visual morphologies in the NIR bands. Summarizing, it is very interesting to see that this naive exercise yields numbers similar to close pairs predictions and also to check that the visually identified merging smooth features in HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5 clearly provide to these galaxies with more mass in their residuals.

gmw_00004189.name: HUDF-5
gmw_00004189.overview: HUDF-5 is a bright elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.667 [arXiv:1810.00002]. It features a shell envelope [arXiv:1810.00002], a fan of stars [arXiv:1602.01846], and low-surface-brightness structures associated with a recent merger event [arXiv:1602.01846].

sources.summary: The paper describes HUDF-5 as a bright elliptical with a shell envelope whose outskirts were over-subtracted in previous HUDF reductions but recovered in ABYSS mosaics.

quotes.quote: HUDF-5 Buitrago2017 is an elliptical galaxy ( z=0.667 , _ 10 (M/M_ ) = 11.19^ +0.09 _ 0.05 ) and one of the brightest objects from the HUDF. It presents a noticeable shell envelope, and is one of the objects most affected by the aggressive sky subtraction from the previous releases.

In Fig. fig:HUDF5 we show the results of the surface brightness analysis for HUDF-5. We found several interesting results: Even after applying a local sky level correction, the XDF mosaics present a significant over-subtraction when comparing to the HUDF12 or our dedicated mosaics. This effect is noticeable at surface magnitudes fainter than 26-27 . Our dedicated mosaics recover a significant amount of light when compared to the HUDF12 mosaics and the XDF. The maximum differences in surface brightness range from =0.5-1.25 , being higher for the deeper images (F105W and F160W).

sources.summary: HUDF-5 displays a fan of stars, red-band surface-brightness bumps, visually evident merger features, and extra residual mass from smooth low-surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: The ubiquity of morphological low surface brightness features displayed by these galaxies is noteworthy (like the shells in HUDF-3 or the fan of stars in HUDF-5).

For the galaxies HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5, a number of quite distinctive surface brightness bumps at magnitude 25 are visible. They are especially strong for the redder bands. In the latter two cases, the association with recent merger events is evident, looking at the visual morphologies in the NIR bands. The reason behind this exercise resides in the fact that some low surface brightness features come from galaxy interations (at least in HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5).

Summarizing, it is very interesting to see that this naive exercise yields numbers similar to close pairs predictions and also to check that the visually identified merging smooth features in HUDF-2, HUDF-3 and HUDF-5 clearly provide to these galaxies with more mass in their residuals.

gmw_00004190.name: NGC 1536
gmw_00004190.overview: NGC 1536 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy classified as SBc [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578]. It exhibits an off-centered central bar [arXiv:2206.01578], irregular arms [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578], and structural asymmetry likely caused by an ongoing interaction [arXiv:2009.14731].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1536 as an asymmetric barred spiral with irregular arms, H-alpha emission following disturbed structure, and low star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1536 (AM 0409-563) is a late-type galaxy showing a clear asymmetry in its structure, likely the result of an on-going interaction.

NGC 1536 The galaxy classification is SBc (Type=4.6 1.8) in HyperLeda . In the continuum image the galaxy shows a thin bar in the central region and irregular arms with signatures of interaction. The \ emission follows roughly the structures seen in the continuum (Figure NGC1536 ). \ regions are asymmetrically distributed with respect to the inner bar. These regions develop along the NW arm and are scattered within the South galaxy body.

Both NGC 1536 and NGC 1543 have been detected in , \ regions are revealed throughout the body of NGC 1536 but the galaxy has a very low SFR (Figure SFR_comparison ). Kilborn2005 noted that this galaxy has ``5-10 times less \ detected than expected''.

sources.summary: UVIT FUV imaging shows NGC 1536 as a barred spiral with an off-centered bar, disk-like Sersic profile, diffuse extended FUV emission, and H-alpha regions along bar and arms.

quotes.quote: The FUV.CaF2 image (Figure NGC1536 , top left panel) shows the irregular arm structure of this barred spiral galaxy (SBc, according to HyperLeda ). We can clearly see its off-centered bar. The average FUV.CaF2 PA =162^ is consistent with the optical one at R _ 25 (Table UVIT-sources ). The average ellipticity, = 0.40 (Table UVIT-results ), differs from the optical one, 0.28 (Table UVIT-sources ) due to ellipticity variations of the FUV.CaF2 emission. The index obtained from the Sersic law fit, n=0.76 0.02, suggests the presence of a disk.

The \ regions found by Ram2020 stand out in red in Figure NGC1536 . They are distributed along the brightest features of the FUV.CaF2 emission (bar and arm segments), although the FUV.CaF2 emission is more diffuse and extended (see also Section Discussion ).

gmw_00004191.name: NGC 1546
gmw_00004191.overview: NGC 1546 is classified as either an early-type or a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2206.01578], featuring a small bulge embedded in a strongly inclined disk [arXiv:2009.14731]. Its structure includes a strong dust lane [arXiv:2009.14731], diffuse outer ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2206.01578], and flocculent H-alpha emission that follows its inner and outer rings [arXiv:2009.14731].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1546 has ambiguous ETG/spiral classification, diffuse outer FUV emission, embedded spiral structure, a pseudo-ring bump, and a disk-like FUV Sersic index.

quotes.quote: This galaxy (Figure NGC1546 ) has un uncertain classification. Comeron2014 classified it as E(b)3/(R')SA()ab, i.e. it could be either an ETG, as suggested by HyperLeda that reports a classification of S0-a, or a spiral with an inner ring () and an outer pseudo-ring (R'). The FUV.CaF2 image (top left panel) shows diffuse emission in the outer regions and the colour-composite image (bottom panel) reveals a spiral structure embedded in a diffuse halo. However, the shape of this galaxy is reminiscent of 3D ETGs mentioned by Buta2015 , in the R continuum and \ bands.

The fit of the surface brightness profile with a S ersic index n=0.98 0.04 is shown in top right panel of Figure NGC1546 . The FUV.CaF2 structure results basically a disk with a bump that peaks at about 26 or 2.6 kpc. Comeron2014 find a inner closed ring () and an outer pseudo-ring (R') in this galaxy. The bump we detect in FUV.CaF2 corresponds to the R' pseudo-ring, that Comeron2014 detect at a=28 8.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1546 as an S0-a or early spiral with an inclined disk, flocculent H-alpha emission, ring-following regions, and a dust lane.

quotes.quote: NGC 1546 This galaxy, considered a S0-a (Type=-0.4 1.7) in Comeron2014 is classified E(b)3/(R')SA()ab, i.e. can be a 3D ETG or a SA with an inner ring. The continuum image in Figure NGC1546 shows a small bulge embedded in a strongly inclined disk (53.3 degree in Hyperleda ). The \ emission shows a flocculent structure inside the disk. The \ regions, which are emphasized in the two-color image, follow the inner and outer rings evidenced by Comeron2014 . A strong dust-lane is visible in the SW ridge of the galaxy.
Its \ regions have a flocculent appearance likely following the inner and outer rings described by Comeron2014 .

gmw_00004192.name: NGC 1549
gmw_00004192.overview: NGC 1549 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731] with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:2009.14731] and anomalous mid-infrared PAH emission [arXiv:2009.14731]. While its optical profile displays a system of shells and plumes [arXiv:2206.01578], far-ultraviolet observations reveal a smooth, round emission profile and an inner blue ring [arXiv:2206.01578].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 1549’s FUV image with optical shells, finding an inner blue ring, smooth round FUV emission, and an E/S0-like FUV profile.

quotes.quote: The FUV.CaF2 image of this galaxy, shown in Figure NGC1549 , contrasts with the large scale optical system of shells and plumes discovered by Malin1983 . These features, due to old stellar populations, are not detected in our red continuum image (Figure NGC1549 , green in the composite image) since the short exposure time (see also the CGS study by Ho2011 ). The colour-composite image evidences a blue ring in the inner part ( 10-20 radius) as revealed from our FUV.CaF2 luminosity profile but not from optical data Ho2011 and \ by Ram2020.

The average ellipticity, =0.07, and position angle, PA =0 ^ account for the roundness and smoothness of the FUV.CaF2 emission unlike the B-band optical evaluation of HyperLeda which provides =0.15 and PA=146.2 ^ at R _ 25 . The PA of I-band image of CGS Ho2011 173.8 ^ 2.7 is consistent with the FUV.CaF2 measure. Our S ersic fit of the surface brightness profile gives an index n=2.86 0.28 suggesting that, in the FUV.CaF2 band, NGC 1549 is a E/S0.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1549 as a true elliptical with smooth amorphous emission, no visible H-alpha regions, LINER nuclear properties, and anomalous PAHs.

quotes.quote: NGC 1549 The galaxy is considered in HyperLeda a true E (Type=-4.3 0.9). The \ emission in Figure NGC1549 (top right panel) shows an amorphous distribution like that of the continuum (top left panel). No \ regions are visible.
The nuclei of the two shell galaxies, NGC 1549 and NGC 1553 are LINERs see e.g. Annibali2010, Rampazzo2013 .
Mid infrared Spitzer IRS spectra of both NGC 1549 and NGC 1553 from Rampazzo2013 show that PAH are present in the nucleus with anomalous emission ratios.

gmw_00004193.name: [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01
gmw_00004193.overview: [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01 is a member galaxy of the Dorado group [arXiv:2206.01578]. It exhibits a complex, irregular morphology in the far-ultraviolet and contains H-alpha star-forming regions [arXiv:2206.01578].

sources.summary: The paper treats [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01 as a Dorado member galaxy with complex irregular FUV morphology, H-alpha regions, and possible distortion by interaction with the Dorado centre.

quotes.quote: [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01 This source, detected by Ram2020 (their Figures 9 and 10) in , probing its association to the Dorado group, reveals a complex and irregular morphology in our colour-composite image (Figure CMI2001 ). This source has been classified as a galaxy in the GALEX database. The bottom panel of Figure CMI2001 shows \ regions within the faint structure of [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01.

In this context, [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01, projecting on an optical shell of NGC 1553 (see also Figure 2 in Malin1983 ), could be distorted by the interaction with the Dorado centre (SCG0414-5559), a region that, at odds with NGC 1533, is \ poor Elagali2019 .

gmw_00004194.name: PGC 75125
gmw_00004194.overview: PGC 75125 is a dwarf companion galaxy to IC 2058 within the Dorado central compact group [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578]. Likely a late S0 galaxy with a disk-like profile [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578], it displays a distorted far-ultraviolet morphology and warped, clumpy emission that suggest an ongoing interaction and gas refueling [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 75125 as a dwarf companion of IC 2058, likely a late S0 with warped clumpy emission suggesting gas refueling.

quotes.quote: PGC 75125 This dwarf galaxy is a companion of IC 2058 according to Firth2006 . They are nearby in projection and have similar heliocentric velocity, V_ hel =26 . According to HyperLeda the galaxy morphological type is T=5.0 3.0.

In the continuum image the galaxy structure appears boxy and without spiral arms. An elongated, distorted \ distribution (Figure PGC075125 ) appears in the outer regions of this galaxy. The \ emission could mark the (warped) disk of the galaxy. We suggest this galaxy is likely a late S0.
The \ emission, crossing PGC 75125 as a clumpy and warped lane, may suggest an in fly gas refueling see e.g. and references therein Domingue2003 following the picture described by Pearson2016 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 75125 as an interacting Dorado compact-group companion to IC 2058, with distorted FUV morphology and a disk-like Sersic profile.

quotes.quote: IC 2058 and PGC 75125 are part of the Dorado central compact group SCG 0414 -5559 Iovino2002 . A distortion of PGC 75125 is evident from the FUV.CaF2 image (Figure IC2058 ). The two galaxies, separated by V_ hel =28 , are likely interacting, as suggested by both the shape of the present FUV.CaF2 emission and \ observations by their Figure 14 Elagali2019 .

The single S ersic law fits of both galaxies luminosity profiles, shown in the top central and right panels of Figure IC2058 , with index n=1.48 0.10 for IC 2058 and n=1.20 0.03 for PGC 75125 trace the presence of a disk in both these galaxies (Table UVIT-results ).
Structural asymmetries in the FUV.CaF2 image of NGC 1566, PGC 75125 and [ CMI2001 ] 4136-01 are additional signatures of interactions.

gmw_00004195.name: NGC 1602
gmw_00004195.overview: NGC 1602 is a gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605600, arXiv:2009.14731] with a highly irregular shape and a light distribution dominated by far-ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2206.01578]. It is interacting with the nearby galaxy NGC 1596, which has stripped gas from NGC 1602 to form two tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0605600, arXiv:2009.14731].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1602 as the gas-rich dwarf companion and main H I reservoir whose tidal tails supplied counter-rotating gas to NGC 1596.

quotes.quote: We find a large \ envelope associated with a nearby companion, the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 1602. The \ covers a region 11 9 13 4 (62 70 kpc ^2 ) and the total \ mass detected is 2.5 0.1 10^9 M_ (assuming an 18 Mpc distance). The \ is centered on NGC 1602 but appears to have two tidal tails, one of which crosses over NGC 1596. The \ located at the position of NGC 1596 has a velocity gradient in the same sense as the ionized gas, i.e.

The redshifts measured in \ are 1510 km s ^ 1 for NGC 1596 and 1568 km s ^ 1 for NGC 1602, its dwarf companion rmgvws82 . Assuming a distance of 18 Mpc (corrected for Virgocentric infall), the projected distance between NGC 1596 and NGC 1602 is 15.7 kpc, less than the distance between the Milky Way and the Large Magellanic Cloud. NGC 1596 is about four times brighter than NGC 1602 in the B- band. It is an S0 galaxy with a B/PS bulge, probably the edge-on view of a thickened bar cb04 . NGC 1602 is a dwarf irregular galaxy (IB(s)m pec).
These properties suggest that most of the gas originally belonged to NGC 1602 and that some of the \ has been stripped by the interaction with NGC 1596, forming the two tidal tails.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1602 as an irregular donor in a physical pair with NGC 1596, with high equivalent width and a plume-linked gas-transfer scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596/ NGC 1602 These two Dorado members, nearby in projection, are separated by V_ hel =230 \ (Figure NGC1596 ). NGC 1596 is a S0 galaxy (Type=-2.0 0.5). No \ regions are visible. NGC 1602 is an Irregular (Type=9.5 0.6).
Concerning Irregular galaxies, NGC 1602 shows the higher value in our sample, EW=5.6 at 30 . In the outer region the curve decreases down to EW=4.2.

The donor-receiver mechanism, via galaxy-galaxy interaction, appears important at the edge of Dorado, considering the pairs (NGC 1566/ NGC 1581, NGC 1596/ NGC 1602) and triplets (NGC 1533, IC 2038, and IC 2039). Gas stripping is another mechanisms in action: NGC 1533 stripped IC 2038, and the same might have happened in the past between NGC1566 and NGC1581 and between NGC1596 and NGC1602. The consequence of this stripping could be linked to the low SFR of the spiral IC 2038 (Figure SFR_comparison ), to the presence of \ regions in the inner ring of NGC1581 and to the \ plume connecting NGC1602 to NGC1596 Chung2006 .

sources.summary: The paper gives the first UV view of NGC 1602, where FUV emission dominates its irregular light, the surface-brightness profile is very irregular, and interaction distorts its halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 1596 and NGC 1602 Our UVIT image is the first UV view of both these systems. Our composite image (Figure NGC1596 ) shows that the FUV.CaF2 emission dominates the light of the irregular galaxy NGC 1602 whereas it is mixed to older stellar populations in NGC 1596. We enhanced the signal of faint structures using ASMOOTH task to look for a FUV connection between the two galaxies following the behaviour the \ emission Bureau2006,Elagali2019 without success.

The surface brightness profile of NGC 1602 is very irregular as the shape of the galaxy which is classified a SBm in optical. The average ellipticity and position angle, 0.3 and 80 ^ are consistent with HyperLeda values (Table UVIT-sources ). The S ersic index, n=0.25 0.04 , results from the irregular shape of the FUV.CaF2 light distribution in this galaxy.

gmw_00004196.name: Tol 0104-388
gmw_00004196.overview: Tol 0104-388 is a compact, low-metallicity H II and blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1208.4260]. The galaxy contains a single giant H II region and exhibits weak He II emission [arXiv:1208.4260].

sources.summary: The paper studies Tol 0104-388 as a compact low-metallicity H ii/BCD galaxy with one giant H ii region, weak He ii emission, mapped line fluxes, extinction, and young starburst properties.

quotes.quote: We selected these galaxies because both objects belong to a subset sample of H ii galaxies with compact morphology and relative low metallicity (12+log(O/H) 7.8-7.9 dex), possibly mimicking the properties one expects for young galaxies at high redshift. In Fig. 1 we show g-band acquisition images of the galaxies Tol 0104-388 and Tol 2146-391. With the present observations, we note that Tol 2146-391 encompasses two main giant H ii regions (GH ii Rs), as shown in Fig. 1. While Tol 0104-388 shows one GH ii R.
We detect, for the first time, very weak He ii 4686 emission line in the spectrum of Tol 0104-388.
The EW(H ) in Tol 0104-388 varies from 0 to 200 and in Tol 2146-391 varies from 0 to 386 . While, the (H ) extinction varies from 0 to 0.66 in Tol 0104-388 and from 0 to 1.33 in Tol 2146-391.

gmw_00004197.name: Tol 2146-391
gmw_00004197.overview: Tol 2146-391 is a compact, low-metallicity blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1208.4260, arXiv:1309.6231]. It encompasses two main giant H II regions [arXiv:1208.4260] and exhibits extended He II emission that is likely produced by radiative shocks [arXiv:1208.4260, arXiv:1309.6231].

sources.summary: The paper treats Tol 2146-391 as a compact low-metallicity H ii/BCD galaxy with two giant H ii regions, a possible weak N/H gradient, and well-mixed metals.

quotes.quote: We selected these galaxies because both objects belong to a subset sample of H ii galaxies with compact morphology and relative low metallicity (12+log(O/H) 7.8-7.9 dex), possibly mimicking the properties one expects for young galaxies at high redshift. In Fig. 1 we show g-band acquisition images of the galaxies Tol 0104-388 and Tol 2146-391. With the present observations, we note that Tol 2146-391 encompasses two main giant H ii regions (GH ii Rs), as shown in Fig. 1.

Although a possible weak N/H gradient is observed in Tol 2146-391, the available data suggest that the metals from previous star-formation events are well mixed and homogeneously distributed through the optical extent of these galaxies. The spatial constancy of the N/O ratio might be attributed to efficient transport and mixing of metals by starburst-driven super-shells, powered by a plethora of unresolved star cluster in the inner part of the galaxies.
