This scenario agrees with the idea that most of the observed He ii 4686 emission line, in our sample of galaxies, is produced by radiative shocks.

sources.summary: The review describes Tol 2146-391 as a compact HII/BCD galaxy with mostly uniform abundances, a slight nitrogen gradient, elevated inner N/O, and He II emission likely linked to shocks.

quotes.quote: In Tol 2146-391, the 12+log(N/H) radial distribution shows a slight decrease with radius. This would argue in favor of heavy elements being produced in a previous burst of star-formation and dispersed within the ISM by starburst-driven super-shells , while the depressed central region could be attributed to radial inflow of relatively low metallicity gas from large radii to the center, thus diluting the abundance of the gas in the nuclear region.

A comparison of log (N/O) versuss 12+log(O/H) in Tol 2146-391 (Figure 3b) reveals that the log (N/O) values increase with the 12+log(O/H). This data point distribution has similar patterns to those found in H ii /BCD galaxies by of increasing N/O ratios with respect to the oxygen abundance. The inner region of Tol 2146-391 (near the peak of H ) presents N/O ratios which are larger than those expected by pure primary production of nitrogen.
In the galaxy Tol 2146-391, we favor the idea of extended He ii 4686 emission being primarily due to radiative shocks in the ISM.

gmw_00004198.name: PG0043+039
gmw_00004198.overview: PG0043+039 is a broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=0.38512 [arXiv:1504.04271]. It is characterized by extreme optical-to-X-ray weakness [arXiv:1504.04271, arXiv:1510.02681], though deep observations have detected it as a weak X-ray source [arXiv:1504.04271, arXiv:1510.02681].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG0043+039 as an unusual BAL quasar with extreme optical-to-X-ray weakness, simultaneous multiwavelength observations, weak X-ray detection, and peculiar UV cyclotron-like spectral humps.

quotes.quote: However, some outliers exist, such as PG0043+039. It is the only quasar in the PG sample (Schmidt Green, schmidt83 ) that was not detected in a dedicated deep XMM-Newton pointing (Czerny et al. czerny08 ). It shows the most extreme optical to X-ray slope of all AGNs in the Brandt et al. sample. PG0043+039 (z=0.38512) has been classified as a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar (Turnshek et al. turnshek94 ) based on a broad CIV absorption.

We executed simultaneous multifrequency observations of PG0043+039 in July 2013 with the original idea of probing the nature and understanding the origin of the X-ray weakness of PG0043+039, while ensuring that we are rid of the potential uncertainties that variability could create. We used the XMM-Newton satellite in the medium X-ray regime, the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) in the far-UV, and the ground-based 10m Hobby-Eberly and Southern African Large Telescopes (HET, SALT) in the optical to investigate this quasar in more detail. Furthermore, this object has simultaneously been observed with the NuSTAR satellite in the hard X-ray range (Luo et al. luo14 ).
PG 0043+039 is clearly visible as a weak point source in the images of all three cameras.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 0043+039 as an extreme X-ray-weak BAL quasar with detected X-ray flux, peculiar FUV features, and increased UV/optical continuum emission.

quotes.quote: The object PG 0043+039 has been identified as a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar based on its UV spectra. However, this optical luminous quasar has not been detected before in deep X-ray observations, making it the most extreme X-ray weak quasar known today. This study aims \ to detect PG 0043+039 in a deep X-ray exposure. The question is what causes the extreme X-ray weakness of PG 0043+039?

We took simultaneous deep X-ray spectra with XMM-Newton, far-ultraviolet (FUV) spectra with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST), and optical spectra of PG 0043+039 with the Hobby-Eberly Telescope (HET) and Southern African Large Telescope (SALT) in July, 2013. We have detected PG 0043+039 in our X-ray exposure taken in 2013. We presented our first results in a separate paper (Kollatschny et al. kollatschny15 ). PG 0043+039 shows an extreme _ ox gradient ( _ ox = 2.37).

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 0043 in its NuSTAR BAL-quasar sample, finding the targets strongly X-ray weak and this object undetected in the imaging.

quotes.quote: The _ OX constraints for the targets are shown in Figure 2, which indicate that their rest-frame 2  keV luminosities are 14 to >330 times weaker than expected from the Steffen2006 _ OX - L_ 2500  relation. These _ OX values have not been corrected for any intrinsic optical/UV reddening, which would render the values even more negative. Note that the spread of the Steffen2006 data points constrains the expected amount of quasar variability e.g., Gibson2012 , and after accounting for the measured flux variability of our targets (see Section 3 below), they are still significantly X-ray weak.

The other three targets, PG 0043, IRAS 07598, and IRAS 14026, are not detected; we also verified the non-detections via visual inspection of the smoothed images. None of the targets is detected in the 24--79 keV band, and the constraints from the non-detections in this band are not as tight as those in the other bands.

gmw_00004199.name: putative host galaxy of GRB 060614
gmw_00004199.overview: The putative host galaxy of GRB 060614 is a faint galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.1254 [arXiv:astro-ph/0608322, arXiv:0809.0595]. Its spectrum exhibits nebular emission lines that indicate ongoing star formation and the presence of young, massive stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0608322].

sources.summary: The host galaxy of GRB 060614 is faint, at z = 0.1254, and shows nebular emission plus ongoing star formation with a modest specific star-formation rate relative to other GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the host galaxy exhibits nebular emission lines (Fig. 1), which reveal ongoing star formation and the presence of young, massive stars. The specific star formation rate, normalized to the host luminosity (B-band magnitude M_ B = -15.5 ), is about 2 M_  yr ^ 1  L_*^ 1 , where M_ is the solar mass and L_* is the typical luminosity of field galaxies. This value is comparable to that exhibited by the Milky Way, and is at the low end of the distribution found for long-duration GRB hosts ^ 11 . The galaxy is also fainter than most GRB hosts ^ 12 .

Figure 1 | Spectrum of the host galaxy of GRB 060614. This is the average of several observations taken with the VLT equipped with the FORS 2 spectrograph in the period 2006 June 20 to July 30. From the emission features (marked with solid bars) we infer z = 0.1254 0.0005 . This confirms the redshift proposed in ref. 28.

sources.summary: The paper treats the host galaxy of GRB 060614 as a z=0.125 galaxy whose chance-alignment probability is small, arguing the GRB-host association is secure despite prior coincidence claims.

quotes.quote: This is a long burst with a duration 100 s, with a host galaxy at redshift z=0.125 del06,fyn06,geh06 .

On the other hand, it has been suggested that GRB 060614 and its host galaxy was just a coincidence rather than a physical association cob06b . By counting the number of galaxies observed by SMARTS in a field centered on the burst, cob06b showed that the probability for a chance superposition of GRB 060614 and a galaxy along the line of sight is 1 .

However, for the case of GRB 060614, calculation of the chance superposition of it and a z<0.125 galaxy with separation <0.5^ leads to a probability P = 0.02 , consistent with the result of gal06 . This small probability indicates that the association of GRB 060614 and its host is secure.

sources.summary: The paper questions whether the putative z=0.125 host galaxy of GRB 060614 is a chance foreground superposition, finding such coincidences rare but plausible in the Swift sample.

quotes.quote: The lack of an observed supernova associated with GRB 060614 appears to require a new paradigm for the formation of (a subset of) long-duration GRBs. This requirement is based on the presumed low redshift of the burst, which was inferred from the spatial coincidence of the afterglow with a z=0.125 galaxy. We explore the possibility that this low-redshift galaxy is a chance superposition along the line of sight to GRB 060614. We examine the galaxy distribution of the field of GRB 060614 and find that the probability of a chance association with a galaxy at least as bright as the putative host is only 0.5 - 1.9 . However, for the current ensemble of 180 Swift GRBs it is likely that several such coincidences have occurred, and given the ``non-standard" nature of GRB 060614 it is not implausible that this is one such occurrence.

gmw_00004200.name: BoRG 0116+1425 747
gmw_00004200.overview: BoRG 0116+1425 747 is a high-redshift galaxy with a best-fitting photometric redshift of approximately 7.87 [arXiv:1805.05038]. At this redshift, the galaxy has an absolute ultraviolet magnitude of -22.0 and an estimated star formation rate of 1.13 solar masses per year [arXiv:1805.05038].

sources.summary: Follow-up HST imaging strengthens BoRG 0116+1425 747 as a probable z approximately 8 galaxy, with low blueward signal, best-fit redshift near 7.9, and measured UV luminosity.

quotes.quote: Meanwhile, BoRG 0116+1425 747, with signal-to-noise S/N < 1 in F098M, now has a stronger probability (99 , compared to 64 previously) of being a z 8 galaxy (an independent analysis with another photometric redshift code confirms the z 8 solution in the latter case; Bridge et al. 2018, in preparation). With the addition of the new data, the secondary solutions encompass a negligible fraction of the probability distribution: 4 for BoRG 0116+1425 630 and 1 for BoRG 0116+1425 747. The best-fitting photometric redshifts are now 1.80^ +0.15 _ 0.10 and 7.87^ +0.29 _ 0.23 for BoRG 0116+1425 630 and BoRG 0116+1425 747 respectively, where the uncertainties include the central 68 of the probability distribution.

However, BoRG 0116+1425 747 has S/N < 1 in both F814W and F098M, and so would still be selected in the z 8 sample. For BoRG 0116+1425 747, assuming z 7.9 , we find M_ UV = -22.0 and a star formation rate ( SFR / M_ yr^ 1 ) = 1.13^ +1.28 _ 0.52 .

gmw_00004201.name: BoRG 0116+1425 630
gmw_00004201.overview: BoRG 0116+1425 630 was initially identified as a bright, compact candidate galaxy at a high redshift of z=8.4 [arXiv:1512.05363]. However, subsequent photometric data indicates it is more likely a low-redshift contaminant at z~1.8 with a low star formation rate [arXiv:1805.05038].

sources.summary: borg 0116+1425 630 is an exceptionally bright, compact z=8.4 candidate with high S/N detections, making it a strong spectroscopic target; the paper disfavors a lower-redshift early-type solution.

quotes.quote: borg 0116+1425 630 This object is exceptionally bright for a z>8 candidate ( =24.53 mag, corresponding to M_ AB 22.8 ). It is detected at high S/N ( S/N_ 140 =12.6 , S/N_ 160 =16.1 ) and has a best photometric redshift solution z=8.4 and compact size ( r_e=0 17 after accounting for the PSF). This source is about 0.5 mag brighter than the four z 7 candidates in the EGS field presented by , one of which shows an emission line consistent with Ly at z=8.68 ( zitrin2015 ; and two others have Ly emission at z=7.73 and z=7.48 ; see oesch2015,roberts-borsani2015 ).

Our new source thus appears an ideal candidate for spectroscopic follow-up, with the potential to elucidate how galaxy formation proceeds for the brightest sources well into the epoch of reionization. The photometric redshift for the galaxy shows two peaks (Figure fig:z9 ), but the lower redshift ( z 1.8 ) early-type SED is disfavored by the current data and by the compact size (see holwerda2015 ). Possibly, part of the emitted flux of such a bright source could hint at the presence of an active galactic nucleus oesch2014 .

sources.summary: The new F098M data make BoRG 0116+1425 630 more likely a low-redshift interloper, removing the formerly brightest z>8 candidate from the luminosity-function sample.

quotes.quote: Figures fig:pz and fig:sed suggest that BoRG 0116+1425 630 is more likely to be a z < 2 contaminant, due to a 3 detection in the F098M filter; the integral of the P(z) contained in the low- z solution has increased from 31 prior to the addition of F098M, to 96 afterwards. With the addition of the new data, the secondary solutions encompass a negligible fraction of the probability distribution: 4 for BoRG 0116+1425 630 and 1 for BoRG 0116+1425 747. The best-fitting photometric redshifts are now 1.80^ +0.15 _ 0.10 and 7.87^ +0.29 _ 0.23 for BoRG 0116+1425 630 and BoRG 0116+1425 747 respectively, where the uncertainties include the central 68 of the probability distribution. With S/N 3 in F098M, BoRG 0116+1425 630 does not meet the new criteria and is therefore removed from the z 9 sample. Assuming that BoRG 0116+1425 630 has the best-fitting redshift of z 1.8 , it is undetected in the rest-frame ultraviolet, giving an upper limit on the star formation rate of ( SFR / M_ yr^ 1 ) < 0.47 , and we find a stellar mass (M_ /M_ ) = 9.93^ +0.28 _ 0.25 .

gmw_00004202.name: NGC 941
gmw_00004202.overview: NGC 941 is an Scd-type companion galaxy to NGC 936 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312198] and belongs to a small group of galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy features a point-like nucleus, a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682], and is a radio source with a strong plume of radio material [arXiv:astro-ph/0312198].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 941 as an Scd companion of NGC 936, a radio source with a plume apparently enclosing a nearby quasar and suggesting ejection-related radio material.

quotes.quote: Fig. 4 shows NGC 941, an ScdIII companion galaxy to NGC 936 at 204 km/sec greater redshift. A quasar about 2 arcmin S of it was found by Arp (1981) in a systematic search for UV excess objects near companion galaxies. In any case NGC 941 is a radio source and Fig. 6 shows it has a strong plume of radio material reaching down and enclosing the quasar.

The quasar appears offset by 30 arcsec from the center of the nvss radio lobe and in order to interpret this configuration it is fortunate that there is a high resolution, FIRST map this region. As it stands at present, however, it seems likely that the z = 1.130 quasar is physically involved with ejection of radio material from NGC 941. Thirdly, a strong radio connection between NGC 941 and the nearby z = 1.130 quasar is displayed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0941 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 0941 : J022827.9-010906 Galaxy shows only a very small, point-like nucleus and is in a small group of galaxies (including NGC 936 and NGC 955). The small dip at 40 is associated with the inner spiral arm region. Although classified as SAB no real bar visible on image or in the profile thus the downbending break is classified as .

gmw_00004203.name: B1555+375
gmw_00004203.overview: The galaxy B1555+375 is a strong gravitational lens system featuring four lensed images in a fold configuration [arXiv:1601.01671, arXiv:astro-ph/0502436]. High-resolution imaging reveals that the lensing galaxy contains a clear edge-on disc component that crosses directly over the lensed images [arXiv:1601.01671, arXiv:1905.04182].

sources.summary: The paper models CLASS B1555+375 as a lens system whose edge-on disc can explain its radio flux-ratio anomaly without invoking extra dark-matter substructure.

quotes.quote: Here we present the case of CLASS B1555+375, which has a strong radio-wavelength flux-ratio anomaly. Our high-resolution near-infrared Keck II adaptive optics imaging and archival Hubble Space Telescope data reveal the lensing galaxy in this system to have a clear edge-on disc component that crosses directly over the pair of images that exhibit the flux-ratio anomaly. We find that simple models that include the disc can reproduce the cm-wavelength flux-ratio anomaly without requiring additional dark matter substructure. Although further studies are required, our results suggest the assumption that all flux-ratio anomalies are due to a population of dark matter sub-haloes may be incorrect, and analyses that do not account for the full complexity of the lens macro-model may overestimate the substructure mass fraction in massive lensing galaxies.

Here we focus on CLASS B1555+375 Marlow99 , which was discovered as part of the Cosmic Lens All-Sky Survey CLASS1,CLASS2 . B1555+375 has four lensed images with a maximum separation of 426 mas in a fold configuration. The two merging lensed images show a strong and persistent flux-ratio anomaly at radio wavelengths, and previous radio monitoring observations ruled out microlensing effects as the cause of the anomaly K03 . As B1555+375 was included in the observational and theoretical analyses of both Dalal2002 and Xu15 , understanding its mass distribution is of key importance to assess any systematics with the inferred level of substructure in the lensing halo with the flux-ratio anomaly method.

sources.summary: The paper models B1555+375 as a faint four-image radio lens with fold-configuration flux anomalies, finding acceptable fits only after relaxing two flux constraints.

quotes.quote: B1555+375 is a faint 4-image radio lens discovered by mar , whose fluxes violate the relation f_A - f_B 0 expected for a lens in a `fold configuration' foldreln . We fit the 5 GHz data from Marlow et al. The position of the lens galaxy with respect to the images has not been measured. The lens and source redshifts are not known, but Marlow et al. \ estimate them to lie in the ranges 1.0 z_s 3.0 and 0.5 z_l 1.0 .

Fitting all four images yields a model with ^2/ = 45.6/2 that reproduces the image positions well but not the flux ratios. Relaxing the flux constraints in front of one image can improve the fit, but the resulting models are unacceptable in that they require the positive parity image A to be demagnified or the negative parity image D to be highly amplified by a clump. The only acceptable models are found when we relax two flux constraints, and in fact there are two good cases (see Table tb:massestbl ).

sources.summary: The paper describes B1555+375 as a radio lens with blended A/B peaks, a predicted fourth image that was later found, and anomalous observed fold ratios.

quotes.quote: B1555+375 images A and B appear in radio observations as one extended object with two separate peaks of brightness. The fourth image was not initially observed but was predicted, searched for and consequently discovered Marlow/Myers:1999 . KGP find an expected range for the value of R_ fold for this lens to be from about -0.07 to 0.2.

Our model using an SIS+ES produces an R_ fold of about 0.003, which goes up to 0.062 if we use a PS+ES. The observed R_ fold , which is greater than 0.23 in all radio observations, exceeds our estimate, suggesting the system has significant small-scale structure or potentially microlensing. The imaging has insufficient resolution in the radio to determine whether the source is extended.

sources.summary: The paper uses B1555+375 as an observed edge-on disc lens whose flux-ratio anomaly is difficult for smooth models but becomes more probable when baryonic disc components are included.

quotes.quote: The idea that baryonic components such as edge-on discs can generate perturbations to the lensing potential similar to those produced by dark substructures and, therefore, can cause flux-ratio anomalies in lensed quasars has been supported by previous investigations of the B1555+375 and B0712+472 systems Hsueh2016,Hsueh17 . Additionally, Gilman2017 have shown that the luminous structure in elliptical lenses may account for 10 to 15 percent of flux-ratio anomalies. In this work, we select galaxies in the Illustris simulation with well-developed disc structure and compare them to typical elliptical lenses.

In Tables tab:cusp and tab:fold , the last four columns indicate that baryons increase the probability of finding systems such as B0712+472 and B1555+375, with the edge-on disc lens sample showing the largest increase of the probability of producing large anomalies. For example, the probabilities of obtaining the observed flux ratio anomalies in B1555+375 and B0712+472 with smooth mass distributions are only 1 and 10 , respectively. By including edge-on discs, these probabilities rise to 8--15 and 30--40 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models CLASS B1555+375 with an edge-on disc crossing the lensed images, an unknown lens-galaxy redshift, and an assumed high-redshift lens.

quotes.quote: CLASS B0712+472 and CLASS B1555+375 both have an edge-on disc that lies across the merging images, where the flux-ratio anomalies are most significant Jackson1998,Hsueh2016,Hsueh2017 . We apply the best-fit models found by Hsueh2017,Hsueh2016 and let the disc mass be the only free parameter. It should also be noted that for the lens system CLASS B1555+375, the redshift of the lensing galaxy is unknown. A recent detection of an emission line in the NIR spectrum suggests that the source redshift is z_s = 1.432 (Fassnacht et al., in prep).

Considering the red colour of the lensing galaxy, it is likely to be at high redshift and we assume the lens redshift to be z_l = 1.0 . The disc mass fraction of CLASS B0712+472 and CLASS B1555+375 is about 15 per cent within the Einstein radius, which is consistent with the range of disc mass fraction in the SWELLS survey Brewer2014 .

sources.summary: The paper reports high-confidence radio fold-relation violation in B1555+375 and later summarizes it as strong new evidence for a radio flux-ratio anomaly.

quotes.quote: Comparing these predictions with the data, we find that five of the the 12 known lenses with fold configurations have flux ratio anomalies: B0712+472, SDSS 0924+0219, PG 1115+080, B1555+375, and B1933+503.
B1555+375: The radio data violate the fold relation at high confidence, as shown more clearly in Figure 9.
It also reveals strong new evidence for fold flux ratio anomalies in B1555+375 (radio) and B1933+503 (radio), plus good but slightly less strong evidence for a fold anomaly in PG 1115+080 (optical).

gmw_00004204.name: B1938+666
gmw_00004204.overview: B1938+666 is a gravitational lens system featuring a passively evolving giant elliptical galaxy as the main lens [arXiv:0802.4044]. The system produces a high signal-to-noise ratio Einstein ring [arXiv:1206.1681] and contains a detected mass substructure at a redshift of 0.881 [arXiv:1406.1170].

sources.summary: The paper models B1938+666 as an almost complete Einstein-ring lens, deriving lens-galaxy properties, source magnification, and constraints on luminous substructure.

quotes.quote: We also present a new smooth mass model for our test system B1938+666 that is used to determine the properties of the lens galaxy and give a robust estimate of the magnification of the NIR component of the background galaxy.

The final reduced images from the AO observations are shown in the lower row of Fig. fig:imgs . The images have three distinct features: a bright, compact lensing galaxy in the centre of the image, a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) Einstein ring, and a set of faint arc structures outside of the ring on the east side.

Overall, we find that the total magnification factor of the NIR emission is 13. We note that a magnification of 176 has been previously reported for B1938+666 from a simple point-source model (see bar02 for some details). This larger magnification was used by rie11 for their analysis of the molecular gas properties of B1938+666, and they found that the CO (3--2) line intensity for B1938+666, and hence the molecular gas mass, was about an order of magnitude lower than for other quasars at a similar epoch. This large discrepancy is almost certainly due to the magnification of the gas emission being over-estimated.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1938+666 as a passively evolving giant elliptical lens with small-separation images and a common rotation measure of about 500 rad m^-2.

quotes.quote: The nature of the lensing object in the systems MG1131+0456 and 1938+666 has remained enigmatic for a long time, although the redshifts have now become available koch:00 , t45 . The main lens in both the systems appears to be a passively evolving giant elliptical, but probably there are two clusters or rich groups of galaxies in the field of MG1131+0456. Still, we believe that Rotation Measure estimates for these systems are important due to the presence of extended structures in the background source.

For the 4-image system 1938+666, the images C1 and C2 are highly magnified and are at sub--arcsecond separation; so they are unlikely to be affected by many of the other systematics discussed earlier. They have a small differential rotation measure of 56 rad m ^ 2 , for the two images separated by approximately 5 kiloparsecs at the lens and the excess position angle extrapolated to zero wavelength is negligible. But they have a common Rotation Measure of almost 500 rad/m ^2 which is also seen in the other two images.

sources.summary: The paper identifies B1938+666 as a two-source lens with extended radio lobes, a fold configuration, an Einstein ring, and possible substructure or variability.

quotes.quote: B1938+666 is a lens with two sources, quite possibly extended radio lobes. The radio lobe-dominated emission picture is further supported by a spectral index of about 0.5. One source creates a four image fold configuration, which we use for further analyses.

The other source produces a double King/Browne:1997 . In the near-infrared and optical wavebands, a slight Einstein ring appears King/Jackson:1998 . The two fold images have a very small flux anomaly as detected using MERLIN, but a much higher value, 0.436 , using VLBI King/Browne:1997 .

sources.summary: The paper uses JVAS B1938+666 as a lensing case with a z=0.881 mass substructure, matching Keck data and testing which density profiles reproduce the SIS-like lensing signal.

quotes.quote: Here, we test the specific case where all of these variables are matched to those found from an analysis of Keck adaptive optics imaging of the gravitational lens system JVAS B1938+666 Laga12 , where a substructure of mass 10 ^8 M _ was detected with the gravitational imaging technique at the 12 confidence level Vegetti12 .

The 12 significance level detection of a mass substructure in the gravitational lens galaxy JVAS B1938+666 at z=0.881 was obtained in a model independent way Vegetti12 . This detection was shown to be consistent with a SIS that has _v= 15.6 km s ^ 1 . In all of the considered cases given above, we set the substructure velocity dispersion and redshift to these measured values, and investigate which combination of mass model parameters can result in a lensing effect (as defined by Equation equ:effect ) that is equal or comparable to what is produced by a SIS with these properties.

gmw_00004205.name: UGC01281
gmw_00004205.overview: UGC 01281 is a nearly edge-on [arXiv:astro-ph/0604576], low-surface-brightness dwarf disk galaxy [arXiv:1103.0699, arXiv:1409.6720] classified as an Sd [arXiv:1409.6720] or Sdm type [arXiv:0808.1789]. It features a warped gas disk [arXiv:1012.3188, arXiv:1103.0699], a low star formation rate [arXiv:1103.0699], and extended neutral hydrogen and H-alpha emission [arXiv:1103.0699].

sources.summary: The paper observes UGC 1281 with VIRUS-P and VLA H I data, models its outer gas disk, accounts for a warp, and sets UVB photoionization limits.

quotes.quote: Despite the sensitivity of the VIRUS-P spectrograph on the McDonald 2.7m telescope to low surface brightness emission and the large field-of-view, we do not detect H to 5 upper limits of 6.4 10^ 19 erg/s/cm ^2 / \ in UGC 7321 and of 25 10^ 19 erg/s/cm ^2 / \ in UGC 1281 in each of the hundreds of independent spatial elements (fibers). We fit gas distribution models from overlapping 21 cm data of HI, extrapolate one scale length beyond the HI data, and estimate predicted H surface brightness maps. We analyze three types of limits from the data with stacks formed from increasingly large spatial regions and compare to the model predictions: 1) single fibers, 2) convolution of the fiber grid with a Gaussian, circular kernel (10 \ full width half maximum), and 3) the coadded spectra from a few hundred fibers over the brightest model regions. None of these methods produce a significant detection ( >5 ) with the most stringent constraints on the HI photoionization rate of (z=0)<1.7 10^ 14 s ^ 1 in UGC 7321 and (z=0)<14 10^ 14 s ^ 1 in UGC 1281.

For UGC 1281, we have reduced the raw data from the VLA archive. It was observed under proposal AZ097 on 1997 December 26 in the most compact (D) configuration for a total of 3 hours on source with interspersed observations of the strong, primary calibrator J0137+3309 (3C48) for which we have adopted the VLA recommended flux density of 15.9 Jy. The observations were spaced over a range of 3 hours in H.A. giving excellent uv-coverage and images with 127 channels of width 2.6 km s ^ 1 after standard on-line Hanning-smoothing. The angular scale that corresponds to the shortest baseline ( 900 ) is sufficiently larger than the largest single-channel galaxy extent ( 285 ) that the array should have recovered the total HI flux.
The fits to UGC 1281 are more constrained with both an inner and outer radius cut. The outer cut is to exclude a known 8 warp Gar02 .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on UGC 1281, finding extended H and H I gas, a low star formation rate, and warp or lag models for declining high-latitude velocities.

quotes.quote: Here we report on deep H i and H observations of the edge-on dwarf galaxy UGC 1281 in order to determine the existence of extra-planar gas and the kinematics of this galaxy. This is the first time a dwarf galaxy is investigated for its gaseous halo characteristics. We have obtained H integral field spectroscopy using PPAK at Calar Alto and deep H i observations with the WSRT of this edge-on dwarf galaxy. These observations are compared to 3D models in order to determine the distribution of H i in the galaxy. We find that UGC 1281 has H emission up to 25 (655 pc) in projection above the plane and in general a low H flux.

Compared to other dwarf galaxies UGC 1281 is a normal dwarf galaxy with a slowly rising rotation curve that flattens off at 60 km s ^ 1 and a central depression in its H i distribution. Its H i extends 70 (1.8 kpc) in projection from the plane. This gas can be explained by either a warp partially in the line-of-sight or a purely edge-on warp with rotational velocities that decline with a vertical gradient of 10.6 3.7 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 .

sources.summary: UGC 1281 is nearly edge-on, has sparse detections but a well-constrained position angle, rotation consistent with long-slit data, and symmetric line profiles relevant to its rotation-curve shape.

quotes.quote: UGC 1281: There were five interstitial pointings for this galaxy. The amount of emission was sparse as less than 50 \ of the fibers had a detection. The \ fibers are shown on the R band image obtained at the KPNO 2m telescope. Fiber velocities were the average of the H , \ and \ lines. The position angle was well-constrained by the data and the \ rotation curve is consistent with the long-slit H rotation curve of dBB .

UGC 1281 is a nearly edge-on galaxy. At high inclination, line-of-sight integration effects become important and may cause an intrinsically steeply rising rotation curve to appear slowly rising. The shape of the emission line profiles can be used to constrain the shape of the rotation curve. If instrumental resolution is high enough and there is no line-of-sight obscuration, then symmetric line profiles indicate a solid-body rotation curve and skewed line profiles indicate a curved, NFW-like rotation curve Kregel . The line profiles in the \ fibers are symmetric.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes UGC 1281 as a resolved edge-on dwarf disk and finds the sample's thick-disk stellar populations have weak color gradients and consistently red giant branches.

quotes.quote: In the present paper we analyze NGC 784, 1560, UGC 1281, and UGCA 442. They span a range of rotation velocities from 95 to 150 km/sec (21 cm line full width at 20 peak). WFPC2 on HST reaches below the tip of the red giant branch (RGB) in these galaxies, allowing us to diagnose the star formation history of their thick disk stellar populations.

The noteworthy feature of Figure 6 is the weakness of the color gradient in the disks of these galaxies out to 2 kpc from the plane. The average over the four galaxies is 0.06 0.02 mag/kpc in the sense that the thick disk is redder further from the thin disk at the 3 level. Column (2) of Table 2 records the weighted average V--I for the region of the disk beyond 900 pcs, and column (3) gives the reddening in the Milky Way according to NED and Schlegel (1998). The giant branches of the thick disk regions of these galaxies are consistently red, with V--I colors ranging from 1.39 to 1.54 (column 4).

sources.summary: The paper models UGC01281 as an LSB disk galaxy whose gas and stellar distributions are used to test ULDM soliton behavior, finding little soliton-shape impact.

quotes.quote: Our second discussion of non-spherical solitons involves two low surface-brightness (LSB) disk galaxies from the SPARC database : UGC01281 and F571-8. We choose these galaxies as representative examples of a larger sample including dozens of well-resolved LSB galaxies. The gas mass distribution for UGC01281 (not relevant for F571-8) is calibrated to approximately match the HI surface brightness data reported in , normalising to the total gas mass reported in .

In Fig. fig:MvsLamugc1281 we plot the M- relation for a soliton in UGC01281. Inspecting Fig. fig:EoverMugc1281 we see that in the neighbourhood of E/M values that conform to the DM-only simulation prediction, the actual E/M for a soliton in UGC01281 is significantly shifted compared to the self-gravitating solution. However, the effect on K/M is much less pronounced: the soliton shape is essentially unaffected.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 1281 as a rotation-curve example, comparing scalar-field dark-matter soliton models and finding repulsive self-interactions can match its observed slope and peak.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the procedure, we shall use the example of the galaxy `UGC 1281' whose observed rotation curve is shown in figure fig:eg_exclusion_procedure using data points. These data points correspond to V_ obs while the solid curves in figure fig:eg_exclusion_procedure correspond to V_ DM in eq. ( eq:observed_vel ). The dark matter velocity V_ DM can be obtained from eq. ( eq:circ_vel ) for the density profile given by eq. ( eq:tot_dens ) in which _ SFDM can be obtained from the numerical solution of GPP equations.

To illustrate the effect of imposing PVC on SFDM solitons with attractive or repulsive self-interactions, we first consider the velocity curve for the galaxy `UGC 1281' for which v_p^ (h) = 51.5 \ km/s .
For `UGC 1281' _ ini = 2 and s = 11942 satisfies the observed slope and peak simultaneously.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1281 as an Sdm galaxy with at least ten HII regions and two fitted star-formation bursts, including one 4 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: UGC 1281: this Sdm galaxy has at least 10 HII regions, which produce a significant H flux. The models indicate a first SF burst 5.5 Gyr ago contributing 73 10 of the luminosity, and a second burst 4 Myr ago. The following nine fits show a possible first burst more than 10 Gyr ago; this is supported by models allowing more than two bursts.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 1281 as a low-surface-brightness edge-on Sd galaxy used for diffuse H-emission limits, noting its inferred photoionization-rate upper limit is six times higher than UGC 7321's.

quotes.quote: Adams \ (2011) used integral-field spectroscopy to set deep limits on H emission from the outskirts of two low surface brightness, edge-on Sd galaxies, UGC 7321 and UGC 1281. They constrained the \ photoionization rate to be _ H < 2.3 10^ 14  s ^ 1 (UGC 7321) and _ H < 14 10^ 14  s ^ 1 (UGC 1281).
However, we are reluctant to adopt such a low value of _ H or _0 from a single object Their stringent limit comes from UGC 7321, whereas the limit toward UGC 1281 is a factor of 6 higher.

gmw_00004206.name: F571-8
gmw_00004206.overview: F571-8 is a low surface brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:1903.03402]. In this system, the baryonic contribution to the gravitational potential is negligible for both the central soliton and the large-scale dark matter halo [arXiv:1903.03402].

sources.summary: The paper uses F571-8 as a clean LSB disk test case, finding baryons have negligible effects on both its ULDM soliton shape and large-scale halo dynamics.

quotes.quote: The next galaxy we consider is F571-8. Soliton properties for this galaxy are presented in Figs. fig:MvsLamf571 and fig:EoverMf571 . Here, for simplicity, we ignore the (negligible) gas contribution in computing the soliton.

Again, K/M for a soliton in F571-8 is unaffected by baryons in the parameter region expected from DM-only simulations. The case of F571-8 is even clearer than UGC01281 because the baryonic effect on the dynamics of the large-scale halo, as seen by inspecting the rotation curve decomposition (bottom panel of Fig. fig:rcsSPARC ), is not larger than 5 . We conclude that if Eq. ( eq:solhalK ) correctly captures the soliton--halo relation of the simulations, then UGC01281 and F571-8 are clean systems in which to constrain the ULDM model, in the sense that the baryonic contribution to the gravitational potential is not important both for the large-scale halo and for the central soliton.

gmw_00004207.name: RX 11340.6+4018
gmw_00004207.overview: RX 11340.6+4018 is an isolated elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.171 [arXiv:2106.13255]. It serves as the prototype for fossil groups, featuring an extended, azimuthally symmetric X-ray halo with a group-scale mass [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052, arXiv:2106.13255].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1340+4017 as one of four isolated X-ray over-luminous elliptical-galaxy systems, with large-radius X-ray emission, azimuthally symmetric surface brightness, and optical light dominated by the central galaxy.

quotes.quote: These selections resulted in a sample of four objects (Table  tab:sample ). Two of them, 1159+5531 and 2114-6800 , were previously detected in the Einstein surveys (Stocke et al. \ 1991 and Griffiths et al. 1992, respectively) and identified with normal elliptical galaxies. We also rediscovered the Ponman et al. \ object, 1340+4017 . The remaining object, 2247+0337 , first appears in our sample. We have measured the redshift of 2247+0337 and adopted the redshifts of others from the literature.

In all four objects, the X-ray emission is detected with 2-3 significance out to a large radius R_ x = 0.5-1 Mpc (Table  tab:sample ). The X-ray surface brightness distribution shows no significant deviations from azimuthal symmetry.
In all objects, at least 70 of light (exluding the background) comes from the central galaxy; the contribution from other galaxies does not exceed the level of background fluctuations.

sources.summary: The review identifies RX 11340.6+4018 as the discovery case and prototype of fossil groups: an isolated elliptical in an extended X-ray halo with group-scale mass.

quotes.quote: This prediction was supported, one year later, by the discovery of RX 11340.6+4018, an apparently isolated elliptical galaxy, at redshift z=0.171 , found in an extended X-ray halo. The estimated X-ray mass was 2.8 10^ 13 M _ , making it a typical group-sized object Ponman1994 . These systems were named as `` fossil groups '' (FGs).
We already mentioned that the prototype of FGs is RX 11340.6+4018, presented in Ponman1994 .

gmw_00004208.name: SDSS J023958.22-083123.5
gmw_00004208.overview: SDSS J023958.22-083123.5 is a galaxy with an almost featureless spectrum [arXiv:1502.06034]. This spectrum is reminiscent of an old stellar population, making it unlikely to have hosted the core-collapse supernova LSQ13cuw [arXiv:1502.06034].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates SDSS J023958.22-083123.5 as a possible LSQ13cuw host, but rejects it because its spectrum and photometric redshifts are inconsistent.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy LSQ13cuw exploded 13 03 W and 0 62 S of SDSS J023958.22-083123.5 (Figure figure:LSQ13cuw_image ). In order to determine whether SDSS J023958.22-083123.5 was indeed the host galaxy of LSQ13cuw, we included it in the slit with the SN on the final epoch (2014 Jan. 24) of our spectroscopic observations. The spectrum was reduced in the same way as the SN spectra (see Figure figure:galaxy for the reduced and calibrated spectrum). It is almost featureless, so a reliable redshift cannot be determined. Photometric redshifts from SDSS are z=0.295 0.042 (KD-tree method) and z=0.219 0.088 (RF method A description of the KD-tree and the RF method can be found at the following url: https://www.sdss3.org/dr8/algorithms/photo-z.php. ), both being significantly higher than the value ( z=0.045 ) we obtained directly from spectra of LSQ13cuw.

Given the unreliability of photometric redshifts, combined with the fact that the spectrum is reminiscent of an old population, and therefore unlikely to give rise to core-collapse SNe, we deem it improbable that SDSS J023958.22-083123.5 hosted LSQ13cuw.

gmw_00004209.name: SDSS J023957.37-083123.8
gmw_00004209.overview: SDSS J023957.37-083123.8 is a faint, extended galaxy that is the host of the supernova LSQ13cuw [arXiv:1502.06034]. Deep imaging reveals an elongated structure with two bright regions, which may represent either two separate, marginally resolved galaxies or components of a single irregular galaxy [arXiv:1502.06034].

sources.summary: The paper favors SDSS J023957.37-083123.8 as the LSQ13cuw host galaxy after deep imaging found a faint extended source near the SN position.

quotes.quote: In the SDSS-DR10 database, there is a faint extended source, SDSS J023957.37-083123.8, at the SN location. Its photometric redshifts are 0.014 0.082 (KD-tree method) or 0.049 0.028 (RF method). The latter would match the redshift of the SN within the errors. However, the SDSS DR10 ' band magnitude of SDSS J023957.37-083123.8 ( m_ ' = 20.88 0.27) is not consistent with the limiting magnitude of the LSQ pre-explosion images ( m_ ' > 21.5).

After aligning and combining the images, we found that the SN had faded beyond detection, and a faint extended source was detected within 0''.5 of the reported position of SDSS J023957.37-083123.8, 1''.3 NE from LSQ13cuw. The source appears elongated in the N-S direction with two bright regions at each end (marked with ``b'' and ``'' in Figure figure:LSQ13cuw_host ; it is unclear whether the two bright regions are two separate galaxies which are marginally resolved in the images, or are components of a single irregular galaxy).

Given the spatial coincidence, we favour SDSS J023957.37-083123.8 as the host galaxy of LSQ13cuw, with M_ ' = 14.06 0.18 (for the entire source ``b'' and ``''), derived using our redshift estimate above.

gmw_00004210.name: NGC 1084
gmw_00004210.overview: NGC 1084 is an almost face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1202.2713, arXiv:1410.8393] featuring a nearly spherical stellar halo with a giant tidal tail [arXiv:1606.08847] and a history of dwarf-satellite mergers [arXiv:2109.01802]. The galaxy exhibits chaotic star formation [arXiv:1202.2713], kinematically distinct regions with shocked gas flows [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:2109.01802], and has hosted five known supernovae [arXiv:1410.8393, arXiv:1302.0170].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1084 as an HII-driven spiral with a bright nucleus, many arms and knots, chaotic star formation, and different northern and southern stellar ages.

quotes.quote: NGC 1084 is a HII driven Sa(s) galaxy located 16.6 Mpc away. More recently, this galaxy has been classified as an Sbc and Sb based on its B and H band images, respectively 2002ApJS..143...73E . The H band image reveals a bright nucleus with an elongated bulge. Many weakly defined spiral arms were detected along with many bright knots.

2007MNRAS.381..511R derived a star formation rate of 2.8 M _ yr ^ 1 along with chaotic star formation that is not necessarily confined to the spiral arms. The northern and southern regions of the galaxy have different star formation rates and typical ages. The north is characterised by a series of short bursts happening intermittently over the past 40 Myr, possibly due to an interaction with a gas rich galaxy 2007MNRAS.381..511R . The southern half of the galaxy is home to much younger stars, < 4 Myr 2007MNRAS.381..511R .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1084 as the almost face-on spiral host of SN 2012ec, gives its distance, prior supernova history, and host-galaxy reddening toward the event.

quotes.quote: SN 2012ec was discovered by Monard2012 in the almost face-on ( i=57^ , Moiseev2000 ) spiral galaxy NGC 1084 on 2012 August 11.039 UT (MJD=56150.04). The SN is located 0.7" E and 15.9" N of the nucleus of the host galaxy NGC 1084 (see Fig FC ). Details of NGC 1084 are presented in Table galprop . NGC 1084 previously hosted 4 known SNe: the Type II-P SN 2009H Li2009 , the Type II SNe 1998dl King1998 and 1996an Nakano1996 , and the Type Ia SN 1963P Kowal1968 .

The distances available in the literature for NGC 1084 are principally based on the Tully-Fisher relation, and we adopt the value = 31.19 0.13 mag, available in the Extragalactic Distance Database Extragalactic Distance Database, http:/ /edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/ Tully2009 . The internal reddening in NGC 1084 was derived using the measured equivalent widths (EW) of NaI D ( 5889 , 5895 ), observed in a low-resolution spectrum at +19 days.

sources.summary: The paper monitored NGC 1084 with Swift, found no UV transient events, and detected significant X-ray variability from an internal extended region likely contaminated by candidate background AGNs.

quotes.quote: NGC 1084 The weekly monitoring of the galaxy NGC 1084 ranged from the beginning of November 2013 to the second week of March 2014. Good data have been collected for 20 epochs by the XRT and for 19 epochs by the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC1084 ). We found no transient events in the UVOT data. In the XRT data we found that the light curve of an extended region (centred on RA = 02:45:59.9, Dec = 07:34:23.3, named NGC1048-I1 All coordinates here and in the following refers to J2000. ) located inside the target galaxy NGC 1084 varies significantly (Fig. NGC1084_X_gal ).

We note however that three candidate active galactic nuclei AGN, Brough2006, Cavuoti2014 stand within the region, possibly causing the observed variability. We found no significant variability in the UVOT data inside the same region, nor in smaller regions around each AGN.

sources.summary: The paper confirms central and outer kinematically distinct regions in NGC 1084, including two-component emission lines, enhanced [NII]/H ratios, and shock-front-like gas flows.

quotes.quote: From the earlier observations by Afanasiev et al. (1988a), two regions with strong velocity deviations from the mean rotation were found. Particularly in the central region, <5 , the rotation velocities obtained by measuring the forbidden emission lines [ NII ] and [ SII ] were systematically lower than those obtained by measuring H . The authors had interpreted this peculiarity as evidence for minibar presence in the center of NGC 1084.

Our IFP data confirms the existence of these kinematically distinct regions. Near the spur, the profile of the H emission line is mostly two-component: one component traces the mean rotation and the other is shifted by 100 - 150 . The appearance of the non-circular component is supplemented by a sharp increase of the [ NII ] /H ratio by a factor of 2 - 6 with respect to the normal one in the neighboring HII regions.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 1084 as having complex peripheral emission-line profiles, an emission spur, peculiar shocked components, and evidence for recent and earlier dwarf-satellite merger activity.

quotes.quote: Of interest is the NGC 1084 galaxy with a complex structure of emission-line profiles found in its periphery. In this galaxy the brightest in the system of regions looks like an emission ``spur'' extending perpendicular to the spiral arm NED now refers to this region as ``NGC 1084 spur''. . A peculiar component in , shifted relative to the main component by 100... +150 and often accompanied by shock excitation in the line is observed between the \ regions. Moiseev2000A&A...363..843M suggested two interpretations of the observed picture associated both with galactic fountains and traces of past interactions. Further evidence for both recent <40 Myr, Ramya2007 and the earlier 1 Gyr Martinez-Delgado2010 merger of a dwarf satellite emerged later.

sources.summary: The paper situates SN 2012ec in NGC 1084, noting four previous supernovae there, a Tully-Fisher distance modulus of 31.19 mag, the C9-region oxygen abundance, and Na I D absorption at the galaxy's recessional velocity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1084 has previously hosted 4 SNe: 2009H 2009CBET.1656....1L , 1998dl 1998IAUC.6992....1K , 1996an 1996IAUC.6442....1N and 1963P. The Tully-Fisher distance 2008ApJ...676..184T , quoted from the Extragalactic Distance Database http://edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/ , is =31.19 0.13 mag. The position of SN 2012ec is coincident with the region C9 identified by 2007MNRAS.381..511R in NGC 1084, for which they derive an oxygen abundance of 12+ O H =8.93 0.1 .
The unresolved Na I D doublet is observed in absorption at the recessional velocity of NGC 1084 at all epochs.

sources.summary: The Dragonfly study reports that NGC 1084 has a nearly spherical stellar halo with a giant tidal tail, a steepening outer profile, and the sample highest halo fraction.

quotes.quote: The galaxies show a remarkable variation in their low surface brightness outskirts NGC 1084 has a nearly spherical halo featuring a giant tidal tail, whereas both NGC 1042 and M101 show spiral arms down to very faint surface brightness levels.

A single power law is not an appropriate model to fit to the outskirts of NGC 1084 since the surface density profile steepens beyond 7R_ s , and therefore the extrapolation is a clear overestimate (Figure rho ).
The galaxy with the highest halo fraction is NGC 1084; this is also the only galaxy to feature a prominent tidal stream in its halo consistent with previously published imaging; MartinezDelgado2010 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1084 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1084 : J024559.7-073437 A large scale gradient (from top to bottom) in the background of the ^ band image is removed with a linear fit. Slightly inclined galaxy sitting clearly in a more roundish outer envelope, beyond which some stream-like remnants of a possible recent interaction are visible. Small edge-on galaxy along the stream path is confirmed to be only a background galaxy.

gmw_00004211.name: CTD 74
gmw_00004211.overview: CTD 74 is a blazar located at a redshift of z=2.3502 [arXiv:2402.04011]. High-resolution radio observations reveal that the active galactic nucleus consistently displays a core-jet structure with a short jet oriented toward the northwest [arXiv:2402.04011].

sources.summary: The paper presents CTD 74 as a z=2.3502 blazar, spatially linked to an IceCube neutrino alert, and analyzes its multi-epoch VLBI core-jet radio structure.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Its coordinates in the International Celestial Reference Frame (ICRF) are 11^ h 25^ m 53.7119^ s right ascension and +26^ 10^ 19.979^ declination

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present high-resolution Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) analysis of the blazar CTD 74 (alternatively: TXS 1123 + 264, J1125 + 2610) that has been statistically linked to an extremely high-energy track-like neutrino alert (EHEA2012-05-23) observed in 2012 , due to its positional proximity. CTD 74 was in the sample of radio-loud AGN having VLBI observations at 8 GHz, and monitored at 22 GHz by the Russian RATAN-600 radio telescope that were analyzed in connection with high-energy ( >200  TeV) track-like neutrino events detected by the IceCube instrument . The directions of such events can be determined with an uncertainty of less than 1^ , and CTD 74 fell into the error region of such event.

CTD 74 is a blazar at a redshift of z=2.3502 0.0001 . Its coordinates in the International Celestial Reference Frame (ICRF) are 11^ h 25^ m 53.7119^ s right ascension and +26^ 10^ 19.979^ declination . In all the analysed data, CTD 74 consistently showed a core--jet structure, with a short jet oriented to the northwest.

gmw_00004212.name: HSCJ015618-010747
gmw_00004212.overview: HSCJ015618-010747 is a galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:2404.18897]. Lens models of the system show broadly consistent estimates for its Einstein radius and position angle [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper compares lens models for HSCJ015618-010747, finding visually similar published models, uncertain near-unity axis ratio in one method, and broadly consistent Einstein radius and position angle estimates.

quotes.quote: HSCJ015618-010747 : In this case, the models of YL, S19 and S23 (see their fig. B.1) are visually similar. The axis ratio, q , predicted by S19 is close to unity and poses difficulty in constraining the PA which deviates from other results. The q values predicted by S23 and network are within the quoted errorbars. The predictions for PA are within the errorbars for YL, S23 and network, while the predictions for _ E are close enough for all the methods.

gmw_00004213.name: HSCJ020241-064611
gmw_00004213.overview: HSCJ020241-064611 is a galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:2404.18897]. The system features two compact lensed images oriented in the north and south directions [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSCJ020241-064611 as a lens with north-south compact images, where several modeling methods agree on Einstein radius and axis ratio but position angle is harder to constrain.

quotes.quote: HSCJ020241-064611 : For this lens, the models of YL, S19 and S23 (see their fig. B.3) look qualitatively similar with one image in the north and the other in the south direction. The model parameters _ E and q , for all four methods, are roughly consistent within the quoted errorbars due to the similarity of models containing two compact images, whereas the PAs of the mass distribution are harder to constrain due to their low ellipticities (i.e. high q ).

gmw_00004214.name: HSCJ022610-042011
gmw_00004214.overview: HSCJ022610-042011 is a gravitational lens system [arXiv:2404.18897]. The lens produces two images of the background source galaxy [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSCJ022610-042011 as a two-image lens where Einstein radius is consistent across methods, axis ratios differ, and position angle remains hard to constrain.

quotes.quote: HSCJ022610-042011 : In this case, the lens produces two images of the source galaxy. The model parameter _ E , for all four methods, is consistent. There are differences in the predicted values of the axis ratio of the lensing potential, although all predictions are >0.5 . We find that the axis ratio predicted by YL and S23 (for model, see their fig. B.7) agree well while those from S19 and network and are roughly within the errorbars. The PAs from S23 and network are within the quoted errorbars, while YL and S19 predict PA values which are close.

gmw_00004215.name: HSCJ085855-010208
gmw_00004215.overview: HSCJ085855-010208 is a strong gravitational lensing system that features a near-Einstein ring [arXiv:2404.18897]. This structure is typical of high axial symmetry and a perfect alignment of the source with the lens center, though the position angle remains uncertain [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSCJ085855-010208 as a near-Einstein-ring lens with high axial symmetry, consistent Einstein radius constraints, and uncertain position angle.

quotes.quote: HSCJ085855-010208 : The lens subtracted image in this lens system shows a near Einstein ring which is typical for a system with axial symmetry and a perfect alignment of the source with the lens center. The models from YL, S19 and S23 (see their fig. B.11) all reflect this feature resulting in values of the axis ratios close to unity for the lens model. The radius of the near perfect Einstein ring further helps each of the models to constrain the parameter _ E in a consistent manner. However, the inference of the PA is quite uncertain given the axial symmetry.

gmw_00004216.name: HSCJ121052-011905
gmw_00004216.overview: HSCJ121052-011905 is a strong gravitational lensing system that features three images forming an almost complete Einstein ring [arXiv:2404.18897]. Two of these images exhibit extended structures that deviate from the tangential direction [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSCJ121052-011905 as an almost Einstein-ring system with three images, extended structures, consistent Einstein radius and axis ratio, and one discrepant position angle.

quotes.quote: HSCJ121052-011905 : In this case, we can see the 3 images almost making an Einstein ring but two of them (the north-east and the west images) have extended structures deviating from the tangential direction. The models of YL and S19 are similar although the source of S19 is more compact than that of YL. These models lead to more smooth and circular configuration than the actual system. The S23 (see their fig. B.15) source model is more clumpy and does not have extended features. The inferred _ E agree with each other because of the similar angular separations of the arcs from the center of the lens potential.

gmw_00004217.name: HSCJ142720+001916
gmw_00004217.overview: HSCJ142720+001916 is a strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:2404.18897]. Different modeling methods for the system agree on its Einstein radius and axis ratio, but they diverge on its source structure and position angle [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper finds that HSCJ142720+001916 models differ in source structure, agree on Einstein radius and axis ratio, but split into distinct position-angle solutions.

quotes.quote: HSCJ142720+001916 : For this lens, S19 and S23 (see their fig. B.22) models appear more accurate with an extended source whereas YL model is more circular with a compact source. The models from all methods agree well on _ E and the predictions for q are also within the errorbar from our network. The inferred PA from S19 and S23 are distinct from both YL and our network, where the latter two are more consistent with each other.

gmw_00004218.name: HSCJ223733+005015
gmw_00004218.overview: HSCJ223733+005015 is a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:2404.18897]. The system is well modeled by two methods that produce qualitatively similar, diametrically opposite images [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper reports that HSCJ223733+005015 is well modeled by two methods with diametrically opposite images, while a third model appears incorrect but some parameters remain within network errors.

quotes.quote: HSCJ223733+005015 : In this case, YL and S19 models contain two diametrically opposite images and these models are qualitatively similar and accurate. As a result, these two methods also agree on the predictions of all the three parameters. The model of S23 (see their fig. B.27) does not seem correct with an arc modelled incorrectly in the south-east direction. However, the results of S23 for _ E and PA are within the errorbars from the network.

gmw_00004219.name: HSCJ230335+003703
gmw_00004219.overview: HSCJ230335+003703 is a quad gravitational lens system [arXiv:2404.18897]. Accurate modeling of the system reveals four distinct lensed images of a compact background source [arXiv:2404.18897].

sources.summary: The paper describes HSCJ230335+003703 as a quad lens where one model captures four compact-source images, other models are less accurate, and position angles agree with network errors.

quotes.quote: HSCJ230335+003703 : For this quad lens system, S19 model looks quite accurate with distinct four images of a compact source. The lens model of YL does not seem accurate. It has an extended source and the model looks more circular than the actual configuration of the lensed images. The model of S23 (see their fig. B.28) also does not do justice to the actual configuration.The inferred PAs from YL, S19 and S23 agree well and are within the errrorbars from the network.

gmw_00004220.name: And XV
gmw_00004220.overview: And XV is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1606.01207, arXiv:1710.09038] that contains no detectable neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1205.5235]. The galaxy ceased all star formation by a lookback time of 6 Gyr [arXiv:1606.01207] and hosts a population of RR Lyrae and anomalous Cepheid variable stars [arXiv:1710.09038].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry for And XV, using resolved stellar populations to compare its CMD coverage, horizontal-branch features, and early star-formation history with other M31 dwarf spheroidals.

quotes.quote: The positions of the observed fields are shown in Figure f4 . As can be seen in Figure f4 , for And I and And II the ACS field covers only part of the galaxy within the half light radius and the parallel WFC3 field contains many member stars. Some member stars were detected in the WFC3 field of And III, but not nearly enough to produce a SFH with reasonable uncertainties. For And XV, And XVI, and And XXVIII, the ACS field of view covers most of the galaxy out to the half light radius and the WFC3 field is distant enough that a minimal number of member stars are expected.

Figure f5 shows that the ACS photometry reaches below the oldest main-sequence turn-offs allowing for very strong constraints on the oldest epochs of star formation. These observations of And I, And II, and And III are 2.5 mag fainter than the deepest CMDs previously obtained for these galaxies dacosta1996, dacosta2000, dacosta2002 . There are several notable features that are common to all six ACS CMDs. All six galaxies show the presence of blue horizontal branch stars (BHBs). This is generally taken as evidence of the presence of stars with ages comparable to the Milky Way GC population see discussion in gallart2005 . To date, all galaxies that have been observed with sufficient photometric depth have shown at least some star formation at the earliest times.

Figure f7 shows a variety of SFHs. However, all six SFHs have the common properties of starting their star formation early (there is significant star formation in the earliest time bin for all six galaxies), having at least 50 of their stars formed by a lookback time of 9 Gyr, and then ceasing all star formation by a lookback time of 6 Gyr. And XV and And XXVIII show the oldest mean stellar populations of the sample, but both are clearly not consistent with a single age population and are clearly not consistent with forming all of their stars before the epoch of reionization (as indicated in Figure f7 by the shaded region).

sources.summary: The HST census treats And XV as an M31 dSph, detects 117 RRLs, finds anomalous Cepheids, and links its richer old population to its star-formation history.

quotes.quote: Under the ISLAndS Initial Star formation and Lifetimes of Andromeda Satellites project (based on very deep, multi-epoch HST ACS and WFC3 data), six M31 dSph satellite companion galaxies were observed: And I, And II, And III, And XV, And XVI and And XXVIII.
We detect 870 RR Lyrae (RRL) stars in the fields of And I (296), II (251), III (111), XV (117), XVI (8), XXVIII (87).

RRLs were detected in all six galaxies with as few as 8 (in And XVI Excluding the RRL star AndXVI-V001 (V0 in Monelli2016 ) as it is a candidate M31 halo field star not belonging to And XVI. ) and as a many as 296 (in And I). The striking difference in the number of RRL between And XVI and And XV, despite having a similar mass, can be explained as a consequence of their different SFHs: the mass fraction already in place at old ages (10 Gyr ago) was only about 50 in And XVI, while it was 90 in And XV (see Figure 7 in Skillman2017 ). A few (3-4) ACs are present in And II, III, XV, and XXVIII, but none have been detected in And I nor in And XVI.

sources.summary: The paper treats And XV as an M31 dwarf spheroidal in the bridge survey and reports no associated H I, with a 5-sigma mass limit of 9.3e3 solar masses.

quotes.quote: And XV And XV lies at a distance of 0.77 Mpc with a heliocentric radial velocity V _ HEL =-323 1.4 km s ^ 1 (V _ LSR =-322 km s ^ 1 ) and a half-light radius of less than 2 \ Letarte2009, Tollerud2011 . No emission was detected at the appropriate velocity to a 5 upper limit N _ HI 3.6 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 for a 25 km s ^ 1 line width. At the distance of And XV this is equivalent to a 5 limit on the \ mass of 9.3 10^3 M _ within a GBT beam.
